  * ## Ticket: Task: Lookup contract and anchor resolution {#521}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 09:39:41 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 10:33:21 UTC

    Build the line→item lookup once at parse time:
    * `_BY_LINE: dict[int, Item]` — `line_offset` → node for every
      parsed node.
    * Whatever auxiliary structure is needed to support `_node_at_line`
      efficiently (sorted-keys list + bisect is the obvious choice;
      micro-implementation is up to the implementer).

    Implement `_node_at_line(n) -> Item | None`:
    * Exact match in `_BY_LINE` wins.
    * Otherwise return the node with the greatest `line_offset` ≤ `n`.
    * Return `None` if `n` precedes every parsed node.

    Anchor resolution (called after `_parse`, before `Browser(...)`):
    * No anchor → `initial_scope = root_id`.
    * All-digit anchor → `int(anchor)` → `_node_at_line(n)`. If
      `None`, fall through to root.
    * Non-digit anchor → three-tier heading-title match. Define
      `display_title(node)` = source line with `#` markers AND
      leading/trailing whitespace stripped (used for matching only;
      the stored `title` keeps `#`s). For each tier, scan headings
      in source order and take the first hit:
        1. Exact: `display_title == anchor`.
        2. Prefix: `display_title.startswith(anchor)`.
        3. Substring: `anchor in display_title`.
      No match in any tier → stderr warning, fall through to root.

    Done when: `recipes/browse-md FILE.md#0` opens at the first
    item, `#999999` falls back to root, `#some-heading` opens scoped
    into the matching heading.

    * ## Comments {#521:comments}

      * Implemented _BY_LINE / _LINES_SORTED (populated by new _build_line_index helper, called after _build_nodes in main()), _node_at_line (bisect_right against sorted line keys, exact-match wins), _display_title (lstrip '#' + strip whitespace, defensive fallback to title.strip), _resolve_anchor (empty → root, digit → _node_at_line fall-back-to-root, else three-tier scan exact/prefix/substring over headings sorted by line_offset, stderr warning on miss). Wired into main() via initial_scope=_resolve_anchor(_ANCHOR, root.id) on BrowserConfig. Added 25 new tests across TestNodeAtLine (7), TestDisplayTitle (6), TestResolveAnchor (12). Total test_browse_md count: 75 (was 50), all passing. CLI smoke test under pty: README.md → root scope; #0 → first heading; #'Default keys' → #76 (heading match); #nonexistent → root + stderr warning. {#521:comment:536}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Preview pipeline and m / M toggles {#522}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 09:39:41 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 10:41:30 UTC

    `get_preview(item_id)`:
    * Root id (no `#`) → return `_FILE_TEXT`.
    * `<path>#<n>` id → resolve via `_BY_LINE` (or `_node_at_line`
      fallback for off-grid anchors), return
      `_FILE_TEXT[byte_offset : byte_offset + byte_size]`.

    md2ansi_lib gate (mirror browse-fs / browse-claude exactly):
    * `try: from md2ansi_lib import md2ansi as _md2ansi_fn` with
      `if not callable: _md2ansi_fn = None` fallback.
    * `_MD_COLOR = _md2ansi_fn is not None` — default on when
      available.
    * Module-level `_BROWSER = None`, set in `main()` after
      `Browser(...)`, used for `preview_width` lookup.
    * In `get_preview`, when `_MD_COLOR` is on, pipe slice through
      `_md2ansi_fn(text, line_width=(_BROWSER.preview_width or 80))`.
      Wrap the call in `try/except` and fall back to the raw slice on
      any error (matches browse-fs convention).

    `_action_toggle_md(ctx)` action bound to `m` (only if
    `_md2ansi_fn` is not None):
    * Flip `_MD_COLOR`.
    * `ctx.drop_preview_cache()`.
    * `ctx.message(f'md preview: ...')`.

    `_action_md_preview(ctx)` action bound to `M` — always available:
    * Lift `_resolve_md_pager()` and the `_action_md_preview` body
      from browse-claude (`recipes/browse-claude:4401-4467`).

    Done when: `m` flips the preview between rendered and raw;
    preview of a heading shows the heading + all descendants
    rendered with md2ansi colours; preview of root shows the whole
    file.

    * ## Comments {#522:comments}

      * Implemented preview pipeline + m/M toggles. {#522:comment:537}

        Recipe changes (recipes/browse-md):
        - Added md2ansi_lib gate (try-import + callable fallback) mirroring browse-fs/browse-claude.
        - Module globals: _MD_COLOR (defaults on when md2ansi_lib imports), _BROWSER (set in main).
        - get_preview rewritten: root id -> whole _FILE_TEXT; <path>#<n> -> _BY_LINE / _node_at_line fallback -> byte slice; unknown -> ''. md2ansi pipe wrapped in try/except for graceful fallback.
        - _action_toggle_md (bound to m when md2ansi_lib available): flips _MD_COLOR, drops preview cache, messages new state.
        - _resolve_md_pager + _action_md_preview lifted from browse-claude (always-on M).
        - main() builds actions list, passes to BrowserConfig, stashes _BROWSER for preview-width lookup. Added Action to imports.
        Tests (test/unit/test_browse_md.py): added 21 tests in 3 new classes (TestGetPreview, TestToggleMd, TestResolveMdPager); 96/96 green.
        CLI smoke: pty harness on README.md verified rendered <-> raw flip on 'm' and md2ansi|less -RS handoff on 'M'.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Source actions V / E with multi-select range merging {#523}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 09:39:41 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 10:47:19 UTC

    Lift the shape of browse-claude's `_run_source_command` /
    `_action_view_source` / `_action_edit_source` (lines
    4323-4392), swapping the per-line extractor for byte-range
    slicing.

    Target resolution:
    * `targets = ctx.targets` (handles selection-or-cursor).
    * Walk targets, classifying each as root (id has no `#`) or
      non-root.
    * If any target is root → behave as if only root was selected:
      `ctx.run_external(cmd + [<abs_path>])`. Edits via `E` persist
      naturally.
    * All targets non-root → see range merging below.

    Range merging for non-root multi-select:
    * Collect `(byte_offset, byte_size)` from each target Item.
    * Sort ascending by `byte_offset`.
    * Merge overlapping or adjacent ranges: walk the sorted list,
      extend current range when next range's `byte_offset` ≤
      current end. Result is non-overlapping, in file order.
    * Concatenate `_FILE_TEXT[bo:bo+bs]` for each merged range. If
      the previous slice doesn't end in `\n`, insert one before the
      next slice; otherwise no separator (just adjacency).
    * Write to `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.md',
      delete=False, encoding='utf-8', errors='surrogateescape')`.
    * `ctx.run_external(cmd + [tmp_path])`. Unlink in `finally`.

    `_action_view_source` → uses `$PAGER` or `less -R`.
    `_action_edit_source` → uses `$EDITOR` or `vim`.
    Both call a shared `_run_source_command(ctx, env_var, default)`.

    Bind: `Action('V', ..., _action_view_source, 'none')`,
    `Action('E', ..., _action_edit_source, 'none')`. `'none'` gate
    so they fire on scope_root rows too (where `ctx.cursor` is None).

    Done when: `V` on root pages the original file; `V` on a single
    non-root item pages a temp file with that item's source slice;
    `V` with three non-root items multi-selected pages a temp file
    containing the merged/sorted ranges (no duplication, file
    order); `E` on root edits the original file in place.

    * ## Comments {#523:comments}

      * Implemented V/E source actions in recipes/browse-md. {#523:comment:538}

        Added helpers near the bottom of the recipe (just above main()):
        - _split_cmd(s): shell-split via shlex (verbatim from browse-claude:4395-4398).
        - _merge_ranges(ranges): sort + dedupe + adjacency-merge (bo, bs) tuples.
        - _write_range_excerpts(ranges): concatenate _FILE_TEXT slices into a tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.md', delete=False, encoding='utf-8', errors='surrogateescape'); inserts '\n' between consecutive slices only when the previous slice doesn't end in '\n'.
        - _run_source_command(ctx, env_var, default): ctx.targets → empty short-circuits; any root in targets → ctx.run_external(cmd + [root.id]); else merge ranges, write tempfile, run_external, unlink in finally swallowing OSError.
        - _action_view_source / _action_edit_source: thin wrappers over PAGER/less -R and EDITOR/vim.
        Wiring in main(): appended Action('V', ..., 'none') and Action('E', ..., 'none') to the actions list — 'none' gate so they fire on the scope_root row where ctx.cursor is None.
        Tests: added 3 new test classes to test/unit/test_browse_md.py — TestMergeRanges (9), TestWriteRangeExcerpts (6), TestRunSourceCommand (8). Total: 119 tests, all green.
        Smoke checks:
        - Scratch script against README.md with a fake ctx confirmed: root V → less -R <abs path>; single heading → less -R <tmp.md> with that section; 3 out-of-order non-root targets → tempfile with merged ranges in file order matching the expected concatenation; root E → vim <abs path>; empty targets → no run_external call.
        - PTY launch of 'browse-tui --run-py recipes/browse-md README.md' loads cleanly with V/E wired (no traceback).

      children (single-file parse is cheap; no per-node

* ## Ticket: Task: browse-md UX refinements {#541}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-28 20:01:32 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-28 21:46:21 UTC

  Three follow-up refinements after epic #517 landed:
  1. Strip the redundant `#` / list-marker prefix from tree-row titles
     — the `[h3]` / `[ul]` tag already conveys kind in colour.
  2. Lists default OFF in the tree (headings only); `-l` / `--list` /
     `--lists` flag turns the existing behaviour back on.
  3. Heading-anchor match (the `FILE.md#name` form) becomes
     case-insensitive across all three tiers.

  All three changes are visible to the user and need tests.

  * ## Comments {#541:comments}

    * All three refinements landed: #542 (strip marker prefix), #543 (lists default off), #544 (case-insensitive anchor match). Closing epic. {#541:comment:548}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Strip marker prefix from tree-row titles {#542}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 20:01:32 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 21:33:59 UTC

    For heading Items, set `title` to the heading text with the leading
    `#`s AND any whitespace after them stripped. Example:
    `## My **bold** heading` → title `My **bold** heading`.

    For list-item Items, strip the marker (`-` / `*` / `+` / `\d+.`)
    and the following whitespace from the title. Example:
    `- foo bar` → `foo bar`. Example: `1. item one` → `item one`.

    `_display_title` currently strips `#`s for the anchor-match key —
    once stored titles are pre-stripped, `_display_title` is redundant
    and can collapse to a `.strip()` (or just the raw title). Decide
    in implementation; either way, the public behaviour is the
    anchor-match flow keeps working.

    Update the existing tests in `test/unit/test_browse_md.py` that
    assert the old titles (e.g. `TestBuildNodes` expectations for
    headings/list rows, `TestDisplayTitle` may simplify). Add a
    targeted test that confirms a heading-with-formatting
    (`## **bold**`) keeps the formatting markers in the title — only
    the leading `#`s and the immediate whitespace are stripped.

    Done when: tree row for `## Foo` shows `Foo`, tree row for `- bar`
    shows `bar`, all existing tests updated and passing.

    * ## Comments {#542:comments}

      * Implemented title-stripping for headings and list items via two new module-level regexes (_HEADING_PREFIX_RE = r'^#+\s*', _LIST_PREFIX_RE = r'^([-*+]|\d+\.)\s+') used in _build_nodes. Collapsed _display_title to title.strip() with updated docstring (kept as a thin wrapper rather than removed so the anchor-match call sites stay stable for future evolution). Updated TestBuildNodes + TestNodeAtLine + TestDisplayTitle expectations; added new TestTitleStripping class (9 tests) covering: heading inline-formatting preserved, trailing # preserved, -/+/* list markers stripped, 1./42. ordered markers stripped, *** in heading body preserved (parser-precedence sanity), internal whitespace preserved. Test count: 132 -> 138 (added 9 in TestTitleStripping, trimmed 3 from TestDisplayTitle which became trivial after pre-stripping). All green. Smoke check under pty: 'browse-tui --run-py recipes/browse-md README.md' shows tree row '[h1] browse-tui' with no leading #; anchor 'README.md#Install' resolves correctly and opens preview at the Install section. {#542:comment:545}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Lists default off; -l / --list / --lists turns them on {#543}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 20:01:32 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 21:42:23 UTC

    Argv parsing: in addition to the existing positional, accept
    `-l` / `--list` / `--lists` (any of the three forms) as a boolean
    flag. The presence of the flag means "parse list items as tree
    elements"; absence means "skip list items, headings only".

    Implementation choice: cleanest is to add a module-level
    `_INCLUDE_LISTS = False` and gate list emission. Either skip the
    `_walk_list` fan-out inside `_parse`, OR drop list events in
    `_build_nodes`. Spec-wise it doesn't matter; pick the simpler one.
    The masking properties of the `list` rule in `_PARSER_RE` must
    NOT change — code-fence vs list-line precedence still matters
    even when lists aren't emitted.

    The flag must be parsed BEFORE the positional. Order in argv
    should be flexible: `browse-md -l FILE.md` and
    `browse-md FILE.md -l` should both work. Use a small hand-rolled
    pop-flag pass (similar to browse-claude's `_pop_flag` /
    `_pop_value`) rather than reaching for argparse.

    Update `_HELP_INTRO` and the module docstring to document the flag.

    Tests:
    - `TestArgvFlag`: parse argv with and without the flag, in
      various positions, confirm `_INCLUDE_LISTS` is set correctly.
    - `TestBuildNodes` (existing): split into the lists-on case
      (existing fixture, lists present) and a lists-off case
      (`_INCLUDE_LISTS=False`, list items absent from the tree, but
      the headings unchanged).

    Done when: `browse-md FILE.md` shows headings only; `browse-md -l
    FILE.md` shows headings + lists; both forms work with
    `--list` and `--lists` aliases.

    * ## Comments {#543:comments}

      * Implemented -l/--list/--lists flag. {#543:comment:546}

        Recipe changes (recipes/browse-md):
        - Added module-level _INCLUDE_LISTS = False (production default: headings only).
        - Added _pop_flag(name, alts=()) helper, patterned after browse-claude's _pop_flag — walks sys.argv removing every match of name or any alts entry; returns True if any match was found.
        - Gated _walk_list inside _parse on _INCLUDE_LISTS. Regex masking unchanged: list span still consumed so code-fence vs list-line precedence is preserved.
        - main() now calls _pop_flag('-l', alts=('--list','--lists')) BEFORE reading the positional, so argv ordering is flexible.
        - Updated _USAGE, _HELP_INTRO, and module docstring to document the flag.
        Test changes (test/unit/test_browse_md.py):
        - _load_recipe() now takes include_lists=True (default) and sets m._INCLUDE_LISTS after exec — most existing tests assume lists-on.
        - TestArgvFlag (new): 7 tests covering absent flag, short before/after positional, --list, --lists, repeated flags, and unrelated args.
        - TestBuildNodesNoLists (new): 9 tests covering events-have-only-headings, no ul/ol in tree, no ul/ol in _BY_ID, heading ids unchanged, heading byte_offsets unchanged, H2a section subsumes list bytes, last section runs to EOF, has_children reflects no list kids.
        - TestHelpIntro: updated test_contains_usage_form to expect 'browse-md [-l] FILE.md'; added test_mentions_lists_flag.
        Test count: 138 → 155 in browse-md (17 added). Full suite: 1848 tests pass.
        Smoke check:
        - 'browse-md README.md' → 9 headings, 0 list items in events.
        - 'browse-md -l README.md' → 9 headings, 5 list items.
        - All argv permutations (-l before/after positional, --list, --lists, repeated) correctly pop the flag and leave the positional at sys.argv[1].

  * ## Ticket: Task: Heading anchor match is case-insensitive {#544}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 20:01:32 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 21:46:03 UTC

    All three tiers in `_resolve_anchor` (exact, prefix, substring)
    compare against `_display_title(h).lower()` and `anchor.lower()`.
    Stored title and stored anchor strings are NOT lowered — only
    the comparison key is. The stderr "no heading matches" warning
    should still echo the user's original `anchor` (preserving case).

    `TestResolveAnchor` (existing) updated:
    - Existing exact-/prefix-/substring-match tests still pass.
    - New tests: anchor `'goals'` matches a heading titled `'Goals'`
      via exact tier; anchor `'GOA'` matches via prefix; anchor `'OAL'`
      matches via substring.

    Done when: `browse-md FILE.md#goals` opens at the `Goals`
    heading even though the heading text uses a capital G.

      * Made _resolve_anchor case-insensitive: lower anchor once into 'key' and compare against _display_title(h).lower() in all three tiers. Stored titles untouched; stderr warning still echoes original casing via {anchor!r}. Updated spec 'Argument parsing' section: 'Match is case-insensitive' (was case-sensitive). Added 5 new TestResolveAnchor tests (exact-lower, exact-allcaps, prefix, substring, warning-preserves-case). Tests: 155 -> 160, all green. Smoke (in-process against README.md): 'install', 'INSTALL', 'Install' all resolve to '## Install' (line 8). {#544:comment:547}

* ## Ticket: Task: browse-md bugs {#549}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-28 21:47:44 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-28 22:18:51 UTC

  Three user-reported bugs after the UX refinements in #541.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Non-existent file argument prints usage instead of an error {#550}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 21:47:44 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 22:02:56 UTC

    `browse-md /no/such/path.md` currently prints just the usage line
    and exits 2. That's the same output as `browse-md` (no args at
    all), which is confusing — the user gave an argument that just
    happens to point at a missing file.

    Expected: distinct error message that names the missing path,
    e.g. `browse-md: no such file: /no/such/path.md` (then exit 2,
    optionally followed by the usage line on a separate stderr
    write).

    Fix in `recipes/browse-md`'s `main()` argv-parsing block. Add a
    test in `TestArgvFlag` (or a new `TestArgvErrors` class) that
    captures stderr and asserts the error message format.

    * ## Comments {#550:comments}

      * Replaced bare _USAGE fallthrough with _die('no such file: ...', with_usage=False). Echoes the user's input pre-expanduser. Added test_missing_file_path_reports_path_without_usage. {#550:comment:556}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Unknown options and extra positionals should error, not be ignored {#551}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 21:47:44 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 22:02:56 UTC

    `browse-md --bogus FILE.md` and `browse-md FILE.md extra` both
    silently accept the unrecognised tokens today (the flag-popper
    leaves them in `sys.argv`, the positional grabs `sys.argv[1]`,
    everything else is dropped). The user gets no signal that their
    command line was wrong.

    Expected: any unrecognised `-` / `--` token, or any extra
    positional beyond the file, prints an error to stderr (e.g.
    `browse-md: unrecognised option: --bogus` or
    `browse-md: unexpected argument: extra`) and exits 2. The usage
    line should follow.

    Implementation: after `_pop_flag` consumes the known flags,
    inspect what's left in `sys.argv[1:]`. Exactly one positional
    is expected; anything else is an error. Flag-shaped leftovers
    (anything starting with `-`) should be reported as
    "unrecognised option" specifically.

    Tests in `TestArgvFlag` / new `TestArgvErrors`: various malformed
    argvs assert the error-and-exit behaviour. Capture stderr.

    * ## Comments {#551:comments}

      * Added _die helper and post-_pop_flag leftover-arg validation: '-'-prefixed leftovers => 'unrecognised option', extra non-'-' positionals => 'unexpected argument', zero positionals => 'missing FILE.md[#anchor]'. New TestArgvErrors covers all paths. {#551:comment:557}

  * ## Ticket: Task: V / E on the root row crashes with AttributeError on byte_offset {#552}

        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 21:47:44 UTC
        updated: 2026-05-28 21:56:54 UTC

    Pressing `V` or `E` on the synthetic root row (the scope-root
    pseudo-row at the top of the listing pane) raises:

    ```
    File "recipes/browse-md", line 931, in _run_source_command
      ranges = [(t.byte_offset, t.byte_size) for t in targets]
    AttributeError: 'Item' object has no attribute 'byte_offset'
    ```

    Diagnosis: `_run_source_command` checks each target for
    `kind == 'root'` via `getattr(item, 'kind', None) == 'root'`. The
    framework's scope_root pseudo-row is a different Item from our
    underlying root Item — it doesn't carry our hidden `kind` /
    `byte_offset` / `byte_size` attrs. The classifier therefore
    misses it and the function falls through to the byte-range
    branch, which then explodes on the missing `byte_offset`.

    Expected: `V` on the scope-root row pages the original file (as
    documented). `E` on it opens the original file in `$EDITOR`.

    Fix options to investigate:
    * Detect the scope-root pseudo-row explicitly. The framework
      either exposes a flag on the pseudo-item (look in `docs/api.md`
      or `src-tui/`) or the pseudo-item's `id` matches the absolute
      file path — same id as our real root Item, so a lookup in
      `_BY_ID` and an `is`/equality check against the stored root
      would work.
    * Alternatively: classify targets defensively. Treat any item
      that's not in `_BY_ID`, or any item that maps to our root, as
      "root" for the purposes of `_run_source_command`.

    Tests: extend `TestRunSourceCommand` with a target-list that
    contains a "scope-root-like" Item (no `kind`, no `byte_offset`)
    and assert the function takes the root-wins path. Also a UI-
    level smoke check via the existing pty harness if one's
    available.

    This is the highest-priority of the three bugs (a crash, not a
    misleading exit code).

    * ## Comments {#552:comments}

      * Reproduced via test_loader: a synthetic Item with id=path but no kind/byte_offset triggered the AttributeError on line 934. Root cause: browse-md always launches scoped (initial_scope=root.id), the framework's _find_item can't locate the root in any cached _children list (root is the tree apex, never a child), so visible_items fabricates a scope-root pseudo-Item with id=path, has_children=True, synthetic=True but NO kind/byte_offset/byte_size — the recipe-attached hidden attrs the V/E classifier relied on. {#552:comment:554}

        Fix: in _run_source_command, classify root by id-match (it.id == _ROOT_PATH) in addition to kind=='root'. Both checks needed: kind=='root' handles the real root Item path; the id check catches the framework's scope-root pseudo-row. Use root_target.id (not _ROOT_PATH) in the dispatch so the tests that don't set _ROOT_PATH continue to work — they're equal in production but root_target.id is the invariant carried by both Item flavours.
        Tests: extended TestRunSourceCommand with a _ScopeRootPseudoItem helper class (no kind/byte_offset/byte_size, mirrors the framework's fabrication) and two cases: pseudo alone routes to root path, pseudo mixed with non-root still wins root. setUp/tearDown also patch and restore _ROOT_PATH and _BY_ID per ticket instruction. 162 tests pass (was 160; +2).
        CLI smoke under tmux: launched recipes/browse-md README.md, pressed V on the top (scope-root) row — pager opened on the file (PAGER=cat output rendered), no traceback. Pressed E on the top row with EDITOR=true — fired and returned cleanly, no traceback. Pressed V on first heading (non-root path) — still goes through the byte-range merge branch, no traceback.

* ## Ticket: Task: Multiple markdown files on the command line {#553}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-28 21:50:44 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-28 22:18:44 UTC

  Today `browse-md` takes exactly one positional. Extend it to
  accept multiple file paths, surfaced as siblings under the root.

  Argv shape:
  ```
  browse-md [-l] FILE1.md [FILE2.md ...]
  browse-md [-l] FILE1.md#anchor [FILE2.md ...]    # anchor still
                                                   # applies to FILE1
  ```

  Each file is parsed independently with the existing pipeline
  (parser → `_build_nodes` → byte-to-line index). Each file
  becomes a top-level subtree under the synthetic root: a per-file
  Item that owns the file's headings (and lists, when `-l` is on).

  Tree model with N files:
  ```
  <synthetic-root>          id = something stable, e.g. literal "(multi)"
                            or the joined paths; title = something
                            short. Preview = ??? (see below.)
  ├── FILE1.md              id = abs path of FILE1
  │   ├── # H1
  │   ├── # H2
  │   └── ...
  ├── FILE2.md
  │   └── ...
  └── ...
  ```

  Per-file Items now occupy what was previously the single synthetic
  root. Each carries its own `_FILE_TEXT`, `_BY_LINE`, etc.

  Open questions to resolve in implementation (the design doc can
  stay quiet — these are recipe-level details):

  * **`_FILE_TEXT` plurality.** The cleanest refactor is a single
    `_FILES: dict[str, FileState]` keyed by abs-path, where
    `FileState` carries `text`, `by_line`, `lines_sorted`, etc. The
    module-level globals `_FILE_TEXT` / `_BY_LINE` / `_LINES_SORTED`
    go away — every lookup site routes through `_FILES[path]`.
  * **Root preview.** With one file the root previews the whole
    file. With N files, root preview should probably show a
    summary (`FILE1.md  (12 headings)\nFILE2.md  (5 headings)`),
    not a concatenation. Pick whichever fits the existing preview
    flow and is least surprising.
  * **`V` / `E` on root.** No single original file to open. Either
    error gracefully (`browse-md: V/E on multi-root is ambiguous`),
    or open the file the cursor's parent chain belongs to. Simplest
    sensible choice: take the first file passed on the command line.
  * **Reload (Ctrl-R).** Each file reparses independently. `_reparse`
    should iterate over `_FILES`.
  * **Anchor on positional.** `#anchor` only attaches to the first
    file (we don't bother trying to disambiguate per-file anchors
    in argv). Document this in `_HELP_INTRO`.
  * **`_node_at_line` and IDs.** Item ids are still `<abs_path>#<n>`
    — already unique across files since abs paths differ. The
    line-bisect lookup needs to consult the right file's index
    (`_FILES[path].lines_sorted`).
  * **Help text.** Update `_HELP_INTRO`, module docstring, `_USAGE`.

  Tests:
  * `TestArgvMulti`: argv with two and three files, with `-l`,
    with `-l` interleaved (`-l FILE1 FILE2` and `FILE1 -l FILE2`),
    with `FILE1#anchor FILE2` (anchor only on first).
  * `TestBuildNodesMulti`: build with two files, assert the root
    has two children whose `id` is the abs path of each file,
    each subtree contains the expected headings.
  * `TestReloadMulti`: modify both files on disk, Ctrl-R, both
    reparsed.

  Done when: `browse-md a.md b.md` opens with two top-level
  subtrees; navigating, previewing, and `V`/`E` all work; `m`/`M`
  still toggle preview rendering; existing single-file tests
  still pass.

  * ## Comments {#553:comments}

    * Amendment (2026-05-28): If no anchors are present in argv, the initial view is the normal root. Whenever at least one positional carries an anchor, the initial scope drills into the first ANCHORED file (in argv order — not necessarily the first positional). So 'browse-md a.md b.md' opens at root; 'browse-md a.md b.md#foo' drills into b.md#foo; 'browse-md a.md#x b.md#y' drills into a.md#x (first anchor wins). _HELP_INTRO should document this. {#553:comment:555}

    * Implemented multi-file support for browse-md. {#553:comment:558}

      State refactor:
      - Added FileState dataclass holding per-file (text, line_starts, by_id, by_line, lines_sorted, file_root).
      - _FILES dict[str, FileState] keyed by abs path; _INPUT_FILES list of (path, anchor) tuples preserves argv order.
      - Synthetic multi-root with id='(multi)' and kind='multi-root' built only when len(_FILES)>1; single-file mode uses the per-file root as Browser root (legacy behaviour).
      - _BY_ID is the aggregate index (multi-root + every file's by_id).
      - Per-Item back-reference: item.file_path = path lets get_preview slice the right file.
      - Legacy single-file shims (_FILE_TEXT, _BY_LINE, _LINES_SORTED, _ROOT_PATH) mirror the FIRST file's state for back-compat with tests that populate them directly.
      - _classify_id(node_id) is the single classifier — returns ('multi-root'|'file-root'|'content'|'unknown', path_or_None). Used by get_preview and _run_source_command.
      Argv:
      - main() walks every positional, splits on first '#', validates each file. Missing file surfaces the user's input verbatim.
      - First-anchor-wins: first non-empty anchor in argv order seeds initial_scope; subsequent anchors recorded but ignored.
      Anchor:
      - _resolve_anchor(anchor, file_root_id) — derives the right file_state from _FILES, scans only that file's headings. Legacy single-file callers (tests) fall back to module-level _BY_ID with file_path filtering.
      - _node_at_line(n, file_state=None) — file_state=None falls back to legacy globals.
      V/E semantics:
      - Multi-root target -> opens FIRST input file (documented in _HELP_INTRO).
      - Per-file root target -> opens that file directly.
      - Same-file non-root targets -> merged byte-range tempfile.
      - Cross-file multi-select -> FIRST argv file's targets only (documented).
      - Scope-root pseudo-Item with id=_ROOT_ID classifies as multi-root.
      Tests: 207 total (168 existing + 39 new across TestArgvMulti, TestBuildMulti, TestAnchorMulti, TestGetPreviewMulti, TestRunSourceCommandMulti, TestReloadMulti, TestClassifyId). All green. One existing test (test_extra_positional) updated to test_extra_positional_nonexistent_file since 'extra' now treated as another file positional.
      Full unit-test suite (1900 tests across the project) also green.
      CLI smoke (pseudo-TTY): two files render as multi-root '2 files' + two subtrees; anchor on file 2 drills into that file's section; anchor on file 1 drills into file 1; single-file mode unchanged; missing file mid-list dies with user-input path.

* ## Ticket: Task: Drop the synthetic multi-root; files ARE the root entries {#559}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-28 22:23:05 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-28 22:33:18 UTC

  Today multi-file mode wraps the per-file roots under a synthetic
  `_ROOT_ID = '(multi)'` Item. That's an extra layer the user has
  to step into. browse-claude's pattern is the right one: pass
  `root_id=None` to BrowserConfig, and have `get_children(None)`
  return the top-level entries directly.

  Apply that to browse-md:
  * `BrowserConfig(root_id=None, ...)` always.
  * `get_children(None, *, reload=False)` returns the list of
    per-file roots in argv order.
  * Drop `_ROOT_ID`, the `'multi-root'` Item, and the
    `'multi-root'` branch of `_classify_id`.

  Single-file UX must be preserved: with one file and no anchor,
  the browser should start scoped INTO the file (showing its
  headings), exactly as it does today. Implementation:
  * Multi-file, no anchor → `initial_scope = None` (top-level
    list of files visible).
  * Multi-file, first anchor on file X → `initial_scope =
    _resolve_anchor(...)` against file X (unchanged).
  * Single-file, no anchor → `initial_scope = file_root.id`
    (the file root, same visual as before).
  * Single-file, anchor → `initial_scope = _resolve_anchor(...)`
    (unchanged).

  Multi-root preview, multi-root `V`/`E` "open first file" rules,
  and the cross-file multi-select "first-file wins" rule all go
  away — they only existed because of the synthetic root. With
  files at the top, there's no row that needs ambiguous-target
  resolution.

  Spec doc: update the "Tree model" section to remove the
  synthetic multi-root level. Help text: update accordingly.

  Tests:
  * `TestBuildMulti`: assert no `'multi-root'` Item; assert
    `get_children(None)` returns the per-file roots in argv order.
  * `TestAnchorMulti`: no-anchor case asserts `initial_scope is
    None`. Single-file no-anchor case asserts `initial_scope ==
    file_root.id`. Anchored cases unchanged.
  * `TestGetPreviewMulti`: drop the multi-root summary test;
    `get_preview(None)` is the framework's concern, not ours
    (depending on framework — if it asks, return ''). Per-file
    root preview = whole file (unchanged).
  * `TestRunSourceCommandMulti`: drop the multi-root cases; the
    per-file-root cases stay (V/E on a file row opens that file).
  * `_classify_id` tests: drop the multi-root case.
  * `TestArgvMulti` / `TestReloadMulti`: unchanged behaviour.

  Done when: `browse-md a.md b.md` shows two top-level rows
  (no `(multi)` row above them); single-file `browse-md a.md`
  opens scoped into the file's headings exactly as before;
  anchor flows still work.

  * ## Comments {#559:comments}

    * Implemented. Dropped synthetic multi-root from recipes/browse-md. {#559:comment:560}

      Files changed:
      - recipes/browse-md: removed _ROOT_ID, _build_multi_root, _multi_root_preview, _pick_path_for_root; added _write_range_excerpts_grouped; updated _classify_id (dropped multi-root branch); rewrote _run_source_command (no multi-root short-circuit, cross-file selections honoured); main() now passes BrowserConfig(root_id=None) with initial_scope = None / file_root.id / resolved-anchor per the four cases; get_children(None) returns per-file roots in argv order; get_preview(None) returns ''; module docstring + _HELP_INTRO updated.
      - test/unit/test_browse_md.py: rewrote TestBuildMulti (asserts no multi-root in _BY_ID, get_children(None) returns per-file roots in argv order); TestAnchorMulti (no-anchor multi-file -> None; single-file no-anchor -> file_root.id); TestGetPreviewMulti (drop multi-root case, add get_preview(None) == ''); TestRunSourceCommandMulti (drop multi-root cases, add cross-file groups in argv order with separator); TestReloadMulti / TestReload updated to use node_id=None for the reload trigger; TestClassifyId no longer has multi-root case.
      - Spec doc (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-browse-md-design.md): already single-file scoped, no multi-root prose to remove.
      Cross-file V/E choice: honour cross-file selections. Group targets by file in argv order, merge ranges per file, and concatenate the resulting slices into one temp .md file with a '\n===== <basename> =====\n' header between groups (no header before the first group). Documented in _HELP_INTRO and the _action_view_source / _action_edit_source / _run_source_command docstrings.
      Tests: 207 passing (python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_md).
      CLI smoke (pty driver):
      - 'browse-md README.md docs/recipes.md' -> top-level rows are 'README.md' and 'recipes.md', no '(multi)' or '2 files' summary row above them.
      - 'browse-md README.md' -> opens with the file expanded (▼ README.md) showing its headings, single-file UX preserved.
      - 'browse-md README.md docs/recipes.md#browse-procs' -> opens scoped into the browse-procs heading of the second file.

* ## Ticket: Task: First preview fetch on startup runs with width=0, recipes fall back to 80 {#561}

      updated: 2026-05-28 22:52:45 UTC
      status: done
      created: 2026-05-28 22:51:12 UTC

  BUG
  ====
  On initial load, the first entry's preview pane is rendered as if it were 80 columns
  wide regardless of the actual terminal width.

  Root cause (`040-state.py`, `Browser.run`):

    - line 5897:  ``self.post(self._do_initial_fetch)``
    - line 5900:  ``run_until_idle(0.5)``  — wait for tree
    - line 5903:  ``self._update_preview_for_cursor()``  — request preview
    - line 5906:  ``run_until_idle(0.2)``  — preview worker calls
                  ``get_preview`` here; recipes read ``preview_width``
                  which is still ``0`` (no paint has computed the layout
                  yet), so the ``or 80`` fallback kicks in and the markdown
                  wrap is sized to 80 columns.
    - line 5920:  ``render_full(self)``  — only here does ``_layout_for``
                  run and set ``browser._preview_width`` to the real value,
                  but the preview text has already been cached at width 80.

  Fix: compute the layout (which sets ``_preview_width`` as a side-effect)
  before the initial ``_update_preview_for_cursor`` kick, so the first
  ``get_preview`` reads the correct width and the cached render matches the
  pane.

  After a SIGWINCH or any later key the preview cache is invalidated and
  rewrapped at the live width, so the bug is only visible on the *very
  first* paint. Still worth fixing — that first impression is what users
  see.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Write failing test: preview worker sees the real preview_width on first fetch {#562}

        updated: 2026-05-28 22:52:45 UTC
        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 22:51:18 UTC

    Mirror the wiring in `test/unit/test_main.py` (terminal, render, context names injected onto the state module). Build a Browser with a `get_preview` callback that captures `browser.preview_width` at call time, and assert that on `Browser.run()` startup the captured value equals the real preview-pane width (not 0, not 80) — running under `_headless=False` so the run() startup branch executes, but with `term_init`/`term_restore` stubbed.

    Key wiring (must match production):

      - `_state.term_size`  → patch `term_size` to return a non-default geometry, e.g. (160, 40), so the preview pane width is unambiguous (not coincidentally 80).
      - `_state.render_full` and `_state.render_partial` → patch to a stub that records calls so the test can also assert that the initial paint happened.
      - `_render._sub_layout`, `_render.layout_panes`, `_render._cursor_item` — wire so `_layout_for` runs end-to-end.

    Test should FAIL before the fix (captured width == 80, or 0 if the recipe inspects without fallback) and PASS after.

    * ## Comments {#562:comments}

      * Failing test added in test/unit/test_main.py::TestBrowserRunFirstPreviewWidth — captures browser.preview_width inside get_preview during run() startup at term_size=(160,40). Pre-fix output: 'width=0 (expected 160)'. {#562:comment:565}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Implement fix in Browser.run() — refresh layout before initial preview kick {#563}

        updated: 2026-05-28 22:52:45 UTC
        status: done
        created: 2026-05-28 22:51:18 UTC

    In `src-tui/040-state.py`, inside `Browser.run()` between the first `run_until_idle` and `_update_preview_for_cursor` (around line 5902), call `_layout_for(self)` so that the preview-pane width is computed and cached on the Browser before the worker reads it.

    `_layout_for` is a bare global resolved against the concatenated namespace in production; in tests it lives on the render module. Resolve it the same way the existing `render_full` / `render_partial` references do (bare-name lookup). Touch only this one site — every later iteration of the loop already calls `_layout_for` via `render_full` / `render_partial`, so the bug is purely a first-paint ordering issue.

    Document the call with a short comment pointing at this ticket so future readers understand why the layout is computed eagerly before painting.

* ## Ticket: Single-file: auto-expand file row instead of scoping in {#566}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-29 00:24:14 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-29 00:29:16 UTC

  Bug: single-file mode sets ``initial_scope = file_root.id``, which
  pushes one entry onto the framework's scope_stack (browse-claude
  inserts a parent scope underneath for this reason; we don't).
  Alt-up from there pops the stack to an empty/undefined state and
  the user sees an empty list.

  Fix per user direction: drop the ``initial_scope = file_root.id``
  branch entirely. Single-file no-anchor leaves ``initial_scope =
  None`` (same as multi-file no-anchor). Auto-expand the file's
  top-level row so the headings are visible immediately — the
  same visual the old single-file UX produced, without the
  scope-stack hack.

  Apply ``b.expand(file_root.id)`` (or whatever the public API
  expects) AFTER ``Browser(...)`` construction and BEFORE
  ``b.run()``, only when ``len(_FILES) == 1`` and ``_ANCHOR`` is
  empty. Multi-file or anchored cases stay as-is.

  Anchored single-file (FILE.md#foo) still drills into the
  anchor — that path is independent.

  Tests:
  * Update existing tests that asserted ``initial_scope ==
    file_root.id`` to assert ``initial_scope is None`` AND that
    the auto-expand call landed on the right id (via a mock).
  * Add a new case: single-file with anchor leaves
    ``initial_scope`` resolved (auto-expand NOT applied — anchor
    drill-in already shows the heading).
  * Add a case: multi-file no anchor still no auto-expand.

  Done when: ``browse-md a.md`` shows the file row at top with
  headings already visible beneath it; alt-up from a heading
  scopes back to the files level (one row visible, not empty);
  multi-file behaviour unchanged; anchored behaviour unchanged.

  * ## Comments {#566:comments}

    * Implemented per ticket: dropped initial_scope=file_root.id branch entirely. Single-file no-anchor now leaves initial_scope=None and calls b.expand(file_root.id) after Browser() construction (before b.run()). Multi-file and anchored paths unchanged. {#566:comment:567}

      Updated test stub: _Stub split into _Stub + _BrowserStub. _BrowserStub stashes the BrowserConfig positional arg on self.config and records expand(...) calls on self.expand_calls.
      TestAnchorMulti updates:
      * _initial_scope helper updated — single-file no-anchor branch removed (now returns None).
      * test_single_file_no_anchor_returns_file_root_id renamed to test_single_file_no_anchor_returns_none, asserts initial_scope is None.
      * Added test_single_file_no_anchor_auto_expands_file_row — drives main() and asserts config.initial_scope is None AND b.expand was called once with the file's root id.
      * Added test_single_file_with_anchor_does_not_auto_expand — config.initial_scope resolves to anchored heading, b.expand NOT called.
      * Added test_multi_file_no_anchor_does_not_auto_expand — config.initial_scope is None, b.expand NOT called.
      All 210 unit tests pass.
      CLI smoke (under tmux):
      * browse-md README.md → top row is README.md, expanded, [h1] visible beneath. j+M-Down drills in; M-Up returns to file row with headings still visible. Not empty.
      * browse-md README.md#Install → opens scoped into Install heading (unchanged).
      * browse-md README.md docs/recipes.md → both files shown collapsed at top level (unchanged).

* ## Ticket: Task: V/E ignore multi-selection, only show cursor item {#568}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-29 01:24:02 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-29 01:37:17 UTC

  User reports: in browse-md, multi-selecting items via space-mark
  and pressing V or E shows only the cursor item, not the
  selection. Expected: V/E operate on the whole selection (per
  the existing `_run_source_command` logic using `ctx.targets`).

  Suspects:
  * `ctx.targets` may not be returning what the recipe thinks —
    check whether `ctx.selected` is populated in browse-md at the
    moment V/E fire. Compare with browse-claude where the same
    pattern works.
  * The `'none'` gate may interact with selection state — verify
    by changing the gate to `'targets'` (or just leaving it
    `'none'` but adding a diagnostic).
  * Selection may be scope-bound and the scope-root pseudo-Item
    auto-expand from #566 may interact with it.

  Workflow:
  1. Reproduce. Drive the recipe through tmux: open
     `recipes/browse-md README.md`, expand the file row, space-mark
     two or three headings, press V (with `PAGER=cat`). Observe
     output.
  2. Add a transient `ctx.message(repr([t.id for t in ctx.targets]))`
     at the top of `_run_source_command` to confirm what targets is.
     Capture the message.
  3. Diagnose. Likely outcomes:
     a) `ctx.targets` IS the cursor only → framework / selection
        bug. Compare with browse-claude's selection flow.
     b) `ctx.targets` IS the selection but the merge / write
        path collapses to the cursor span somehow.
  4. Fix the underlying issue. Remove the diagnostic.
  5. Add a test that exercises the multi-select V path via the
     mock ctx — `targets` returning two items, assert
     `run_external` was called with a tempfile that contains
     BOTH ranges.

  Done when: selecting 3 headings + V opens a $PAGER on a temp
  file containing all 3 sections (merged ranges, file order).

  * ## Comments {#568:comments}

    * Diagnostics: ran multiple scenarios via tmux with PAGER capturing argv + diagnostic file logging targets/selected/cursor. Cannot reproduce 'V shows only cursor item'. In every multi-select case (same-file headings, cross-file headings, h1 + h2 mix, anchor-scoped same-file), ctx.selected and ctx.targets are BOTH populated correctly and PAGER receives a tempfile containing all selected sections. The framework space-mark + 'none' gate work fine. {#568:comment:569}

      Did discover an UNRELATED AttributeError: when scoping into a heading anchor (e.g. 'docs/api.md#Browser') and pressing V on the scope-root pseudo-Item, _run_source_command crashes because the pseudo-Item has no byte_offset attr — this is the heading-scope analogue of #552 (which handled the FILE-root pseudo-Item). _is_root only catches the file-root pseudo-Item (it.id in _FILES); a heading-scope-root pseudo-Item slips through and goes into the by_path[None] branch which assumes byte_offset.
      Per ticket Step 4: 'If the issue is pure framework wiring (selection doesn't reach our code), the unit test may still pass with mock ctx.targets — that's fine; the test guards against regressions in our consumer code'. So: removing diagnostic, adding a regression test that exercises multi-target two-non-root-in-same-file via mock ctx (existing test_three_non_root_out_of_order_merged_file_order already does this, but adding an explicit ticket-anchored variant per Step 3).
    * Resolution: Could not reproduce the user-reported symptom. Multi-select V/E works correctly in browse-md across every scenario tested via tmux drive (same-file headings, cross-file headings, single-file/multi-file argv, anchor-scoped opens, cursor on scope-root). In all cases ctx.targets returned every selected item, _run_source_command grouped + merged ranges per file, and PAGER (captured via a shim) received a tempfile containing every selected section. {#568:comment:570}

      Hypothesis on the user's report: they may have multi-selected a file_root row plus a heading and observed root-wins (documented behavior — any root target → open that file directly, skipping the byte-range merge for heading siblings). That would look like 'only one item shown' from the user's perspective. Alternatively this may be a stale report from before #552 (V/E scope-root crash fix) or #559 (no synthetic multi-root).
      Changes:
      - No recipe change (no defect found in _run_source_command).
      - Added test_multi_select_two_non_root_same_file_both_ranges_in_tempfile to TestRunSourceCommand. The test guards the consumer-code path the ticket worried about: given a ctx with ctx.targets returning two items, both ranges must land in the tempfile. Per Step 3's caveat: 'the test guards against regressions in our consumer code, and the real fix would be in framework configuration which a unit test can't cover'.

      Smoke: marked 3 headings (Install, Default keys, License) in README.md via tmux drive, V → PAGER tempfile contained all three sections.
      Separate finding (NOT this ticket, leaving for a follow-up if user files): pressing V on the scope-root pseudo-Item for an anchored heading scope (e.g. open 'docs/api.md#Browser', V on top row) raises AttributeError: 'Item' object has no attribute 'byte_offset'. This is the heading-scope analogue of the file-root case #552 fixed — _is_root only catches scope-roots whose id is in _FILES, but heading-scope-root pseudo-Items have ids like '<path>#<line>' which are NOT _FILES keys. Symptom only manifests when no normal target is in the selection (cursor IS the pseudo-Item). Different ticket if confirmed.

* ## Ticket: Task: V/E crashes on scope-root pseudo-Item for an anchored heading scope {#571}

      status: open
      created: 2026-05-29 01:38:18 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-29 01:38:18 UTC

  Discovered during #568 investigation. Reproduction:
  1. `browse-md docs/api.md#Browser` (or any anchored heading
     scope — drills the browser into a heading rather than a file
     root).
  2. Cursor lands on the synthetic scope-root pseudo-Item for that
     heading.
  3. Press `V` (or `E`).
  4. Crash: `AttributeError: 'Item' object has no attribute
     'byte_offset'`.

  Root cause (the heading-scope analogue of #552): `_is_root` in
  `_run_source_command` only recognises a scope-root pseudo-Item
  whose id is in `_FILES` (or matches `_ROOT_PATH`). For an
  anchored heading scope, the pseudo-Item's id is the heading id
  (`<path>#<line>`), which is NOT a key in `_FILES` — so `_is_root`
  returns False, the function falls through to the byte-range
  branch, and the pseudo-Item lacks `byte_offset`/`byte_size`.

  Fix options:
  * Treat any item missing `byte_offset` as a scope-root sentinel
    and route to the file the heading belongs to. Open just that
    file (V on a heading-scope row should preview the file the
    heading lives in, or the heading's slice — pick the most
    natural one).
  * Cleaner: when the pseudo-Item's id matches an item in
    `_BY_ID`, copy that real item's attributes into a target list
    and proceed with the byte-range path.

  Test:
  * Add a `_HeadingScopeRootPseudoItem` helper (no `kind`,
    no `byte_offset`, but id matches a real heading's id).
    Assert `_run_source_command` either opens the file directly
    or merges the matching real heading's range — whichever the
    fix decides on.

  Done when: `browse-md docs/api.md#Browser` and `V` on the
  scope-root row succeeds (opens either the file or the heading's
  slice, your choice).

* ## Ticket: Task: V/E unified target semantics + first-file separator {#572}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-29 01:44:33 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-29 01:54:02 UTC

  Two related fixes to `_run_source_command`:

  ### 1. Consistent target semantics

  Current behaviour: ANY file-root in the target set triggers
  "root-wins" — opens that file directly and discards every other
  target. Result: selecting a file-root plus three headings opens
  just the file, dropping the headings.

  New rule (single shortcut, otherwise combine):

  * **Exactly one target, and it is a file-root** → open the
    original file directly (no temp file). Edits via E persist.
    This covers both "cursor on a file row, no selection" and "one
    file row space-marked, nothing else".
  * **Anything else** → produce a temp file. Every target
    contributes:
    * A file-root target expands to that file's full byte range
      `(0, len(file_text))`.
    * A content target expands to `(byte_offset, byte_size)`.
    Group all expanded ranges by file in argv order, merge per
    file (the merge naturally absorbs sub-ranges when a file-root
    is present in the same group), concatenate per-file groups
    into one temp `.md`, pass to `$PAGER` / `$EDITOR`.

  No more "first-file wins" silent drop; no more "any root in the
  selection dominates".

  ### 2. First-file separator in multi-file output

  Current behaviour: the first per-file group is written without
  a header; subsequent groups get `\n===== <basename> =====\n`.
  Result: when reading the temp file the user can't tell where
  the first file's content ends and the second's begins without
  noticing the separator partway through.

  New rule:

  * If the temp file groups **more than one file** → emit the
    `===== <basename> =====\n` header BEFORE every group,
    including the first one.
  * If the temp file represents a **single file** → no header
    (unchanged — the user already knows which file).

  ### Tests

  * Existing tests for the root-wins branch need to be updated:
    multi-target selections that include a file-root should NOT
    take the root-wins path anymore. Re-baseline assertions.
  * Add: cursor on file-root + 0 selection → opens file directly
    (the single-target shortcut).
  * Add: one file-root space-marked + cursor elsewhere → opens
    file directly (still single target since space-mark removes
    cursor from targets when selection is non-empty per
    `ctx.targets` semantics — verify).
  * Add: file-root A space-marked + heading from file A → temp
    file with the whole-file range (subset absorbed).
  * Add: file-root A + heading from file B → temp file with two
    groups, header before each (including the first).
  * Update existing multi-file test to expect a header before
    the first group too.

  Done when: per-file shortcut works only for the single-target
  case; mixed selections combine all targets; multi-file temp
  files have the `=====` header before every group including the
  first.

  * ## Comments {#572:comments}

    * Implemented two unified target-semantics changes in `_run_source_command`: {#572:comment:573}

      1. Replaced root-wins early-return with a single-target shortcut: only `len(targets) == 1 and _is_root(targets[0])` opens the original file directly. Any larger selection (even one root + N headings) flows through the unified expansion path: file-roots expand to `(path, 0, len(file_text))`, content targets keep their `(byte_offset, byte_size)`. Ranges are grouped by file in argv order then merged per file — the whole-file range naturally absorbs sub-ranges from the same file.
      2. `_write_range_excerpts_grouped` now emits the `===== <basename> =====` header before EVERY group (including the first) when `len(groups) > 1`. Single-file groups still get no header.
      Tests in `test/unit/test_browse_md.py`:
      - Re-baselined `test_root_mixed_with_non_root_root_wins` → `test_root_mixed_with_non_root_combines_into_tempfile` (now expects merged whole-file content, no header).
      - Re-baselined `test_scope_root_pseudo_item_mixed_with_non_root_root_wins` → `test_scope_root_pseudo_item_mixed_with_non_root_combines`.
      - Re-baselined `test_first_per_file_root_wins_when_multiple_selected` → `test_two_roots_combines_with_headers` (two roots → headers before both, A's first).
      - Updated `test_cross_file_groups_by_file_in_argv_order` to expect header before the FIRST group too (output starts with A's `=====`).
      - Added `test_single_root_cursor_only_opens_file`, `test_single_root_marked_alone_opens_file` (single-file class).
      - Added `test_root_plus_content_same_file_combines`, `test_root_A_plus_content_B_combines_with_headers` (multi-file class).
      Test count: 211 → 215, all green (`python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_md`).
      CLI smoke verified under tmux with a mock PAGER:
      - cursor on a.md, V → opens /tmp/.../a.md directly.
      - space-mark a.md alone, V → opens /tmp/.../a.md directly.
      - space-mark a.md + space-mark heading in b.md, V → tempfile with `===== a.md =====` then whole a.md body then `===== b.md =====` then the heading slice.
      - space-mark two headings in a.md, V → tempfile with the merged a.md ranges, no header.
      Files changed:
      - recipes/browse-md
      - test/unit/test_browse_md.py

* ## Ticket: Task: Fix test_browse_md stub-replacement on test-ordering failure {#574}

      status: done
      created: 2026-05-29 01:55:42 UTC
      updated: 2026-05-29 01:56:38 UTC

  `test_browse_md._stub_browse_tui` short-circuits with
  `if 'browse_tui' in sys.modules: return`. If
  `test_browse_claude_render` (or any earlier importer) installed
  its own `_Stub` first, the browse-md tests that rely on
  `_BrowserStub` (which captures `config` and `expand_calls`) fail
  with `AttributeError: '_Stub' object has no attribute 'config'`.

  Fix: drop the early-return short-circuit. Always install the
  `_BrowserStub` for `browse_tui.Browser` (and `_Stub` for
  everything else) regardless of whether a previous test
  installed a different stub. The function should be idempotent
  for browse-md's own use and also robust against discover
  ordering.

  Verification: `python3 -m unittest discover test/unit` should
  pass with no errors (currently 3 errors in TestAnchorMulti).

  * ## Comments {#574:comments}

    * Removed the 'if browse_tui in sys.modules: return' early-return in _stub_browse_tui. Now always builds a fresh types.ModuleType('browse_tui') with Browser=_BrowserStub and BrowserConfig/Item/Action=_Stub, overwriting any stub left over by other test files. {#574:comment:575}

      Verification:
      $ python3 -m unittest discover test/unit 2>&1 | tail -3
      Ran 1909 tests in 2.133s
      OK
      $ python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_md 2>&1 | tail -3
      Ran 215 tests in 0.127s
      OK

* ## Ticket: Task: Implement `--path-sep` path-split tree mode {#597}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-01 07:01:02 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-01 07:45:30 UTC

  Add a fourth hierarchy-detection mode to `Browser.from_flat_tree` plus a
  `--path-sep CHARS` CLI flag that splits each entry's `id` on a separator and
  builds a tree, synthesizing intermediate nodes.

  Full design: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-path-split-tree-design.md`.
  Read it before starting any subtask.

  Key decisions: node id = full prefix path; leaf title = last segment unless
  the row carried an explicit title; path-aware empty-segment handling (leading
  sep preserved, doubled/trailing collapse); precedence
  `parent > depth > path-split > flat`. Single-file program built from
  `src-tui/NNN-*.py` via `./build-tui.sh`; tests run with `./run-tests.sh`.

  * ## Ticket: Task: `expand_path_rows` helper + unit tests {#598}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 07:01:02 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 07:11:35 UTC

    **File:** `src-tui/030-data.py` (data layer). Add a pure function
    `expand_path_rows(rows, sep)` near `to_item`.

    **Contract:** input `rows` is an iterable of raw rows (each `Item`, `str`,
    `tuple`, or `dict`, per `to_item` shapes) and a non-empty separator string.
    Returns a `list[dict]` of node rows with `id`, `parent`, and `title` set,
    including one dict per synthesized intermediate node, ordered so that
    grouping by `parent` yields first-seen sibling order. Do NOT set
    `has_children` here (the consumer derives it).

    **Per-row id + explicit-title extraction (pre-coercion):**
    - `dict`: id = `row['id']`; carry every other key onto the leaf;
      explicit_title = `'title' in row`.
    - `str`: id = the string; no extras; explicit_title = False.
    - `tuple`: positional `(id, title, tag, tag_style, has_children, hidden)`;
      explicit_title = `len(row) >= 2`.
    - `Item`: id = `it.id`; carry `tag`/`tag_style` + extras; explicit_title =
      `it.title != str(it.id)`.

    **Segmenting (path-aware, from spec):** `lead = sep if id.startswith(sep)
    else ''`; `segs = [s for s in id.split(sep) if s]`. Node at prefix depth
    `k` (1-indexed) has `id = lead + sep.join(segs[:k])`, `title = segs[k-1]`.
    Skip entries with no non-empty segments. Walk each entry's prefixes
    shallow→deep; emit a node dict the first time each prefix id is seen
    (intermediate = id/parent/title only; leaf = id/parent/title + carried
    fields + explicit-or-segment title). Top-level node `parent = None`. When a
    later explicit row's id equals an already-emitted node, merge its carried
    fields and explicit title onto that dict (explicit wins).

    **Tests:** new `test/unit/test_path_rows.py`. Cover: basic nesting +
    intermediate synthesis (`docs/api/auth.md` family); explicit-prefix merge;
    absolute vs relative (`/etc/x` != `etc/x`); doubled (`a//b`) + trailing
    (`a/b/`) collapse; empty / all-sep entries skipped; single-segment entry;
    multi-char separator (`::`, `.`); first-seen sibling ordering; explicit
    leaf `title` overrides segment. Follow the `test/unit/_loader.py` pattern
    used by sibling tests. Run `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_path_rows`.

    * ## Comments {#598:comments}

      * Implemented expand_path_rows(rows, sep) in src-tui/030-data.py right after to_item, plus a pre-coercion extractor _split_path_row and an Item-extras helper _item_extras. Added test/unit/test_path_rows.py (28 tests, all green; full suite 2355 OK). Decisions/edge cases: (1) Unified leaf handling — the deepest prefix of each row gets carried fields + explicit-or-segment title whether it was freshly created or pre-synthesized as an intermediate, which naturally covers the explicit-prefix merge case (explicit wins, no duplicate node, position preserved). (2) Carried metadata never clobbers the structural id/parent keys, so a stray parent column on a leaf cannot break the synthesized tree (path-split only runs when no parent/depth column exists anyway, but this is a cheap safety net). (3) Item explicit_title = (it.title != str(it.id)); dict explicit_title = 'title' in row; tuple = len>=2; str = False. (4) id is str()-coerced before segmenting. has_children intentionally not set (consumer derives). Used the test/unit/_loader.py load() pattern like sibling tests. {#598:comment:602}

      * Code-review follow-up: tightened _item_extras to derive its exclusion set from the dataclass itself (dataclasses.fields(it)) instead of a hardcoded structural subset. This stops declared cache/provenance fields (preview, preview_render, synthetic, scope_title) from leaking onto leaf dicts when real Items flow in via the API, restoring dict/Item parity. tag/tag_style are now excluded by _item_extras too (they are declared fields) and remain handled explicitly by _split_path_row for Item rows, so no double-handling. Added 'import dataclasses' to 030-data.py's import block. Tidied the tuple branch to dict(zip(('tag','tag_style'), row[2:4])). Added regression test test_declared_item_fields_do_not_leak. python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_path_rows -> Ran 29 tests, OK. {#598:comment:603}

  * ## Ticket: Task: `from_flat_tree` path_sep kwarg + integration tests {#599}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 07:01:02 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 07:29:55 UTC

    **File:** `src-tui/040-state.py`, `Browser.from_flat_tree` (~line 6693).
    Depends on `expand_path_rows`. Add an explicit `path_sep: str = None`
    keyword (before `**browser_kwargs`).

    **Detection:** keep `has_parent` / `has_depth` unchanged. Add
    `use_path = (not has_parent and not has_depth and path_sep is not None)`.
    When `use_path`: replace `rows` with `expand_path_rows(rows, path_sep)`,
    then run the SAME parent-pointer grouping the `has_parent` branch uses
    (group by each item's `parent`, `None` -> `root_id`). After grouping, set
    `has_children = True` on every node whose `id` is a key in
    `children_by_parent`. Everything downstream (`upsert`/`complete` batch,
    synthesized eager `get_children`) stays as-is. `expand_path_rows` resolves
    by bare name (same as `to_item`/`upsert` already do across the concatenated
    modules).

    **Tests:** add focused tests following the existing `from_flat_tree` test
    pattern (see `test/unit/test_state_indexes.py`). Verify: `path_sep` builds
    the synthesized tree and `visible_items` shows it with correct
    `has_children` markers and no runtime fetch; precedence — a `parent` column
    beats `path_sep`, a `depth` column beats `path_sep`, `path_sep` beats flat.

    * ## Comments {#599:comments}

      * Implemented path_sep kwarg in Browser.from_flat_tree (src-tui/040-state.py). Added 'path_sep: str = None' before **browser_kwargs; materialized rows=list(rows) before to_item coercion so the raw rows can be re-iterated. has_parent/has_depth detection unchanged; added use_path = (not has_parent and not has_depth and path_sep is not None). When use_path, rebuilt items via expand_path_rows(rows, path_sep) and reused the existing parent-pointer grouping (extended 'if has_parent' to 'if has_parent or use_path' — top-level parent=None maps to root_id identically). After grouping, set it.has_children=True for every item whose id is a key in children_by_parent (incoming has_children ignored per design). Downstream batch/upsert/complete unchanged — has_children rides through via _fields_of_item (reads Item.__dict__). Precedence parent>depth>path>flat holds. Added 9 tests in test/unit/test_state_indexes.py (TestFromFlatTreePathSep + TestFromFlatTreePathSepPrecedence) and wired _state.expand_path_rows in the loader block. No-runtime-fetch proven with a get_children that raises (eager cache + all _loading False) plus full expanded visible_items render. Full suite: Ran 2364 tests, OK (skipped=4). {#599:comment:604}

      * Code-review fixes: (1) path-mode has_children now DERIVED unconditionally — changed the loop to 'it.has_children = it.id in children_by_parent' so a carried True on a leaf (dict row with has_children=True flowing through to_item) is overridden to False; (2) annotation path_sep: str = None -> Optional[str] = None to match file convention (Optional already imported). (3) Added regression test test_carried_has_children_on_leaf_is_overridden: {'id':'docs/a.md','has_children':True} with path_sep='/' yields leaf has_children=False and synthesized 'docs' has_children=True. test.unit.test_state_indexes: Ran 34 tests, OK. Full suite ./run-tests.sh: Ran 2365 tests, OK (skipped=4), exit 0 (re-ran twice clean; an initial single FAILED was a flaky binary-spawning UI test that did not reproduce). {#599:comment:605}

  * ## Ticket: Task: CLI `--path-sep` wiring + tests {#600}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 07:01:02 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 07:40:50 UTC

    **File:** `src-tui/080-cli.py`. Depends on the `from_flat_tree` kwarg.

    - Add argument `--path-sep` (metavar `CHARS`, `default=None`) in
      `build_argparser`, grouped near `--record-sep`/`--input`.
    - `_build_eager_browser`: pass `path_sep=args.path_sep` to
      `from_flat_tree`. After `rows = list(parse_input(...))`, if
      `args.path_sep is not None` and any row has a `'parent'` or `'depth'`
      key, write the stderr warning
      `browse-tui: --path-sep ignored: rows carry explicit parent/depth`
      (precedence then drops `path_sep` inside `from_flat_tree`).
    - `run_tui`: if `args.path_sep is not None and args.children_cmd`, write
      `error: --path-sep requires --root-cmd (eager mode)` and return 2.

    **Tests:** follow existing CLI test patterns. Cover: `--path-sep /` end to
    end (parse -> `from_flat_tree` -> tree built; a metadata column rides onto
    the leaf); `--path-sep` + `--children-cmd` returns exit 2 with the message;
    `--path-sep` + a `parent`/`depth` column emits the stderr warning. Run
    `./build-tui.sh` first if any test spawns the built `./browse-tui` binary.

    * ## Comments {#600:comments}

      * Implemented in src-tui/080-cli.py: (1) added --path-sep CHARS arg (default None) right after --record-sep in build_argparser; (2) _build_eager_browser now passes path_sep=args.path_sep to Browser.from_flat_tree and, after parse_input, writes the exact stderr warning 'browse-tui: --path-sep ignored: rows carry explicit parent/depth' when any parsed dict row has a 'parent'/'depth' key; (3) run_tui rejects --path-sep + --children-cmd with 'error: --path-sep requires --root-cmd (eager mode)' and returns 2, checked alongside the children/root validation. Tests: added TestPathSep to test/unit/test_cli.py (argparse default/capture/help, eager-builder tree synthesis, metadata column riding onto leaf via --fields id,size, children-cmd exit-2 + exact message, parent-column and depth-column warning + explicit mode winning). Also wired _state.expand_path_rows = _data.expand_path_rows in the test module so path-split builds resolve under the loader. Surprise: CLI default --root-id is '' (not None), so top-level synthesized nodes parent to '' — assertions check '' accordingly. Full suite: Ran 2373 tests, OK (skipped=4). {#600:comment:606}

      * Code-review fix: changed the _build_eager_browser warning condition from key-presence ('parent' in r / 'depth' in r) to value-semantics (r.get('parent') is not None or r.get('depth') is not None), matching from_flat_tree's precedence which only drops path_sep on a non-None value. A null-valued column (JSON parent:null/depth:null) now correctly stays silent and path-split still runs; empty-string parent='' from tsv/csv still warns ('' is not None). Added two regression tests to TestPathSep (null parent, null depth) asserting no stderr and synthesized prefix nodes present. Fixed the spec CLI-wiring bullet wording from 'has a parent/depth key' to 'has a non-None parent/depth value'. Tests: test.unit.test_cli Ran 98 OK; full ./run-tests.sh Ran 2375 OK (skipped=4). {#600:comment:607}

* ## Ticket: browse-find: build a directory tree via --path-sep {#609}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-01 09:01:41 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-01 09:03:58 UTC

  Use the new `--path-sep` feature so `recipes/browse-find` presents `find`
  output as a real directory tree instead of a flat path list.

  **Change `recipes/browse-find`:** add `--path-sep /` to the `exec "$BIN" …`
  invocation (keep `--record-sep null` and `--input tsv --fields id` as-is —
  path-split runs after record/field parsing). `find` emits full paths
  (`./src/foo.py`, or absolute when ROOT is absolute), so splitting the `id`
  on `/` nests them; the existing dir-vs-file preview-cmd already covers the
  synthesized intermediate directory nodes. Update the top-of-file comment
  and the `--help` heredoc so they describe a directory-tree picker (it was a
  flat list). Keep the script's structure/quoting otherwise unchanged.

  **Other shell recipes (already assessed — confirm, no code change):**
  `browse-files` (`ls -1A`) and `browse-ls` (`ls -lA`) are single-directory,
  non-recursive pickers whose ids are bare filenames with no `/`, so
  `--path-sep` does not apply — leave them as-is. The Python (`--run-py`)
  recipes build trees lazily through the `get_children` API and don't use the
  CLI hierarchy flags.

  **Update `docs/recipes.md`** (the `### recipes/browse-find` section, ~L296):
  reword the description to say it builds a directory tree, and add
  `--path-sep /` to the "Demonstrates:" line. Bump the "~26 lines" count if it
  changes.

  **Verify:** `./recipes/browse-find --help` shows the updated help and exits
  0; `./build-tui.sh` stays clean. (The recipe launches an interactive TUI, so
  don't try to drive the UI; a `--help` check + confirming the flag composes
  is sufficient. The `--path-sep` behavior itself is already covered by the
  unit/CLI suite.) Do NOT git commit — leader commits after review.

  * ## Comments {#609:comments}

    * Implemented: added '--path-sep /' to recipes/browse-find exec line (between --record-sep null and --preview-cmd) so find's full-path output nests into a directory tree; the existing dir-vs-file --preview-cmd already covers synthesized intermediate dir nodes. Updated the top-of-file comment and the --help heredoc to describe a 'recursive directory-tree picker' (was 'file picker'/flat list). Updated docs/recipes.md browse-find section: reworded to 'directory-tree picker' + noting paths split on /, added '--path-sep /' to the Demonstrates line, bumped source count ~26 -> ~28 lines (+2 physical lines added). {#609:comment:610}

      browse-files / browse-ls assessment (confirmed, no change): both are single-directory, non-recursive ls pickers (browse-files: 'ls -1A $DIR'; browse-ls: 'ls -lA $DIR | tail -n +2'). Their ids are bare filenames with no '/', so --path-sep has nothing to split and does not apply. Left as-is. Python --run-py recipes build trees lazily via get_children and don't use CLI hierarchy flags.
      Verify: './recipes/browse-find --help' shows updated directory-tree text, exit 0. './build-tui.sh' clean (exit 0). Binary --help lists --path-sep; eager-only validation fires ('--path-sep requires --root-cmd' with --children-cmd). 'bash -n recipes/browse-find' OK. Did NOT launch the interactive TUI (per ticket; path-sep behavior covered by unit/CLI suite).

  Single source of truth for the grammar; behavior verified-equivalent

  * ## Ticket: Task: Swap parsing to md2ansi_scan + hard dependency {#611}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 06:59:13 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 07:22:22 UTC

    Core change. In recipes/browse-md:
    - Imports: `from md2ansi_lib import md2ansi as _md2ansi_fn,
      md2ansi_scan as _md2ansi_scan` wrapped in try/except ImportError
      that sets both to None.
    - main() gates first thing: `if _md2ansi_scan is None: _die(
      'requires the md2ansi_lib module (expected alongside the recipe)',
      with_usage=False)`.
    - Rewrite `_parse` per spec §3: flag-gated kind set
      (_SCAN_KINDS_HEADINGS / _SCAN_KINDS_WITH_LISTS chosen by
      _INCLUDE_LISTS), iterate md2ansi_scan spans, emit identical
      (kind, payload) event tuples. heading -> (span.subtype, {...});
      list span -> _walk_list(span.start, span.text, line_starts).
    - Change `_walk_list(match, line_starts)` ->
      `_walk_list(base, text, line_starts)` (spec §4); body otherwise
      unchanged.
    - Remove dead grammar: _MD_H1.._MD_H6, _MD_HR, _MD_FRONTMATTER,
      _MD_CODE_GEN, _MD_BLOCKQUOTE, _MD_TABLE, _MD_LIST, _RULES,
      _PARSER_RE, the block-rule-table section comment, and the
      old per-match dispatch loop. Keep _HEADING_KINDS/_HEADING_EVENT_KINDS
      (used by _build_nodes), _LIST_ITEM_RE, _HEADING_PREFIX_RE,
      _LIST_PREFIX_RE.

    Tests (test/unit/test_browse_md.py):
    - Add `recipes/` to sys.path in `_load_recipe` so the now-hard
      import resolves (idempotent). Check test_main.py for the same
      need.
    - Rewrite TestWalkList: stop using `_PARSER_RE.search`; call
      `_walk_list(0, <span-text>, line_starts)` directly.
    - Add a missing-dependency gate test: set `_md2ansi_scan = None`,
      call main() with argv naming a real temp file, assert
      SystemExit(2) + the stderr message.
    - Add a TestParse assertion that heading events are identical with
      _INCLUDE_LISTS off vs on.

    DONE = `./run-tests.sh` green; do NOT touch the external md2ansi
    binary path (M action / _resolve_md_pager).

    * ## Comments {#611:comments}

      * Swapped _parse to consume md2ansi_lib.md2ansi_scan (one source of truth) and made md2ansi_lib a hard dependency. Removed dead duplicate grammar (_MD_H1..H6, _MD_HR, _MD_FRONTMATTER, _MD_CODE_GEN, _MD_BLOCKQUOTE, _MD_TABLE, _MD_LIST, _RULES, _PARSER_RE, block-rule-table comment, old finditer dispatch loop). Changed _walk_list(match, line_starts) -> _walk_list(base, text, line_starts). Added _SCAN_KINDS_HEADINGS/_SCAN_KINDS_WITH_LISTS constants and main() gate (_die when _md2ansi_scan is None). KEPT _md2ansi_fn render gate untouched (that collapse is #612). Tests: added recipes/ to sys.path in _load_recipe, rewrote TestWalkList to call _walk_list(0, span_text, line_starts), added TestParse heading-events-identical assertion, added TestMissingDependencyGate. test_main.py does NOT load the recipe (only a comment mentions browse-md), so no path change needed there. Full unit suite green: Ran 1938 tests OK; full discover -s test/ green on re-run (one earlier test.ui.test_suspend SIGSTOP failure was flaky timing, passes in isolation and on re-run, unrelated). {#611:comment:600}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Collapse the now-redundant optional render gate {#612}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 06:59:13 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 07:22:22 UTC

    Depends on the hard-dependency ticket. md2ansi_lib is now
    guaranteed after main()'s gate, so remove the optional gating
    (spec §5):
    - `_MD_COLOR = _md2ansi_fn is not None` -> `_MD_COLOR = True`.
    - get_preview: `if _MD_COLOR and _md2ansi_fn is not None:` ->
      `if _MD_COLOR:` (keep the broad except-Exception raw-slice
      fallback).

      `if _md2ansi_fn is not None:` guard).

      md2ansi_lib is importable") and the import-block comment to
      describe the hard requirement.
    - Keep `_md2ansi_fn` as the render seam name (get_preview calls it,
      tests monkeypatch it).

    DONE = `./run-tests.sh` green; render-path tests (TestGetPreview,
    TestToggleMd) still pass via the `_md2ansi_fn` monkeypatch.

Rewrote the module docstring m line, the _MD_COLOR comment, the get_preview docstring, the _action_toggle_md docstring, and the actions-build comment. _md2ansi_fn kept as the render seam. External md2ansi binary path (_resolve_md_pager, M action) untouched. Tests: repurposed the obsolete test_md_color_on_but_fn_none_returns_raw (modeled the impossible-in-prod _md2ansi_fn is None case) into test_md_color_default_on_renders_via_real_library, which asserts the new contract — fresh recipe defaults _MD_COLOR True with _md2ansi_fn bound to the real library, and get_preview actually renders (output differs from raw slice + contains an ANSI escape). Updated the stale TestToggleMd setUp comment. Kept TestGetPreview.setUp default _MD_COLOR=False. Full suite green: Ran 2329 tests, OK (skipped=4). {#612:comment:601}

Changed 'm       Toggle md2ansi coloring (requires md2ansi_lib).' to 'm       Toggle md2ansi coloring.' for consistency with the already-updated module-docstring m line — the parenthetical was misleading now that m is unconditionally bound and md2ansi_lib is a hard dependency. Help-text test (TestHelpIntro.test_mentions_each_custom_action) still passes (it only checks the key appears at line start). browse-md suite green: Ran 230 tests, OK. {#612:comment:602}

* ## Ticket: Task: Tree-event lifecycle hooks (approved spec 2026-06-01) {#628}
