  * ## Ticket: Task: Uniform payloads for the existing four hooks {#629}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 08:59:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 09:08:25 UTC

    Change the existing hooks to the uniform (ctx, subject) convention: on_cursor_change(ctx, id), on_selection_change(ctx, ids), on_scope_change(ctx, scope_id, prev_scope_id, direction), on_quit(ctx, code). Update the fire methods in src-tui/040-state.py: _fire_cursor_change_if_pending (~:4473), _fire_selection_change (~:4510), _fire_scope_change (~:4501), _fire_on_quit (~:4519). For scope: capture current_scope(state) BEFORE the transition in Browser.scope_into (~:4138) and scope_out (~:4176) and thread (scope_id, prev_scope_id, direction='in'/'out') into _fire_scope_change. No shipped recipe registers these hooks (verified), but update test/unit/test_lifecycle_hooks.py and any test helpers that do. TDD: adjust/extend the tests first, then implement. Spec: "Uniform payload convention", Decisions 1/3/5/6.

    * ## Comments {#629:comments}

      * Migrated the existing four hooks to the uniform (ctx, subject) convention. {#629:comment:606}

        Framework (src-tui/040-state.py):
        - _fire_cursor_change_if_pending: passes the resolved cursor id (None on placeholder/empty) computed just before firing.
        - _fire_selection_change: passes sorted(state.selected) as a list. Sorted for a deterministic, stable payload (set order is unspecified); spec says recipes wanting order should sort, so a stable framework default is friendly + keeps tests deterministic.
        - _fire_scope_change: gained (scope_id=None, prev_scope_id=None, direction=None) params; a bare call (used by recipe browse-claude and the no-transition test path) still passes Nones.
        - _fire_on_quit: passes self._quit_code.
        Scope direction/prev threading: Browser.scope_into/scope_out capture self.scope BEFORE the state transition (prev_scope_id) and read self.scope AFTER (scope_id), passing direction 'in'/'out'. Used the scope-stack-tip-or-None convention (Browser.scope / ctx.scope), NOT the current_scope() free function which returns root_id at root -- the spec and tests require None at root. Also threaded the SAME payload through the keyboard action handlers _scope_down/_scope_up in src-tui/070-actions.py (they fire _fire_scope_change directly and bypass Browser.scope_into/out); without this, a keyboard scope change would deliver (None,None,None) while the programmatic API delivers the real values, violating the spec's source-agnostic contract.
        Tests: rewrote test/unit/test_lifecycle_hooks.py to assert the new payloads (cursor id incl. None-on-empty; selection id-list incl. empty-on-clear; scope in/out from-root/nested with prev + direction; quit code incl. default 0). Updated the 1-arg on_scope_change lambdas in test/unit/test_scope_api.py to (ctx, *a). Added two keyboard-path payload tests in test/unit/test_scope_stack.py (alt-down -> ('A',None,'in'); alt-up -> (None,'A','out')) and wired _state.Context in that file's loader (refactored its _ctx_for to share the one module-level Context instance). No loader cross-module additions needed in _loader.py. TDD: tests failed first with 'missing positional argument', now green.
        Results: ./build-tui.sh OK; test/unit 1946 passed; test/async_ 240 passed.
        Follow-up: docs/api.md still documents the old (ctx)->None convention (Lifecycle hooks section ~L536-570 and the BrowserConfig dataclass ~L1238-1241) -- left for the docs ticket since spec outline step 8 updates all ten hooks together and the new six don't exist yet. Filing as a follow-up under 597.

  * ## Ticket: Task: on_expand / on_collapse with batched id-list payloads {#630}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 08:59:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 09:22:52 UTC

    Add on_expand(ctx, ids) / on_collapse(ctx, ids) via a drain-time diff of state.expanded — the same model as on_cursor_change. Add BrowserConfig fields (near :2595) and self._on_expand/_on_collapse storage (near :2868); init self._last_expanded=set() in __init__. Add _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending beside _fire_cursor_change_if_pending: added=expanded-_last, removed=_last-expanded; fire on_collapse(list(removed)) then on_expand(list(added)); re-snapshot _last_expanded. Wire into the drain block (~:5976) in documented order. Scope re-baseline: in Browser.scope_into/scope_out (already edited in the prior ticket), after the transition set self._last_expanded=set(self._state.expanded) so restored per-scope sets do NOT fire. Exceptions -> self.error(...); no-op when handler is None. TDD covering: single ->/<- fires one-element list; Alt-Right/Left (_expand_recursive/_collapse_recursive) and collapse_all fire ONE call with the full id list; re-press on already-expanded fires nothing; startup b.expand before run() fires on first drain; scope transition fires neither expand nor collapse. Spec: API on_expand/on_collapse, Semantics "Firing mechanism" + "Scope transitions", Decision 2.

    * ## Comments {#630:comments}

      * Implemented on_expand/on_collapse via unconditional drain-time set-diff (NO pending flag). Choice rationale: every expanded-set mutation site (_do_expand, _nav_left discard, _expand_recursive/_collapse_recursive, collapse_all, expand_subtree, scope restore) writes state.expanded directly; a pending flag would require instrumenting all of them (the #500 failure mode the spec forbids). The set is small so a per-drain 'set != set' diff is cheap and catches every mutation automatically — the design intent. _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending: removed=last-expanded fires on_collapse(list) first, added=expanded-last fires on_expand(list); each guarded on handler-is-None and wrapped try/except -> self.error('on_collapse/on_expand: ...'); _last_expanded re-snapshotted at end ALWAYS (even with no handler) so a later-registered handler can't see a stale delta. Drain order: inserted _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending() BEFORE _fire_cursor_change_if_pending() in the run() loop (~:6062), matching spec 'on_collapse -> on_expand -> ... -> on_cursor_change'. _last_expanded seeded empty in __init__ so startup/initial_scope expansions fire on first drain. Re-baseline sites (set _last_expanded=set(state.expanded) AFTER the transition's _fire_scope_change): Browser.scope_into & Browser.scope_out (040-state.py), AND the keyboard _scope_down/_scope_up handlers in 070-actions.py (they bypass the Browser methods, operating on state directly + calling _browser._fire_scope_change, per #629). Note: the free scope_into() already adds the scope-row id to state.expanded synchronously, so the keyboard handler's posted ctx.expand(id) for an uncached scope row is a no-op-on-already-present-id and does NOT leak a spurious on_expand — pinned by test_keyboard_scope_down_into_uncached_no_spurious_expand. {#630:comment:609}

      * Tests: added 19 cases to test/unit/test_lifecycle_hooks.py (TestOnExpandCollapse: 14, TestScopeReBaseline: 5). Covers keyboard right->on_expand([id])/left->on_collapse([id]) once; ctx.expand(other); re-press right on expanded fires nothing; alt-right/_expand_recursive + alt-left/_collapse_recursive + collapse_all + expand_subtree each ONE call with full id list; add+remove in one drain fires both; net-zero fires nothing; startup b.expand before run fires on first drain; missing handlers silent (baseline still advances); exception->Browser.error. Scope: scope_into/scope_out (Browser) and keyboard alt-down/alt-up fire on_scope_change but NEITHER expand NOR collapse; genuine expand after scope fires normally; uncached keyboard scope-down regression guard. Test file now also loads 050-render + 070-actions to drive real keys via dispatch_key. Full suite: unit 1964 OK, async 240 OK. build-tui.sh green. {#630:comment:610}

  * ## Ticket: Task: on_children_loaded with batched parent-id list {#631}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 08:59:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 09:39:22 UTC

    Add on_children_loaded(ctx, parent_ids) firing when state._loading[pid] transitions True->False. BrowserConfig field + storage; init self._children_loaded_pending=set(). Prefer a single _set_loading(pid, value) helper, or instrument the True->False sites (~:1297 complete op, ~:4586 apply_children_results); on True->False add pid to the pending set. Add _fire_children_loaded_if_pending draining the set to ONE on_children_loaded(list) per tick; wire into the drain after expand. Rules to honor: an already-cached expand does NOT fire (no fetch); [] fires with empty children; None return does not fire; a get_children error fires once with cached_children==[]; refresh refires. TDD across unit + test/async_ timing: uncached expand fires after worker delivery with cached_children populated; cached expand fires on_expand but not on_children_loaded; generator fires once at completion; refresh over several expanded parents batches per drain. Spec: API on_children_loaded, Semantics, test plan.

    * ## Comments {#631:comments}

      * Implemented on_children_loaded(ctx, parent_ids). Instrumented the TWO genuine-settlement sites via a single module-level choke point _set_loading(state, pid, value, *, settled=True): (1) the 'complete' op apply in apply_ops (worker batch tail / generator StopIteration / synthesised-empty error delivery), and (2) apply_children_results (legacy set_children deque). settled=True appends pid to a per-pass state._settled_parents list (reset at the start of apply_ops and at the start of apply_children_results, mirroring _preview_kicks); the Browser-level callers (_apply after apply_ops, and apply_children_results after its loop) move it into self._children_loaded_pending. clear_children is EXCLUDED: it pops _children (cached_children->None) and keeps the bare state._loading[pid]=False with no settled flag, so it never fires (verified by unit + the cache-revert assertion). The None-return path posts no 'complete' so loading stays True and never fires; generator per-yield batches carry no 'complete' so only StopIteration settles -> one fire. _fire_children_loaded_if_pending drains the pending set into ONE on_children_loaded(list) per tick, always clears the set (even with no handler / on throw), try/except->error('on_children_loaded: ...'). Drain placement: collapse->expand->children_loaded->cursor (inserted between _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending and _fire_cursor_change_if_pending). Tests: 10 unit (TestOnChildrenLoaded + TestDefaultsAreNoOp) + 8 async (test/async_/test_children_loaded.py). Had to wire Context into test/async_/_helpers.py (Browser._make_ctx_for_hook builds Context, which the isolated test load doesn't inject) -- production concatenated build resolves it fine (verified end-to-end). Full suites green: 1973 unit, 248 async. {#631:comment:612}

  * ## Ticket: Task: on_search_change, on_filter_change, on_resize {#632}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 08:59:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 09:50:46 UTC

    Add the three remaining drain-diff hooks. BrowserConfig fields + storage; init _last_search_query='', _last_filters=(), _last_size=None. _fire_search_change_if_pending diffs Browser._search_query (~:3018, property :4085) -> on_search_change(ctx, query). _fire_filter_change_if_pending diffs tuple(self.filters) (property :3466) -> on_filter_change(ctx, filters). _fire_resize_if_pending: at the g_resize_flag observation points (~:5982, :6012) compare term_size() to _last_size and, on change, fire on_resize(ctx, cols, rows) and update _last_size. Wire all three at the FRONT of the drain order (resize, search, filter). TDD: search per-keystroke coalesces to final value per drain and clear fires once with ''; filter set/add/clear fire, identical re-set is a no-op, add_filter('') is a no-op; resize fires once on changed size, nothing on unchanged. Spec: API Phase 2/3, Semantics.

    * ## Comments {#632:comments}

      * Implemented on_search_change / on_filter_change / on_resize as drain-diff hooks (same model as on_cursor_change). Three diffs in src-tui/040-state.py: (1) BrowserConfig fields + self._on_* storage; init _last_search_query='', _last_filters=(), _last_size=None, _resize_pending=False. (2) Fire methods: _fire_search_change_if_pending diffs self._search_query (unconditional, always re-snapshots even with no handler); _fire_filter_change_if_pending diffs tuple(self.filters) (property already drops empties, so add_filter('') and identical re-set are natural no-ops); _fire_resize_if_pending. (3) Drain wiring at FRONT in documented order: resize -> search -> filter -> collapse -> expand -> children_loaded -> cursor. {#632:comment:613}

        RESIZE APPROACH: staged-flag (not fire-at-observation, not per-tick diff). Set self._resize_pending=True at BOTH g_resize_flag observation points (~:6191 pre-key, ~:6219 post-read_key); _fire_resize_if_pending (first in the fire sequence) consumes the flag, reads term_size(), diffs (cols,rows) vs _last_size, fires once on change. Mirrors _cursor_change_pending so term_size is only read on an actual SIGWINCH, not every loop tick. Because the fire sequence runs before the flag-observation block, a resize observed this iteration fires on the next drain ('next drain' semantics per spec).
        TERM_SIZE GUARD: mirrors _clamp_split / _list_pane_height_safe — ts=globals().get('term_size'); return if None; call inside try/except (return on raise, e.g. OSError no-tty); return if not cols or not rows (headless (0,0)). None of those fire garbage or touch _last_size. Pending flag is always cleared so a single SIGWINCH never re-fires.
        TESTS (test/unit/test_lifecycle_hooks.py, headless + explicit _fire_*_if_pending like existing hook tests): search fires on change / clear fires once with '' / identical re-set no-op / coalesce-to-final / payload==ctx.search_query / exception->error. filter set/add/clear fire / identical re-set no-op / add_filter('') no-op / payload==ctx.filters / exception->error. resize fires once on changed term_size / unchanged no-op / no-pending => no term_size read / (0,0) no fire / term_size raising graceful / term_size missing graceful / second drain no refire / exception->error. term_size stubbed onto _state (build resolves by concatenation). Extended TestDefaultsAreNoOp for the three new fire methods. 24 new tests; file total 72.
        VERIFY: ./build-tui.sh clean; unit 1996 OK; async 248 OK.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Update docs/api.md for the uniform hook family {#602}

    Docs only, no code. Replace the Lifecycle hooks section of docs/api.md with the uniform-payload version covering all ten hooks; add the cached-vs-uncached composition pattern, the scope re-baseline rule, and the on_scope_change direction+prev_scope_id payload. Add the five new fields to the BrowserConfig listing. Ensure every documented signature matches what was actually implemented in the prior tickets. Spec: Implementation outline item 8.

    * ## Comments {#602:comments}

      * From r599 review: docs/api.md ordering note must reflect ACTUAL behavior — on_scope_change fires inline (immediately on transition), while expand/collapse/cursor/selection/search/filter/resize fire at drain-end. Also note insert-mode marker movement is a (source-agnostic) trigger for on_expand/on_collapse. {#602:comment:611}

      * ALSO (from r599+r601): the documented 'Ordering within a drain' is not literally realized — on_scope_change and on_selection_change fire synchronously at their mutation sites (during drain_main_queue), while resize/search/filter/collapse/expand/children_loaded/cursor fire in the post-drain _fire_*_if_pending sequence. Fix in this ticket: (a) docs/api.md ordering note, (b) the spec's 'Ordering within a drain' section, (c) the in-source drain-order comment in src-tui/040-state.py (~the _fire_*_if_pending block). State the real contract: on_cursor_change fires last (post-expansion) is the guarantee; other cross-hook ordering is unspecified. {#602:comment:614}

      * docs/api.md: replaced the Lifecycle hooks subsection (Browser) to document ALL TEN hooks with uniform (ctx, subject) signatures in a signature+when+notes table; added the cached-vs-uncached composition pattern (on_expand stashes intent / on_children_loaded fulfils, + firing rules), the scope re-baseline rule (scope transitions fire on_scope_change, not expand/collapse), the on_scope_change direction+prev_scope_id semantics (None at root either end), and the insert-mode-marker-movement awareness note for on_expand/on_collapse. Added the five new fields (on_expand/on_collapse/on_children_loaded/on_search_change/on_filter_change/on_resize) to the BrowserConfig dataclass listing in source order with inline signature comments. Updated the stale on_query_change(text) run_in_slot example to the now-real on_search_change(ctx, query). {#602:comment:615}

        Ordering-accuracy fixes in THREE places to match the real contract (on_cursor_change fires last; other cross-hook order unspecified): (a) new 'Firing order within a drain' subsection in docs/api.md; (b) spec 'Ordering within a drain' section rewritten; (c) the in-source drain-order comment near the _fire_*_if_pending block in src-tui/040-state.py rewritten to state that on_scope_change/on_selection_change fire SYNCHRONOUSLY at their mutation sites during drain_main_queue while resize/search/filter/collapse/expand/children_loaded/cursor fire in the post-drain pass with cursor last. Rebuilt via ./build-tui.sh (comment confirmed present in the built browse-tui artifact).
        Cross-checked all ten signatures against src-tui/040-state.py _fire_* methods: on_cursor_change(ctx,id) L4623; on_selection_change(ctx,ids=sorted) L4657; on_scope_change(ctx,scope_id,prev_scope_id,direction) L4641-42 (called inline at L4284 'in' / L4331 'out'); on_quit(ctx,code) L4822; on_collapse(ctx,list(removed)) L4690 then on_expand(ctx,list(added)) L4696; on_children_loaded(ctx,parent_ids) L4725; on_search_change(ctx,query) L4750; on_filter_change(ctx,filters) L4771; on_resize(ctx,cols,rows) L4808. ALL MATCH the spec — no spec-vs-code signature mismatch found. Verified the documented ctx surface used in examples exists (ctx.cached_children returns None vs [], ctx.expand/cursor_to/search_query/scope/filters/message/invalidate_preview).   * ## Ticket: Task: Migrate browse-md to on_expand {#603}

    Replace the right-arrow override with the hook. Delete _action_expand (recipes/browse-md:1375) and its 'right' Action binding; register on_expand in BrowserConfig running the lone-heading cascade (_lone_heading_child_id) per id in ids. Remove the duplicated startup cascade (recipes/browse-md:1604-1612) but KEEP the startup b.expand(auto_expand_id) — it now fires on_expand. Confirm the already-expanded -> step-to-first-child behavior is preserved by the framework default _nav_right (or add a minimal handler if a test shows otherwise).     * ## Comments {#603:comments}

      * Migrated browse-md to the on_expand hook. {#603:comment:619}

        REMOVED:
        - _action_expand (the right-arrow override, ~35 lines) — the framework default _nav_right now handles right-arrow (expand collapsed; step cursor onto first child when already expanded).
        - Its Action('right', ...) binding from the recipe action list.
        - The duplicated startup lone-heading cascade in main() (the second copy). Kept the startup b.expand(auto_expand_id) itself.
        WIRED:
        - New module-level _on_expand(ctx, ids): for each id, cid = _lone_heading_child_id(id); if cid is not None: ctx.expand(cid). Registered via on_expand=_on_expand in BrowserConfig(...). The cascade now lives in exactly one place and fires for every expansion source (keyboard, programmatic, startup b.expand before run()). Cascade self-terminates: a heading's child is a section, not a lone-heading file, so the follow-on expand re-fires on_expand once and stops. cascade id comes from the pre-built tree (_FILES), so it does not depend on children being cached (no on_children_loaded composition needed).
        VERIFIED (behavior, not just green):
        - New TestOnExpandCascadeLive builds a REAL framework Browser (loaded like test_lifecycle_hooks.py) wired with the recipe's get_children + on_expand=_on_expand, expands a file-root headlessly (post-queue drain + _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending settle pass, pumped until the expanded set stabilizes). Asserts BOTH the file-root AND its lone heading end up in state.expanded ({path, path#0}). Tests: test_expanding_file_root_cascades_to_lone_heading, test_startup_expand_before_run_cascades (startup path), test_two_headings_no_cascade (negative).
        - Proved the test is load-bearing: with the same Browser built WITHOUT on_expand, only {path} is expanded; WITH the hook, {path, path#0}. So the cascade is genuinely what lands the heading.
        - Already-expanded -> step-into-first-child: covered by test_already_expanded_step_into_first_child driving 'right' through dispatch_key on a real Browser — cursor advances to the first child row (cursor 0 -> 1) and the expanded set is unchanged. Confirms _nav_right preserves the old _action_expand step-into behavior; no custom handler needed.
        - Replaced the old TestActionExpand with TestOnExpand (unit-level per-id cascade decision via a _CascadeCtx stub) and updated TestStartupLoneHeadingCascade -> TestStartupAutoExpand (main() now posts only b.expand(file_root); also asserts b.config.on_expand is _on_expand). The stub Browser doesn't fire hooks, so the live class carries the end-to-end cascade verification.
        TESTS: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_md -> Ran 232, OK. python3 -m unittest discover -s test/unit -> Ran 2000, OK. No tmux/UI test exists for browse-md (only test/unit/test_browse_md.py), so the headless live test is the verification, as the ticket anticipated.
        NOTE: _action_expand used ctx.expand(target_id, autoscroll=True) for the user-driven expand; _nav_right does the same (autoscroll=True). The cascade expand uses ctx.expand(cid) with default autoscroll=False, matching the old cascade. Equivalent behavior.
      * Leader review (r603): APPROVE-WITH-NITS. Accepted nit: already-expanded step-into now uses framework default _nav_right (first VISIBLE child) vs old cursor_to(first child); differs only with an active filter hiding the first child, where the new behavior is arguably more correct. Not a regression; no test added (low value). {#603:comment:620}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Migrate browse-claude tree-right to on_expand + on_children_loaded {#604}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-01 08:59:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-01 10:38:09 UTC

    Replace the expand behavior of _action_tree_right (recipes/browse-claude:4815). Register on_expand + on_children_loaded implementing the documented composition: on_expand -> for each id, if ctx.cached_children(id) is not None jump to latest voice now, else defer in a pending set; on_children_loaded -> fulfill deferred ids. Remove the 'right' override binding and the user-driven .then() chains it relied on (_chain_expand_then_cursor for user expansion). DECISION POINT for the worker/reviewer: _action_tree_right also jumped one level deeper when re-pressing on an already-expanded row — on_expand does NOT fire on re-press, so either keep a minimal 'right' handler for that re-press-only case or accept the framework default step-into; document the choice in a plan comment. Build, then run test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py and test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py; keep green. Spec: "What this retires" (browse-claude).

    * ## Comments {#604:comments}

      * Migrated browse-claude tree-right to on_expand + on_children_loaded. {#604:comment:621}

        BEHAVIORS ENUMERATED (read _action_tree_right in full, old lines 4815-4859):
          (guard1) ctx.cursor is None -> no-op (also non-normal/scope_root rows, since ctx.cursor returns None there).
          (guard2) row not expandable (has_children False) -> no-op. Framework default _nav_right no-ops here too.
          (a) collapsed expandable -> ctx.expand(id, autoscroll=True).then(_land); _land jumps to _latest_voice_among_children (single level), guarded latest is not None and != id.
          (b) already-expanded re-press -> jump to _latest_voice_among_children one level down (single level), guarded latest and != id.
          (c) cross-file preview invalidation via _invalidate_if_cross_file(id) -- fired ONLY on the collapsed-expand path (line 4859), NOT on the re-press path (which returns early). Mode-agnostic (flat+tree) via get_children. Confirmed no other behaviors.
        MAPPING ONTO HOOKS (composition pattern from spec Semantics):
          - module-level pending set _AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP.
          - on_expand(ctx, ids): per id -> _invalidate_if_cross_file(id) [behavior c, now fires only on the real collapse->expand transition, which is exactly the old "row transitions into active state" semantics]; then if ctx.cached_children(id) is not None -> jump now [behavior a, cached]; else add to pending [behavior a, uncached].
          - on_children_loaded(ctx, parent_ids): per pid, if in pending -> discard + jump [behavior a, uncached fulfilment]. Gated on membership so background refresh/tail refetch settlements never yank the cursor.
          - Registered on_expand=_on_expand, on_children_loaded=_on_children_loaded in BrowserConfig. The framework default _nav_right now performs the actual expand (autoscroll) for the collapsed case; the hooks only ADD the jump + invalidation, and now fire for EVERY expansion source (keyboard, programmatic, deep-link, startup) -- which the old .then() could not.
          - Shared helper _jump_to_latest_voice(ctx, id) used by both hooks and the minimal handler.
        RE-PRESS DECISION (behavior b): chose OPTION B -- minimal Action(right) handler that handles ONLY the already-expanded re-press (single cursor_to to the latest voice), and delegates the collapsed case to a plain ctx.expand(id, autoscroll=True) (letting on_expand do the post-expand jump + invalidation).
          Justification: on_expand does NOT fire on re-press (no state change), and children list CHRONOLOGICALLY (oldest-first; verified get_children docstring lines 726/3262 + a live probe). So the framework default step-into would land on the FIRST child = OLDEST voice, whereas browse-claude jumps to the LATEST voice. These genuinely differ. "Jump to latest voice" is browse-claude\047s signature gesture (it is literally the action description string) and the UI test test_tree_right_on_session_jumps_to_latest_turn_root exercises the latest-voice drill. Option B preserves it for one cheap cursor_to without duplicating any framework expand internals (collapsed case is one ctx.expand call). Option A (accept first-child step-into, like browse-md #603) was rejected because browse-md headings are not chronological/voice-ordered so first-child is fine there, but for claude it would regress the latest-voice UX.
        DEEP-LINK / .then() KEPT vs REMOVED:
          - REMOVED: the user-driven right-arrow .then(_land) chain inside _action_tree_right (behavior a expand-then-jump). That sequencing is now the on_expand/on_children_loaded composition.
          - KEPT: _chain_expand_then_cursor (used by _focus_latest_voice_when_ready startup deep-link and _action_toggle_tree t-toggle) -- these are recipe-initiated deep-link/startup chains, NOT user right-arrow expansion. Verified both callers still need per-call sequencing. Did NOT break deep-linking.
          - Interaction analysis: on_expand now ALSO fires on the deep-link/startup b.expand(...) calls. Traced the timing: Pending.then() callbacks resolve synchronously in apply_children_results (during the drain), BEFORE the post-drain _fire_*_if_pending hook block (040-state.py:6302-6303). So _focus_latest_voice_when_ready._fire (which checks "cursor still on scope_root") runs before the hook moves the cursor -> its cancel guard passes -> deep-link proceeds. And the hook + the deep-link CONVERGE: _latest_voice_among_children(deepest turn-root) == _last_voice_id(file) (both the final voice record), so even where the hook fires last it targets the same row. Not coincidental -- they agree by construction. refresh() (Ctrl-R, t-toggle) preserves state.expanded so fires NO on_expand; it refires on_children_loaded for refetched parents but those are not in the pending set -> no yank. Tail worker uses update_data upserts (no expand transition) -> no yank.
        VERIFIED CACHED AND UNCACHED JUMP:
          - Added test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py::TestOnExpandJumpComposition (6 tests, all pass) driving the recipe hook callbacks directly against a real on-disk session + fake ctx with controllable cached_children:
              * test_uncached_expand_defers_then_children_loaded_jumps: cached_children->None => no immediate cursor_to, id parked; on_children_loaded => cursor_to(<sess>#2 latest voice) + pending cleared.
              * test_cached_expand_jumps_immediately: cached_children->[list] => cursor_to(<sess>#2) now, no defer.
              * test_children_loaded_for_non_pending_parent_does_not_jump: settlement not in pending => no cursor_to (no refresh/tail yank).
              * test_cross_file_id_invalidated_on_expand: only bare .jsonl + #agent: invalidated, #prompt: left alone.
              * test_jump_no_voice_among_children_is_noop / test_jump_skips_when_latest_voice_is_self: guards.
          - tmux IS available (/usr/bin/tmux). Full UI suite test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py: 23/23 pass, incl. test_tree_right_on_session_jumps_to_latest_turn_root (re-press + sequential expand), test_J_K_jump_between_voice_rows (uncached session expand lands on latest voice), test_tree_flag_lands_on_latest_voice + test_t_toggle_preserves_cursor (deep-link .then chains intact), test_drills_into_subagent etc.
        TEST RESULTS: test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py 255 pass (249 prior + 6 new). Full test/unit: 2006 pass, OK. test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py: 23 pass, OK.
        Files changed: recipes/browse-claude; test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py. Did NOT touch _action_scope_down (#605).
      * FIX (review should-fix): _AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP leak on collapse-before-settle. {#604:comment:622}

        Bug: expand an UNCACHED umbrella (id parked in _AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP), then collapse it before the async fetch settles. on_collapse is not handled, so the id stayed parked; the framework fires on_children_loaded for that parent regardless of expanded state (apply_children_results -> _set_loading(..., settled=True) unconditionally). _on_children_loaded then found the id parked and called cursor_to(latest_child) onto a now-hidden child, installing a sticky anchor that yanks the cursor on a later re-expand/delivery.
        Fix (recipes/browse-claude, _on_children_loaded): for each parked pid, ALWAYS discard from _AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP, but only _jump_to_latest_voice(ctx, pid) if pid in ctx.state.expanded. Discard-always prevents a stale entry from causing a spurious jump on a later settlement; the still-expanded check prevents the jump when the user collapsed before settle. This is the "recipe checks still-expanded" guard the spec composition pattern prescribes (design spec L600-603, L530-532). Confirmed ctx.state.expanded is the live framework set (Context.state -> _browser._state; already used in _action_tree_right L4917 and state.expanded at L2305).
        Regression test (test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py::TestOnExpandJumpComposition.test_collapse_before_settle_discards_without_jumping): expand uncached (park) -> remove the id from the expanded set (simulated collapse) -> fire _on_children_loaded([pid]) -> assert NO cursor_to AND the id discarded from _AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP. Confirmed LOAD-BEARING: with the guard reverted the test FAILS (cursor_to fired onto <sess>#2, the hidden child); with the fix it passes. To support the test the fake ctx in _make_ctx gained a controllable state.expanded (defaulting to an _AllExpanded sentinel so the other composition tests are unaffected); the existing uncached-jump and non-pending tests were unchanged and still pass.
        Tests: test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py 256 pass (was 255; +1 regression). Full test/unit discovery: 2007 pass, OK. test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py: 23 pass, OK. Files changed: recipes/browse-claude; test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py. Left in working tree (no git add/commit). Did NOT touch _action_scope_down (#605).

  * ## Ticket: Task: Migrate browse-claude scope-down to on_scope_change {#605}

        status: done

    Delete _action_scope_down (recipes/browse-claude:4779) and its 'alt-down' binding entirely, including the mirrored scope_into / _recompute_filter_hidden / _needs_redraw / _fire_scope_change internals (the #500 source). Register on_scope_change(ctx, scope_id, prev_scope_id, direction): when direction=='in' and scope_id is a cross-file id, call ctx.invalidate_preview(scope_id) — the framework's own scope_into now performs the cursor reset / filter recompute / redraw. Build, then run test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py and relevant unit tests; keep green. Spec: "What this retires" (browse-claude), Decisions 5/6.

    * ## Comments {#605:comments}

      * POINT-BY-POINT COMPARISON (override _action_scope_down vs framework default _scope_down in src-tui/070-actions.py:409). Verified empirically with throwaway repros (now deleted) + code reading. {#605:comment:623}

        Override (_action_scope_down, recipes/browse-claude:4779) did, in order:
         1. cur=ctx.cursor; bail if None          -> _scope_down: item=ctx.cursor; if None: return  [MATCH]
         2. bail if not has_children              -> _scope_down: if not has_children: return        [MATCH]
         3. scope_into(state, target_id)          -> _scope_down: scope_into(state, item.id)         [MATCH]
         4. state.cursor = 0                      -> _scope_down: state.cursor = 0                    [MATCH]
         5. ctx.expand(target_id) if uncached     -> _scope_down: if id not in _children: ctx.expand  [MATCH] (kicks the async children fetch; verified empirically the uncached target gets fetched)
         6. _recompute_filter_hidden if _filters  -> _scope_down: ABSENT, but proven NO-OP on this path (see below)
         7. _needs_redraw.add('all')              -> _scope_down: ctx._browser._needs_redraw.add('all') [MATCH]
         8. _fire_scope_change()                  -> _scope_down: _fire_scope_change(scope, prev, 'in') [MATCH, now with direction+prev (#629)]
         9. _invalidate_if_cross_file(target_id)  -> _scope_down: ABSENT == THE MIGRATION TARGET -> on_scope_change
        ITEM 6 (the only divergence besides invalidation) is a verified no-op on the keyboard scope-down path: BOTH the override and the framework _scope_down read ctx.cursor FIRST and bail when it is None. ctx.cursor resolves through the FILTERED visible_items, so it returns None for any _filter_hidden=True row -> the user can never put the cursor on a hidden row to press alt-down. Hence scope-down can only target a VISIBLE row (matching or scaffold, _filter_hidden=False), for which the scope-row exemption inside _recompute_filter_hidden changes nothing. Decisive repro: scoping into a visible scaffold via _scope_down with vs without a trailing _recompute produced IDENTICAL visible trees and IDENTICAL _filter_hidden flags. The #500 scope-row-stays-hidden bug is only reachable via the PROGRAMMATIC Browser.scope_into/scope_out (which scope into any id regardless of cursor/visibility) and is pinned by test/async_/test_browser.py::TestFilterRecomputeOnScopeChange (uses b.scope_into(...), not the keyboard handler). The #500 commit a0b2e10 added the recompute to all three sites (Browser.scope_into, Browser.scope_out, recipe override) for symmetry; on the keyboard override it is functionally redundant.
        CONCLUSION: framework default _scope_down covers every override behaviour EXCEPT cross-file preview invalidation. Safe to delete the override + alt-down binding and move invalidation to on_scope_change(direction=='in').
      * IMPLEMENTED. recipes/browse-claude: {#605:comment:624}

         - Deleted _action_scope_down (the #500 source) entirely — the mirrored scope_into / state.cursor=0 / ctx.expand-if-uncached / _recompute_filter_hidden / _needs_redraw.add('all') / _fire_scope_change internals are all gone. alt-down now binds to the framework default _scope_down (its Action('alt-down', ...) registration removed).
         - Added _on_scope_change(ctx, scope_id, prev_scope_id, direction) and registered it as on_scope_change in BrowserConfig (alongside #604's on_expand/on_children_loaded). Body: . This carries the override's trailing cross-file preview invalidation, now firing for EVERY scope-in source (alt-down, programmatic ctx.scope_into, startup initial_scope) instead of only the overridden key. Reused the existing _invalidate_if_cross_file helper (which gates on _is_cross_file_id and calls _BROWSER.invalidate_preview).
        INVALIDATION MAPPING: override's final  (always, after scope-in) -> hook's  gated on direction=='in'. Scope-OUT does NOT invalidate (the row scoped out of stays as-is; the shallower scope_root was already heavy from its earlier scope-in).
        IMPORTS REMOVED (grepped first — only the deleted override used them):  (free function) and  from the browse_tui import.  kept (17 other uses). Also removed the now-dead  and  stub entries in test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py (their comments explicitly said 'used by the alt-down override'); kept mod.set_preview_op / mod.visible_items (still imported).
        VERIFIED:
         - Unit: test_browse_claude_render (261, +5 new) green; full test/unit discover = 2012 green.
         - Async: full test/async_ discover = 248 green — INCLUDING test/async_/test_browser.py::TestFilterRecomputeOnScopeChange, the #500 pin (uses programmatic b.scope_into, which still does its own _recompute — untouched).
         - tmux UI: test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude = 23 green (incl. test_pid_lands_on_session_with_preview, which scopes in programmatically via --pid and now drives the cross-file preview upgrade through on_scope_change; and test_drills_into_subagent).
         - Added TestOnScopeChangeCrossFileUpgrade (5 tests) in test_browse_claude_render.py: scope-IN cross-file (.jsonl, #agent:) -> invalidate_preview fires; scope-IN in-file (#prompt:0) -> no invalidate; scope-OUT (incl. to root) -> no invalidate.
        NO test asserted the old override's behaviour, so none needed updating (the deleted scope_into/recompute stubs aside).
        SURPRISE / BEHAVIOUR-GAP CHECK: the framework keyboard _scope_down does NOT call _recompute_filter_hidden (only Browser.scope_into/scope_out do). Investigated whether dropping the override's recompute loses the #500 fix. It does NOT: both the override and _scope_down read ctx.cursor first and bail on None, and ctx.cursor returns None for any _filter_hidden=True row (filtered out of visible_items) — so the keyboard path can only scope into a VISIBLE (non-hidden) row, for which the scope-row exemption is a no-op. Decisive repro confirmed identical visible tree + _filter_hidden flags with vs without the trailing recompute. The #500 bug is only reachable via the programmatic API and stays pinned by TestFilterRecomputeOnScopeChange. No behaviour dropped.
      * IMPLEMENTED. recipes/browse-claude: {#605:comment:625}

        - Deleted _action_scope_down (the #500 source) entirely — the mirrored scope_into / state.cursor=0 / ctx.expand-if-uncached / _recompute_filter_hidden / _needs_redraw.add('all') / _fire_scope_change internals are all gone. alt-down now binds to the framework default _scope_down (its Action('alt-down', ...) registration removed).
        - Added _on_scope_change(ctx, scope_id, prev_scope_id, direction) and registered it as on_scope_change in BrowserConfig (alongside #604's on_expand/on_children_loaded). Body: when direction == 'in', call _invalidate_if_cross_file(scope_id). This carries the override's trailing cross-file preview invalidation, now firing for EVERY scope-in source (alt-down, programmatic ctx.scope_into, startup initial_scope) instead of only the overridden key. Reuses the existing _invalidate_if_cross_file helper (which gates on _is_cross_file_id and calls _BROWSER.invalidate_preview).
        INVALIDATION MAPPING: override's final _invalidate_if_cross_file(target_id) (always, after scope-in) -> hook's _invalidate_if_cross_file(scope_id) gated on direction == 'in'. Scope-OUT does NOT invalidate (the row scoped out of stays as-is; the shallower scope_root was already heavy from its earlier scope-in).
        IMPORTS REMOVED (grepped first — only the deleted override used them): scope_into (free function) and _recompute_filter_hidden from the browse_tui import. _BROWSER kept (17 other uses). Also removed the now-dead mod.scope_into and mod._recompute_filter_hidden stub entries in test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py (their comments explicitly said "used by the alt-down override"); kept mod.set_preview_op / mod.visible_items (still imported).
        VERIFIED:
        - Unit: test_browse_claude_render = 261 (+5 new) green; full test/unit discover = 2012 green.
        - Async: full test/async_ discover = 248 green — INCLUDING test/async_/test_browser.py::TestFilterRecomputeOnScopeChange, the #500 pin (uses programmatic b.scope_into, which still does its own _recompute — untouched).
        - tmux UI: test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude = 23 green (incl. test_pid_lands_on_session_with_preview, which scopes in programmatically via --pid and now drives the cross-file preview upgrade through on_scope_change; and test_drills_into_subagent).
        - Added TestOnScopeChangeCrossFileUpgrade (5 tests): scope-IN cross-file (.jsonl, #agent:) -> invalidate_preview fires; scope-IN in-file (#prompt:0) -> no invalidate; scope-OUT (incl. to root) -> no invalidate.
        NO test asserted the old override's behaviour, so none needed updating (the deleted scope_into/recompute stubs aside).
        SURPRISE / BEHAVIOUR-GAP CHECK: the framework keyboard _scope_down does NOT call _recompute_filter_hidden (only Browser.scope_into/scope_out do). Investigated whether dropping the override's recompute loses the #500 fix. It does NOT: both the override and _scope_down read ctx.cursor first and bail on None, and ctx.cursor returns None for any _filter_hidden=True row (filtered out of visible_items) — so the keyboard path can only scope into a VISIBLE (non-hidden) row, for which the scope-row exemption is a no-op. Decisive repro confirmed identical visible tree + _filter_hidden flags with vs without the trailing recompute. The #500 bug is only reachable via the programmatic API and stays pinned by TestFilterRecomputeOnScopeChange. No behaviour dropped.
      * Leader review (r605): APPROVE-WITH-NITS, no blockers/should-fix. Verified the override-vs-default comparison, the _recompute_filter_hidden no-op proof, and the #500 async pin (passes via the untouched programmatic path). Fixed two stale docstrings flagged in review: _invalidate_if_cross_file ('right-arrow and scope-down overrides' -> on_expand/on_scope_change hooks) and _on_scope_change (dropped the inaccurate 'startup initial_scope' claim — construction-time scope setup runs before the loop and does not fire the hook, matching the old override). {#605:comment:626}

* ## Ticket: Optimization: skip hook fire-path prep when the callback is unset {#627}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-01 21:33:41 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-01 21:38:40 UTC

  Follow-up to the lifecycle-hooks epic (#628). The new hooks' drain-time fire methods in src-tui/040-state.py do non-trivial preparation (set diffs, set() snapshots, tuple() building, term_size() syscall, pending-set harvesting) EVERY drain regardless of whether the user installed the callback. Skip that work entirely when the callback is None, matching the existing _fire_cursor_change_if_pending (which early-returns before its visible_items lookup). Targets: (1) _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending (~:4661) — early-return when BOTH on_expand and on_collapse are None, before the `expanded != _last_expanded` diff and the `set(expanded)` snapshot; (2) _fire_filter_change_if_pending (~:4754) — early-return when on_filter_change is None, before building `tuple(self.filters)`; (3) _fire_search_change_if_pending (~:4729) — early-return when on_search_change is None; (4) _fire_resize_if_pending (~:4775) — after clearing _resize_pending, early-return when on_resize is None, before reading term_size(); (5) the on_children_loaded harvest sites (~:3782 in _apply, ~:4894 in apply_children_results) — only `_children_loaded_pending.update(state._settled_parents)` when on_children_loaded is not None (so the pending set stays empty and _fire_children_loaded_if_pending short-circuits). Leave the existing on_cursor_change/on_selection_change/on_scope_change/on_quit as-is (they already guard None before prep). The scope re-baseline of _last_expanded (Browser.scope_into/out + keyboard handlers) may stay unconditional — it only runs on rare scope transitions, not per drain — but note it in a comment. Update the now-inaccurate docstrings that claim "_last_* is always re-snapshotted even with no handler" to reflect the skip (and the accepted consequence: the snapshot goes stale while unset, which is fine because hooks are construction-time-fixed; matches on_cursor_change). TDD: keep all existing lifecycle tests green (they set handlers, so the prep still runs and fires correctly); add a focused test or two proving the skip when unset (e.g. with no on_filter_change, the filters tuple is not diffed / _last_filters not advanced; or assert via a spy that the prep path is not taken). Build with ./build-tui.sh, run test/unit + test/async_, keep green.

  * ## Comments {#627:comments}

    * Implemented the fire-path skip-when-unset optimization. Five sites in src-tui/040-state.py: {#627:comment:628}

      (1) _fire_expand_collapse_if_pending: early-return when BOTH _on_expand AND _on_collapse are None, before the expanded!=_last_expanded diff and the set(expanded) snapshot.
      (2) _fire_filter_change_if_pending: early-return when _on_filter_change is None, before building tuple(self.filters).
      (3) _fire_search_change_if_pending: early-return when _on_search_change is None.
      (4) _fire_resize_if_pending: clear _resize_pending FIRST (so a single SIGWINCH never lingers), then early-return when _on_resize is None, before reading term_size().
      (5) on_children_loaded harvest (two sites: _apply after apply_ops, and apply_children_results): _children_loaded_pending.update(_settled_parents) is now gated on _on_children_loaded is not None, so the pending set stays empty when unset and _fire_children_loaded_if_pending short-circuits on 'if not _children_loaded_pending'. Left the _set_loading(..., settled=True) list-append into _settled_parents unconditional (cheap, reset each pass).
      For (1)-(4) I also removed the now-dead inner 'if self._on_* is not None:' re-check (the early-return makes it always-true), matching the _fire_cursor_change_if_pending model.
      Tradeoff: the _last_* snapshot (expanded/filters/search/size) is no longer re-snapshotted while the handler is unset, so it may go stale. Accepted because hooks are construction-time-fixed (set via BrowserConfig / before run()), matching on_cursor_change which likewise doesn't advance _last_cursor_id when unset.
      Scope re-baseline of _last_expanded (Browser.scope_into/scope_out in 040-state.py, _scope_down/_scope_up in 070-actions.py) LEFT unconditional with a one-line comment at each of the four sites noting it's intentional — runs only on rare scope transitions, not per drain.
      Docstrings: replaced the now-false 'always re-snapshotted even with no handler' claims in all four affected fire methods with the new behavior (prep+snapshot skipped when unset; snapshot may go stale; fine because construction-time-fixed; matches on_cursor_change).
      Load-bearing verification (test/unit/test_lifecycle_hooks.py): converted the four existing 'missing handler still advances baseline / still clears pending' tests to assert the NEW skip behavior (snapshot NOT advanced / pending stays empty) and added test_missing_handler_skips_term_size_read_but_clears_flag for resize and test_one_handler_set_still_runs_prep for the both-unset boundary. Ran the converted/new tests against the PRE-optimization code: exactly 5 failures (one per site) confirming they are load-bearing; all pass after. All existing handler-installed tests stayed green (the main safety net).
      build-tui.sh OK. Full suites: test/unit 2014 OK, test/async_ 248 OK.

* ## Ticket: Epic: browse-git improvements — color, decorations, modes {#613}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-02 01:29:59 UTC

  Implement the approved design in
  `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-browse-git-improvements-design.md`.
  Read that spec first — it is the contract. Two small framework
  prerequisites land first, then the recipe is built up mode by mode.
  All work happens in the `worktree-browse-git-improvements` worktree.
  Recipe tickets all edit the single file `recipes/browse-git`, so they
  run sequentially. Build the binary (`./build-tui.sh`) before UI tests;
  run `python -m pytest test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py -q` and the
  relevant unit tests after each ticket.

  * ## Ticket: Framework: item.chips trailing colored chips {#614}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:16:25 UTC

    Add an optional `chips: list[(text, style)]` rendering to the
    **default** formatter so recipes can show several colored labels
    after the title without losing the selection / ▼▶ / indent chrome.

    Scope:
    - In `src-tui/050-render.py` `format_item_segments`, in the
      `kind=='normal'` default path (after the title segment), append one
      segment per chip in `getattr(item, 'chips', None) or ()`, styled
      via the existing `_TAG_STYLE` map (same `[..]` bracket look as the
      `tag` segment). Color is the segment `fg`; never embed ANSI in text.
    - Do NOT touch the `format_item is not None` override branch.
    - Document `item.chips` in `docs/api.md` next to the `tag` /
      `tag_style` description on `Item`.
    - Unit test: `format_item_segments` on an Item with two chips emits
      the two extra colored segments with the expected fg/bold; an Item
      without chips is unchanged (regression guard).

    Keep it ~6 lines in the formatter. No new public types.

    * ## Comments {#614:comments}

      * Implemented item.chips in the default formatter. {#614:comment:623}

        src-tui/050-render.py format_item_segments: after the title segment in the kind=='normal' default path, append one segment per (text, style) in getattr(item, 'chips', None) or (), styled via _TAG_STYLE.get(style, _TAG_STYLE['']) exactly like tag_style; segment text is ' [text]' (color on fg, never embedded). Docstring updated with the new chip-segment layout line. The format_item override branch is untouched.
        docs/api.md: added a 'chips' row to the 'attributes honoured by the list renderer' table on Item, next to tag/tag_style/row_bg/row_fg.
        test/unit/test_render.py (TestFormatItemSegmentsDefault): added test_chips_emit_trailing_colored_segments (two chips -> two ' [text]' segments after the title with green/yellow fg+bold), test_chip_unknown_style_falls_back_to_default (unknown style -> _TAG_STYLE['']), and test_no_chips_unchanged (regression guard: no chips / [] / None all render identically with no '[' segments).
        Verified: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_render -> Ran 107 tests OK; ./run-tests.sh -> Ran 2380 tests OK (skipped=4, pre-existing).

  * ## Ticket: Framework: ctx.collapse(id) / browser.collapse(id) {#615}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:20:37 UTC

    Add the missing single-node collapse, symmetric to the existing
    `expand(id)`. Needed for the recipe's Enter = flip-expand.

    Scope:
    - Add `Browser.collapse(id)` (in `src-tui/040-state.py`, near
      `expand` / `collapse_all`) that posts onto the main thread and
      does `state.expanded.discard(id)` plus the same dirty/repaint marks
      that the Left-arrow handler `_nav_left` uses
      (`mark_visible_dirty` / cursor-change). Mirror `expand`'s
      thread-safety and return type.
    - Add the `Context.collapse(id)` pass-through in
      `src-tui/060-context.py` alongside `expand` / `collapse_all`.
    - Document both in `docs/api.md` (Context pass-throughs + Browser
      thread-safe ops, beside `expand`).
    - Unit test: after `collapse(id)`, `id` is absent from
      `state.expanded`; collapsing an already-collapsed id is a no-op.

    * ## Comments {#615:comments}

      * Implemented Browser.collapse(id) (src-tui/040-state.py, right after expand) + Context.collapse(id) pass-through (src-tui/060-context.py, after expand). Mirrors collapse_all's idiom: posts onto the main thread via self.post(_do), where _do runs state.expanded.discard(id) + mark_visible_dirty + mark_cursor_changed (the exact effect of the Left-arrow _nav_left handler's collapse branch). Return type is None (not Pending): no fetch is involved, so there is nothing to await -- consistent with collapse_all/expand_subtree which also return None. Documented in docs/api.md in 3 places (Context signature block + summary table beside expand; Context push-API pass-throughs beside collapse_all; Browser thread-safe ops beside expand). Added 5 unit tests in test/unit/test_collapse_expand.py (TestCollapse: removes id / no-op when already collapsed / folds subtree from visible list; TestContextPassthroughs: collapse + collapse no-op). Targeted test 15/15 OK; full ./run-tests.sh 2385 tests OK (skipped=4). {#615:comment:624}

  * ## Ticket: Recipe: structural backbone (ids, helpers, args, errors, keys) {#633}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:27:55 UTC

    Rebuild the recipe scaffold. No per-mode listing logic yet beyond a
    stub — this ticket establishes the spine that later mode tickets
    fill in.

    Scope (all in `recipes/browse-git`):
    - Module globals: `_log_limit`, `_mode` (default 'commits'),
      `_browser` (stashed in main for `preview_width`), `_revs`,
      `_paths`.
    - `_parse_id(item_id)` — `kind, _, rest = item_id.partition(':')`,
      then parse `rest` per the spec's ID table (`file`/`status`/`reflog`
      via `split(':',1)`; `stash` 1-or-2 parts; `ref` keeps all of rest;
      `commit` is the sha).
    - `_git_color(*args)` → `git -c color.ui=always <args>` stdout.
    - `_colorize_diff(raw)` → pipe through
      `delta --no-gitconfig --paging never --width <_browser.preview_width
      or 80>` when `shutil.which('delta')`, else return git-colored diff
      (caller passes already-`--color=always` text, or fall back to raw).
      Decide the seam so callers can produce the diff once.
    - md2ansi soft import (`_md2ansi_fn`, `_MD_COLOR`) per browse-fs.
    - `main()`: pop `-n` (existing helper) and `--mode NAME` (validate
      against the 5 names; unknown → stderr + exit 2); classify remaining
      positionals (after `--` = pathspec; `os.path.exists` = pathspec;
      `git rev-parse --verify --quiet '<arg>^{commit}'` ok = rev; else
      stderr "unknown path or revision" + exit 2) into `_revs`/`_paths`.
    - Fail-fast: `git` missing or not a work tree → stderr + `sys.exit(2)`
      BEFORE constructing the Browser (remove the in-TUI error row for
      these two cases).
    - Wire keys/handlers: `on_enter` = flip expand/collapse using
      `ctx.expand` / `ctx.collapse` (no-op on leaves; never quits);
      backtick (`` ` ``) action opens `ctx.pick` of the 5 modes → set
      `_mode`, retitle, `ctx.refresh()`; `E` action edits the working-tree
      file for `file:`/`status:` rows via $EDITOR; `m` action (only when
      `_md2ansi_fn`) toggles `_MD_COLOR` + invalidates preview. Leave `v`
      / `e` to the built-ins.
    - `get_children` / `get_preview` dispatch on `_parse_id` kind with a
      thin `commits`-mode implementation so the app still runs; later
      tickets flesh out each kind.

    Unit tests: `_parse_id` (all kinds incl. colon paths / slash
    refnames), `_colorize_diff` returns `\x1b[`-bearing text on both the
    delta and git-fallback paths (monkeypatch `shutil.which`), positional
    classification (path / rev / `--` / unknown→SystemExit).

    * ## Comments {#633:comments}

      * Rebuilt recipes/browse-git scaffold: KIND-prefixed ids + _parse_id, _git_color / _colorize_diff colorization helpers (delta-or-git-color seam), md2ansi soft import + m toggle, --mode validation, -n limit, positional rev/path/-- classification with fail-fast (git missing / not a work tree -> stderr + exit 2 before launch). Wired on_enter expand/collapse toggle, backtick mode picker, E working-tree edit. Commits mode is fully functional (log rows -> file rows -> colored diff + colored show --stat); status/ref/stash/reflog kinds are stubs for #634+. Added test/unit/test_browse_git.py (24 tests). Full suite green: new unit 24/24 OK; test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py 3/3 passed; ./run-tests.sh 2409 tests OK (4 pre-existing skips). {#633:comment:625}

  * ## Ticket: Recipe: commits mode end-to-end {#634}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:41:33 UTC

    Flesh out the default mode with decorations, chips, status-colored
    files, and colorized previews.

    Scope (`recipes/browse-git`):
    - Root listing: `git log --format='%H%x00%D%x00%an%x00%ar%x00%s'
      -n LIMIT [<_revs>] [-- <_paths>]`. Build each commit Item:
      `id='commit:<sha>'`, `tag=sha[:7]` yellow, `title=subject`,
      `has_children=True`, and `chips` = ref-decoration chips parsed from
      `%D` (HEAD green-bold, local branch cyan, remote blue, `tag:`
      yellow) followed by a dim `author · reldate` chip. `show_ids='never'`.
    - `%D` decoration parser → `list[(text, style)]` (own helper, unit
      tested).
    - File drill-down (`commit:<sha>` children): `git show --name-status
      --format= <sha>` → `file:<sha>:<path>` rows with the A/M/D/R status
      letter in `tag` (green/yellow/red/cyan; show new path for renames).
    - Preview: `file:` leaf → `_colorize_diff` of `git show <sha> --
      <path>`. `commit:` node → md2ansi(message via `git show -s
      --format=%B`) when `_MD_COLOR` + `_git_color('show','--stat',
      '--format=', sha)`.
    - Title reflects mode + any path/rev filter.

    Unit tests: `%D` parser (mixed refs → correct chips), `--name-status`
    XY/letter parsing incl. rename. tmux: commits list renders; a
    branch-head commit shows its decoration chip; Enter toggles a
    commit's file list open and closed.

    * ## Comments {#634:comments}

      * Fleshed out commits mode end-to-end. Root listing now uses git log --format='%H%x00%D%x00%an%x00%ar%x00%s' (splitlines then split NUL); each commit Item gets ref-decoration chips parsed from %D plus a dim 'author · reldate' chip. Added _parse_decorations(deco, remotes=None) -> [(text,style)] (HEAD green, local branch cyan, remote-tracking blue, tag yellow) — remote vs slash-bearing local branch is disambiguated by a known-remotes set (auto-detected via git remote, cached; passable for unit tests) so a local 'feature/x' stays cyan while 'origin/main' is blue. NOTE: the framework chip styler only honors named palette styles and has no bold-green entry, so the HEAD chip uses 'green' (not green-bold). File drill-down uses git show --name-status; added _parse_name_status(text) -> [(letter, display_path)] mapping A/M/D/R(/C/T) via _STATUS_LETTER_STYLE (green/yellow/red/cyan), showing the NEW path for renames/copies (id keys on it too). Commit preview = md2ansi(message via show -s --format=%B) + _git_color show --stat; file leaf = _colorize_diff seam. Window title now bakes in mode + any rev/path filter (no runtime retitle API). Tests: unit module 34/34 OK (added TestParseDecorations + TestParseNameStatus); UI 5/5 (added branch-head decoration-chip + Enter open/close toggle); ./run-tests.sh 2421 tests OK (4 pre-existing skips). {#634:comment:626}

  * ## Ticket: Recipe: reflog mode {#635}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:51:03 UTC

    Scope (`recipes/browse-git`):
    - Root listing when `_mode=='reflog'`: `git reflog
      --format='%H%x00%gd%x00%cr%x00%D%x00%gs' -n LIMIT` → Items
      `id='reflog:<n>:<sha>'`, `tag=sha[:7]` yellow, `title=<%gs>`,
      `has_children=True`, `chips` = `HEAD@{n}` dim + `reldate` dim + the
      `%D` decoration chips (reuse the commits parser).
    - Children: `file:<sha>:<path>` (reuse the commits file path + diff).
    - `reflog:` node preview: same md2ansi message + colored `show
      --stat <sha>` as a commit node.

    tmux: `--mode reflog` lists reflog entries with selector + date.

    * ## Comments {#635:comments}

      * Added reflog mode. Root listing (_reflog_root) runs git reflog --format='%H%x00%gd%x00%cr%x00%D%x00%gs' -n LIMIT; per-line build factored into _reflog_row(n, line) (unit-testable, returns None for empty/under-filled lines). id='reflog:<n>:<sha>' carries the 0-based enumeration index for uniqueness (reflog revisits the same sha). tag=sha[:7] yellow, title=%gs action subject, chips = dim %gd selector (HEAD@{n}) + dim %cr reldate + _parse_decorations(%D) (reused). Children of reflog:<n>:<sha> reuse _commit_files(sha); preview reuses _commit_preview(sha) — a sha is a sha, only the root listing + row chips differ. Wired the 'reflog' kind in get_children/get_preview using parsed[2]. Also fixed the carryover #634 nit: _STATUS_LETTER_STYLE comment now reads 'letter -> tag_style'. Tests: unit 38/38 OK (added TestReflogRow: full record id/chips, index carried, malformed/empty -> None); UI 6/6 (added --mode reflog lists HEAD@{ selector + commit action subject); ./run-tests.sh 2426 tests OK (4 pre-existing skips). Verified on screen: reflog rows show [HEAD@{n}] [reldate] [HEAD] [main] chips and drill into files + colored preview. {#635:comment:627}

  * ## Ticket: Recipe: status (working-tree) mode {#619}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:57:39 UTC

    Scope (`recipes/browse-git`):
    - Root listing when `_mode=='status'`: `git status --porcelain` →
      one `status:<XY>:<path>` row per entry; `tag` = status letter
      colored (A/M/D/R/?? → green/yellow/red/cyan/dim); `title` = path.
      Clean tree → single dim "working tree clean" row (non-expandable).
    - Leaf preview by `XY`: staged part → `_colorize_diff(git diff
      --cached -- path)`; worktree part → `_colorize_diff(git diff --
      path)`; untracked `??` → render file as an addition (e.g.
      `git diff --no-index -- /dev/null path`); when both staged and
      worktree apply, concatenate with headers.

    Unit test: XY → which diff command(s). tmux: `--mode status` lists a
    modified file with its status tag.

    * ## Comments {#619:comments}

      * Added status (working-tree) mode. Chose git status --porcelain -z over the line-based --porcelain: -z never quotes paths (paths with spaces/odd chars survive verbatim) and renames become two NUL fields (new path first, then old) which is simpler than parsing the inline ' -> ' arrow. Three pure helpers (all unit-tested): _parse_porcelain_z(data)->[(XY,path)] (splits NUL fields; for R/C consumes the extra old-path field, keeps new path); _status_tag(xy)->one letter (X if set else Y; '?' for '??'); _status_diff_plan(xy,path)->[(header,argv)] selecting staged (X set, not ?), unstaged (Y set, not ?), or untracked (--no-index /dev/null path) — MM yields BOTH. _status_root builds status:<XY>:<path> leaves (XY kept exactly 2 chars so _parse_id round-trips space-bearing codes); clean tree -> single dim non-drillable 'working tree clean' row (id status:clean). _status_preview runs each planned diff git-colored through _colorize_diff, concatenating two-sided diffs under '── staged ──'/'── unstaged ──' headers. Added '?':'dim' to _STATUS_LETTER_STYLE. IMPORTANT edge case: git diff --no-index exits non-zero whenever files differ; changed _git_color to return stdout-or-stderr (prefer non-empty stdout) instead of gating on rc==0, so the untracked diff is captured. Tests: unit 54/54 OK (TestPorcelainParse incl. rename/copy/spaces, TestStatusTag, TestStatusDiffPlan covering M /space-M/MM/A/space-D/??); UI 7/7 (--mode status shows a worktree-modified file with an [M] tag); ./run-tests.sh 2443 OK (4 pre-existing skips). Verified on screen: [M]/[?] tags, unstaged + two-sided (MM) previews render colored. {#619:comment:628}

  * ## Ticket: Recipe: stash mode {#620}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 01:06:31 UTC

    Scope (`recipes/browse-git`):
    - Root listing when `_mode=='stash'`: `git stash list
      --format='%gd%x00%cr%x00%gs'` → `stash:<n>` rows, `tag='stash@{n}'`
      yellow, `chips=[reldate dim]`, `title=<%gs>`, `has_children=True`.
      Empty → single dim "no stashes" row.
    - Children: `git stash show --name-status <n>` → `stash:<n>:<path>`
      rows with status-letter tag.
    - `stash:<n>` node preview → `_colorize_diff(git stash show -p
      stash@{n})`; `stash:<n>:<path>` leaf → `_colorize_diff(git show
      stash@{n} -- path)`.

    tmux: `--mode stash` lists stashes (use a temp repo with a stash).

    * ## Comments {#620:comments}

      * Added stash mode. _stash_root runs git stash list --format='%gd%x00%cr%x00%gs'; per-line build in _stash_row(line) (returns None for malformed/empty/bad-selector). id='stash:<n>' where n is extracted from the %gd selector via _stash_index('stash@{n}')->'n' (not enumeration), tag=selector yellow, chips=[(reldate,'dim')], title=%gs (WIP on ...), has_children=True. Children of stash:<n> via _stash_files(n): git stash show --name-status stash@{n} reusing _parse_name_status + _STATUS_LETTER_STYLE for status-letter tags. Previews: stash:<n> node -> _colorize_diff(_git_color('stash','show','-p','stash@{n}')); stash:<n>:<path> leaf -> _colorize_diff(_git_color('show','stash@{n}','--',path)). get_children/get_preview distinguish node vs leaf by len(parsed) (2 vs 3). EMPTY-ROW HANDLING: empty stash list -> single dim non-drillable row with sentinel id 'stash:none'; its non-numeric n is guarded both in _stash_files (str(n).isdigit() else []) and get_preview (n.isdigit() else ''), so the sentinel never routes to a stash ref. All git refs built as proper 'stash@{n}' strings (not bare n). Tests: unit 62/62 OK (TestStashIndex, TestStashRow incl. index-from-selector/malformed/bad-selector/empty); UI 8/8 (--mode stash lists stash@{0} + WIP on subject); ./run-tests.sh 2452 OK (4 pre-existing skips). Verified on screen: stash rows + reldate chips, node diff preview, drill into [M] file + per-file diff. {#620:comment:629}

  * ## Ticket: Recipe: branches mode {#621}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:12:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 01:11:59 UTC

    Scope (`recipes/browse-git`):
    - Root listing when `_mode=='branches'`: `git for-each-ref
      --sort=-committerdate --format=...` over refs/heads, refs/remotes,
      refs/tags → `ref:<refname>` rows, `tag` = kind
      (branch/remote/tag) colored, `title=<refname>`, `has_children=True`.
    - `ref:<refname>` children: `git log
      --format='%H%x00%D%x00%an%x00%ar%x00%s' <refname> -n LIMIT` →
      `commit:<sha>` rows (reuse the commits builder). From there the
      commit→file→diff drill-down is shared.
    - `ref:` node preview → `_git_color('show','--stat', refname)`.

    tmux: `--mode branches` lists at least the current branch; expanding
    it reveals commits.

    * ## Comments {#621:comments}

      * Added branches mode + refactored the log listing for reuse. REFACTOR: extracted _commit_log_items(revs, paths) from _commits_root — it builds the git log --format=... argv from explicit revs/paths and returns the decorated commit Items. _commits_root() now just calls _commit_log_items(_revs, _paths); ref drill-down calls _commit_log_items([refname], []). No duplication; the commit->file->diff drill-down below is shared unchanged. _branches_root runs one git for-each-ref --sort=-committerdate over refs/heads refs/remotes refs/tags with --format='%(refname)%00%(refname:short)'; per-line _parse_for_each_ref_line(line)->(kind, short) classifies kind from the FULL refname prefix (refs/heads/->branch, refs/remotes/->remote, refs/tags/->tag), returns None for blank/no-NUL/unknown namespace. Rows: id='ref:<short>', tag=kind word colored via _REF_KIND_STYLE (branch cyan / remote blue / tag yellow), title=short, has_children=True. Wired 'ref' kind: get_children -> _commit_log_items([refname],[]); get_preview -> _commit_preview(refname) (a refname resolves as a commit-ish, so the existing message+show --stat node preview works unchanged — cleanest reuse). _parse_id already keeps the full refname after 'ref:' so origin/main slashes survive. Tests: unit 69/69 OK (TestForEachRefParse: branch incl. slash-bearing, remote, tag, unknown-namespace skip, blank/no-NUL, kind-style palette); UI 9/9 (--mode branches lists main with [branch] tag, Right drills to commit subjects); ./run-tests.sh 2460 OK (4 pre-existing skips). Verified on screen: [branch]/[tag] rows, ref node preview, drill into commits with full chips then files+diffs. {#621:comment:630}

* ## Ticket: browse-claude: pick up subagents stored under a cwd-relocated (worktree) project dir {#644}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-02 00:52:17 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-02 01:40:20 UTC

  **Problem.** After a recent Claude Code update, `browse-claude` stops
  showing subagents for sessions that entered a git worktree mid-run
  (e.g. session `f9292f70-…` in `-home-ubuntu-sandvault-src-browse-tui`).
  The transcripts exist on disk but the recipe looks in the wrong dir.

  **Root cause / findings (verified against live data):**
  * Subagent transcripts still live at
    `<CLAUDE_ROOT>/<encoded_proj>/<session_id>/subagents/agent-<agentId>.jsonl`
    (+ `agent-<agentId>.meta.json` = `{agentType, description, name, toolUseId}`).
  * The change: `<encoded_proj>` is derived from the **cwd at dispatch
    time**, where `encode(cwd) = cwd.replace('/','-').replace('.','-')`.
    When a session enters a worktree, its subagents land under the
    worktree's project dir, NOT the project dir holding the main `.jsonl`.
  * Wiring into the main session: each dispatch is an `Agent` (or legacy
    `Task`) tool_use; the matching tool_result user record carries
    `toolUseResult.agentId` (+ `agentType`, `prompt`, final `content`,
    stats). `isSidechain` is now always false/absent — NOT a marker.
  * **Legacy is a strict subset:** non-worktree sessions keep cwd at the
    project root, so `encode(cwd)` == the session's own project dir →
    co-located, exactly today's behavior. KEEP LEGACY WORKING.

  **The recipe's broken assumption.** `_subagents_dir()` (recipes/browse-claude:601)
  and `_count_subagents()` (:610) derive the subagents path purely from the
  session `.jsonl`'s own directory. ~10 call sites funnel through this.
  `_outer_subagent_group_id()` (:4550) makes the same assumption in reverse.

  **Acceptance:** session `f9292f70` (and the worktree fixture) shows its
  4 subagents in BOTH flat (`--no-tree`) and tree views, drill-in works,
  and all existing `test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py` tests stay green.
  Worker model = Opus, effort = high. Leader commits per ticket; workers
  do NOT self-commit. Run a review subagent (goal met? overengineered?
  stale code?) after each non-trivial ticket.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Resolution layer + flat-mode pickup (TDD) {#645}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:52:17 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 00:59:46 UTC

    Add the cwd-aware resolution helpers and route the FLAT-mode subagent
    paths through them. Legacy co-located layout must keep working
    unchanged (it is the first candidate).

    **New helpers (place near `_subagents_dir`, recipes/browse-claude:601):**
    * `_encode_project_path(cwd)` — `cwd.replace('/','-').replace('.','-')`.
      (Forward of `_decode_project_path` at :202; that decode is lossy/display-only — do NOT reuse it.)
    * `_session_cwds(session_jsonl_path)` — return the set of distinct
      `cwd` strings in the session file. Cheap single-pass (regex over raw
      bytes, e.g. `rb'"cwd"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"'`, no full JSON parse).
      Cache by `(path, size, mtime)` in a module dict to avoid re-reads.
    * `_candidate_subagents_dirs(session_jsonl_path)` — ordered, deduped,
      existing-only list: legacy co-located dir first
      (`splitext(session)[0]/subagents`), then
      `<CLAUDE_ROOT>/<encode(cwd)>/<sid>/subagents` for each session cwd.
    * `_resolve_agent_jsonl(session_jsonl_path, agent_id)` — first existing
      `agent-<id>.jsonl` across candidate dirs; fall back to the legacy
      co-located path STRING when none exist (so callers' `os.path.isfile`
      checks behave exactly as before for truly-absent agents).

    **Route flat-mode through them:**
    * `_count_subagents` (:610): count distinct agent files across candidate
      dirs. Keep the legacy fast path cheap — check co-located first; only
      scan cwds when co-located is absent/empty. Update caller `_list_sessions`
      (:559) to pass the session `.jsonl` path.
    * `_list_subagents_for_session` (:634): enumerate across all candidate
      dirs, dedup by agent_id (first wins); read each meta from the SAME dir
      its agent file was found in.
    * `_list_subagent_messages` (:711) + get_children flat `#agent:` branch
      (:425) + the flat `#agent:` paths in tail/preview/resolve that you do
      NOT cover in ticket #2: use `_resolve_agent_jsonl`.

    **Tests (extend test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py):** add a
    `relocated`/`worktree` variant to `_make_fake_claude` — keep the
    session `.jsonl` under `-home-test-project/` but put its subagents
    under a cwd-derived project dir (e.g. records carry
    `cwd=/home/test/project/.worktrees/wt` →
    `-home-test-project--worktrees-wt/<sid>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl`), and
    include an `Agent` tool_use + tool_result with `toolUseResult.agentId`.
    Write the test FIRST and watch it fail, then implement. Assert flat
    list shows the subagent description, count tag shows the sub, and
    drill-in shows the subagent transcript. Run `./run-tests.sh` (or the
    browse-claude test module) — all green.

    * ## Comments {#645:comments}

      * Implemented cwd-aware resolution layer + routed flat-mode pickup. Helpers added near _subagents_dir: _encode_project_path (forward of lossy _decode), _session_cwds (regex over raw bytes, cached by (path,size,mtime) in _SESSION_CWDS_CACHE), _candidate_subagents_dirs (co-located dir FIRST, then <CLAUDE_ROOT>/<encode(cwd)>/<sid>/subagents per distinct cwd, deduped+existing-only), _resolve_agent_jsonl (first existing across candidates, falls back to legacy co-located STRING). Routed: _count_subagents (now takes session .jsonl path; co-located fast path short-circuits before scanning cwds; dedups agent ids), _list_sessions caller updated to pass full path, _list_subagents_for_session (enumerate all candidate dirs, dedup by agent_id first-wins, meta read from same dir as agent file), _list_subagent_messages (via _resolve_agent_jsonl). Flat #agent: get_children branch already routes through _list_subagent_messages. Added 'import re'. TDD: 3 new tests (test_relocated_subagent_count_tag, test_relocated_subagent_listed_under_session, test_drills_into_relocated_subagent) + 'relocated' param on _make_fake_claude. Watched them fail (no sub tag, PROBE-DESC not listed), then green. Edge case: relocated fixture surfaces the cwd-derived dir as an empty 0-sess project in the root listing (expected real-world behavior). Deferred to #646: tree-mode #agent: branches at :421, and the tree-only _subagents_dir uses at 2879/2943/3051/3368/3749/3822/4137/4192. Full suite green: 2466 tests OK. {#645:comment:638}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Tree-mode pickup across project dirs (TDD) {#646}

Pre-fix the test FAILS (relocated subagent surfaces as an 'orphan' top-level row because td.agent_link is never built, so PROBE_TURN1_REPLY navigation lands wrong); post-fix it PASSES (subagent appears inline under the Task umbrella and drills into PROBE_SUBAGENT_PROMPT).   * ## Ticket: Task: Reverse mapping for cross-project-dir subagents (t-toggle ancestry) (TDD) {#636}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-02 00:52:17 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-02 01:34:01 UTC

    `_outer_subagent_group_id(path)` (recipes/browse-claude:4550) maps a
    subagent `.jsonl` back to its `<parent_session_path>#agent:<aid>` outer
    row by assuming the session `.jsonl` is a sibling of the `<sid>` dir
    (:4570-4571). For a relocated subagent the sibling doesn't exist → it
    returns None and t-toggle ancestry from inside the subagent breaks.

    Fix: when the co-located sibling `<sid>.jsonl` is absent, locate the
    real session `.jsonl` by searching `<CLAUDE_ROOT>/*/<sid>.jsonl`
    (session ids are UUID-unique; first match wins). Keep the co-located
    fast path. Consider caching the lookup.

    **Test:** from inside a relocated subagent's record, `t`-toggle to tree
    mode and confirm the ancestry expands to the subagent group row (reuse
    the relocated fixture). TDD: failing first.

    * ## Comments {#636:comments}

      * Review of #645 confirmed APPROVE. Scope note for #646: route ALL remaining _subagents_dir(...)/#agent: resolution sites through _resolve_agent_jsonl — found via grep, not line numbers (which shifted). Reviewer flagged _cursor_tail_path (flat live-tail branch) and _scope_card_path (preview header) as flat-affecting, plus tree get_children #agent: branch, _resolve_path_for_bust, _resolve_path, _gather_line_source, and _maybe_link_subagent. {#636:comment:639}

      * Fix: _outer_subagent_group_id keeps the co-located fast path (<sess_dir>.jsonl sibling); when that's absent (worktree-relocated subagent), falls back to _session_path_for_sid(sid) which searches <CLAUDE_ROOT>/*/<sid>.jsonl (UUID-unique, first match wins) with a small _SESSION_PATH_BY_SID cache (validated on hit, misses not cached). Test home: chose a direct-call unit test in test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py::TestAncestorIdsForSubagent (reuses the existing _load_recipe/_stub_browse_tui loader) over a UI test. A tmux t-toggle test does NOT reliably fail: the framework's expanded set survives a toggle, so a cursor parked in an already-expanded subagent subtree stays put even with the reverse mapping broken. The bug only surfaces when the chain must be rebuilt from scratch, which is exactly what _outer_subagent_group_id computes -> direct call pins it deterministically. (An early UI-test attempt installed a competing bare-object() browse_tui stub in sys.modules that clobbered the render-test loader and broke 80 tests; removed it entirely.) {#636:comment:644}

    human-misattribution. Spec §1–§4.

      full preview = `status`/`summary`/`output_file` header + `<result>`
      via `_md_voice`.

      `user` (not agent-reply).

Full user preview rendering lives in _render_user (line ~1500); _user_body_text renders the string via _md_voice. _summarise_message user fallback (line ~1297) currently dumps raw XML via _extract_text. {#648:comment:651}
