(2) Full-preview rendering wired in _render_user (recipes/browse-claude ~line 1525): after the tool_result branch, before the default head/body, route _is_task_notification(obj) to new _render_task_notification(text). * ## Ticket: Task: Markdown document subtrees (browse-claude & browse-md) {#656}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-03 05:17:04 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-03 08:22:53 UTC

  Implement expandable markdown document subtrees in browse-claude, and
  bring the same reference-following to browse-md. A message that
  contains markdown headings or references existing .md files becomes
  expandable into an inline "markdown" document plus one node per
  referenced file, each drillable into its own heading tree, recursively.

  Design spec (read the section relevant to your ticket before coding):
  docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-markdown-document-subtrees-design.md

  Shared markdown-structure logic lives in a new recipes/md_doc.py
  (hard dep for browse-md, optional/graceful for browse-claude). A new
  framework Item.boundary flag marks self-contained foreign subtrees.

  Subagents: all Opus, effort high. Each non-trivial ticket is followed
  by a review subagent (goal met? overengineered? stale code left?).

  * ## Comments {#656:comments}

    * BASELINE (branch point off main, pre-existing, NOT caused by this epic): full suite ./run-tests.sh = 2636 tests, 10 failures, all in test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py — TestAskUserQuestion x4, TestSendMessage x4, TestTaskNotification x2. Only 2 fail in isolation (python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render): _ROW_BG_FOR_KIND agent-send/agent-reply expect 22/23 but recipe was intentionally overridden to 17 (stale tests). The other 8 fail only under discover = shared-module-state pollution / test ordering. browse-claude ticket workers (#660-662): expect these; do NOT fix them; make NEW tests isolation-robust (set up the globals you depend on, e.g. _MD_COLOR); verify in isolation AND full suite. {#656:comment:667}

  * ## Ticket: Task: md_doc.py — shared markdown-structure module {#657}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 05:25:33 UTC

    Create recipes/md_doc.py: a framework-agnostic module (NO browse_tui /
    Item imports) holding the markdown-structure logic, unit-testable in
    isolation. Self-registers as a plugin like md2ansi_lib.py does.

    Provide:
    - build_doc_tree(text, *, include_lists=False) -> a structural tree of
      heading (and optionally list) nodes carrying level, title,
      line_offset, byte_offset, byte_size, children. Lift + generalize
      browse-md's _build_nodes heading/list logic, line/byte indexing, and
      the boundary rule. Return plain structural nodes (dataclass/dict),
      NOT Item objects.
    - find_md_refs(text) -> ordered captured strings matching the regex
      \b[^\s"\\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b (excludes whitespace and "/\/$/*).
    - resolve_md_ref(captured, *, doc_dir, cwd, project_root) -> abspath or
      None: first existing of (absolute/~ as-is; relative to doc_dir; to
      cwd; to project_root). Return realpath/abspath.
    - md_heading_trigger(text) -> bool using regex (?:^|\n)[ \t]*# (cheap
      detection gate; pure regex, no md2ansi_scan).
    - compose_md_id(base, abspaths, line_offset=None) and parse_md_id(id)
      -> (base, abspaths, line_offset). Each non-inline segment is
      urllib.parse.quote('file://'+abspath, safe=''); chain with '#md:';
      empty segment = inline; trailing '#<lineoffset>' for a heading.
      parse_md_id finds the first '#md:', splits the chain on '#md:', and
      peels a trailing numeric '#<lineoffset>' off the last segment.
    - A process-wide abspath -> (text, doc_tree) cache + clear_cache().

    Spec sections: Architecture overview; Heading-tree building; Reference
    detection, resolution, labels; ID scheme.

    Tests (test/unit/test_md_doc.py, no browse_tui stub needed):
    build_doc_tree nesting + byte-range slicing on fixtures; trigger/ref
    regexes true/false (real heading, code-fence-only #, ref-only, none;
    ref regex excludes "/\/$/*); resolve_md_ref base precedence +
    non-existent -> None; compose/parse round-trip incl. paths containing
    #/~/?/spaces and the #<lineoffset> suffix; cache hit + clear_cache.

    Done when: module imports standalone and all md_doc unit tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#657:comments}

      * Implemented recipes/md_doc.py + test/unit/test_md_doc.py. {#657:comment:665}

        PUBLIC API (final signatures):
        - @dataclass MdNode(kind, level, title, line_offset, byte_offset, byte_size, children)
        - build_doc_tree(text, *, include_lists=False) -> list[MdNode]  (top-level nodes, no synthetic root; [] when no headings)
        - find_md_refs(text) -> list[str]  (regex \b[^\s"\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b, doc order, dups kept)
        - resolve_md_ref(captured, *, doc_dir, cwd, project_root) -> str|None  (abs/~ as-is; else doc_dir>cwd>project_root first-existing; returns realpath)
        - md_heading_trigger(text) -> bool  (pure regex (?:^|\n)[ \t]*# )
        - compose_md_id(base, abspaths, line_offset=None) -> str / parse_md_id(item_id) -> (base, list[abspaths], line_offset|None)
        - get_doc(abspath) -> (text, doc_tree)  [process-wide cache] / clear_cache()
        KEY DECISIONS / things decided where ambiguous:
        - Lifted browse-md's _line_starts/_line_of/_walk_list/_parse(->_scan_events)/_build_nodes 3-pass logic verbatim, dropping all Item/by_id/by_line construction. build_doc_tree returns plain MdNode roots; recipes own the synthetic-root + Item mapping.
        - 'byte_offset'/'byte_size' are CHARACTER offsets into the decoded str (what md2ansi_scan reports and what callers slice). Kept the names for continuity w/ browse-md + spec; documented in module + MdNode docstrings.
        - Added get_doc(abspath) as the cache accessor (spec said 'abspath->(text,doc_tree) cache + clear_cache' but didn't name the getter). Caches headings-only trees (the shape both recipes' ref-following needs); clear_cache() empties it.
        - parse_md_id raises ValueError on a non-#md: id (callers gate on '#md:' in id first); line-offset peeled via rfind('#')+isdigit() (encoded segs have no raw #, so unambiguous). 0 offset round-trips distinctly from None.
        - Self-registers as plugin name='md_doc' under guarded try/except, mirroring md2ansi_lib. No browse_tui import otherwise; imports md2ansi_lib for md2ansi_scan only.
        - find_md_refs returns dups (dedup is caller's job, best by resolved abspath).
        TESTS: 39 tests, all pass. Also reran test_browse_md alongside (shared md2ansi_lib) -> 273 total OK.
        Final output:
        Ran 39 tests in 0.007s
        OK
        (build_doc_tree nesting/byte-range/fence/lists; trigger+ref true/false incl. "/\/$/* exclusions; resolve precedence+None+~+realpath; id round-trip incl. #/~/?/space/embedded-#md:/0-offset; cache hit + clear_cache.)

  * ## Ticket: Task: Framework Item.boundary flag + boundary-aware recursive expansion {#658}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 05:39:51 UTC

    Add `boundary: bool = False` to the framework Item dataclass in
    src-tui/030-data.py with a docstring: marks a self-contained foreign
    subtree; recursive/multi-expansion treats it as a leaf (expand TO it,
    never THROUGH it, even when its children are already cached); the
    "don't fold descendants into an ancestor preview cascade" half is
    honoured by recipes.

    In src-tui/040-state.py, make recursive/multi-expansion (expand_subtree
    and the Alt-Right path) stop at boundary nodes: expand the boundary
    node itself but do not recurse into its children, even if cached. Lazy
    expansion already skips uncached subtrees; this covers the cached case
    and makes the contract explicit. NOT a behaviour change for existing
    rows (nothing sets boundary yet).

    Spec section: boundary — new framework Item flag.

    Tests: Item.boundary defaults False; recursive/multi-expand of an
    ancestor does NOT expand a CACHED boundary node's children; a
    non-boundary cached node still expands. Put in the appropriate
    framework suite (test/unit or test/async_).

    Done when: framework tests pass and existing tests stay green.

    * ## Comments {#658:comments}

      * Done. Added Item.boundary: bool = False (030-data.py, after hidden) with a docstring describing expand-TO-not-THROUGH semantics and the recipe-owned preview-cascade half. Made both recursive-expansion descendant-walks stop at boundary nodes: {#658:comment:666}

        - expand_subtree._walk (040-state.py ~4426): after state.expanded.add(pid), look up the Item via _items_by_id and return before recursing if boundary=True (covers the cached case).
        - _expand_recursive._expand_subtree (070-actions.py, alt-right path ~708): after state.expanded.add(c.id), skip the recursive call when c.boundary is True.
        Both add the boundary node to expanded (expand TO it) but never recurse into its children. Also added boundary to _PROMOTION_DATA_FIELDS (040-state.py) and the to_item dict known-set (030-data.py) for parity with hidden. Verified the push-API ops (upsert/set_item/mod) pick up the new declared field automatically via _item_field_names().
        Tests: Item.boundary default+settable (test_item.py); expand_subtree TO-not-THROUGH a CACHED boundary node + non-boundary control + direct-on-boundary (test_collapse_expand.py TestExpandSubtreeBoundary); alt-right TO-not-THROUGH + control (test_actions.py TestExpandRecursiveBoundary). Negative-control check confirmed the 3 stop-at-boundary tests fail without the code (and controls pass). +7 tests, all green.
        Full suite (./run-tests.sh): 2636 tests, 4 skipped, 10 failures — ALL 10 pre-existing on the clean baseline (verified by stashing: same 10 failures, all in test_browse_claude_render.py TestSendMessage/TestTaskNotification/TestAskUserQuestion, unrelated to boundary). Zero new failures; behaviour identical for all existing rows (nothing sets boundary yet).

  * ## Ticket: Task: browse-claude — fix _decode_project_path hyphen mangling {#659}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 05:54:55 UTC

    _decode_project_path (recipes/browse-claude) reverses Claude's
    /-and-.->- encoding by replacing EVERY - with /, mangling real hyphens
    (browse-tui -> browse/tui). Used at the project title (~line 521) and
    project preview (~line 2687).

    Add _real_project_path(project_dir): return the true path from the
    first readable session record's stored "cwd" (reuse _session_cwds /
    read a record), HOME->~ collapsed for display; fall back to the lossy
    _decode_project_path only when no record/cwd is available. Point the
    two display sites at it.

    Also add a helper returning (cwd, project_root) for a session/project:
    cwd from records; project_root = git root (walk up from cwd for .git)
    else the real project dir. Consumed by the reference resolver in the
    build ticket.

    Spec section: Bug fix: _decode_project_path.

    Tests (extend test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py stub pattern):
    _real_project_path preserves a real hyphen when a record carries cwd,
    and falls back to the lossy decode when none; git-root discovery
    returns the .git ancestor.

    Done when: hyphens are preserved in the project display and tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#659:comments}

      * Implemented in recipes/browse-claude (after _session_cwds, ~line 671-771): {#659:comment:668}

        - _collapse_home(path): HOME->~ display collapse (small reused helper).
        - _real_project_cwd(project_dir): true cwd from the project's sessions via the existing _session_cwds byte-regex scan; PREFERS the cwd whose _encode_project_path(cwd)==dir basename (pins canonical cwd for worktree sessions carrying extra dispatch cwds, order-independent), else any recorded cwd, else None.
        - _real_project_path(project_dir): _collapse_home(_real_project_cwd(...)); falls back to lossy _decode_project_path only when no readable cwd. Hyphen now faithful (~/browse-tui, not ~/browse/tui).
        - _find_git_root(start): nearest ancestor (inclusive) with a .git dir OR file (worktree/submodule), else None.
        - _session_cwd_and_root(session_jsonl_path) -> (cwd, project_root): cwd from records; project_root = git root above cwd else cwd; unreadable-cwd fallback returns (None, expanduser(lossy-decode)) so the resolver (#660) gets a real joinable abspath (no leftover ~).
        Display sites repointed: _list_projects title (was ~521) and _preview_project header (was ~2687) now call _real_project_path(full path). _decode_project_path kept verbatim as the fallback.
        Tests: new TestRealProjectPath in test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py (8 tests) using tmp dirs + HOME swap; setUp/tearDown save/restore HOME and clear _SESSION_CWDS_CACHE so they pass in isolation AND under full discover. Covers: real-hyphen preserved + HOME collapse; encoding-match preference over extra worktree cwds; lossy fallback (empty dir + record-without-cwd); git-root ancestor (dir and .git-file) + None when absent; _session_cwd_and_root git-ancestor / cwd-fallback / no-cwd->expanded-abspath.
        Results: isolation python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render = 303 ran, 2 failures (same pre-existing _ROW_BG_FOR_KIND ones). Full ./run-tests.sh = 2644 ran, 10 failures (== baseline), 4 skipped. Zero new failures.
        Note flagged: when a session's cwd has no .git ancestor, project_root == cwd (the spec's 'else the real project dir' fallback).

  * ## Ticket: browse-claude — detection + #md: get_children build {#660}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 06:16:26 UTC

    Import md_doc in a try/except (graceful degrade like the md2ansi
    import); when absent the whole feature is a no-op.

    Detection (item delivery): in _tree_item (and the flat-mode builders)
    set has_children=True when md_doc.md_heading_trigger(_message_md_text(
    rec)) OR the .md ref regex matches the record's string content — via a
    short-circuiting depth-first walk over rec's string leaves, NOT
    json.dumps. Define _message_md_text(rec) = joined text parts + any
    tool_result text.

    get_children '#md:' routing (add a branch before the generic '#' leaf
    return):
    - message <base>: [inline doc node if md2ansi_scan finds >=1 heading]
      + [file doc node per existing ref found in the record (raw line /
      string leaves), deduped by abspath, sorted by label]. inline node id
      <base>#md:, title "markdown", boundary False; file node id
      <base>#md:<enc>, boundary True.
    - inline document <base>#md:: top-level heading nodes only (its refs
      live at the message level).
    - file document <base>#md:<segs>: top-level heading nodes + file doc
      node per existing ref in THAT file's text (resolve via
      md_doc.resolve_md_ref, doc_dir=dirname(file), cwd/project_root from
      the project-path ticket). No cycle/alias detection — expand in place.
    - heading node ...#<lineoffset>: the sub-headings nested under it.
    Use md_doc.compose_md_id/parse_md_id for ids and the md_doc
    abspath->(text,tree) cache.

    Self-heal: in on_children_loaded, if a message/md node's children
    settle to [], issue mod(id, has_children=False).

    Spec sections: The uniform node model; ID scheme; Detection
    (item-build) and lazy build; Reference detection/resolution; Recursion.
    Depends on md_doc and the project-path helper.

    Tests: detection sets has_children on heading/ref messages, not on
    plain ones; get_children builds inline + file docs + nested headings;
    recursion (file -> file) expands; self-heal flips has_children off on
    empty; graceful no-op when md_doc is absent.

    Done when: tests pass and existing browse-claude tests stay green.

    * ## Comments {#660:comments}

      * Implemented detection + #md: get_children build in recipes/browse-claude (+20 tests). {#660:comment:669}

        IMPORT: 'import md_doc as _md_doc' in try/except (mirrors md2ansi); _md_doc None => whole feature no-op (detection, routing, self-heal all gate on it).
        DETECTION (_md_has_children, ~1640): md_heading_trigger(_message_md_text(rec)) OR _record_has_md_ref(rec). _message_md_text joins ALL text parts + tool_result text (string or sub-part text). _record_has_md_ref does a SHORT-CIRCUITING DFS over rec's string leaves (_iter_str_leaves), find_md_refs per-leaf, stops at first hit (no json.dumps, no os.stat). Wired into _tree_item, _list_messages; _list_subagent_messages inherits via _list_messages.
        ROUTING (get_children, ~475): added '#md:' branch (->_md_subtree_children) BEFORE the generic '#' leaf return, and made the plain message-id case call _md_message_children when _md_doc present (else []). Both mode-agnostic.
        ID ENCODING via md_doc codec: inline doc = compose_md_id(base,[]) -> '<sess>#<n>#md:' (boundary=False, title 'markdown'); file doc = compose_md_id(base,[abspath]) -> appends quoted file:// seg (boundary=True, optimistic has_children=True); heading = compose_md_id(base,chain,line_offset=L); recursion nests the chain (report.md -> appendix.md = two #md: segs). parse_md_id drives the 4-way dispatch in _md_subtree_children (line_offset set=>heading; abspaths empty=>inline; abspaths set=>file doc).
        RESOLUTION wiring: cwd,project_root = _session_cwd_and_root(<session jsonl path>) (#659 helper). doc_dir = cwd for the message/inline doc, dirname(file) for a file doc. _md_resolved_refs resolves via resolve_md_ref, keeps existing-only, dedups by abspath, sorts by label. Label (_md_ref_label) is relpath vs project_root, else cwd, else ~-collapsed. Message-level refs gathered across ALL string leaves (prose+tool paths), so a prose ref surfaces as a message sibling, not under the inline node.
        SELF-HEAL: _on_children_loaded -> _md_self_heal: if parent is md-managed (#md: id OR plain <jsonl>#<n> via _is_md_message_id) and cached_children settled to [], push ctx.update_data([mod(id, has_children=False)]). Guarded on _md_doc and _BROWSER not None.
        ALSO: get_preview returns '' for '#md:' ids (before the generic message branch) so #661 can add the rich preview without a crash now. Ctrl-R full-wipe path also calls _md_doc.clear_cache().
        Tag/style minimal (md=blue file / dim inline; h1..h6 colour map) — full styling is #661.
        TESTS: new TestMarkdownSubtrees (20) in test_browse_claude_render.py; _load_recipe_with_md_doc reloads with recipes/ on sys.path (md_doc live) then restores sys.path+sys.modules so other classes keep the md-less baseline. Covers detection (heading/prose-ref/tool-path-ref True; plain/quote False; code-fence optimistic True; graceful None no-op), message->inline+file (sorted/deduped/existing-only/no-inline-without-heading), inline->headings, heading->subheadings, file->headings+nested-ref, recursion file->file, boundary flags, get_children routing (+leaf without md_doc), get_preview '' for #md:, self-heal flip + skip non-empty/non-md parents. setUp/tearDown clear _TREE_CACHE/_SESSION_CWDS_CACHE/_md_doc.clear_cache() and save/restore _BROWSER/_TREE_MODE.
        RESULTS: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render => Ran 323, FAILED (failures=2) — exactly the 2 documented pre-existing (agent_send/agent_reply stripe 22/23-vs-17). ./run-tests.sh => Ran 2664, FAILED (failures=10, skipped=4) — identical to the documented baseline (verified by stashing: baseline was Ran 2644, same 10 names). Zero new failures.
        AMBIGUITY RESOLVED: ticket sketched inline node as compose_md_id(base, ['']) with a '?'; the codec's contract is the EMPTY chain [] for inline (round-trips to abspaths==[]), so used [].

  * ## Ticket: browse-claude — #md: preview routing + tags/styling + labels {#661}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 06:42:29 UTC

    In get_preview add a '#md:' branch BEFORE the generic '#' message path:
    - document node -> the document's full text (inline message text, or
      file text via the md_doc cache), rendered through _md2ansi_fn
      honouring the existing _MD_COLOR toggle.
    - heading node (...#<lineoffset>) -> the heading's byte-range section
      (byte_offset : byte_offset+byte_size from the cached doc_tree), same
      rendering.

    styled gray/dim, file docs styled blue. Heading nodes [h1]..[h6] with
    the level->colour map (red, yellow, green, blue, magenta, gray).

    Labels: compute the file-doc label once and store on the Item —
    relative to project_root if inside, else relative to cwd if inside,
    else a ~-collapsed abspath (the id stays abspath-canonical).

    Spec sections: Preview; Tags & styling; Reference detection/resolution
    (label). Depends on the build ticket (id scheme).

    Tests: get_preview routes a document id to full text and a heading id
    to its section slice; md2ansi honoured under _MD_COLOR; labels render
    relative to project_root/cwd with abspath fallback.

    Done when: tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#661:comments}

      * Implemented #md: preview routing + finalized tags/styling/labels (items 1-3). {#661:comment:670}

        PREVIEW (item 1): get_preview '#md:' branch now calls new _preview_md_node(item_id) (browse-claude ~1887), checked BEFORE the generic '#' message path (guarded by _md_doc is not None). Routing via md_doc.parse_md_id -> (base, abspaths, line_offset):
        - document node (line_offset None): full text -> _md_voice(text). text comes from new helper _md_chain_doc(base, abspaths) (~1812) which returns (text, doc_tree): non-empty chain -> md_doc.get_doc(abspaths[-1]) (cache); empty chain -> _message_md_text(rec) of the <base> jsonl line + build_doc_tree. (None,None) on unreadable/bad-line -> '' .
        - heading node (line_offset set): section slice text[node.byte_offset : node.byte_offset+node.byte_size], node located by _md_node_at_line(doc_tree, line_offset); rendered via _md_voice. Missing node -> '' .
        _md_voice already honours _MD_COLOR and falls back to raw on md2ansi error, matching the recipe's md-render guards. Refactored _md_subtree_children to reuse _md_chain_doc (removed duplicated dispatch).
        TAGS/STYLING (item 2): already set to spec by #660 - kept. Docs [md] inline (tag_style 'dim') / file (tag_style 'blue'); headings [h1]..[h6] via _MD_HEADING_STYLE = red,yellow,green,blue,magenta,gray (== browse-md _HEADING_STYLES). Only finalized stale '(see #661)' placeholder comments.
        LABELS (item 3): already to spec by #660 - kept. _md_ref_label(abspath, cwd, project_root): rel to project_root if inside, else rel to cwd if inside, else _collapse_home(abspath); computed once in _md_resolved_refs, stored as Item.title; id stays abspath-canonical via compose_md_id. Anchors from _session_cwd_and_root. Inline node title stays 'markdown'.
        HEADING SECTION-SLICE LOCATION: doc_tree cached per-document (md_doc.get_doc) or built from inline text; _md_node_at_line walks it depth-first for the MdNode with matching line_offset; slice = text[byte_offset:byte_offset+byte_size] (md_doc boundary rule -> includes nested sub-sections, stops at next sibling-or-shallower heading).
        FILES: recipes/browse-claude (preview routing, _preview_md_node, _md_chain_doc, comment cleanup); test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py (extended TestMarkdownSubtrees).
        TESTS: added 9 (preview inline/file full text, heading section-slice w/ boundary asserts incl. sibling exclusion, missing-offset empty, _MD_COLOR toggle on=ANSI/off=raw, label precedence pure-fn, inline title 'markdown'); replaced the stale test_get_preview_md_id_returns_empty with _unreadable_returns_empty. setUp now also saves/restores _MD_COLOR (plus existing _BROWSER/_TREE_MODE + clear_cache).

        status: done

    Set boundary=True on md file-doc nodes (verify from the build ticket),
    subagent-group rows, and bare .jsonl session rows.

    Migrate id-shape checks to the attribute:
    - _walk_umbrella: replace the "'#agent:' in cid" skip with
      child.boundary.
    - _is_cross_file_id: consult the Item's boundary (look it up via
      _BROWSER.get_item(item_id)); preserve today's behaviour for #agent: /
      bare-.jsonl rows. The two ifs serve different purposes (cascade-skip
      vs metadata->cascade preview upgrade) — keep both working.

    Spec section: boundary — new framework Item flag (consumers +
    migration). Depends on the framework boundary flag and the build ticket.

    Tests: parity — subagent-group and bare-.jsonl rows behave as before
    (cascade skip + preview upgrade); md file docs are boundary;
    _walk_umbrella skips boundary children.

    * ## Comments {#662:comments}

      * Done. (1) boundary=True set on the rows the OLD _is_cross_file_id matched: subagent-group #agent: rows in _list_subagents_for_session (~929) and _subagent_pseudo_item (~4597) — orphan rows inherit it via _orphan_subagents_for_session's delegation; and bare .jsonl session rows in _list_sessions (~602). md inline node stays boundary=False; md file-doc nodes already had boundary=True. (2) get_preview reorder: moved the '#md:' branch to BEFORE _is_cross_file_id (now ~3089, was after at ~3084). Reason: boundary-based _is_cross_file_id returns True for md file-docs, which would hijack their preview; md ids never contain #agent:/end in .jsonl so checking #md: first steals no cross-file id. Umbrella branch left where it was. (3) _walk_umbrella: replaced "'#agent:' in cid" skip with getattr(child,'boundary',False). (4) _is_cross_file_id: now consults _BROWSER.get_item(item_id).boundary; falls back to the OLD id-shape predicate (#agent: OR (#-less .jsonl)) when get_item returns None OR _BROWSER lacks get_item (defensive getattr — needed so existing cross-file-preview test fakes without get_item don't AttributeError). Tests: extended test_browse_claude_render.py — new TestBoundaryMigration class (boundary on subagent/session rows incl orphans+pseudo; _is_cross_file_id parity via boundary-when-loaded, fallback-when-None, fallback-when-no-get_item, non-cross shapes; _walk_umbrella skips boundary child via _collect_umbrella_preview) plus 3 tests in TestMarkdownSubtrees (md-doc-live load): the reorder regression guard (md file-doc boundary=True + get_item-providing _BROWSER → asserts _is_cross_file_id True AND get_preview routes to _preview_md_node returning file text, not the cross-file card), inline-md routing, and md-file-doc fallback. Zero new failures. {#662:comment:673}

No new test needed — existing test_gather_umbrella_lines_stops_at_subagent_boundary (TestViewEditSource) already exercises this skip end-to-end through real get_children (subagent child now carries boundary=True) and passes.   * ## Ticket: Task: browse-md — adopt md_doc.build_doc_tree (refactor _build_nodes) {#663}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 07:38:20 UTC

    Refactor browse-md's _build_nodes to build its Items from
    md_doc.build_doc_tree (include_lists honours the -l flag) instead of
    its own parsing. Keep browse-md's Item construction, anchor resolution,
    per-file roots, and V/E actions. md_doc becomes a HARD dependency
    (browse-md already hard-deps md2ansi_lib): main() dies with a clear
    message if the md_doc import fails.

    Spec section: browse-md adoption (first bullet). Depends on md_doc.

    Tests: existing browse-md tests still pass (parity after refactor); the
    hard-dep gate errors cleanly when md_doc is absent.

    Done when: browse-md behaves identically and all browse-md tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#663:comments}

      * Refactored _build_nodes onto md_doc.build_doc_tree (ticket #663, first browse-md-adoption bullet only; ref-following is #664, NOT done here). {#663:comment:675}

        WHAT CHANGED (recipes/browse-md, -96 lines, 1523->1427):
        - md_doc is now a HARD dependency: added 'try: import md_doc / except ImportError: md_doc=None' alongside the md2ansi_lib import, and a second main() gate ('if md_doc is None: _die("requires the md_doc module ...", with_usage=False)') mirroring the existing _md2ansi_scan gate. Imports stay guarded so the module remains importable for unit tests; absence enforced at runtime.
        - _build_nodes(events, file_text, line_starts, path) rewritten: structural tree now comes from md_doc.build_doc_tree(file_text, include_lists=_INCLUDE_LISTS). Recursively maps each MdNode -> Item, wiring item._children from MdNode.children under the per-file root Item. byte_offset/byte_size/line_offset/title(pre-stripped)/kind/level taken straight from the MdNode. The whole pass-1/2/3 (~90 lines) collapsed into the recursive mapper.
        MdNode -> Item mapping details:
        - id = f'{path}#{node.line_offset}' (unchanged shape).
        - heading: tag = f'h{level}', tag_style = _HEADING_STYLES[level], kind='heading'.
        - list-item: tag_style='dim', kind='list-item'. MdNode does NOT carry ul-vs-ol, so the tag is read off the parallel _parse(file_text) 'events' stream (verified 1:1 aligned with the tree's pre-order by byte_offset). This keeps the 'events' param meaningful (not vestigial). A length-mismatch guard falls back to 'ul' so a mismatched caller can't IndexError.
        - line_size: MdNode has byte_size but NOT line_size. Recomputed here by the same boundary rule (heading: next heading at level<=L; list: next heading OR list at indent<=L; EOF -> len(line_starts)).
        - root: per-file root Item created first and seeded into by_id FIRST (bare-path id never collides), so by_id key order is pre-order identical to before.
        DELETED (now owned by md_doc; verified dead post-refactor):
        - _LIST_ITEM_RE, _HEADING_PREFIX_RE, _LIST_PREFIX_RE (titles arrive pre-stripped from MdNode).
        - _SCAN_KINDS_HEADINGS / _SCAN_KINDS_WITH_LISTS, _HEADING_EVENT_KINDS, _LIST_EVENT_KINDS.
        - the bodies of _line_starts/_line_of/_walk_list/_parse and _build_nodes pass-1/2/3.
        - 'import re' (no longer used; bisect kept for _node_at_line).
        KEPT / DELEGATED (still pinned by tests &/or used by _reparse):
        - _line_starts/_line_of -> thin delegations to md_doc._line_starts/_line_of.
        - _walk_list -> delegates to md_doc._walk_list.
        - _parse -> delegates to md_doc._scan_events(text, md_doc._line_starts(text), _INCLUDE_LISTS).
        - _HEADING_KINDS kept (TestParse pins it; documents _parse's heading event vocabulary).
        - _md2ansi_scan kept as the md2ansi_lib gate sentinel (also guards _md2ansi_fn used by get_preview).
        - _reparse unchanged: still does events=_parse(text); _build_nodes(events, text, ls, path).
        PARITY EVIDENCE: A/B'd new vs git-HEAD _build_nodes Item trees + by_id key order: 17 hand fixtures x 2 flag settings = 0 mismatches; 4000 randomized fuzz inputs x 2 flag settings = 0 mismatches (byte-identical id/title/tag/tag_style/kind/level/byte_offset/byte_size/line_offset/line_size/file_path/has_children and identical by_id order).
        NOTE (minor, acceptable for a refactor): _reparse now scans twice (_parse + build_doc_tree). Negligible (only on parse/reload). Eliminating it would need an MdNode marker field = a md_doc change, out of scope.
        TESTS:
        - test/unit/test_browse_md.py: 234 -> 235 (added TestMissingDependencyGate.test_missing_md_doc_exits_two_with_message mirroring the md2ansi gate test: sets r.md_doc=None, expects exit 2 + 'requires the md_doc module' + 'browse-md:' + no usage line; refactored shared helpers in that class). ALL GREEN.
        - test/unit/test_md_doc.py: 47, unchanged, GREEN.
        - ./run-tests.sh: 2691 tests, FAILED(failures=10) = exactly the documented pre-existing baseline (all in test_browse_claude_render.py: TestSendMessage x4, TestTaskNotification x2, TestAskUserQuestion x4). Flaky test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid passes 3/3 on isolated re-run. ZERO new failures.

  * ## Ticket: Task: browse-md — reference-following (file/heading gain [md] children) {#664}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 05:18:00 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 07:58:44 UTC

    Add markdown reference-following to browse-md: a file root / heading
    gains [md] children for the .md files it references (md_doc.find_md_refs
    + resolve_md_ref), using the #md: chain codec composed onto browse-md's
    base id (<file_path> / <file_path>#<lineoffset>). Referenced-file nodes
    are boundary=True. Resolution context: doc_dir = the file's dir; cwd =
    process cwd; project_root = git root. browse-md's existing heading id
    <path>#<lineoffset> is already consistent with the #<lineoffset> suffix.

    Spec section: browse-md adoption (second bullet). Depends on the
    browse-md adoption ticket and the framework boundary flag.

    Tests: a markdown file referencing another .md shows an expandable [md]
    child that drills into the referenced file's headings; boundary set;
    relative labels.

    Done when: tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#664:comments}

      * DONE (impl + tests + verify). {#664:comment:684}

        ID SCHEME: #md: chain composed onto browse-md's per-file base id <F> via md_doc.compose_md_id/parse_md_id. Ref child of <F>: <F>#md:<enc(B)> (file-doc, boundary=True); its heading: <F>#md:<enc(B)>#<lineoffset>; nested ref: <F>#md:<enc(B)>#md:<enc(A)>. NO inline segment (browse-md base IS a file) — every chain segment is a file, so parse_md_id always yields non-empty abspaths.
        WHERE REFS ATTACH: at the DOCUMENT level (after headings, never under a heading). Per-file root children = eager in-file top-level headings + lazy [md] ref nodes. A referenced-file node expands to ITS headings + ITS refs, recursively (expand-in-place, no cycle detection — lazy materialization makes a cycle finite per drill).
        LAZY ROUTING: get_children — #md: ids -> _md_subtree_children (parses chain, get_doc cache); per-file-root ids (node_id in _FILES) -> eager headings + lazily-resolved _md_file_item per existing ref; reload-at-root clears md_doc cache then _reparse. get_preview — #md: routed FIRST (before _classify_id) -> _preview_md_node (doc node=full text, heading node=byte-slice) via _md_render md2ansi seam. _reparse augments root.has_children with _file_has_existing_ref (cheap; referenced files NOT read at reparse).
        RESOLUTION CONTEXT (_md_resolution_context): doc_dir=dirname(file being scanned); cwd=os.getcwd(); project_root=git-root walked up from doc_dir (_find_git_root) else doc_dir. Existing-only, dedup by realpath abspath, label via _md_ref_label (rel to project_root, else cwd, else ~-collapsed). Ref nodes boundary=True; headings boundary=False.
        md_doc: UNCHANGED (no shared helper added — all logic reused from md_doc; recipe-specific mappings copied from browse-claude per house style).
        TESTS: TestMdRefFollowing (16). VERIFY: test_browse_md 251 OK, test_md_doc 47 OK, ./run-tests.sh failures=10 (pre-existing baseline, all test_browse_claude_render.py), zero new failures.

    * ## Ticket: Task: Implement #md: reference-following helpers in browse-md {#676}

          updated: 2026-06-03 07:53:30 UTC
          status: done
          created: 2026-06-03 07:50:11 UTC

      * ## Comments {#676:comments}

        * Added #md: helpers to recipes/browse-md mirroring browse-claude: _collapse_home, _find_git_root, _md_resolution_context (doc_dir=dirname(file), cwd=os.getcwd(), project_root=git-root-walkup-or-dir), _md_ref_label, _md_resolved_refs, _file_has_existing_ref (cheap has_children half), _md_heading_item, _md_file_item (boundary=True), _md_node_at_line, _md_chain_doc (file-only; no inline segment), _md_subtree_children, _md_render (md2ansi seam), _preview_md_node. _MD_HEADING_STYLE aliases _HEADING_STYLES. {#676:comment:680}

  * ## Ticket: md_doc.find_md_refs: capture absolute and ~ paths (regex fix) {#671}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 06:48:50 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 06:55:31 UTC

    BUG found during #661 review. find_md_refs's regex
    `\b[^\s"\\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b` anchors on a leading WORD boundary, so a
    leading `/` or `~` is dropped: `/home/u/report.md` -> `home/u/report.md`
    and `~/notes.md` -> `notes.md`. Consequences: resolve_md_ref's
    absolute/`~` branch (spec resolution rule #1) is effectively DEAD CODE,
    and absolute tool file_paths (e.g. a Write to /abs/report.md — a PRIMARY
    use case) are silently dropped because the de-slashed token resolves
    relative to cwd and doesn't exist.

    Fix in recipes/md_doc.py find_md_refs: replace the leading `\b` with a
    negative-lookbehind for a path char so the token starts at the first
    non-separator char INCLUDING a leading `/` or `~`, e.g.
    `(?<![^\s"\\$*])[^\s"\\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b`. Keep the same excluded chars
    (whitespace, `"`, `\`, `$`, `*`) and the trailing `\b` (so `.mdx` and a
    trailing `.` are still excluded). Verify:
    - '/home/u/report.md' -> '/home/u/report.md'
    - '~/notes.md' -> '~/notes.md'
    - '{"file_path": "/abs/x.md"}' -> '/abs/x.md'
    - relative refs unchanged ('report.md', 'docs/notes.md')

    Tests:
    - test/unit/test_md_doc.py: absolute path and ~ path preserved; relative
      + char-exclusions still hold; resolve_md_ref resolves an absolute
      existing path via its absolute branch and a ~ path via expanduser.
    - test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py: integration test that a record
      carrying an ABSOLUTE .md file_path (to an existing temp file) now
      produces a file-doc child (previously dropped).

    Baseline (do NOT fix/touch): test_browse_claude_render has 2 pre-existing
    isolation failures / 10 under full discover. Add zero new failures.
    Verify: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_md_doc and
    test.unit.test_browse_claude_render, then ./run-tests.sh.

    Done when: absolute/~ .md paths are captured and resolved; tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#671:comments}

      * Fixed find_md_refs regex in recipes/md_doc.py. {#671:comment:672}

        REGEX CHANGE (_MD_REF_RE):
          before: \b[^\s"\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b
          after:  (?<![^\s"\$*])[^\s"\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b
        The leading \b (word-boundary, anchors on first WORD char) is replaced by a
        negative lookbehind asserting the previous char is an excluded separator
        (whitespace/"/\/$/*) OR start-of-string, so the token starts at the first
        NON-separator char including a leading / or ~. Same excluded chars; trailing
        \b kept (so .mdx and a trailing . still excluded). Docstring/comment updated to
        explain the lookbehind.
        find_md_refs BEFORE -> AFTER (the bug-report string):
          'see /home/u/report.md and ~/notes.md and report.md'
          before: ['home/u/report.md', 'notes.md', 'report.md']   (leading /, ~ dropped)
          after:  ['/home/u/report.md', '~/notes.md', 'report.md']
        Confirmed cases after fix:
          '/home/u/report.md'        -> ['/home/u/report.md']
          '~/notes.md'               -> ['~/notes.md']
          '{"file_path": "/abs/x.md"}' -> ['/abs/x.md']
          'wrote report.md and docs/notes.md' -> ['report.md', 'docs/notes.md'] (unchanged)
          exclusions intact: '"report.md"'->['report.md'], 'a\b.md'->['b.md'],
          '$HOME/x.md'->['HOME/x.md'], 'docs/*.md'->[], 'a.mdx'->[],
          'see report.md. done'->['report.md'].
        FILES CHANGED:
          recipes/md_doc.py  — regex + comment.
          test/unit/test_md_doc.py — +8 cases: capture absolute / ~ / abs-in-JSON,
            relative-unchanged, mdx+trailing-dot exclusion; and two end-to-end
            find_md_refs->resolve_md_ref tests proving the absolute branch and the ~
            (expanduser) branch are now reachable (were dead code).
          test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py — +1 integration test
            (TestMarkdownSubtrees.test_message_children_absolute_tool_path_yields_file_doc):
            a Write tool_use with an ABSOLUTE .md file_path (file OUTSIDE the project,
            so it can ONLY resolve via the absolute branch) now produces a file-doc
            child; asserts id round-trips to the abspath. Isolation-robust (clear_cache
            + globals save/restore via the existing setUp).
        TEST RESULTS:
          python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_md_doc -> OK, Ran 47 tests (was 39).
          python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render ->
            FAILED (failures=2): exactly the 2 pre-existing isolation failures
            (test_agent_send_stripe_and_tag_style_registered, test_agent_reply_stripe_is_teal).
            Ran 332 (was 331; +1 my test). Zero new failures.
          ./run-tests.sh -> FAILED (failures=10, skipped=4), Ran 2681. Baseline 10
            unchanged; my test not among them.
        FLAG: the lookbehind also includes a leading punctuation char that the old \b
        incidentally dropped (e.g. '(report.md'->['(report.md'], 'see [docs/x.md]'->
        ['[docs/x.md']). This matches the documented intent (only whitespace/"/\/$/*
        are excluded); such malformed tokens just fail resolve_md_ref (return None), so
        no behavioral harm. No case where a previously-CORRECT path capture regresses.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Lift _find_git_root + _md_node_at_line into md_doc (dedup) {#685}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 08:04:37 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 08:17:18 UTC

    Cleanup from the #664 review (serves the epic's "share common parts in
    md_doc" goal). Two helpers are now duplicated VERBATIM in both
    recipes/browse-claude and recipes/browse-md, and both are pure /
    framework-agnostic — one of them literally walks md_doc's own MdNode
    tree, so it belongs in md_doc:
    - `_md_node_at_line(tree, line_offset)` — deepest MdNode at a line
      offset (walks an md_doc.MdNode tree).
    - `_find_git_root(start)` — nearest ancestor (inclusive) holding a
      `.git` dir or file, else None (pure path walk-up).

    Do:
    - Add them to recipes/md_doc.py as PUBLIC functions `node_at_line(tree,
      line_offset)` and `find_git_root(start)` (move the bodies + dense
      docstrings). Add md_doc unit tests for both (node_at_line: exact
      match, deepest-containing, before-first→None; find_git_root: .git dir,
      .git file, none→None, terminates at fs root).
    - recipes/browse-claude: delete its local `_md_node_at_line` and
      `_find_git_root`; call `md_doc.node_at_line` / `md_doc.find_git_root`.
      These are only reached on the md path (gated by `_md_doc is not
      None`), so there's no NameError when md_doc is absent — VERIFY that
      (esp. `_session_cwd_and_root`/the resolver are the only callers of
      the git-root helper).
    - recipes/browse-md: delete its local `_md_node_at_line` and
      `_find_git_root`; call `md_doc.*`. md_doc is a hard dep there, so no
      gating needed.
    - Do NOT extract the other parallel helpers (_md_ref_label,
      _collapse_home, _md_resolved_refs, _md_heading_item, _md_render,
      _md_chain_doc, etc.) — the #664 review judged their Item/recipe
      coupling makes a shared version not worth the churn. Scope = exactly
      these two.

    Parity: behavior must be identical; the existing browse-claude /
    browse-md / md_doc suites are the guard. Baseline: test_browse_claude_
    render has 2 pre-existing isolation failures / 10 full; suspend UI test
    flaky. Add ZERO new failures.

    Verify: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_md_doc test.unit.test_browse_md
    test.unit.test_browse_claude_render, then ./run-tests.sh (stays at 10).

    Done when: both helpers live only in md_doc, both recipes consume them,
    all suites green.

    * ## Comments {#685:comments}

      * Done. Moved both helpers into recipes/md_doc.py as PUBLIC functions (bodies byte-identical to the recipes' copies): {#685:comment:686}

        - node_at_line(tree, line_offset): placed after build_doc_tree; recursive call renamed to node_at_line. Exact line_offset DFS match, None otherwise (verified the recipe behavior is exact-match, not range-containing).

        - browse-claude: _session_cwd_and_root -> _md_doc.find_git_root; _md_subtree_children + _preview_md_node -> _md_doc.node_at_line. Local _find_git_root and _md_node_at_line deleted.
        - browse-md: _md_resolution_context -> md_doc.find_git_root; _md_subtree_children + _preview_md_node -> md_doc.node_at_line. Local copies deleted.
        md_doc-absent gating (browse-claude): VERIFIED. find_git_root's only caller is _session_cwd_and_root, whose only callers are _md_message_children (get_children L496, gated by '_md_doc is not None') and _md_subtree_children (get_children L488, gated by '_md_doc is not None and #md: in id'). node_at_line callers: _md_subtree_children (same gate) and _preview_md_node (get_preview L3102, gated by '_md_doc is not None else'). Every caller is behind a _md_doc-present gate; no NameError/AttributeError on the dormant path in production.

Updated 3 test references in test_browse_claude_render.py that called the now-moved helpers directly: TestMarkdownSubtrees -> self.r._md_doc.node_at_line; TestRealProjectPath now loads via _load_recipe_with_md_doc (so _md_doc is live for _session_cwd_and_root, which the md-less loader left as None) and its two _find_git_root tests -> self.r._md_doc.find_git_root.

* ## Ticket: Task: browse-claude markdown follow-up fixes (nav + agent-result render) {#687}

      status: done

      updated: 2026-06-03 11:16:21 UTC

  Two bugs found while verifying the markdown-subtrees work (branch
  worktree-markdown-doc-subtrees). Both in recipes/browse-claude. Baseline:
  test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py has 2 pre-existing isolation
  failures / 10 under full ./run-tests.sh discover; the suspend UI test is
  known-flaky. Add ZERO new failures.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Right-arrow should move into md nodes' first child on expand {#688}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 10:46:39 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 11:03:25 UTC

    Bug: pressing the right-arrow on an umbrella/session moves the cursor
    INTO its children (lands on the latest voice child) on a single press,
    but on a markdown node (a message with md children, the inline
    "markdown" node, a file-doc node, or a heading) the cursor stays put —
    so md nodes feel like right-arrow "does nothing."

    Root cause: the on_expand hook `_on_expand` (recipes/browse-claude:5715)
    calls `_jump_to_latest_voice` (5701), which lands on the latest child
    whose `row_bg` is set (`_latest_voice_among_children`, 5368). md
    doc/heading/file nodes never set `row_bg`, so the jump no-ops and the
    cursor stays on the md row.

    Fix: when an md-managed node is expanded and has NO voice child to jump
    to, land the cursor on its FIRST child instead (the structural analog
    of "latest voice"). "md-managed" = `_md_doc is not None` and the id is
    a plain message id `<jsonl>#<n>` (with md children) or a `#md:` node.
    Integrate into the EXISTING async-safe mechanism (the
    `_AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP` park + `_on_children_loaded` settle), so it works
    whether the children are already cached or fetched async — mirror how
    the voice-jump defers. Do NOT regress the umbrella/session voice-jump
    (those still land on latest voice), and do nothing when there are no
    children (e.g. a self-healed false-positive). Reproduce first (drive an
    md node expand + the right-arrow in the async/UI harness) to confirm the
    exact current behavior, then fix.

    Tests: expanding a message-with-md (and an inline "markdown" node, and a
    file-doc node) lands the cursor on the first child; an umbrella still
    lands on latest voice (no regression); a childless/false-positive node
    doesn't move the cursor.

    * ## Comments {#688:comments}

      * REPRODUCTION (confirmed in a real in-process framework Browser wired with the recipe's get_children + on_expand/on_children_loaded hooks, md_doc on sys.path so the md feature is live): {#688:comment:690}

        MD MESSAGE (jsonl#0 whose text has a '# Heading'): get_children returns ONE child, the inline 'markdown' md-doc node id '<sess>#0#md:' with row_bg UNSET (None) and hidden UNSET. _latest_voice_among_children(<sess>#0) returns None (no child has row_bg). So:
          - PRESS 1 (right on collapsed md message): the row EXPANDS (#0 enters state.expanded, #0#md: becomes visible at depth 2) but the CURSOR STAYS on #0 -- _on_expand -> _jump_to_latest_voice no-ops because latest is None.
          - PRESS 2 (right, already expanded): _action_tree_right's already-expanded branch calls _jump_to_latest_voice again -> still None -> cursor STILL stays on #0. (The framework _nav_right 'step into first child' branch is never reached because the recipe overrides 'right' with _action_tree_right.)
          => md nodes feel like right-arrow does nothing (cursor-wise) on BOTH presses.
        UMBRELLA CONTRAST (no regression target): #prompt:0 children are voice rows with row_bg set (#0 row_bg=235, #1 row_bg=17). PRESS 1 expands AND lands the cursor on #1 (latest voice) in a single press. This is the behavior md must mirror structurally (first child instead of latest-voice).
      * FIX (recipes/browse-claude): {#688:comment:691}

        - New _first_visible_child(item_id) (~5373): structural analog of _latest_voice_among_children — first direct child by listing order via get_children (mode-agnostic), skipping any 'hidden' child (respects the same visibility the voice-jump respects); None when no visible child / no children.
        - New _is_md_managed_id(item_id) (~5835): the shared 'md-managed' predicate = _md_doc is not None AND ('#md:' in id OR _is_md_message_id(id)). Encapsulates the definition that was inlined in _md_self_heal.
        - _jump_to_latest_voice (~5722): after the latest-voice lookup, if it returned None AND the row is md-managed, fall back to _first_visible_child. Single integration point — because _jump_to_latest_voice is the shared lander called by BOTH _on_expand (cached path) and _on_children_loaded (async settle path), the first-child landing automatically reuses the existing _AWAITING_VOICE_JUMP park/settle exactly as the voice-jump does (uncached expand parks the id; on_children_loaded lands once the fetch settles, still gated by the 'still expanded' check). It also fires for the _action_tree_right re-press case.
        - _md_self_heal (~5814): refactored to use _is_md_managed_id (removed the duplicated '#md: or _is_md_message_id' inline check); behavior-preserving (test_self_heal_* still pass).
        No-regression: umbrellas/sessions are NOT md-managed (#prompt:/#tool:/#span:/#agent:/bare .jsonl all return False), so they keep landing on the latest VOICE child and never hit the first-child fallback. md nodes never have a row_bg child, so the fallback only ever fires for them (it can never override a real voice jump). Childless / false-positive rows: _first_visible_child returns None -> no cursor move (and on_children_loaded still self-heals the arrow).
        TESTS (test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py, added to TestMarkdownSubtrees — loaded with md_doc live via _load_recipe_with_md_doc, isolation-robust: saves/restores _BROWSER/_TREE_MODE/_MD_COLOR + clears md_doc cache):
        - test_expand_md_message_lands_on_first_child (cached): message-with-md -> cursor lands on inline 'markdown' first child.
        - test_expand_inline_md_node_lands_on_first_child: #md: inline node -> first heading.
        - test_expand_file_doc_lands_on_first_child: file-doc (boundary) node -> first heading.
        - test_uncached_md_message_defers_then_lands_on_first_child: uncached path parks id, on_children_loaded lands on first child (mirrors voice-jump defer).
        - test_umbrella_still_lands_on_latest_voice_no_regression: 3-record turn (user/tool_use/asst) where first(#0)!=latest(#2); umbrella lands on #2 (latest voice). No regression.
        - test_false_positive_md_message_does_not_move_cursor: code-fence '#' false positive, build empty -> no cursor move at expand nor at settle (settle self-heals only).
        - test_first_visible_child_helper / test_first_visible_child_skips_hidden / test_is_md_managed_id_predicate: helper unit coverage.
        REPRO RE-CONFIRMED post-fix in the same in-process Browser harness: PRESS 1 on a collapsed md message now expands AND lands the cursor on its inline 'markdown' child in a single press; umbrella PRESS 1 still lands on the latest voice (#1/#2).
        VERIFY:
        - python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render: Ran 350 tests, FAILED (failures=2) — exactly the 2 pre-existing standalone color-isolation failures (test_agent_send_stripe..., test_agent_reply_stripe...); my 9 new tests pass.
        - ./run-tests.sh: Ran 2729 tests, FAILED (failures=10, skipped=4). The 10 are byte-identical to the pristine tree (verified by stashing both changed files and re-running) — all pre-existing TestSendMessage/TestTaskNotification/TestAskUserQuestion color+markdown isolation failures; none in TestMarkdownSubtrees/TestOnExpandJumpComposition. Zero new failures. No flaky 11th suspend failure observed.
        - TestOnExpandJumpComposition (md-less voice-jump) still green; async test_children_loaded green; recipe compiles.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Agent tool_result preview should render markdown (md2ansi) {#689}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 10:46:39 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 11:15:20 UTC

    Bug: an Agent/Task call's tool_result IS a markdown report (it even gets
    a synthetic "markdown" child), but the tool_result message preview shows
    UNRENDERED markdown — inconsistent with the inline "markdown" child
    (which renders via md2ansi) and with the voice renderers.

    Root cause: `_fmt_tur_subagent` (recipes/browse-claude:2626) appends the
    report content raw (lines ~2643/2645: `out.append(p.get('text',''))` /
    `out.append(content)`), unlike `_render_assistant`/`_render_user`/
    `_fmt_tur_ask_user_question` which render text through `_md_voice`.

    Fix: render the subagent report BODY through `_md_voice` (md2ansi when
    the _MD_COLOR toggle is on; falls back to raw on md2ansi failure / when
    off) — collect the text parts (or the bare-string content), join, and
    `_md_voice` them. Keep the header/stats line (agentType/status/duration)
    as-is (not through md2ansi). Scope strictly to `_fmt_tur_subagent` — do
    NOT md2ansi other tool_results (Bash/Read/Grep are raw output and must
    stay raw).

    Tests: with _MD_COLOR on, a subagent tool_result body contains md2ansi
    ANSI (e.g. heading styling); with it off, raw text; the header/stats are
    unchanged.

    * ## Comments {#689:comments}

      * Fixed _fmt_tur_subagent (recipes/browse-claude ~2636): the report BODY now renders through _md_voice instead of being appended raw. Collect each type=='text' content part's text (or the bare-string content) into chunks, join with '\n\n' (filtering empties, mirroring _message_md_text/_user_body_text), then append _md_voice(joined). Head (🤖 agentType [status] id=) and stats (Ns · N tools · N tokens) lines stay EXACTLY as-is — not markdown. Scope limited to _fmt_tur_subagent; no other _fmt_tur_* touched. {#689:comment:692}

        Tests: added 3 tests to TestMarkdownSubtrees (test_browse_claude_render.py ~7782) — that class loads via _load_recipe_with_md_doc() so _md2ansi_fn is live, and its setUp save/restores _MD_COLOR (isolation-robust; each test also sets _MD_COLOR explicitly):
        - test_subagent_report_body_md2ansi_when_color_on: MD_COLOR on → body heading rendered (md2ansi heading CSI \x1b[0;38;5;226m present, literal '# ' marker gone).
        - test_subagent_report_body_raw_when_color_off: MD_COLOR off → raw '# Report Heading' survives.
        - test_subagent_header_stats_unchanged_by_md_toggle: 🤖/general-purpose/[completed]/12.3s/5 tools/1,234 tokens present both ways; the head+stats prefix (before the body) is byte-identical on/off.
        Verify: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render → FAILED (failures=2) = exactly the 2 pre-existing isolation failures (test_agent_send_stripe_and_tag_style_registered, test_agent_reply_stripe_is_teal), my 3 pass, existing test_subagent + test_render_tool_result_subagent_shape still pass. ./run-tests.sh → FAILED (failures=10, skipped=4) = documented baseline, zero new failures (flaky suspend test did not recur). No existing test needed adjustment — existing test_subagent's body-text assertion still holds since the body is preserved (just rendered).

* ## Ticket: Task: Implement list columns (composable row-format hooks) {#691}

      status: open
      created: 2026-06-03 11:08:10 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-03 11:08:10 UTC

  Per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-list-columns-design.md. Goal:
  recipes assemble aligned columns themselves via three init-bound
  row-format hooks plus measurement/formatting primitives — NOT a
  declarative column engine. Work happens in worktree
  worktree-list-columns (absolute path
  /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/list-columns).

  Sequencing: Stages 1-3 all touch src-tui/050-render.py, so run them
  sequentially. Stages 4 and 5 touch different recipe files and may run
  in parallel after Stage 3. Per-ticket: Opus/high-effort worker, then a
  review subagent (goal met? overengineered? stale code?); the leader
  commits after review. Known-baseline failures (ticket #690, browse-claude
  stripe colors) are unrelated — do not fix them in these tickets.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Stage 1 — cell_* helpers + styles API {#692}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 11:08:10 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 11:16:07 UTC

    Spec sec D + Goal 4. Pure, additive public helpers; no behavior change.
    Module-level so the sys.modules['browse_tui'] alias exports them.

    Implement (reuse _char_width/_visible_len/_truncate_by_cells — do not
    duplicate width logic):
    - cell_width(s) -> int (display cells, wide-char aware).
    - cell_ljust / cell_rjust / cell_center(s, width, fill=' ') — pad to
      width cells; s unchanged if already wider; fill must be 1 cell.
    - cell_trim(s, width, *, where='end', ellipsis='...', word_boundary=False)
      with ellipsis default the single char U+2026 — no-op if it fits; else
      trim to width cells with ellipsis at end/start/middle; word_boundary
      (middle only) prefers a space near the cut; account ellipsis width;
      no overshoot on wide chars.
    - cell_fit(s, width, *, justify='left', trim='end', ellipsis=U+2026,
      fill=' ', word_boundary=False) — cell_trim if too wide else
      justify-pad; ALWAYS returns exactly width cells.
    - style(name) -> (fg, bold) wrapping _TAG_STYLE (unknown/'' -> (None,
      False)); STYLE_NAMES: frozenset of valid names; constants MARKER_FG=4,
      ID_FG=3, DIM_FG=242 (public faces of _MARKER_COLOR/_ID_COLOR/_PENDING_FG).

    Tests (test/unit/, follow repo style): spec "Test coverage / Stage 1"
    plus style()/STYLE_NAMES/constants.
    Acceptance: ./build-tui.sh green; new tests pass; imports from
    browse_tui work. Do NOT commit.

    * ## Comments {#692:comments}

      * Stage 1 done (TDD). Added module-level public API in src-tui/050-render.py: {#692:comment:663}

        - cell_width/cell_ljust/cell_rjust/cell_center/cell_trim/cell_fit (reuse _char_width/_visible_len/_truncate_by_cells; new _suffix_by_cells walker for start/middle tail extraction; _char_width_total/_check_fill for 1-cell fill/ellipsis validation).
        - styles API near _TAG_STYLE: style(name), STYLE_NAMES (frozenset), MARKER_FG=4/ID_FG=3/DIM_FG=242.
        Decisions: cell_fit re-pads after trim so wide-char straddle still yields exactly width cells; cell_trim accounts ellipsis within width (returns just trimmed ellipsis when width<ellipsis width); middle prefix gets the extra cell (ceil); word_boundary snaps head to last space only when cut>0 (keeps the space, never empties a non-empty head); center extra pad goes right (matches str.center).
        Tests: test/unit/test_cell_helpers.py (44 tests). Full suite: 2634 tests, only the 2 pre-existing #690 failures, 0 new. browse_tui alias exports verified via --run-py recipe. Not committed (leader commits after review).

  * ## Ticket: Task: Stage 3 — max_col_width + per-parent cache {#694}

        status: done

    Spec sec C. Content-auto column width, cached per parent.
    - RowContext.max_col_width(field, parent_id=None) -> int: parent_id

      max(cell_width(str(getattr(child, field, ''))) ...) (missing -> 0).

      (040-state.py:448); also clear in cache_invalidate_subtree /
      cache_invalidate_all.

    hit no re-scan; invalidation on refresh/update_data/subtree; flat list
    == global).

    * ## Comments {#694:comments}

      * Stage 3 (max_col_width + cache + invalidation). VERIFIED choke points: {#694:comment:671}

        - _index_drop_children (040-state.py:448) is THE documented drop/replace choke point; called by apply_children_results (REFRESH path, :5088) and cache_invalidate_subtree (:581). Drop _col_width_cache[pid] here => covers refresh + subtree for free.
        - cache_invalidate_all (:587) clears _children directly => clear whole _col_width_cache.
        - _drop_subtree_indexes (:803) and _apply_clear_children (:1325) pop _children WITHOUT _index_drop_children (comment at :1312 says so) => explicit pop needed.
        - update_data ops _apply_upsert/set/mod (:925/1064/1168) and _apply_remove (:1287) mutate child lists IN PLACE via setdefault+append/insert/del (NOT through _index_drop_children) => explicit pop of affected parent(s) needed (ticket lists upsert+mod as a required test).
        DECISION: cache lives on STATE (_col_width_cache field near _children), per leader's correction — invalidation choke points are all state-level. RowContext.max_col_width reads/fills self._browser._state._col_width_cache. Iterate state._children.get(pid, ()) directly (plain list, NOT .list). Deviation from spec (browser._col_width_cache) noted.
      * IMPLEMENTED + GREEN. Production: (1) State._col_width_cache dict field near _children; (2) _col_width_drop(state,pid) helper; (3) wired drops into _index_drop_children (covers refresh+subtree), cache_invalidate_all (clear), _drop_subtree_indexes (per level), _apply_clear_children (parent), _apply_remove (parent), and apply_ops upsert/set/mod branches (old+new parent, structural-only); (4) RowContext.max_col_width(field, parent_id=_PARENT_DEFAULT sentinel) reading self._browser._state._col_width_cache, iterating state._children.get(pid,()) (plain list), measuring max(cell_width(str(getattr(c,field,'')))...,default=0), memoised; cached-0 distinguished from uncomputed via 'is not None'. Tests: 23 in test_render.py (TestMaxColWidthMeasurement/Cache/Invalidation) + 6 end-to-end real-Browser in test_state_indexes.py (TestColWidthCacheInvalidation). Full suite: 2688 tests, 2 failures = ONLY the pre-existing #690 browse-claude stripe color tests (_ROW_BG_FOR_KIND 17 vs 22/23), 4 pre-existing skips. No new failures. Not committed (leader commits after review). {#694:comment:672}

* ## Ticket: md_doc.find_md_refs: handle markdown link / autolink syntax {#698}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-03 15:13:07 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-03 15:20:48 UTC

  BUG (real-world, found post-merge): markdown files reference each other with
  standard links like `[docs/cli.md](docs/cli.md)`, `<docs/api.md>`, inline
  code `` `report.md` ``, reference-defs `[x]: docs/ref.md`, wiki
  `[[docs/wiki.md]]`. find_md_refs's regex
  `(?<![^\s"\\$*])[^\s"\\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b` does NOT exclude the link/code
  delimiters `( ) [ ] < >` and backtick, so on a markdown link it captures the
  whole blob `[docs/cli.md](docs/cli.md` → resolve_md_ref returns None → the
  ref is silently dropped. Net effect: browse-md (and browse-claude prose
  links) don't surface references written as markdown links — the COMMON case.
  Confirmed against the repo README: its 5 `[docs/x.md](docs/x.md)` links all
  resolve to None today.

  Fix (recipes/md_doc.py find_md_refs): add the markdown link/autolink/wiki
  delimiters `( ) [ ] < >` AND inline-code backtick to the excluded character
  set (in BOTH the negative-lookbehind and the token class), keeping the
  existing exclusions (whitespace, `"`, `\`, `$`, `*`) and the leading `/`/`~`
  capture from #671. Recommended:
  `(?<![^\s"`\\$*()\[\]<>])[^\s"`\\$*()\[\]<>]+\.(?:md|MD)\b`
  Then `[docs/cli.md](docs/cli.md)` yields `docs/cli.md` (twice — label +
  target — deduped by abspath downstream), `<docs/api.md>` → `docs/api.md`,
  `` `report.md` `` → `report.md`, bare/`/abs`/`~` refs unchanged. Update the
  regex's comment/docstring to explain it strips link/code wrappers. Note the
  accepted trade-off: a filename literally containing `()[]<>`/backtick won't
  be captured (rare; markdown-link support is far more valuable).

  Tests (test/unit/test_md_doc.py): inline link `[t](p.md)` (assert the path,
  deduped), autolink `<p.md>`, inline-code `` `p.md` ``, reference-def
  `[x]: p.md`, wiki `[[p.md]]`, bare `docs/p.md`, `/abs/p.md` + `~/p.md`
  unchanged (no #671 regression), `.mdx`/trailing-dot still excluded.
  Add a browse-md regression test (test/unit/test_browse_md.py): a file whose
  text references another existing `.md` via a MARKDOWN LINK surfaces it as an
  expandable `[md]` child (previously dropped).

  Baseline: test_browse_claude_render.py has 2 pre-existing isolation failures
  / 10 under full discover; suspend UI test flaky. Add ZERO new failures.
  Verify: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_md_doc test.unit.test_browse_md
  test.unit.test_browse_claude_render, then ./run-tests.sh (stays at 10).

  This is a trivial fix done directly on main (no worktree).

  Done when: markdown-link refs resolve; both recipes surface them; tests pass.

  * ## Comments {#698:comments}

    * Fixed find_md_refs regex in recipes/md_doc.py to handle markdown link/autolink/wiki/inline-code syntax. {#698:comment:699}

      Regex change (both negative-lookbehind AND token class):
        OLD: (?<![^\s"\\$*])[^\s"\\$*]+\.(?:md|MD)\b
        NEW: (?<![^\s"`\\$*()\[\]<>])[^\s"`\\$*()\[\]<>]+\.(?:md|MD)\b
      Added to the excluded set: backtick, ( ) [ ] < >. Kept existing exclusions (\s " \ $ *) and the leading /~ capture from #671.
      Before/after on README.md find_md_refs:
        BEFORE: 5 polluted tokens like "[docs/cli.md](docs/cli.md" -> all resolve to None (refs silently dropped).
        AFTER: clean tokens; 5 distinct docs/*.md links now resolve to EXISTING files (docs/cli.md, docs/api.md, docs/recipes.md, docs/internals.md, docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-browse-tui-design.md).
      Case checks all pass: [docs/cli.md](docs/cli.md) -> [docs/cli.md, docs/cli.md]; <docs/api.md> -> [docs/api.md]; `report.md` -> [report.md]; [x]: docs/ref.md -> [docs/ref.md]; [[docs/wiki.md]] -> [docs/wiki.md]; bare/abs/~ unchanged (no #671 regression); a.mdx and trailing-dot still excluded.
      Updated the regex comment to explain it strips link/code/autolink wrappers and notes the accepted trade-off (a filename literally containing ()[]<>/backtick is no longer captured; rare, markdown-link support is far more valuable).
      Tests:
      - test/unit/test_md_doc.py: added inline-link, autolink, inline-code, reference-def, wiki, and bare-relative cases.
      - test/unit/test_browse_md.py: added TestMdRefFollowing.test_markdown_link_ref_surfaces_as_md_child — a file linking [label](B.md) now surfaces B.md as an expandable [md] child (dropped before the fix).
      Results: test_md_doc + test_browse_md GREEN (318 tests). test_browse_claude_render: exactly the 2 pre-existing isolation failures. ./run-tests.sh: 10 failures (all pre-existing in test_browse_claude_render), zero new. Did NOT commit (leader commits after review).

* ## Ticket: Epic: tig-like working-change rows in browse-git commits view {#700}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-03 23:01:17 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-03 23:56:26 UTC

  Add up to four synthetic group rows at the TOP of the commits-mode log,
  each shown ONLY when non-empty, in this order:

    1. Untracked changes  — porcelain `??`
    2. Tracked changes    — unstaged modifications to tracked files (worktree col Y set, not `?`)
    3. Staged changes     — index col X set (not `?`)
    4. Conflicts          — unmerged set: DD AU UD UA DU AA UU

  Design decisions (locked with the user):
  - Synthetic rows are EXPANDABLE parents that drill into their affected
    files, reusing the existing `status:<XY>:<path>` leaf kind (status tag,
    diff preview, and `E`-to-edit all come for free).
  - The parent's own preview = the combined diff for that bucket.
  - Conflicts are EXCLUSIVE: an unmerged entry (e.g. `UU`) must NOT also
    appear under Staged/Tracked. A non-conflict two-sided entry (`MM`) DOES
    appear under both Staged and Tracked (matches existing `_status_diff_plan`).
  - Generated only at the commits-mode ROOT (never in the branches-mode ref
    drill-down, which shares `_commit_log_items`). Suppressed when a
    positional rev is given (`_revs` non-empty); honors the `_paths` filter.
  - New parent id kind `wc:` → `wc:untracked` / `wc:tracked` / `wc:staged`
    / `wc:conflicts`.

  Conventions: single-file recipe `recipes/browse-git`; unit tests load it
  via SourceFileLoader with a stubbed `browse_tui` (see test/unit/test_browse_git.py);
  UI smoke tests build a temp repo + tmux (test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py).
  TDD within each ticket. Leader commits per ticket after a review subagent.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Classification core + id/diff-plan/tag changes (TDD) {#701}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 23:01:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 23:43:43 UTC

    Pure, unit-testable helpers + small extensions to existing parsers. NO
    root/children/preview wiring yet (that is the next ticket).

    Add to `recipes/browse-git`:
    - `_UNMERGED_CODES = frozenset({'DD','AU','UD','UA','DU','AA','UU'})`
    - `_is_conflict(xy)` → `xy in _UNMERGED_CODES`.
    - `_classify_worktree(rows)` → dict with keys
      `'untracked'|'tracked'|'staged'|'conflicts'`, each a list of `(xy, path)`.
      Rules: conflict codes go ONLY to `conflicts`; `??` → `untracked`;
      otherwise X set (not ' '/'?') → `staged` AND Y set (not ' '/'?') →
      `tracked` (a code may land in both). `rows` is the output of the
      existing `_parse_porcelain_z`.
    - Extend `_parse_id`: `wc` kind → `('wc', <bucket>)`. Update the
      module + function docstrings' id table.
    - Extend `_status_diff_plan`: unmerged codes return a single
      `[('conflict', ['diff', '--', path])]` (the combined `--cc` view),
      BEFORE the existing staged/unstaged split — so a `UU` leaf shows one
      conflict diff, not two empty sections. Keep `??` and the normal X/Y
      split unchanged.
    - Add `'U': 'red'` to `_STATUS_LETTER_STYLE` so unmerged tags are styled.
      (`_status_tag('UU')` already returns `'U'`; verify `'AA'`→`'A'`,
      `'DD'`→`'D'` still resolve to a styled letter.)

    Unit tests in test/unit/test_browse_git.py (new TestClasses, matching the
    existing SourceFileLoader pattern):
    - `_parse_id('wc:staged')` → `('wc','staged')` etc.
    - `_classify_worktree`: exclusivity of conflicts; `MM` in both
      staged+tracked; `??` only untracked; `M ` staged-only; ` M` tracked-only;
      empty input → all-empty buckets.
    - `_status_diff_plan` for each unmerged code → single `('conflict', [...])`.
    - `_STATUS_LETTER_STYLE['U']` exists / is non-empty.

    * ## Comments {#701:comments}

      * Added classification core to recipes/browse-git (TDD): _UNMERGED_CODES frozenset, _is_conflict(xy), _classify_worktree(rows) bucketing into untracked/tracked/staged/conflicts (conflict-exclusive, MM in both staged+tracked). Extended _parse_id with 'wc:<bucket>' kind + updated module and _parse_id docstring id tables. Extended _status_diff_plan: conflict codes return single [('conflict',['diff','--',path])] BEFORE the X/Y split. Added 'U':'red' to _STATUS_LETTER_STYLE. No root/children/preview wiring (deferred). Added 5 TestCase classes (15 tests); browse-git suite now 92 tests, all green (was 77). {#701:comment:705}

      * Added two helpers to recipes/md_doc.py in a new 'References-umbrella id' section (right after the #md: codec, before the parse cache): refs_umbrella_id(docid) -> docid + '#refs' (append), and split_refs_umbrella(item_id) -> docid if item_id.endswith('#refs') else None (endswith+slice). Module-level _REFS_MARKER = '#refs' with a comment explaining collision-safety: encoded #md: segments have no raw '#', heading ids end '#<digits>', message bases end '#<digits>' — none end in '#refs', so endswith is sufficient (no regex). Added TestRefsUmbrellaId to test/unit/test_md_doc.py: round-trip for a message base (/p/s.jsonl#3) and a #md: file-doc id, plus split returns None for a plain message id, a #md: doc id, a #md:…#<offset> heading id, a bare path, and a #prompt: umbrella id. test_md_doc: 72 tests OK (was 66). test_browse_md: 252 OK. {#701:comment:704}

  * ## Ticket: Wire synthetic rows: root + children + preview (TDD) {#702}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 23:01:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 23:48:22 UTC

    Build on the classification core. Add to `recipes/browse-git`:
    - `_WC_GROUPS` = ordered `[('untracked','Untracked changes'),
      ('tracked','Tracked changes'), ('staged','Staged changes'),
      ('conflicts','Conflicts')]` (defines display order + labels).
    - `_worktree_status(paths)` → run `git status --porcelain -z [-- paths]`,
      return `_parse_porcelain_z(result.stdout)` (or `[]` on failure — degrade
      quietly, don't inject an error row at the top of the log).
    - `_worktree_groups(paths)` → classify `_worktree_status(paths)`; for each
      `(bucket,label)` in `_WC_GROUPS` with a NON-empty file list, emit
      `Item(id=f'wc:{bucket}', title=label, has_children=True)`. Returns [] when
      the tree is clean.
    - `_worktree_files(bucket, paths)` → re-run status+classify, return the
      bucket's files as `status:<XY>:<path>` leaves. FACTOR the status-leaf
      construction shared with `_status_root` into a helper
      `_status_leaf(xy, path)` and use it in both places (kill the duplication).
    - `_worktree_preview(bucket, paths)` → combined colored diff for the bucket:
      staged → `diff --cached -- <bucket paths>`; tracked/conflicts →
      `diff -- <bucket paths>`; untracked → concat per-file
      `diff --no-index /dev/null <path>`. Use `_git_color` + `_colorize_diff`.
      Scope by the bucket's own paths so e.g. tracked-preview doesn't pull in
      staged-only files.
    - `_commits_root()`: prepend `_worktree_groups(_paths)` ONLY when
      `not _revs`; then the existing `_commit_log_items(_revs, _paths)`.
    - `get_children`: dispatch `wc` → `_worktree_files(parsed[1], _paths)`.
    - `get_preview`: dispatch `wc` → `_worktree_preview(parsed[1], _paths)`.

    Confirm the synthetic parents + their `status:` children fall through
    `git_row_content` to `default_row_content` (no `col_sha`) — they should
    render with the ▼/▶ marker and plain title, NOT as commit columns.

    Unit tests:
    - `_worktree_groups` ordering + non-empty-only filtering, by stubbing
      `_worktree_status` to return canned rows (monkeypatch on the loaded module).
    - rev-scope suppression: with `_revs` set, `_commits_root` emits NO `wc:`
      rows (stub `_commit_log_items` / `_worktree_status`).
    - `_worktree_files(bucket)` returns `status:`-id leaves for that bucket only.
    - `_status_leaf` dedup: `_status_root` still builds identical items.

    * ## Comments {#702:comments}

      * Implemented synthetic worktree rows in recipes/browse-git: added _WC_GROUPS constant, _status_leaf() helper (factored shared status-leaf construction out of _status_root, now used by both _status_root and _worktree_files), _worktree_status/_worktree_groups/_worktree_files/_worktree_preview. Wired _commits_root to prepend _worktree_groups(_paths) only when not _revs; added wc dispatch to get_children and get_preview. Verified wc parents + status leaves carry no col_sha so they fall through git_row_content to default_row_content (no change needed there). Tests: 92 -> 103 (added TestWorktreeGroups, TestCommitsRootWorktreeScope, TestWorktreeFiles, TestStatusLeafDedup). All green. {#702:comment:706}

      * Implemented the [links] References umbrella at both levels. {#702:comment:705}

        UMBRELLA BUILDER: _md_refs_umbrella_item(docid) — id=md_doc.refs_umbrella_id(docid), title='References', tag='links', tag_style='cyan' (muted cross-file grouping hue, distinct from inline 'dim' and file-doc 'blue'), kind='md-refs', has_children=True (authoritative — built only when a ref exists), boundary=False (same-document grouping like the inline 'markdown' node).
        SHORT-CIRCUIT DETECTION: added _has_any_existing_ref(text, doc_dir, cwd, project_root) mirroring browse-md's _file_has_existing_ref — stops at the first captured .md token that resolve_md_ref resolves. _md_message_children and the file-doc branch of _md_subtree_children now call this and append ONE umbrella node instead of per-ref _md_file_item. Doc-expand does ONLY this cheap check; the full _md_resolved_refs (resolve all / dedup by abspath / sort by label) runs lazily in _md_refs_umbrella_children on umbrella expand.
        ROUTING ORDER: get_children and get_preview both gain a split_refs_umbrella(id) branch placed BEFORE the '#md:' and plain-message branches (a file-doc umbrella id <base>#md:<enc>#refs contains '#md:', so the #refs test must win). _md_refs_umbrella_children splits to docid: docid with '#md:' => file-doc level (parse_md_id + dirname anchor, refs chained onto abspaths); else message level (_session_cwd_and_root + whole-record string-leaf scan, refs chained off the base). Ref file-docs keep their exact prior ids (<docid>#md:<enc>) and boundary=True — IDs unchanged.
        PLAIN-LIST PREVIEW: _preview_md_refs_umbrella reuses the umbrella's own get_children for matching labels and emits a count header + one label per line, NEVER through _md_voice/md2ansi (asserted no ANSI even with _MD_COLOR on).
        ID THREADING: _is_md_managed_id adds the split_refs_umbrella check so the message-level umbrella (<jsonl>#<n>#refs, which lacks '#md:' and isn't a plain message id) is md-managed and right-arrow lands on its first ref; the file-doc umbrella already matched via '#md:'. _md_self_heal needs no special case — the umbrella is only built when a ref exists so get_children is non-empty (a doc going unreadable would correctly retract the arrow). _bust_caches_for/_resolve_path_for_bust already map any <path>#... id to <path>.
        TESTS: updated 8 existing tests that asserted the old direct-children shape (refs now under the umbrella) + renamed 2. Added 8 new tests: always-wrap single ref, plain-list preview (no ANSI / labels present / not the file body), preview-empty-when-unreadable, _is_md_managed_id covers both umbrella shapes, right-arrow lands into umbrella's first ref, lazy short-circuit (spy proves _md_resolved_refs NOT called on doc-expand, called once on umbrella-expand), file-doc umbrella routes before #md: in both routers.
        VERIFY: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render => Ran 360 tests, OK. ./run-tests.sh => Ran 2865 tests, OK (skipped=4). Zero new failures vs baseline.
