* ## Ticket: Task: References umbrella — group discovered .md refs under a [links] node {#705}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-03 23:01:17 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-03 23:31:26 UTC

  Today discovered .md references attach DIRECTLY as children of their owning
  document (the message/reply for whole-record refs, and each file-doc for its
  own refs), interleaved after headings — which reads as confusing. Group them
  instead under ONE synthetic "References" node (tag [links], title
  "References") per document, at BOTH levels (message + every file-doc),
  mirroring browse-claude's existing <prompt>/<tool> umbrella idiom.

  Decisions (settled with the user):
  - BOTH levels (message and file-docs, recursively).
  - ALWAYS wrap (even a single ref) for consistency.
  - Umbrella preview = a PLAIN, non-markdown list of the ref labels (NOT md2ansi).
  - LAZY / efficient (decision #4): a document's expansion does ONLY a
    short-circuit "any existing ref?" check to decide whether to add the single
    References node; the FULL resolve/build of the ref list is deferred to the
    umbrella's OWN expansion. Since the short-circuit is resolve-based, the
    umbrella never appears empty (no extra self-heal/state churn for it).

  Id shape: References umbrella id = `<docid>#refs` (docid = a message base
  `<jsonl>#<n>` or a `#md:` file-doc id). The ref file-doc nodes UNDER it keep
  their existing ids `<docid>#md:<enc>` (the umbrella is a tree-grouping parent,
  NOT a new hop in the resolution chain) — so resolve/recursion/get_doc are
  untouched. The umbrella is boundary=False (same-doc grouping; Alt-Right
  reveals the ref entries); the ref file-docs under it stay boundary=True
  (Alt-Right stops there — no file reads).

  Affects recipes/md_doc.py (tiny shared id helpers), recipes/browse-claude,
  recipes/browse-md.

  Baseline is GREEN (./run-tests.sh = 2852 tests, 0 failures, 4 skipped) —
  main was fixed concurrently (#690). Every ticket must keep it at ZERO
  failures (do NOT assume the old "10 baseline").

  Subagents: all Opus, effort high; per non-trivial ticket a review subagent
  (goal met? overengineered? stale code?).

  * ## Ticket: md_doc: References-umbrella id helpers (#refs marker) {#706}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 23:01:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 23:03:23 UTC

    Add two tiny shared helpers to recipes/md_doc.py for the new References
    umbrella id shape `<docid>#refs` (docid = a message base `<jsonl>#<n>` or a
    `#md:` file-doc id — never a heading; refs attach at the document level):
    - refs_umbrella_id(docid) -> docid + '#refs'
    - split_refs_umbrella(item_id) -> the docid if item_id ends with the '#refs'
      marker, else None.
    The marker is unambiguous: percent-encoded `#md:` segments contain no raw
    '#', heading ids end in '#<digits>', message bases end in '#<digits>' — none
    end in '#refs'. Keep it dead simple (append / endswith+strip); document the
    marker and why it can't collide.

    Tests (test/unit/test_md_doc.py): round-trip refs_umbrella_id/split for a
    message base and a `#md:` file-doc id; split_refs_umbrella returns None for a
    plain message id, a `#md:` doc id, a `#md:…#<lineoffset>` heading id, and a
    non-md id.

    Baseline GREEN (0 failures); add zero. Done when: helpers + tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#706:comments}

      * Added two helpers to recipes/md_doc.py in a new 'References-umbrella id' section (right after the #md: codec, before the parse cache): refs_umbrella_id(docid) -> docid + '#refs' (append), and split_refs_umbrella(item_id) -> docid if item_id.endswith('#refs') else None (endswith+slice). Module-level _REFS_MARKER = '#refs' with a comment explaining collision-safety: encoded #md: segments have no raw '#', heading ids end '#<digits>', message bases end '#<digits>' — none end in '#refs', so endswith is sufficient (no regex). Added TestRefsUmbrellaId to test/unit/test_md_doc.py: round-trip for a message base (/p/s.jsonl#3) and a #md: file-doc id, plus split returns None for a plain message id, a #md: doc id, a #md:…#<offset> heading id, a bare path, and a #prompt: umbrella id. test_md_doc: 72 tests OK (was 66). test_browse_md: 252 OK. {#706:comment:704}

  * ## Ticket: browse-claude: group refs under a [links] References umbrella {#707}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 23:01:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 23:13:09 UTC

    Replace the direct ref-children attachment with ONE synthetic References
    umbrella per document, lazily. Depends on the md_doc helpers.

    - _md_message_children and _md_subtree_children (the file-doc branch):
      instead of appending _md_file_item per resolved ref, append ONE References
      umbrella node (id = md_doc.refs_umbrella_id(docid); tag 'links'/[links];
      title 'References'; boundary=False; has_children=True) WHEN the document has
      >=1 existing ref — detected via a SHORT-CIRCUIT check that resolves refs and
      stops at the first existing one (do NOT build the full list here). Add a
      cheap _has_any_existing_ref helper (mirror browse-md's _file_has_existing_ref
      short-circuit) over the same text/record the full builder uses. docid is the
      message base (message level) or the file-doc id (file-doc level).
    - get_children routing: add a References-umbrella branch
      (md_doc.split_refs_umbrella(node_id) is not None) BEFORE the '#md:' and
      plain-message branches (a file-doc's umbrella id `<base>#md:<enc>#refs`
      contains '#md:', so the '#refs' check MUST come first). It returns the
      document's refs as file-doc nodes: split to docid, recover the doc's
      (base, abspaths) + resolution context, run the FULL _md_resolved_refs
      (resolve all, dedup by abspath, sort by label), build _md_file_item per ref
      — ids unchanged `<docid>#md:<enc>`, file-docs stay boundary=True. THIS is the
      lazy point (full resolve happens on umbrella expand).
    - Message level: the umbrella groups the whole-record refs (as today); the
      inline 'markdown' node stays headings-only. Message children become
      [inline 'markdown' (if headings), References umbrella (if any ref)].
    - File-doc level: children become [headings…, References umbrella (if any ref)].
    - Preview (_preview_md_node / get_preview): a References-umbrella id renders a
      PLAIN, non-markdown list of the ref labels (one per line; a simple header is
      fine) — NOT through _md_voice/md2ansi. Doc/heading previews unchanged.
    - Thread the new id through: _is_md_managed_id (so the right-arrow first-child
      landing works — expanding the umbrella lands on its first ref), _md_self_heal
      (the umbrella is created only when a ref exists, so it won't be empty; just
      make sure self-heal/landing treat it as md-managed and don't mis-handle it),
      and styling.
    - boundary: umbrella False, ref file-docs True (unchanged).

    Efficiency (decision #4): document-expand does ONLY the short-circuit any-ref
    check; the full resolve is deferred to umbrella-expand.

    Tests (test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py, isolation-robust): a message
    with refs shows a [links] References umbrella whose children are the sorted/
    deduped refs; a file-doc with refs likewise; always-wrap (single ref still
    wrapped); the umbrella preview is a plain list (no ANSI); right-arrow lands
    into the umbrella's first ref; boundary flags correct; the doc-expand path
    does NOT build the full ref list (only adds the umbrella). Baseline GREEN; add
    zero failures (full ./run-tests.sh stays at 0).

    Done when: refs are grouped under [links] References at both levels; tests pass.

    * ## Comments {#707:comments}

      * Implemented the [links] References umbrella at both levels. {#707:comment:705}

        UMBRELLA BUILDER: _md_refs_umbrella_item(docid) — id=md_doc.refs_umbrella_id(docid), title='References', tag='links', tag_style='cyan' (muted cross-file grouping hue, distinct from inline 'dim' and file-doc 'blue'), kind='md-refs', has_children=True (authoritative — built only when a ref exists), boundary=False (same-document grouping like the inline 'markdown' node).
        SHORT-CIRCUIT DETECTION: added _has_any_existing_ref(text, doc_dir, cwd, project_root) mirroring browse-md's _file_has_existing_ref — stops at the first captured .md token that resolve_md_ref resolves. _md_message_children and the file-doc branch of _md_subtree_children now call this and append ONE umbrella node instead of per-ref _md_file_item. Doc-expand does ONLY this cheap check; the full _md_resolved_refs (resolve all / dedup by abspath / sort by label) runs lazily in _md_refs_umbrella_children on umbrella expand.
        ROUTING ORDER: get_children and get_preview both gain a split_refs_umbrella(id) branch placed BEFORE the '#md:' and plain-message branches (a file-doc umbrella id <base>#md:<enc>#refs contains '#md:', so the #refs test must win). _md_refs_umbrella_children splits to docid: docid with '#md:' => file-doc level (parse_md_id + dirname anchor, refs chained onto abspaths); else message level (_session_cwd_and_root + whole-record string-leaf scan, refs chained off the base). Ref file-docs keep their exact prior ids (<docid>#md:<enc>) and boundary=True — IDs unchanged.
        PLAIN-LIST PREVIEW: _preview_md_refs_umbrella reuses the umbrella's own get_children for matching labels and emits a count header + one label per line, NEVER through _md_voice/md2ansi (asserted no ANSI even with _MD_COLOR on).
        ID THREADING: _is_md_managed_id adds the split_refs_umbrella check so the message-level umbrella (<jsonl>#<n>#refs, which lacks '#md:' and isn't a plain message id) is md-managed and right-arrow lands on its first ref; the file-doc umbrella already matched via '#md:'. _md_self_heal needs no special case — the umbrella is only built when a ref exists so get_children is non-empty (a doc going unreadable would correctly retract the arrow). _bust_caches_for/_resolve_path_for_bust already map any <path>#... id to <path>.
        TESTS: updated 8 existing tests that asserted the old direct-children shape (refs now under the umbrella) + renamed 2. Added 8 new tests: always-wrap single ref, plain-list preview (no ANSI / labels present / not the file body), preview-empty-when-unreadable, _is_md_managed_id covers both umbrella shapes, right-arrow lands into umbrella's first ref, lazy short-circuit (spy proves _md_resolved_refs NOT called on doc-expand, called once on umbrella-expand), file-doc umbrella routes before #md: in both routers.
        VERIFY: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_browse_claude_render => Ran 360 tests, OK. ./run-tests.sh => Ran 2865 tests, OK (skipped=4). Zero new failures vs baseline.

  * ## Ticket: browse-md: group refs under a [links] References umbrella {#708}

* ## Ticket: Task: browse-git commit graph column (--tree, synthetic filler rows) {#711}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-03 23:45:14 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-04 00:36:29 UTC

  Add a tig-style compact commit-graph column to recipes/browse-git (commits
  mode + ref drill-down). Cheapest approach: let `git log --graph` do ALL lane
  assignment; render its output lines directly — commit lines become commit
  Items, FILLER lines become inert synthetic Items (NO fold, NO lane algorithm).
  Glyph-substitute git's art. Toggle via --tree/--no-tree (default OFF) and an
  interactive 't' hotkey.

  All work in recipes/browse-git — NO framework changes, NO separate module
  (single user, per request). Fillers are inert: id 'filler:N', has_children
  False, _parse_id->'other', so on_enter / actions / get_children already no-op
  on them (verified). Cursor can land on a filler (skip-on-nav is T2).

  Glyph map (str.translate): '*'->'·', '|'->'│', '/'->'╱'(U+2571),
  '\'->'╲'(U+2572), '_'->'─'. All nodes become '·'; git's connectors show merges.

  Layout: the graph column goes AFTER the date column, before chips/subject —
  [sha][author][date][graph][chips][subject] — so the fixed columns stay aligned
  and git's vertical lines line up. Filler rows blank-pad the sha+author+date
  width, then the graph art. NEVER truncate the graph cell.

  Baseline note: this worktree branched from main after the test-failure fix, so
  the suite should be green except the known-flaky test_suspend (tmux). Add no
  NEW failures.

  * ## Ticket: Tree-mode graph: git --graph fetch + glyph substitution + commit/filler rendering + --tree flag + 't' toggle (default off) {#712}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 23:45:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-03 23:56:28 UTC

    All in recipes/browse-git. See parent for the design.

    FETCH (when _tree_mode on): `git log --graph --no-color --format=<F> -n
    <_log_limit> [revs] [-- paths]` where <F> is the NUL-... use the unit
    separator \x1f: format = \x1f%H\x1f%an\x1f%ar\x1f%s\x1f%D. git prepends its
    graph art to every output line. Parse each line by splitting on \x1f:
    - >1 part  -> COMMIT line: part[0] is the graph art; the rest are
      H/an/ar/s/D. Build the existing commit Item (id='commit:'+sha,
      col_sha=sha[:7], col_author=an, col_date=ar, title=s,
      chips=_parse_decorations(D), has_children=True) AND set
      item.col_graph = _graph_translate(art).
    - 1 part   -> FILLER line: the whole line is art. Build inert
      Item(id=f'filler:{n}', title='', has_children=False) with
      .col_graph=_graph_translate(art); n = a unique running index.
    Preserve git's line order (commits + fillers interleaved).

    _graph_translate(art): str.translate with the glyph map (parent). Preserve
    internal spacing (git's alignment); trailing-space rstrip is fine.

    GATING: module global _tree_mode (default False). Add the graph fetch as the
    commits builder when _tree_mode else the existing plain _commit_log_items.
    Wire into _commits_root AND the get_children 'ref' drill-down (both build a
    commit log). Keep the plain path byte-identical when _tree_mode is False.

    git_row_content:
    - commit row (col_sha set) AND col_graph present: insert
      (item.col_graph + ' ', GFG, False) AFTER the date column segment, before
      chips/subject. GFG monochrome — use style('') (terminal default) or 'dim';
      pick one, keep simple.
    - filler row (col_graph set, col_sha None): return a single blank-pad segment
      of width = max_col_width('col_sha')+2 + max_col_width('col_author')+2 +
      max_col_width('col_date')+2, then (item.col_graph, GFG, False). Use
      ctx.max_col_width.
    - all other rows: unchanged (fall to default_row_content).

    CLI: parse --tree / --no-tree in main() (mirror the existing limit/flag
    popping); set _tree_mode (default False).
    HOTKEY: Action('t', 'Toggle commit graph (--tree)', toggle_tree, 'none');
    toggle_tree flips _tree_mode and ctx.refresh().

    TESTS (test/unit/test_browse_git.py, existing stub-load pattern): parse a
    commit line -> commit Item + col_graph (glyph-substituted) + col_*; parse a
    filler line -> inert filler Item (id 'filler:', has_children False, col_graph
    set, no col_sha); _graph_translate maps all 5 glyphs + preserves spaces;
    git_row_content commit-row inserts graph after date / filler-row blank-pad +
    graph / tree-off commit row unchanged; toggle_tree flips _tree_mode.
    ACCEPTANCE: ./build-tui.sh green; new tests pass; full suite adds NO new
    failures (test_suspend flake aside). Do NOT git commit.

    * ## Comments {#712:comments}

      * Implementation plan + key decisions: {#712:comment:703}

        - Glyph map as module _GRAPH_GLYPHS/_GRAPH_TABLE (str.maketrans); _graph_translate(art)=art.translate(table).rstrip() (preserve internal lane spacing, drop only trailing pad).
        - Module global _tree_mode=False. New seam _log_items(revs,paths): routes to _commit_graph_items when _tree_mode else _commit_log_items. Wired _commits_root AND get_children 'ref' branch through it; off-path calls _commit_log_items directly => byte-identical.
        - _commit_graph_items: git log --graph --no-color --format=\x1f%H\x1f%an\x1f%ar\x1f%s\x1f%D -n LIMIT [revs] [-- paths]. Split each line on \x1f: len>1 => commit (parts[0]=art, parts[1:6]=H/an/ar/s/D -> same columnar Item + .col_graph); len==1 => inert filler Item id filler:N (running n), has_children=False, .col_graph only. Preserve git line order. Verified against real git output: first commit-line art is '* ' (non-empty); filler lines (|\, |/) contain no \x1f -> single element.
        - git_row_content: GFG=style('') (terminal default, monochrome). Filler (col_sha None, col_graph set): [(' '*pad, GFG,False),(graph,GFG,False)] where pad=w(col_sha)+2+w(col_author)+2+w(col_date)+2. Commit + col_graph present: append (graph+' ',GFG,False) after date col, before chips. Non-commit/non-filler unchanged (fallback measures no column).
        - CLI _pop_tree_arg(default): pops all --tree/--no-tree, last wins; wired in main() before _classify_positionals. Action('t',...,toggle_tree,'none'); toggle_tree flips _tree_mode + message + ctx.refresh(). Help text + module docstring updated.
        Next: tests, build+full suite.
      * DONE + verified. {#712:comment:704}

        Files changed: recipes/browse-git, test/unit/test_browse_git.py.
        - Unit: 99 browse-git tests pass (77 baseline + 22 new across _graph_translate, _commit_graph_items, _log_items routing, git_row_content graph/filler, _pop_tree_arg, toggle_tree).
        - Full suite via ./run-tests.sh: 2874 tests OK (skipped=4); no new failures (test_suspend passed this run).
        - Real-repo smoke: --graph parse yields interleaved commits+fillers; rendered rows show [sha][author][date][graph][chips][subject], filler blank-pad = sha+author+date span so lanes align under commit graph col; glyphs ·│╱╲─ correct; tree-off path sets NO col_graph -> byte-identical rows.
        - pty: --tree launches with graph visible; runtime 't' toggles graph on; ref drill-down (get_children('ref:HEAD')) honors _tree_mode via _log_items.
        Decision: empty/leading art element is fine — commit vs filler is decided by split('\x1f') count (>1 commit, ==1 filler), not art truthiness; parts[:6] unpacks art+5 fields. toggle_tree mirrors switch_mode (message then refresh).

  * ## Ticket: Task: Skip filler rows on up/down cursor navigation (on_cursor_change) {#713}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-03 23:45:14 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 00:12:05 UTC

    Depends on the graph ticket (filler rows must exist). All in recipes/browse-git.

    GOAL: when up/down moves the cursor onto a filler row (id 'filler:...'),
    bounce it past the filler in the direction of travel to the next non-filler
    row, so up/down effectively skips fillers. Landing on a filler by other means
    (jump/search) is acceptable — only fix up/down.

    APPROACH (no framework change): set on_cursor_change=_skip_fillers in
    BrowserConfig. Track the previous cursor index in a module global. On each
    change: read ctx.cursor (+ id) and ctx.cursor_index; if the cursor id starts
    with 'filler:':
    - direction = +1 if cursor_index > prev_index else -1 (default +1).
    - find the next NON-filler row id in that direction and ctx.cursor_to(it); if
      none that way (a run at top/bottom), reverse direction.
    Update prev_index after. GUARD against loops: only re-move when ON a filler;
    moving to a non-filler won't re-trigger.

    INVESTIGATE the cleanest way to enumerate visible rows in order to find the
    next non-filler id: commits mode is FLAT (no nesting), so the visible order ==
    the get_children list order — the recipe can keep/read its last-built commit
    list (commits + fillers, in order) and use cursor_index to find neighbors.
    Confirm ctx exposes cursor_index and cursor_to (it does). Prefer a framework
    visible-items accessor if one exists and is clean; otherwise use the recipe's
    own ordered list.

    TESTS: simulate the cursor landing on a filler -> on_cursor_change moves it to
    the next commit in the travel direction (down and up); a run of consecutive
    fillers; top/bottom edge (reverse). Use a fake ctx exposing
    cursor/cursor_index/cursor_to over a synthetic commit+filler list.
    ACCEPTANCE: build green; tests pass; no new suite failures. Do NOT git commit.

    * ## Comments {#713:comments}

      * Implemented _skip_fillers on_cursor_change hook (no framework change). recipes/browse-git: added module globals _graph_row_ids (ordered (id,is_filler) build list) + _prev_cursor_index; _commit_graph_items now records _graph_row_ids; new _skip_fillers + _next_non_filler helpers; wired on_cursor_change=_skip_fillers into BrowserConfig. Direction inferred from cursor_index delta sign (down default on non-decrease); neighbour found by scanning _graph_row_ids by ID position (not visible index, so commit expansion can't desync); reverses at top/bottom edges. Loop guard: non-filler cur_id just records position and returns; bounce does NOT update _prev_cursor_index (the non-filler re-fire does). No-op when tree off / cur_id not a filler / None. Added TestSkipFillers (14 tests) covering down/up single+run, top/bottom reverse, equal-index default, non-filler/None/tree-off no-ops, re-entry/no-loop, and _graph_row_ids population. build-tui.sh green; full suite 2888 OK (skipped=4, the known-acceptable skips); test_browse_git.py 113 passed. {#713:comment:705}

      * Review follow-up: fixed branches-mode filler-id collision. Namespaced filler ids as filler:<ns>:<n> (n still resets per build; ns makes ids globally unique) and replaced the single _graph_row_ids list with _graph_rows_by_ns dict keyed by build ns. _commit_graph_items now takes ns and writes its ordered list under that key; _log_items threads ns through (_commits_root->'root', ref drill-down->the ref item's id). _next_non_filler scans across _graph_rows_by_ns.values() to find the one list owning the (globally-unique) filler id. _parse_id still maps filler:... to kind 'other' (inert) via first-':' partition. Tests updated to filler:<ns>:<n> shape; added cross-ref scenario (ns 'ref:A'/'ref:B' resolve to their OWN neighbour, no teleport) + concurrent-build coexistence. Build green; full suite 2890 OK (skipped=4); test_browse_git.py 115 passed. Not committed (leader commits). {#713:comment:706}

* ## Ticket: Epic: browse-md directory args + relative root labels {#714}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-04 08:56:40 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-04 09:12:06 UTC

  Improve the `browse-md` recipe (`recipes/browse-md`) with two behaviors:

  1. When the resolved file list spans more than one directory, label each
     per-file root relative to a single shared anchor instead of bare
     basename (which collides/ambiguates across dirs).
  2. Accept directory positionals (expand to the directory's markdown files,
     non-recursive) and treat no positionals as `browse-md .`.

  Decisions (settled with the user):
  * A1 — multi-dir labeling reuses the existing `_md_ref_label(abspath, cwd,
    project_root)` helper with ONE shared anchor pair: `cwd = os.getcwd()`,
    `project_root = md_doc.find_git_root(cwd) or cwd`. (git-root → cwd →
    `~`-collapse.)
  * B — relative labeling applies IFF the files are NOT all in the same
    directory. All-in-one-dir (incl. single file) keeps bare basename.
  * C — directory scan matches extensions `.md` and `.MD` only (exact case,
    those two literals).
  * D — if the aggregate resolved file list is empty, hard-error via `_die`.
  * E — files and directories may be mixed; dedup by abspath preserving
    first-seen order; sort each directory's matches alphabetically; NO
    anchors on directory positionals (the `#` split is file-only).

  All work happens in the worktree at ABSOLUTE path
  `/home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.worktrees/worktree-browse-md-dirs`.
  Tickets touch the same file (`recipes/browse-md`) so they run SEQUENTIALLY.
  Follow TDD: write failing tests first, then implement.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Relative per-file-root labeling {#715}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 08:56:40 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 08:59:25 UTC

    Add `_root_label_map(paths)` to `recipes/browse-md`:
    * `paths` = abs paths of the FINAL (post-expansion) file list.
    * `dirs = {os.path.dirname(p) for p in paths}`; if `len(dirs) <= 1`
      return `{p: os.path.basename(p)}` for each (preserves today's
      single-dir / single-file UX).
    * else compute `cwd = os.getcwd()`,
      `project_root = (md_doc.find_git_root(cwd) or cwd) if md_doc else cwd`,
      and return `{p: _md_ref_label(p, cwd, project_root)}`.

    Wire into `_reparse`: it already builds `input_files` at the top. Compute
    `labels = _root_label_map([p for p, _ in input_files])` once before the
    loop, and after each `file_root` is built set
    `file_root.title = labels[path]`. Leave `_build_nodes`'s
    `title=os.path.basename(path)` default unchanged (override afterward).

    TDD tests (test/unit/test_browse_md.py): same-dir → basenames; multi-dir
    → `_md_ref_label`-style relative labels; single file → basename. Add a
    focused test class. Run the file's existing tests to confirm no
    regression in root titles.

    * ## Comments {#715:comments}

      * Implemented _root_label_map(paths) in recipes/browse-md next to _md_ref_label: single-dir/single-file -> basenames, multi-dir -> _md_ref_label against cwd's git root (else cwd). Wired into _reparse: labels computed once before the per-file loop, file_root.title overridden after _build_nodes. _build_nodes basename default left unchanged. Added TDD test class TestRootLabelMap + a _reparse wiring test test_per_file_root_titles_relative_when_spanning_dirs in TestBuildMulti. Full file: 260 passed, 4 subtests passed. {#715:comment:719}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Directory args + no-args `.` default {#716}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 08:56:40 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 09:03:41 UTC

    Add to `recipes/browse-md`:
    * `_MD_EXTS = ('.md', '.MD')`
    * `_dir_md_files(dirpath)` — non-recursive listing of entries in
      `dirpath` whose extension is in `_MD_EXTS`, returned as abs paths
      sorted by name. (Use `os.scandir`/`os.listdir`; `os.path.splitext`
      for the ext.)
    * `_collect_input_files(positionals)` — for each raw positional:
      - if `os.path.isdir(os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(raw)))` →
        expand to `_dir_md_files`, each paired with empty anchor (NO `#`
        split for dirs).
      - else split once on `#` → `(file_part, anchor)`; abspath the
        file_part; if `os.path.isfile` → append `(path, anchor)`; else
        `_die('no such file or directory: <raw>', with_usage=False)`.
      - dedup by abspath, preserving first-seen order.
      - track "first anchor wins" across the whole expanded list (first
        non-empty anchor + its path).
      - if the final list is empty → `_die('no markdown files ...')`.
      - return `(input_files, first_anchor, first_anchor_path)`.

    Rework `main()`: after flag-popping and the leftover-option check, do
    `if not positionals: positionals = ['.']`, then replace the current
    inline isfile loop with a call to `_collect_input_files(positionals)`.
    The directory check runs on the raw token BEFORE any `#` split so a real
    file containing `#` is unaffected.

    TDD tests: `_dir_md_files` (filter to .md/.MD, sorted, non-recursive,
    ignores non-markdown + subdirs); `_collect_input_files` (dir expansion,
    mixed files+dirs, dedup, no-anchor-on-dir, first-anchor-wins, empty
    aggregate raises SystemExit, nonexistent path raises SystemExit). Use
    tmp dirs with fixture files.

    * ## Comments {#716:comments}

      * Implemented #716 via TDD. Added _MD_EXTS=('.md','.MD'), _dir_md_files(dirpath) (non-recursive os.scandir listing of .md/.MD files, abs paths sorted by basename), and _collect_input_files(positionals) (RAW-token isdir check -> _dir_md_files w/ empty anchor; else partition('#') split + isfile validate, _die('no such file or directory: <raw>', with_usage=False); dedup by abspath first-seen; first-anchor-wins; empty aggregate -> _die('no markdown files to browse')). Reworked main(): no-args now defaults positionals=['.'] (after the flag-pop/unrecognised-option check), and the inline positional loop is replaced by _collect_input_files(). Added TestDirMdFiles + TestCollectInputFiles. Updated 4 pre-existing argv tests for the new behavior (no-args now browses cwd; error message wording 'no such file or directory'). Suite: 269 passed. {#716:comment:720}

* ## Ticket: Epic: browse-tui bug fixes + features batch {#725}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-04 10:25:27 UTC

  Six items split across the engine (`src-tui/*.py`, concatenated by
  `build-tui.sh` into the `browse-tui` binary) and the recipes
  (`recipes/browse-claude`, `recipes/browse-git`, run via
  `browse-tui --run-py`).

  Build note: after editing `src-tui/*.py` you MUST run `./build-tui.sh`
  (or `./run-tests.sh`, which rebuilds). Recipe files ARE the source — no
  build step. Run `./run-tests.sh` (full suite, ~73s, 2943 tests) before
  closing any ticket; targeted unit tests with
  `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_NAME`.

  Subtasks are ordered for sequential execution (engine files
  `040-state.py` / `050-render.py` overlap heavily, so they go one at a
  time). All work happens in this worktree.

  * ## Ticket: browse-git: accept a repo directory on the command line {#726}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 06:36:28 UTC

    BUG 2. `browse-git /path/to/other/repo` fails — the recipe always runs
    git in the current working directory and never honors a supplied repo
    directory.

    Findings (file:line in `recipes/browse-git`):
    - `_run_git()` (~267-274) runs `git <args>` with no `-C` / no `cwd` —
      always the inherited cwd.
    - Precondition check in `main()` (~1544-1556) runs
      `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` against cwd BEFORE any argv
      parsing, so launching from outside a repo fails immediately.
    - `_classify_positionals()` (~1499-1527) treats an existing path as a
      pathspec filter (`_paths`), not a repo pointer — so a directory arg
      gets searched as a path inside the cwd repo.

    Goal: `browse-git <dir>` (where `<dir>` is, or is inside, a git repo
    that differs from cwd) browses THAT repo. A directory that is a
    pathspec inside the current repo must keep working as a filter (don't
    break the existing pathspec behavior).

    Suggested approach (pick the cleanest): before the precondition check,
    detect a leading directory positional that resolves to a git work tree
    (e.g. `git -C <dir> rev-parse --show-toplevel` succeeds), chdir into
    its toplevel, and remove that arg from argv so it isn't re-read as a
    pathspec. Keep all existing git invocations working via the new cwd
    (or thread a repo dir through `_run_git`). Distinguish "directory that
    is its own repo root" (→ browse it) from "subdir filter of the current
    repo" (→ leave as pathspec).

    Acceptance:
    - `browse-git /tmp/somerepo` (from an unrelated cwd) browses
      /tmp/somerepo. Verify headlessly / via a throwaway repo.
    - Existing pathspec-filter behavior (a path inside the current repo)
      still works.
    - Full test suite green; add a focused test if the recipe has a test
      harness for arg/repo resolution.

    * ## Comments {#726:comments}

      * Reproduced: from a non-repo cwd, 'browse-git <repo>' exits 2 with 'not inside a git work tree' (precondition check runs git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree in cwd before any argv parsing). Also confirmed even inside a repo, a directory arg pointing at another repo is treated as a pathspec filter in the cwd repo, not a repo pointer. {#726:comment:721}

      * Fix implemented in recipes/browse-git: added _pop_repo_dir() helper (mirrors _pop_mode/_pop_tree style) called in main() right after the git-on-PATH check and BEFORE the is-inside-work-tree precondition. It detects a leading directory positional, runs 'git -C <dir> rev-parse --show-toplevel'; if that succeeds AND differs from the cwd's toplevel (or cwd isn't a repo), it os.chdir() into the toplevel and removes the arg from argv so _classify_positionals doesn't re-read it as a pathspec. Same-repo subdir / after '--' / rev / plain non-repo dir all stay pathspecs (no chdir). Updated the --help Usage + auto-classify paragraph. Verified via tmux (lists both commits of the target repo from a non-repo cwd) and direct unit-style exercise of all 7 cases. {#726:comment:722}

      * Tests added: 7 unit tests in TestPopRepoDir (test/unit/test_browse_git.py) using real throwaway git repos covering chdir-from-non-repo, cross-repo redirect + trailing-arg preservation, same-repo-subdir stays pathspec, after-'--', leading flag, non-repo dir, leading rev. Plus 1 UI test test_repo_dir_arg_browses_that_repo (test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py) launching the binary from a non-repo cwd with the repo dir as arg. Full suite: Ran 2951 tests, 1 FAIL = test_suspend.test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid (SIGSTOP/SIGCONT timing-flaky, UNRELATED to this change; passes in isolation). All browse-git unit (155) + UI tests green. Changed files: recipes/browse-git, test/unit/test_browse_git.py, test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py. {#726:comment:723}

  * ## Ticket: browse-claude: rebind `h` to `.` and default to voice-only {#727}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 08:10:12 UTC

    FEATURE 1. Two changes in `recipes/browse-claude`.

    Change A — rebind hotkey. Line ~6318:
    `Action('h', 'Toggle voice-only filter', _action_toggle_filter, 'none')`
    → bind the SAME action to `.` (period) instead of `h`. `.` is currently
    unbound (no conflict); `h` is bound only here. Update help/usage text
    that mentions the `h` key: the `--no-show-all` blurb (~6158-6159) and
    the in-app key list (~6175) should say `.` instead of `h`.

    Change B — make voice-only the default. Module global at line ~3374:
    `_FILTER_VOICE_ONLY = False` → `True`. The existing CLI flag parsing
    (~6203-6209) already lets `--show-all` force it OFF and `--no-show-all`
    force it ON, so flipping the default keeps `--show-all` as the escape
    hatch and makes `--no-show-all` a redundant no-op. Update the
    usage/help text to describe the new default (voice-only on; `--show-all`
    to see everything).

    IMPORTANT — tests: flipping the default will break any test that boots
    browse-claude with no flag and expects all rows. Audit and update:
    - `test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py` (TestVoiceOnlyFilter, ~4957+)
    - `test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py` (e.g.
      `test_no_show_all_boots_in_voice_only` ~1185, flat/tree voice cases
      ~1359-1453)
    Add/adjust a test asserting the default boot is voice-only and that the
    `.` key toggles it (and that `h` no longer toggles).

    Acceptance:
    - Pressing `.` toggles voice-only; `h` does nothing.
    - Default launch (no flags) is voice-only; `--show-all` shows all rows.
    - Help text consistent. Full test suite green.

    * ## Comments {#727:comments}

        Change B: flipped module default _FILTER_VOICE_ONLY = False -> True. CLI parsing unchanged (--show-all forces OFF, --no-show-all redundant no-op forces ON); updated the flag-block comment + usage text to describe voice-only as default with --show-all as the escape hatch.

Used wait_stable/wait_for markers, no fixed sleeps (#724 style).

  * ## Ticket: Engine: make the info-bar `hint` a recipe-overridable variable {#728}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 08:21:00 UTC

    FEATURE 2. The hint line ` /:search  ?:help  q:quit ` is a hardcoded
    literal at `src-tui/050-render.py` ~line 2554 inside `render_info_bar`.
    Make it overridable with a PUBLIC get/set API.

    Required: a public getter and setter (the user explicitly wants get/set
    as public API). A constructor field is optional/nice-to-have. Follow the
    EXISTING `search_query` / `set_search_query` pattern:
    - Browser: private `self._hint` (default ` /:search  ?:help  q:quit `),
      `@property hint` getter, and thread-safe `set_hint(text)` that posts a
      mutation marking `self._needs_redraw.add('info')`.
      (search_query precedent: 040-state.py ~4450 getter, ~4460 setter.)
    - Context (060-context.py): pass-through `@property hint` and
      `set_hint(text)` (precedent ~250-256).
    - Optionally add `hint: str = ' /:search  ?:help  q:quit '` to
      BrowserConfig and assign in `__init__`.
    - Renderer (050-render.py ~2554): read
      `getattr(browser, 'hint', DEFAULT)` instead of the literal, with the
      same default as fallback so callers without a hint-aware browser still
      work. Keep the existing behavior where search/filter mode prompts
      override the hint.

    Name the accessor `hint` (the user's term), not `hint_text`.

    Acceptance:
    - `ctx.hint` reads it; `ctx.set_hint('...')` changes it and the info bar
      repaints. Default unchanged.
    - New tests (extend test/unit/test_search_api.py or a new
      test_hint_api.py) covering default, getter, setter+redraw signal, and
      ctx pass-through. Document in docs/api.md alongside search_query.
    - Build + full suite green.

    * ## Comments {#728:comments}

      * Implemented hint get/set API. Added DEFAULT_HINT constant + BrowserConfig.hint field + self._hint init in 040-state.py; Browser.hint getter and thread-safe Browser.set_hint (posts mutation, marks 'info' dirty). Context.hint/set_hint pass-throughs in 060-context.py. Renderer reads getattr(browser,'hint',DEFAULT_HINT) in 050-render.py. New test/unit/test_hint_api.py (10 tests). Render unit tests (test_render_cache, test_preview_render_cache, test_preview_tail) inject _render.DEFAULT_HINT=_state.DEFAULT_HINT (same idiom as _render.Mode). Docs in docs/api.md (ctx pass-through list + BrowserConfig field). {#728:comment:729}

  * ## Ticket: Engine bug: vertical-split children pane not cleared when it hides {#729}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 08:39:39 UTC

    BUG 3. In vertical split, when the children/preview pane toggles on/off
    — via hotkey, OR by moving the cursor between an entry WITH children and
    one WITHOUT — the pane fails to redraw: stale content stays on screen.

    Findings:
    - `mark_cursor_changed()` (040-state.py ~1887-1908) correctly marks
      'list'/'children'/'preview' dirty.
    - `render_partial()` (050-render.py ~2807-2909) only renders the
      children pane when `children_rect is not None` (~2843-2859). When the
      pane transitions visible→hidden (`children_rect` becomes None), nothing
      clears the rows it previously occupied, so old content persists.
    - Cache reconcile (`_reconcile_pane_caches` ~348-386) stamps a sentinel
      rect for the hidden pane but never clears the drawn rows; the
      diff/padding clear only fires when `end_row()` is called (which doesn't
      happen for a pane that isn't rendered).

    Goal: when a pane that was visible becomes hidden (geometry shrinks /
    pane disappears), its previously-drawn area is cleared. Prefer a
    targeted fix (clear the vacated rows on a visible→hidden transition);
    forcing a full repaint when pane visibility changes is acceptable if
    cleaner, but don't regress incremental-redraw performance broadly.

    REPRODUCE FIRST (systematic debugging): drive a headless Browser / pty
    so cursor moves between a branch row and a leaf row in vertical split and
    confirm stale pane content, then fix, then confirm cleared.

    Acceptance: toggling the pane (hotkey + cursor move across has-children
    boundary) leaves no stale content. Add a render test. Build + full suite
    green.

    * ## Comments {#729:comments}

      * REPRODUCED (red). Root cause: render_partial() only repaints the children pane when children_rect is not None; when a pane goes visible->hidden, nothing clears the rows it previously occupied. The vacated area is only cleared as an INCIDENTAL side-effect of a neighbouring pane (preview in 'v', list in 'm') repainting over it with full-width padding. On cursor-move this masks the bug (mark_cursor_changed marks list+children+preview dirty, so the covering pane repaints). But the update_data path (040-state.py ~4141) marks ONLY 'list'+'children' dirty (not 'preview'/'all'). So when an update_data op turns the cursor branch into a leaf (children pane vacates), the covering pane is NOT repainted and stale children rows remain. Confirmed via headless VT-grid harness: layouts v, pc, h all leave stale 'a1 a1xx'/'a2 a2yy' in the old children region; 'm' is incidentally clean (list pane, which IS dirty, expands over the rows). {#729:comment:730}

      * FIX (green). Added a visible->hidden clear to _reconcile_pane_caches() in src-tui/050-render.py: before calling cache.update_rect(rect), if the new layout rect is None and the cache's prior rect is a real Rect (isinstance(prev, Rect) excludes the disappeared-pane sentinel), blank the vacated rows via clear_columns(row, prev.left, prev.right) for each row in prev.top..prev.bottom. Single per-frame site that already owns the layout->cache mapping and runs inside begin_sync/end_sync for BOTH render_full and render_partial, so the fix is uniform across paths and layouts (v/m/pc/h). One-shot: once hidden the cache holds the sentinel (not a Rect) so the guard is False on steady frames -> no re-fire, incremental fast path preserved. {#729:comment:731}

        Regression tests: test/unit/test_render_cache.py::TestVacatedPaneClear (3 tests + a small _apply_to_grid VT helper, plus children-grid cross-ref wiring at module load). test_children_pane_cleared_when_branch_becomes_leaf FAILS before / PASSES after (stale 'a1xyz'/'a2xyz' in vacated children columns). Two fast-path guards (one-shot clear, no-clear-on-stable-branch) pass in both states.
        Verified: red->green on the headless harness for layouts v/pc/h (m was incidentally clean). Full suite: Ran 2967 tests in 72.3s, OK (skipped=4).

  * ## Ticket: Engine bug: no redraw after ctx.pick() exits {#719}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 08:51:24 UTC

    BUG 4. After `ctx.pick()` returns, the main UI fails to repaint properly.

    Findings:
    - `pick()` (060-context.py ~784-807, loop in `_pick_on_info_bar` ~935)
      sets `browser._needs_redraw.add('all')` on exit (~1073/1077).
    - Main loop (040-state.py ~6558): `while not self._quit_requested:` is
      checked BEFORE the render block (~6628-6635). If an action calls
      `ctx.pick()` then `ctx.quit()`, `_quit_requested` is already True at the
      next while-check, so the loop exits before the pending 'all' redraw
      renders — the picker overlay / stale frame is left on screen.

    Goal: a redraw requested before exit is honored; after pick() returns the
    UI repaints correctly whether or not the action also quits. Likely fix:
    render any pending `_needs_redraw` before breaking on quit (reorder so the
    render block runs before the quit-break), and/or ensure pick()'s restore
    forces a clean full repaint. Confirm bug 3's fix and this don't conflict
    in render_partial/full.

    REPRODUCE FIRST: use the pick demo recipe (test/ui/recipes/pick_demo.py)
    or a headless harness to drive an action that pick()s then returns (and a
    variant that pick()s then quit()s); confirm the missing/garbled repaint,
    fix, reconfirm.

    Acceptance: after pick() the screen is correct in both the
    return-to-UI and quit-after-pick cases. Add a test. Build + full suite
    green.

    * ## Comments {#719:comments}

      * Root cause (confirmed via tmux repro): the broken case is (a) pick()-then-RETURN-to-UI, NOT (b) pick()-then-quit. On quit, term_restore() emits \033[?1049l which discards the alt screen, so the overlay vanishes — case (b) is clean, and the main-loop quit-ordering hypothesis is a non-issue. {#719:comment:732}

        For case (a): _pick_on_info_bar draws its prompt + options with direct move/write/clear_columns, bypassing the per-pane row cache. On exit it only set _needs_redraw={'all'}; the next render_full then cache-HIT every list/preview/info row (identical buffered bytes + unchanged rect via end_row) and emitted nothing, leaving the whole picker overlay on screen.
        Two-part fix in _pick_on_info_bar._restore(): (1) browser._pane_cache.clear() so render_full re-emits unconditionally (same recovery as screen-lost/resume after external alt-screen overdraw); (2) blank the preview rect + info bar first, because a freshly-cleared cache takes end_row's 'first paint' branch which emits NO trailing clear (assumes blank cells below content) — without the pre-blank the picker's last option (e.g. a trailing 'wontfix' on a blank preview row) survived.
      * VERIFIED red->green. Regression tests added in test/ui/test_pick.py::TestPickRedrawOnExit (test_overlay_gone_after_cancel / _after_select) drive the new no-quit recipe test/ui/recipes/pick_noquit_demo.py. {#719:comment:733}

        RED (fix stashed, rebuilt): both new tests FAIL — 'Status>' prompt + all options ('open','in-progress','done','wontfix') still on screen, PREVIEW-LINE-ONE never reappears.
        GREEN (fix restored, rebuilt): all 6 pick UI tests pass.
        Full suite: ./run-tests.sh -> Ran 2969 tests in 72.7s, OK (skipped=4).
        Files changed: src-tui/060-context.py (fix in _pick_on_info_bar._restore), test/ui/test_pick.py (regression tests), test/ui/recipes/pick_noquit_demo.py (new recipe). No conflict with #729's _reconcile_pane_caches (freshly-cleared caches have prev=None, not a Rect, so the visible->hidden blanking doesn't misfire).

  * ## Ticket: Engine/recipe bug: fast-load leaves top-level preview blank {#720}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:28:58 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 10:22:32 UTC

    BUG 1. `browse-claude file.json` — when the data/preview becomes
    available very fast at launch, the topmost (file/root level) preview pane
    stays stuck blank and never fills in.

    Findings / hypothesis (needs confirmation via repro):
    - Startup `run()` (040-state.py ~6516-6546): posts initial fetch, calls
      `_update_preview_for_cursor()` (~6529), then `run_until_idle(~0.2)`
      (~6532), then first render (~6546).
    - browse-claude `get_preview()` returns a STREAMING generator; the
      framework pauses it at a line cap (`_stream_preview_from_generator`
      ~6178-6390, cap ~6225). `run_until_idle()` treats a paused generator as
      idle (~5299-5304), so the first render can happen with the generator
      paused mid-stream and no further demand signal — leaving the pane blank.
    - On the next loop, `_update_preview_for_cursor()` (~6709-6798) returns
      early when the cursor id is unchanged (~6756-6766) without re-checking a
      paused/empty preview, so it never recovers.
    - Possible synthetic-scope-item interaction: the top row is a synthetic
      scope item (visible_items ~1695-1707) whose preview cache may not carry
      over when promoted to the real item.

    REPRODUCE FIRST (systematic debugging): there are prior probes in
    `.scratch/` (preview_throttle_probe.py, confirm_preview_link.py,
    session_link_probe.py, repro_*.py) and a `.scratch/slow-preview-recipe` /
    `instrumented-browse-git`. Build a deterministic fast-load repro (a small
    .jsonl that delivers preview faster than the throttle), confirm the blank
    pane, then fix at the smallest correct point (e.g. send a preview-demand
    signal / re-evaluate paused preview on the first render, or don't treat a
    paused-but-empty preview as satisfied on the initial cursor update).

    Acceptance: launching browse-claude on a file (fast delivery) shows the
    top-level preview populated, not blank. Add a regression test using a
    headless/pty harness with fast delivery. Build + full suite green.

    * ## Comments {#720:comments}

      * ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (evidence via in-process repro of REAL recipe+engine, .scratch/widen_repro.py + instrumentation log). It is a RECIPE bug, not (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) engine race. On load browse-claude fires THREE competing cursor jumps for the scope_root open: {#720:comment:734}

          A. _focus_latest_voice_when_ready -> _chain_expand_then_cursor -> cursor_to(LATEST MESSAGE, e.g. <F>#188). This is the intended deep landing; it expands the ancestor umbrella #prompt:181 on the way.
          B1. on_expand hook for the ancestor #prompt:181 expansion -> _jump_to_latest_voice(#prompt:181) -> #188 (harmless, same target).
          B2. on_expand/on_children_loaded hook for the SCOPE_ROOT (<F>) expansion -> _jump_to_latest_voice(<F>) -> #prompt:181 (the TOP-LEVEL UMBRELLA).
        B2 competes with A. They race through the post queue. When B2's cursor_to(#prompt:181) resolves LAST, the cursor ends on the umbrella row, whose preview is a slow heavy STREAMING umbrella cascade. Until the first chunk lands the pane is blank -> the user sees 'stuck blank'. When loading is slow the timing differs and A's #188 (a fast plain-string message preview) tends to win/settle. 'fast=blank' = the deferred jumps fire early and B2 wins the cursor onto the not-yet-streamed umbrella.
        Evidence: instrumented log shows DO_CURSOR_TO #188 then DO_CURSOR_TO #prompt:181 (B2 last) -> REQUEST_PREVIEW #prompt:181 -> WORKER_FETCH #prompt:181 -> blank. Reproduced deterministically by delaying the umbrella generator's first chunk (>=0.3s) so the umbrella stays un-composed while the jump lands.
      * FIX + VERIFICATION (revised after discovering the first attempt was too broad). {#720:comment:735}

        FIX (recipes/browse-claude, +30 lines, engine untouched):
        - New module set _DEFERRED_FOCUS_SCOPE_ROOT. _focus_latest_voice_when_ready (the no---item deferred focus) adds target_jsonl to it BEFORE its b.expand(target_jsonl) fires.
        - _on_expand skips its single-level _jump_to_latest_voice for ids in that set (the cross-file preview upgrade still runs). So the scope_root's startup expand no longer fires the competing top-level-umbrella jump; the deep-landing focus (-> latest MESSAGE) wins. With --item (no deferred focus) the set is empty, so the scope_root keeps its prior latest-voice landing.
        WHY NOT the first attempt (skip when id == ctx.scope): that suppressed the scope_root jump UNCONDITIONALLY, which broke the --item flat-mode landing that the SendMessage round-trip UI tests rely on (with --item 0 the cursor is still expected to land on the latest voice via the on_expand scope_root jump). The ownership-set approach only steps aside for the no---item focus path.
        REGRESSION TESTS (test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py, TestSubagentUmbrellaVoice, deterministic, no sleeps/threads — drive _on_expand directly with a real 2-turn fixture + fake ctx):
        - test_on_expand_does_not_jump_on_scope_root: scope_root claimed in _DEFERRED_FOCUS_SCOPE_ROOT -> _on_expand must NOT cursor_to (still invalidates preview, doesn't park). RED before fix (jumped to #prompt:2), GREEN after.
        - test_on_expand_scope_root_jumps_when_focus_not_active: set empty (--item path) -> scope_root STILL jumps to latest voice (guards the flat-mode/SendMessage behavior).
        - test_on_expand_still_jumps_on_non_scope_umbrella: a non-claimed umbrella still jumps.
        VERIFICATION:
        - Unit RED->GREEN: neutralizing the _on_expand skip makes test_on_expand_does_not_jump_on_scope_root fail (cursor_to=['...#prompt:2']); restoring -> all green.
        - In-process repro (real recipe+engine, umbrella first-chunk delay to widen the jump-vs-stream window): clean recipe reproduced the cursor stranding on #prompt:181 with preview=None (8 stuck across a delay sweep at one point); with the fix the cursor lands on #188 every time, 0 stuck. (The in-process race is timing-sensitive — the unit test is the deterministic guard.)
        - Previously-broken SendMessage UI tests now pass; tree-flag latest-voice test passes.
        - Full suite: Ran 2972 tests in 73.0s, OK (skipped=4). Engine source + browse-tui binary unchanged from HEAD; .scratch cleaned.
      * CONSUME-ONCE follow-up (code-review finding): _DEFERRED_FOCUS_SCOPE_ROOT was add-only, leaking for the process lifetime. Reachable misfire: in a no---item session, after a manual collapse + re-expand of the scope_root row (nav-left drops it from expanded, nav-right -> ctx.expand fires _on_expand([scope_root]) again), the bare 'continue' would wrongly suppress the legitimate drill-in jump-to-latest-voice forever. (Re-scope / t-toggle are safe — the framework re-baselines _last_expanded.) {#720:comment:736}

        Fix (recipes/browse-claude, _on_expand): change the skip to discard-then-continue so only the FIRST (startup) expand of that scope_root is suppressed:
            if id in _DEFERRED_FOCUS_SCOPE_ROOT:
                _DEFERRED_FOCUS_SCOPE_ROOT.discard(id)
                continue
        _invalidate_if_cross_file still runs before the skip. Add-before-the-owning-expand ordering guarantees the id is present when the startup _on_expand runs, so the startup jump stays suppressed; consuming here is robust whether _fire proceeds or cancels (both ride the same b.expand(target_jsonl) resolution).
        New regression test (TestSubagentUmbrellaVoice.test_on_expand_scope_root_claim_is_consumed_once): register sess in _DEFERRED_FOCUS_SCOPE_ROOT; 1st _on_expand([sess]) -> no jump AND sess gone from the set; 2nd _on_expand([sess]) -> latest-voice jump NOW fires. RED before discard (assertNotIn 'claim must be consumed' fails — sess still in set), GREEN after. The original test_on_expand_does_not_jump_on_scope_root still passes under the bare-continue regression (it only checks the first expand), so the new test specifically pins the leak.
        Verification: full module test_browse_claude_render OK (365); full suite Ran 2973 tests OK (skipped=4). Engine source + browse-tui binary unchanged from HEAD.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Fix flaky tests (suspend test + any others encountered) {#724}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-04 06:39:40 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-04 07:51:38 UTC

    During this batch the full suite intermittently fails on
    `test/ui/test_suspend.py::TestSuspend::test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid`
    — it passes in isolation but fails in a full run, i.e. a timing/ordering
    flake (SIGSTOP/SIGCONT race), NOT a regression from our changes.

    Goal: make the suspend test (and any other test that flakes during this
    work) DETERMINISTIC — fix the root cause, don't just retry/skip. Typical
    fixes: replace fixed `sleep()`s with poll-until-condition waits;
    synchronize on an observable state change (process state in /proc, output
    marker, pid file) instead of wall-clock timing; ensure SIGCONT is only
    sent after the child is confirmed stopped, and assertions only run after
    the child confirms it resumed.

    Steps:
    - Reproduce the flake (run the full suite and/or the suspend test in a
      tight loop, e.g. `for i in $(seq 1 50); do python3 -m unittest
      test.ui.test_suspend.TestSuspend.test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid
      || break; done`, and also under the parallel runner
      `./run-tests-parallel.sh` if that's where it shows up).
    - Find the race in test/ui/test_suspend.py (and the suspend handling it
      exercises). Make the test wait on real state transitions.
    - If you encounter OTHER flaky tests while running the suite during this
      batch, fix them here too (or create sibling tickets under #725 and note
      them).

    Acceptance: the suspend test passes reliably across many consecutive runs
    (e.g. 50x loop green) and in the full suite; no `sleep`-based timing
    assumptions left that cause the flake. Document the root cause in a ticket
    comment.

    * ## Comments {#724:comments}

      * ROOT CAUSE (test/ui/test_suspend.py::test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid): {#724:comment:725}

        Reproduced ~6% in isolation (3/50, 4/40). On failure the process is stuck in ps STAT 'T<l' (STOPPED) — it never resumed after SIGCONT. /proc shows SigPnd=0 (SIGCONT WAS delivered and consumed) yet State=T (stopped) again.
        The flake is a SIGTTOU re-stop race. The test launches a pipeline 'printf ... | python3 ...', so browse-tui's process group (pgid = the now-exited printf leader) is NOT the terminal's foreground process group — at the time the test runs, bash is foreground (verified: pgid != tpgid; tpgid points at bash). The test then sends a BARE os.kill(pid, SIGCONT) to that *background* group. SIGCONT briefly continues the process; its _handle_sigcont() (src-tui/020-terminal.py:412) calls _enter_raw() which does tcsetattr (tty.setraw) + writes escape sequences to the controlling tty. A background-group process doing terminal output gets SIGTTOU (default disposition = stop). python3 neither ignores nor blocks SIGTTOU (verified SigIgn=0x01001000 = SIGPIPE+SIGXFSZ only), so the kernel RE-STOPS it -> stays 'T' -> test times out polling for 'S'.
        This is expected kernel job-control behavior for resuming a background group via bare SIGCONT, not an engine regression. The original step 3 (t.fg() then wait_for prompt) only 'passed' because the double-SIGCONT path usually crashed the program with termios.error EINTR on tcsetattr (verified traceback), which happened to return to the bash prompt — i.e. the test was asserting on accidental/crash behavior.
        Direct-SIGSTOP-then-fg-resume also leaves the TUI wedged (resumes to 'S<l+' foreground but never repaints and never reads keys; ^L and q echo as literal text) — a known unsupported path (external SIGSTOP freezes mid-select with the tty in raw mode and no handler). The clean, supported suspend/resume path (Ctrl-Z + fg) is covered by test_ctrl_z_then_fg_round_trip, which is rock solid (30/30).
      * FIX (suspend): rewrote test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid to resume via bash job control (t.fg()) instead of a bare os.kill(pid, SIGCONT). fg() makes the job's process group the terminal's foreground group BEFORE delivering SIGCONT, so _handle_sigcont's tcsetattr/terminal writes are legal and never draw SIGTTOU -> no re-stop race. Added a _wait_state(pid, accept, timeout) poll helper (synchronises on the kernel-reported process state, not a sleep) and accept any running state ('SRD'), not just 'S'. Dropped the old step-2 bare-SIGCONT poll-for-'S' and the step-3 interactive-q assertion (the latter only 'passed' via an EINTR crash on tcsetattr; direct-SIGSTOP resume leaves the TUI wedged, a known unsupported path). The clean Ctrl-Z+fg path is fully covered by test_ctrl_z_then_fg_round_trip (30/30). Verified: 50x green in isolation, 60x green under concurrent UI-suite load, full module 30x green. {#724:comment:726}

        OTHER FLAKES FIXED in test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py (surfaced under the parallel runner / heavy concurrency this batch):
        1. test_t_toggle_preserves_cursor — had time.sleep(0.15) after each 't' toggle then capture(). The fixed sleep masked a real async-child-load race: on flat->tree the expanded turn-root briefly shows '⧗ loading…' before PROBE_TURN2_REPLY renders. Reproduced at ~13% (4/30). Fixed: synchronise on a MODE-SPECIFIC marker absent in the other mode ('[Explore' for flat, '<prompt>' for tree -> proves the toggle was processed, not a stale frame) THEN wait_for('PROBE_TURN2_REPLY') (proves the async child-load finished). Removed the local 'import time'. Verified 40x isolated + 30x under load green.
        2. test_flat_sendmessage_round_trip_renders — after K, did cap2 = wait_for('→ PROBE_WORKER_7'), but that string is also in the always-present list row '[agent-send] 🔧 SendMessage(→ PROBE_WORKER_7: …)', so it matched before the preview rendered, then asserted PROBE_SEND_BODY (preview-only) was present -> flaked under load. Fixed: wait_for('PROBE_SEND_BODY') (lives only in the SendMessage message field -> preview-only), then assert the '→ PROBE_WORKER_7' header is in that same capture. Verified 30x under heavy CPU load green.
        NOT changed (deliberately): test_K_scrolls_viewport_to_keep_cursor_on_screen and test_K_backward_from_collapsed_tail_lands_on_last_voice timed out only under 8x CPU oversubscription (8 concurrent full-module runs on 8 cores); they use correct wait_for polling on the right markers and pass at the runner's real concurrency. Their 'failure' is genuine work not finishing within 3s under 2x oversubscription, not a logic race — bumping timeouts would chase an artificial bar.
        VERIFICATION SUMMARY:
        - suspend 50x isolated: 50/50; 60x under concurrent UI suite: 60/60; module 30x: 30/30
        - t_toggle 40x isolated + 30x under load: green (was ~13% flaky)
        - sendmessage round-trip 30x under heavy load: green
        - ./run-tests.sh full suite: Ran 2951 tests — OK (skipped=4)
        - ./run-tests-parallel.sh: -j4 12/12 + 6/6 clean; -j8 6/6 clean (was ~1/8 flaky on test_recipe_browse_claude)
        FILES CHANGED: test/ui/test_suspend.py, test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py (test-only; no src-tui changes, no rebuild needed).

* ## Ticket: browse-claude: real scope-root session item on direct launch (fix blank top preview) {#730}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-04 11:50:38 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-04 12:06:40 UTC

  Follow-up to #720. CONFIRMED root cause (live trace on the user's file):
  on `browse-claude <file.jsonl>` (direct launch / scope-into-session), the
  TOP scope-root row is the engine's SYNTHETIC placeholder —
  `synthetic=True, boundary=False, kind=None` (engine creates it at
  `src-tui/040-state.py:1724` in `visible_items` when the real scope item
  isn't in `_items_by_id`). browse-claude's `_is_cross_file_id` trusts the
  loaded item's `boundary` attribute, so it returns False for the
  placeholder → `_get_preview_impl` falls through to `return ''` → the
  topmost (file-level) preview is permanently BLANK (Ctrl-R can't recover —
  the item stays synthetic). The id-shape fallback (bare `.jsonl` ⇒
  cross-file ⇒ umbrella) only runs when NO item is loaded, which is why a
  slower load papers over it ("blank only when it loads too fast"). #720
  fixed the deep cursor landing (that works — `#188` preview renders);
  this is a DISTINCT, pre-existing bug.

  browse-claude's real session Item (rich title/tag/scope_title +
  `boundary=True`, kind='session') is only built when listing a PROJECT's
  sessions (recipe ~633-661) — which never runs on direct launch.

  FIX B (primary): browse-claude supplies a REAL scope-root session Item on
  direct launch so the engine never synthesizes a placeholder. The real
  item must be present in the browser's item index for the INITIAL scope
  before the first `visible_items`/preview pass, so `_find_item` /
  `_items_by_id.get(scope_root_id)` returns it (engine
  `src-tui/040-state.py:1719-1730`). Reuse the existing session-item shape
  (id=path, kind='session', boundary=True, scope_title=full path, tag with
  msg/sub counts + mtime — mirror the builder at recipe ~633-658). Cover
  the launch shapes that scope into a session: a `.jsonl` file argument
  (and the project-dir case if it also scopes to a session). Investigate
  the cleanest registration path (e.g. seeding `_items_by_id` at startup /
  an init hook / the push API) and match existing recipe conventions; don't
  invent new engine API if a recipe-side seed suffices.

  FIX A (safety net, one line): in `_is_cross_file_id`, treat a SYNTHETIC
  placeholder (`getattr(item,'synthetic',False)`) like "item not loaded" so
  the id-shape fallback applies (bare `.jsonl` / `#agent:` ⇒ cross-file).
  Keeps the preview correct even if a synthetic placeholder ever appears.
  (User: "if B is done A isn't needed, but it's a cheap just-in-case.")

  REPRODUCE FIRST (systematic debugging, red→green): drive the example file
  `/home/ubuntu/sandvault/.claude/projects/-home-ubuntu-sandvault-src-browse-tui/6f491247-ce6c-4a25-8932-53dc336b2588.jsonl`
  via the pty/tmux harness (test/ui/fixtures/) or a headless Browser, and
  assert the scope-root top-row preview is BLANK (preview None/empty)
  before, and shows the umbrella cascade after. If engine-level
  instrumentation helps, a saved debug patch is at
  `.scratch/debug-instrumentation-720.patch` (apply → `./build-tui.sh` →
  capture via BROWSE_TUI_DEBUG=<path> → revert; do NOT commit it).

  Acceptance:
  - On direct launch (fast AND slow), the topmost scope-root row shows the
    session umbrella preview, not blank.
  - Scope header is a real item on direct launch (B): not synthetic; has a
    proper title/tag. Verify the engine no longer synthesizes a placeholder
    for the initial scope.
  - Regression test (deterministic, marker/poll waits — no fixed sleeps)
    that fails before and passes after.
  - Build + full suite green.

  * ## Comments {#730:comments}

    * DONE. CONFIRMED root cause (reproduced deterministically): on direct launch (browse-claude <file.jsonl> / --file / --pid), main() scopes straight into target_jsonl before its parent dir is listed, so the scope-root row has no Item in _items_by_id. visible_items (040-state.py:1699-1706) synthesizes a placeholder (synthetic=True, boundary=False, kind=None, raw-path title). _is_cross_file_id trusted the placeholder's boundary=False -> _get_preview_impl fell to 'return '' -> top preview permanently BLANK. (Distinct from #720's deep-cursor-landing fix.) {#730:comment:731}

      REPRODUCED: (1) headless in-process drive of the REAL recipe+engine startup on a synthetic .jsonl -> top row synthetic=True, preview len=0 [BLANK]. (2) Real example file via pty grid: navigate to top scope-root row (g/k) -> preview region 0 nonblank lines [BLANK], row shows raw path. AFTER fix both show the umbrella cascade + a rich '[N msg ...]' tag on the header row.

Builds the rich session Item via the new shared _session_item() helper (extracted from _list_sessions so there's ONE source of truth) and registers it DIRECTLY into b._state._items_by_id + _parent_of_id + _children[parent_scope]. Direct (not update_data) is deliberate: update_data's _apply snapshots visible_items BEFORE applying ops, which would synthesize the stub first and then only patch it (leaving synthetic=True). Synchronous-before-run() guarantees the real Item is indexed ahead of the first visible_items pass, so no placeholder is ever synthesized -> header row is a real item (title=sid, scope_title=full path, tag='N msg ...', boundary=True, kind='session'). The eventual lazy _list_sessions(parent_scope) rebuilds the identical item under the same id (transient seed, reconciled, not a dup).
      - FIX A (one-line safety net): _is_cross_file_id now treats a synthetic placeholder like 'item not loaded' (the loaded-item branch gates on ), so the id-shape fallback (bare .jsonl / #agent: => cross-file) applies and the preview stays correct even if a synthetic stub ever appears.

FAILS on HEAD recipe (row = raw path, no ' msg' tag -> wait_for times out) and PASSES with the fix.
      Full suite: Ran 2989 tests in 72.8s, OK (skipped=4). Targeted: test_browse_claude_render 365 OK; test_recipe_browse_claude 36 OK. Scratch cleaned (only debug-instrumentation-720.patch left, untouched).
    * DONE. CONFIRMED root cause (reproduced deterministically): on direct launch (browse-claude FILE.jsonl / --file / --pid), main() scopes straight into target_jsonl before its parent dir is listed, so the scope-root row has no Item in _items_by_id. visible_items (040-state.py:1699-1706) synthesizes a placeholder (synthetic=True, boundary=False, kind=None, raw-path title). _is_cross_file_id trusted the placeholder boundary=False, so _get_preview_impl fell to return '' -> top preview permanently BLANK. Distinct from #720 (deep-cursor-landing). {#730:comment:732}

* ## Ticket: Epic: browse-fs & browse-md recipe label/preview fixes {#733}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-05 03:10:10 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-05 03:17:52 UTC

  Two independent recipe bug fixes. Both are small, focused changes to a
  single function (plus docstrings + new tests) and each gets a review
  subagent. TDD: write the failing test first, then the minimal fix.

  * ## Ticket: browse-fs: typed directory preview (dirs-first + "/" suffix) {#734}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 03:10:10 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 03:11:35 UTC

    **Bug.** `recipes/browse-fs` `get_preview(path)` for a directory does
    `'\n'.join(sorted(os.listdir(path))[:200])` — a raw, untyped listing:
    no dirs-first ordering and no trailing-slash suffix on directories. It
    is inconsistent with the children view (`get_children`), which sorts
    `(not _is_dir(d), d.name.lower())` (directories first, case-insensitive)
    and renders each title as `d.name + ('/' if is_dir else '')`.

    **Goal.** Make the directory preview match the children view: directories
    first, then files, sorted case-insensitively by name, with a `/` suffix
    on directory names. Keep the existing ~200-entry cap.

    **Where.** `recipes/browse-fs`, the `os.path.isdir(path)` branch of
    `get_preview` (around lines 100-101). Reuse the existing module-level
    `_is_dir(dirent)` helper for both the sort key and the suffix so the
    ordering/suffix logic is the single source of truth shared with
    `get_children`. A small named helper (e.g. `_dir_listing(path)`) built on
    `os.scandir` + `_is_dir` is fine; keep it minimal — do NOT re-implement
    the full columnar `fs_row_content` (perms/size/mtime); this is just the
    plain-text preview pane, only ordering + the `/` suffix change.

    **Tests.** Add a unit test in `test/unit/test_browse_fs.py` over a temp
    dir mixing files and subdirs (incl. mixed case to prove case-insensitive
    sort) asserting: directories come first, each dir line ends in `/`, files
    have no suffix, ordering is case-insensitive. Write it failing first.

    **Notes.** Update the `get_preview` / module docstring wording that
    describes the directory preview as a bare `os.listdir`. Run
    `python -m pytest test/unit/test_browse_fs.py` (and the browse-fs UI test
    if quick) before closing.

    * ## Comments {#734:comments}

      * Wrote failing test TestGetPreviewDir.test_dirs_first_with_slash_suffix_case_insensitive (mixed-case dirs Bravo/alpha + files Zeta.txt/apple.txt); confirmed it failed against raw os.listdir (got ['Bravo','Zeta.txt','alpha','apple.txt']). Fix: added _dir_listing(path) built on os.scandir + _is_dir reusing the same (not _is_dir, name.lower()) sort key and '/' suffix as get_children, 200-entry cap; get_preview dir branch now calls it. Updated module docstring (os.listdir -> dirs-first directory listing). Final: 8 passed in 0.05s {#734:comment:736}

  * ## Ticket: browse-md: consistent general labelling for top-level items {#735}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 03:10:10 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 03:14:37 UTC

    **Bug.** `recipes/browse-md` `_root_label_map(paths)` special-cases the
    common "all inputs share one directory" case to bare basenames
    (`{p: os.path.basename(p) ...}` when `len(dirs) <= 1`), and only falls
    back to project-root-relative labels (`_md_ref_label`) when inputs span
    more than one directory. But auto-discovered markdown *links* are ALWAYS
    labeled relative to the auto-discovered project root via `_md_ref_label`.
    So top-level rows and link rows label the same file inconsistently when
    all top-level files happen to be in one directory.

    **Goal.** Make labelling consistent: always use the general
    project-root-relative labelling (`_md_ref_label` against cwd's git root,
    else cwd) for top-level items too — drop the `len(dirs) <= 1` basename
    short-circuit. A file directly in the project root still renders as its
    bare basename naturally (relpath from root), so the common case is
    unchanged; a file in a subdir now shows its subdir-relative path,
    matching how links to the same file are labeled.

    **Where.** `recipes/browse-md`, `_root_label_map` (around lines 729-748).
    Reduce it to the relative branch only (compute `cwd` + `project_root`,
    return `{p: _md_ref_label(p, cwd, project_root) for p in paths}`). Keep
    the `md_doc`-unavailable fallback (`project_root = cwd`).

    **Tests.** Add a unit test in `test/unit/test_browse_md.py` for
    `_root_label_map`: with multiple files in a single subdir under a git
    root, assert labels are project-root-relative (include the subdir), NOT
    bare basenames; with a file directly in the root, assert it is still the
    bare basename. Write it failing first (the current single-dir branch
    returns basenames).

    **Notes.** Update the now-stale docstrings describing the single-dir
    basename behavior: `_root_label_map`'s own docstring, the `_reparse`
    inline comment near the `labels = _root_label_map(...)` call, and the
    module docstring paragraph (lines ~12-19) that says "Each top-level row
    is normally labeled by its bare basename ... when the final file list
    spans more than one directory the labels go relative". Run
    `python -m pytest test/unit/test_browse_md.py` before closing.

    * ## Comments {#735:comments}

      * Implemented #735: _root_label_map now always uses project-root-relative labels via _md_ref_label (dropped the len(dirs)<=1 basename short-circuit). Updated module docstring, _reparse inline comment, and the helper docstring. Tests: rewrote TestRootLabelMap (added test_single_subdir_uses_root_relative_not_basename which fails against old code; kept root-relative/match_md_ref_label cases) and updated TestBuildMulti.test_per_file_root_titles_are_basenames -> test_per_file_root_titles_match_md_ref_label. Final pytest: 269 passed, 4 subtests passed in 0.64s. {#735:comment:737}

* ## Ticket: Epic: Meta rows — non-content rows the cursor skips {#758}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-05 05:08:20 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-05 08:15:15 UTC

  Implement the `meta` row primitive per
  docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-meta-rows-design.md. A recipe marks a row
  `meta=True`: the cursor skips it (best-effort, preventive, unified
  keyboard+mouse), it is never selectable, and it is excluded from search/filter
  by default. Rows may carry raw ANSI-string content (any row). browse-git's
  filler workaround is deleted; browse-claude gains orphaned-subagent dividers.

  Constraints: all work in the worktree at absolute path
  /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/meta-rows . Build with
  ./build-tui.sh, test with ./run-tests.sh. Baseline is 2989 tests, 0 failures,
  4 skipped — keep the suite green at every ticket. String IDs only (the
  hashable-ID idea is a separate epic, out of scope here).

  * ## Ticket: Data model: Item.meta flag {#759}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 05:08:20 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 05:13:40 UTC

    Spec section 1. Add `meta: bool = False` to the Item dataclass
    (src-tui/030-data.py) after `boundary`, with a docstring paragraph in the
    hidden/boundary style: meta=True is a non-content row — cursor skips it,
    never selectable, excluded from search/filter, has_children ignored. Add
    'meta' to BOTH `known` field-sets in to_item (the dataclass-fields set near
    line 197 and the dict-extras set near line 240) so dict/Item coercion accepts
    it.
    Tests (test/unit/test_item.py): to_item accepts meta via dict and via Item;
    default is False; meta is a declared field so it does not leak through
    _item_extras. Build + targeted tests green.

      * Implemented Item.meta flag (spec §1). Added 'meta: bool = False' to the Item dataclass in src-tui/030-data.py right after 'boundary', with a docstring paragraph in the hidden/boundary style (cursor skips it best-effort, never selectable, excluded from search/filter, has_children ignored). Added 'meta' to the known dataclass-field set in to_item's dict branch (030-data.py). Added 6 tests to test/unit/test_item.py: meta default False + settable (construction); to_item accepts meta via dict + default False via dict; passthrough Item preserves meta; and meta is a declared field so it does NOT leak through _item_extras (new TestItemExtras class + dict-coercion assertion). Targeted module 34->40 tests, all green. Full ./run-tests.sh: Ran 2996 tests, OK (skipped=4). {#759:comment:743}

  * ## Ticket: Visible tree: emit kind='meta' {#760}

        status: done

    kind='meta' when getattr(child,'meta',False) else 'normal'. Meta rows are
