      * Implemented spec section 2 (visible tree emit kind='meta'). {#760:comment:744}

        Changes:
        - src-tui/040-state.py _emit_children (~1758): compute is_meta = getattr(child,'meta',False); kind = 'meta' if is_meta else 'normal'. Recursion guard short-circuits on is_meta so a meta row is ALWAYS a leaf — never recursed into and never emits a 'pending' placeholder, even with has_children=True and/or id in state.expanded.
        - src-tui/040-state.py VisibleEntry.kind docstring (~297): documented the 'meta' value (non-content leaf, cursor-skipped, never selectable).
        _PROMOTION_DATA_FIELDS finding (verified, NO CHANGE NEEDED): 'meta' is deliberately NOT added. grep confirms the ONLY synthetic-stub creation site is visible_items (~1711, scope_item.synthetic=True), always fabricated with has_children=True and rendered kind 'normal'. _promote_synthetics only promotes when an incoming item's id matches an existing synthetic stub; a meta divider is an ordinary leaf from get_children that is never scoped into, so it can never be that incoming item. Nothing to propagate onto. Added an explanatory comment above _PROMOTION_DATA_FIELDS recording this.
        Tests: test/unit/test_visible_tree.py new TestMetaKind (6 tests): meta leaf -> kind='meta'; meta+has_children not recursed; meta forced into expanded set not recursed; meta expanded emits no pending placeholder; mixed normal/meta sibling ordering preserved; meta divider nested under an expanded normal parent (correct depth/kind, normal siblings still recurse).
        Full suite via ./run-tests.sh: Ran 3002 tests, OK (skipped=4). 0 failures (was 2996 after #759; +6 new).

  * ## Ticket: Cursor skip: unified _resolve_landing + rewire move sites {#736}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 05:08:20 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 05:31:39 UTC

    Spec sections 3.1-3.3. Add _next_landable(vis, start, direction) (linear
    scan, inclusive; landable = kind != 'meta') and _resolve_landing(vis, target,
    before) (direction = sign(target-before), tie down; scan, fall back reverse;
    None when no landable row). Route every move through the resolver:
    _nav_down/up/pgdn/pgup (src-tui/070-actions.py:101-167), _nav_home/end (:118),
    _click_list_row (:1770), _select_toggle_down/up (:502,526). cursor_to (API)
    stays EXACT — no skip. Landing is tolerated, not an error.
    Tests (test/ui/test_navigation.py, test/unit/test_actions.py): arrows+page
    skip one meta and a short run; home/end reach first/last landable; click onto
    meta skips in the click's direction; space/alt-space skip meta; cursor_to onto
    a meta id lands exactly; an all-meta list parks without crashing.

    * ## Comments {#736:comments}

      * Implemented unified meta-skip resolver. {#736:comment:745}

        Helpers (src-tui/040-state.py, just before mark_cursor_changed — alongside the visible-tree/cursor-move helpers):
        - _next_landable(vis, start, direction): inclusive linear scan for first kind!='meta' row; None off the end.
        - _resolve_landing(vis, target, before): direction=+1 if target>=before else -1 (tie→down); scan, fall back reverse; None when no landable row.
        Move sites rewired (src-tui/070-actions.py) via a shared _move_cursor_to(browser, target) that resolves target vs current cursor and only sets cursor + mark_cursor_changed when the index actually changes (None or same-index = park/no-op):
        - _nav_down/_nav_up/_nav_pgdn/_nav_pgup: compute clamped target, route through _move_cursor_to (clamp preserved by the pre-call bounds computation/guard).
        - _nav_home/_nav_end: target 0 / len-1; resolver's reverse-fallback lands first/last landable; PIN_FIRST/PIN_LAST still set; mark only on real change. (Note: PIN re-snap onto a leading/trailing meta is the separate #3.4 anchor work; _reanchor_cursor converts the pin to an id-anchor on the landed row immediately after, so the move result holds.)
        - _click_list_row: target=clicked idx through _move_cursor_to (skip in click's travel direction); dropped the now-redundant cursor!=new_idx guard.
        - _select_toggle_down/up: toggle the landable cursor row as today, then route the STEP through _move_cursor_to (now also fires mark_cursor_changed so the moved-to item's children/preview refresh — old code under-redrew).
        - cursor_to (Context.cursor_to / _do_cursor_to): UNCHANGED — lands on the meta row exactly.
        Edge cases: empty/all-meta parks (None); down-arrow with only meta below resolves back to current index → true no-op (no spurious _cursor_change_pending).
        Tests: test/unit/test_actions.py TestMetaRowSkip (arrows single+run both dirs, page, home/end at edges, select-toggle down/up, cursor_to-lands-exactly, all-meta parks, down-only-meta-below no-op asserts _cursor_change_pending stays False) + 2 click-skip cases in TestMouseDispatch; test/ui/test_navigation.py TestMetaRowSkip (tmux end-to-end Down/Up over a run, Home/End at edges) with new recipe test/ui/recipes/meta_rows.py. Wired _actions._resolve_landing=_state._resolve_landing into all 14 loader test files (same convention as mark_cursor_changed).
        Full suite: Ran 3021 tests, OK (skipped=4), 0 failures.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Clamp / anchor / delete-displacement + on_empty {#737}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 05:08:20 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 06:01:01 UTC

    Spec section 3.4. Route the cursor clamps (initial / post-refresh, near
    040-state.py:4130,5259,5971) and the re-anchor/displacement path
    (_compute_anchor_snapshot :6950, _reanchor_cursor :7005,
    _apply_hide_displacement :7038, _apply_cursor_anchor :7097) replacement index
    through _resolve_landing (before = pre-change cursor) so they land on a
    landable row, skipping an adjacent meta row. Add BrowserConfig.on_empty =
    'wait' (default) | 'exit': when no landable row exists, park ('wait') or quit
    with the cancel code ('exit').
    This is the scroll-geometry-sensitive ticket: verify _snap_list_scroll_to_row
    (:6916) and _active_list_row (:6937) stay consistent when the cursor's
    landable row and viewport math diverge.
    Tests: deleting the row under the cursor when a meta row is adjacent
    re-anchors onto a landable row; a refresh that shrinks the list snaps off a
    meta row; on_empty 'exit' quits on an all-meta list, 'wait' parks.

    * ## Comments {#737:comments}

      * Implemented clamp/anchor/displacement landability + on_empty (spec 3.4). {#737:comment:746}

        PRODUCTION (src-tui/040-state.py):
        - BrowserConfig.on_empty='wait'(default)|'exit'; validated in __init__ (like show_ids); stored as self._on_empty.
        - New _handle_no_landable(): on_empty=='exit' -> _do_quit(1,'') (cancel code). No-op for 'wait' (park).
        - New _clamp_cursor_landable(before): backstop after _apply_cursor_anchor. Past-end cursor -> _resolve_landing(len-1, before) scans up off trailing meta. In-range-on-meta -> snap to landable UNLESS genuinely anchored there (see distinction below). No-landable (empty/all-meta) -> park + _handle_no_landable. Fires mark_cursor_changed only on actual index move.
        - 3 clamp sites (update_data._apply, apply_children_results, _settle_children_empty) replaced the (cursor>=len?->len-1:0) block with _clamp_cursor_landable(cursor).
        - _apply_cursor_anchor: PIN_FIRST -> _next_landable(0,+1); PIN_LAST -> _next_landable(len-1,-1); all-meta pin -> _handle_no_landable. Tier-0 (primary) honored EXACTLY (even meta). Tier>=1 (fallback) routed through _resolve_landing.
        - _reanchor_cursor: PIN survival check now compares cursor against the landable pin target (not raw 0/len-1) so a pin parked past a leading/trailing meta isn't spuriously dropped.
        - _apply_hide_displacement: walk-back replacement index (and row-0 fallback) routed through _resolve_landing(before=pre_cursor, scans up); all-meta-surviving -> _handle_no_landable.
        - Browser.nav_home/nav_end: set PIN_* then _apply_cursor_anchor() (lands on first/last LANDABLE) instead of cursor=0/len-1 directly. (The keyboard _nav_home/_nav_end in 070-actions were already routed by #736.)
        EXPLICIT-META vs PIN/DISPLACEMENT distinction: an explicit cursor_to(meta_id) sets _cursor_anchor=[meta_id]; _apply_cursor_anchor lands it via a TIER-0 (primary) hit, which is honored verbatim, and the tier-0 snapshot refresh keeps primary==cursor-row-id. So in _clamp_cursor_landable's in-range-on-meta branch, anchored_here = (anchor[0] is not a sentinel AND anchor[0]==cursor-row-id) means 'explicit target, keep'; a MISMATCH means the anchored id is gone and the stale index merely fell on a meta (a displaced re-snap) -> skip to landable. PIN sentinels and tier>=1 fallbacks are always skipped to landable.
        _compute_anchor_snapshot: NO change needed — it records ids only (primary + next/prev/ancestors), never picks a replacement cursor index; landability is enforced at apply-time (tier>=1 skip + clamp). Noted rather than touched.
        SCROLL-GEOMETRY FINDING (item 3): _snap_list_scroll_to_row and _active_list_row need NO change. Both operate purely in visible-list SLOT space, which already counts meta rows (meta occupy slots per #760). _active_list_row returns state.cursor (a slot index incl. meta above it); _snap_list_scroll_to_row keeps that slot in [scroll, scroll+height) and is kind-agnostic. Once the cursor lands on a LANDABLE slot (the clamp/anchor/move work), the viewport math treats it like any slot. Added TestMetaScrollGeometry (2 tests) to lock this in: End past an interior meta lands on the last landable slot and the scroll counts the meta slot above it; Home past a leading meta keeps the landed cursor visible.
        TESTS: test/async_/test_browser.py +TestMetaLandability (15) +TestMetaScrollGeometry (2), all via real headless Browser. Covers: delete/hide under cursor adjacent to meta re-anchors off it; refresh shrink past meta; full-replacement stale cursor landing on meta snaps to landable; PIN_FIRST/LAST past leading/trailing meta + pin survives reanchor; cursor_to(meta) lands exactly (regression); on_empty exit/wait on all-meta + truly-empty + shrink-to-all-meta; invalid on_empty rejected; no spurious cursor-change.
        Updated test/ui/test_navigation.py TestMetaRowSkip._launch_on_a: #736 deferred initial-clamp landability to this ticket, so the cursor now starts on 'a' (first landable, skipping leading 'top' meta) directly — removed the stale settling Down that now overshoots to 'b'.
        Full suite: 3038 tests, 0 failures, 4 skipped (./run-tests.sh).

  * ## Ticket: Rendering: meta chrome + content as segments OR ANSI string (any row) {#738}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 05:08:20 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 06:30:26 UTC

    Spec sections 4 and 4.1. Meta chrome reduces to aligned indentation: the
    renderer forces a blank selection-marker and blank expander for kind=='meta'
    (even if has_children was left True). Allow format_row_content / format_row to
    return a str (ANSI allowed) OR a segment list, for ANY row (normal and meta);
    the renderer detects str vs list. Default meta content = the title segment.
    Cursor row and search-match rows collapse content to VISIBLE TEXT (segment fg
    AND embedded SGR both dropped) under the reverse/highlight overlay — exactly
    as today's segment collapse. Non-cursor: segments via the per-segment writer;
    str rendered via _truncate_visible (full sanitize/reset/bg lands in the next

  * ## Ticket: ANSI string handling: sanitize / truncate / reset / bg-restore {#739}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 05:08:20 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 07:03:26 UTC

    Spec section 4.2. For a raw ANSI-string row content AND the preview pane:
    sanitize keeping only SGR (\e[...m), stripping all other CSI and bare ESC
    (extend/share _sanitize_preview at 050-render.py:142, robust per-sequence —
    not a greedy regex); truncate ANSI-aware via _truncate_visible; conditional
    trailing reset (nothing if no ANSI present, \e[m if any); background restore
    (re-emit \e[<row-bg>m after reset) ONLY when a row bg is set AND the content
    contains a 40-49 / 100-109 code.
    Tests (test/unit/test_preview_sanitize.py, test/unit/test_render.py): non-SGR
    escapes stripped, SGR kept; no trailing reset for plain strings, \e[m when
    ANSI present; bg-restore fires only under both conditions; the preview path
    stays correct.

    * ## Comments {#739:comments}

      * Implemented all four ANSI byte-rules (spec 4.2). Shared sanitizer: new _sanitize_ansi(s) in src-tui/050-render.py (robust per-sequence scan — keeps \e[...m SGR, drops all other complete CSI, dangling \e[, and bare/non-CSI ESC). {#739:comment:748}

        Rule 1 (sanitize on receipt): row content -> _normalize_content (040-state.py) now runs _sanitize_ansi on the str branch before any width math / write; preview -> render_preview (050) calls _sanitize_ansi(text) right after _sanitize_preview's control-char pass (ansi_on only). Both paths now call the SAME function so they behave identically. _sanitize_preview itself is UNCHANGED, so test_preview_sanitize.py stays green (no adaptation needed).
        Rule 2 (truncate): unchanged — _truncate_visible in _write_segments' ANSI branch (#738).
        Rule 3 (conditional reset): _write_segments tracks whether emitted content carried any SGR; if so, write('\e[m') after content. Plain rows emit nothing extra (byte-for-byte unchanged).
        Rule 4 (bg restore): gated on row_bg set AND content carrying a 40-49/100-109 SGR param (new _has_bg_sgr helper). Emits the bare bg SGR \e[48;5;Nm after the rule-3 reset (raw write, not set_style, so it's the exact restore byte and distinct from the stripe pad).
        ROW-BG FINDING: a row DOES carry a background today via item.row_bg (256-color int), read in render_list as row_bg=getattr(item,'row_bg',None) (050-render.py:1768) and threaded into _write_segments. browse-claude sets item.row_bg on real items (voice stripes, e.g. :968,:1071,:4559). Those rows currently return SEGMENTS, so rule 4 doesn't fire for them yet — but the source is real, so rule 4 is correctly wired and forward-compatible: the moment a recipe returns an ANSI string with a bg code on a row_bg row, the stripe is restored. The cursor/reverse path sets reverse via set_style (not a bg int) and goes through _collapse_visible+_write_highlighted (escapes stripped), so it never triggers rules 3/4 — correct. Tests construct the rule-4 precondition directly (item.row_bg=17 + content with \e[41m) through the real render_list.
        Tests: +31 (3052 -> 3083). test_render.py: TestSanitizeAnsi, TestHasBgSgr (direct unit), plus rules 1/3/4 (+negatives) and a plain-str no-reset regression through render_list; updated the two #738 passthrough tests for the new sanitize+reset. test_preview_tail.py: TestPreviewSharedSanitizer (end-to-end render_full: non-SGR CSI + bare ESC stripped, SGR kept). run-tests.sh: 3083 tests, 0 failures, 4 skipped.

  * ## Ticket: _sanitize_ansi: regex-based, not char-by-char {#754}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 17:44:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 17:52:01 UTC

    `_sanitize_ansi` (src-tui/050-render.py) currently scans character-by-
    character — inefficient. Reimplement it with a compiled regex + a single
    `re.sub` (greedy): KEEP complete SGR (`\e[...m`), STRIP every other complete
    CSI, a dangling `\e[...` with no final byte, and a bare/non-CSI `\e`. Keep the
    no-ESC fast path if it helps. Behavior must be IDENTICAL to the current
    implementation — all existing `_sanitize_ansi` tests must stay green
    (SGR kept, other CSI + bare ESC stripped, dangling `\e[` stripped, idempotent),
    and the shared preview path (full + streaming append) stays byte-identical.
    Add a brief equivalence note/test if useful. Files: src-tui/050-render.py;
    tests in test/unit/test_render.py and test/unit/test_preview_sanitize.py /
    test_preview_tail.py if they assert sanitize output.

    * ## Comments {#754:comments}

      * Reimplemented _sanitize_ansi as a single greedy re.sub over a compiled regex (src-tui/050-render.py). {#754:comment:758}

        Regex (_SANITIZE_ANSI_RE): \x1b\[[^@-~]*([@-~])|\x1b\[[^@-~]*\Z|\x1b
        Three left-to-right alternatives, one ESC each:
         1. complete CSI: ESC[ + non-final bytes [^@-~]* + one final byte [@-~] (captured grp 1). Bounded char-class => never swallows inter-escape text; mirrors the old while-loop skip condition not('@'<=c<='~') exactly.
         2. dangling CSI: ESC[ + non-finals + \Z (true end, not pre-newline) => strip remainder (matches the old 'break').
         3. bare ESC: lone ESC / non-CSI escape => strip the ESC byte only.
        Replacement (_sanitize_ansi_repl): keep match iff grp 1 == 'm' (SGR), else ''. Kept the no-ESC fast path (returns the same object => identity preserved).
        Added 'import re' to 050-render.py: production concatenation already imports re (from 020) so it's a no-op there, but the module-level re.compile must resolve when the file is exec'd standalone in the isolated unit-test load.
        Equivalence proof: scratch harness (now deleted) loaded the REAL new _sanitize_ansi from source and compared byte-for-byte against a verbatim copy of the old char-by-char version over: the full hand-crafted battery (all existing test cases + edges: \e at end, \e[31 dangling, \eM, \e[?25l, intermediates \e[1 q, adjacent escapes, final bytes @/~/0x7f, high bytes \x80/\xff, trailing newline, empty); 500,000 random fuzz strings; and 111,111 exhaustive strings up to length 5. Result: ALL EQUIVALENT, identity-return preserved.
        Verification: 14/14 _sanitize_ansi tests green; preview + render suites green incl. the streaming append path (test_preview_render_cache append tests); full ./run-tests.sh => Ran 3103 tests, OK (skipped=4), 0 failures.

  * ## Ticket: Task: bg-restore via row_bg attribute (drop _has_bg_sgr) {#755}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 17:44:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 18:00:42 UTC

    Simplify the rule-4 background-restore in `_write_segments`
    (src-tui/050-render.py): STOP scanning the content string for bg codes
    (`_has_bg_sgr`). The row background is already a distinct attribute
    (`item.row_bg`, already threaded into `_write_segments`). After the
    conditional trailing reset, restore the row bg whenever `row_bg` is set AND
    any ANSI was emitted (the `\e[m` reset clears the bg regardless of whether the
    content itself set one, so the old content-bg-code check was unnecessary).
    DELETE `_has_bg_sgr` and its tests; update the bg-restore tests to the new
    condition (row_bg set + ANSI present -> restore; no row_bg OR no ANSI -> no
    restore). Update spec section 4.2 rule 4 in
    docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-meta-rows-design.md to the simpler wording
    (restore gated on the row bg attribute, not a content scan). Files:
    src-tui/050-render.py, test/unit/test_render.py, the spec.

    * ## Comments {#755:comments}

      * Done. Deleted _has_bg_sgr (src-tui/050-render.py) + the content_bg loop tracking. Rule-4 restore now gated purely on the row_bg attribute: after the rule-3 conditional \e[m reset, when row_bg is not None we write(f'\033[48;5;{row_bg}m'). This is slightly MORE correct than before — the reset clears the bg even for fg-only content, so the attribute gate keeps the stripe alive in that case (the old content-bg-code gate missed it). Updated the _write_segments docstring and spec sec 4.2 rule 4 (restore driven by row_bg attribute, NOT a content scan; dropped the '40-49/100-109 content code' condition). Tests: deleted the TestHasBgSgr class (positional-parse cases) and reworked the bg-restore tests via the real _write_segments/render path: (1) row_bg + bg-bearing ANSI -> \e[m\e[48;5;17m; (2) NEW row_bg + fg-only ANSI -> restore STILL fires (the case the old gate missed); (3) row_bg + plain content -> no reset, no restore; (4) ANSI but no row_bg -> reset, no restore. Zero remaining _has_bg_sgr/content_bg refs in src/tests/spec (only historical .PLAN.md ticket records mention them). REDUNDANCY FINDING: rule-4 restore is now load-bearing ONLY in the no-pad/exact-fill case. When row_bg is set, effective_pad=max(pad_to,max_width), so whenever pos<max_width the trailing-pad branch runs set_style(bg=row_bg) right after the rule-4 byte, superseding it. Empirically confirmed by driving _write_segments: under-fill emits [...write('\e[48;5;17m'), set_style(bg=17), write(spaces)...] (rule-4 byte immediately overwritten); exact-fill (pos==max_width) skips the pad branch entirely, so rule-4 is the only re-assertion of the row bg after the reset. Kept rule-4 per directive; candidate to drop in a future ticket (would only affect the exact-fill case). Full suite: 3089 tests, OK (skipped=4), 0 failures. {#755:comment:759}

  * ## Ticket: browse-git: native colored git graph {#756}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-05 17:44:54 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-05 18:18:22 UTC

    Now that rows can carry raw ANSI content, render git's NATIVE colored graph
    instead of the monochrome reconstruction. In `_commit_graph_items`
    (recipes/browse-git), run `git log --graph --color=always ...` (instead of
    `--no-color`) and pass the colored graph art THROUGH as raw ANSI row content
    (the content hook may return a str or an ANSI-bearing segment per #738; the
    framework sanitizer keeps only SGR, so it is safe). Drop the monochrome
    `_graph_translate`-to-terminal-default art path (keep only any structural
    translation still required). Preserve column alignment (sha/author/date +
    graph + subject); filler (meta) rows carry the colored connector art as raw
    ANSI. Verify end-to-end via tmux on a `--no-ff` merge repo: the graph art is
    COLORED and still aligns under the commit column; the cursor still skips
    fillers; fillers stay unselectable. Update test/unit/test_browse_git.py graph
    tests for the colored output and add a UI assertion that color is present +
    aligned. Files: recipes/browse-git, test/unit/test_browse_git.py,
    test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py. (This is the feature requested as a ticket.)

    * ## Comments {#756:comments}

      * Implemented native colored git graph. {#756:comment:760}

        GIT INVOCATION: _commit_graph_items now runs 'git log --graph --color=always ...' (was --no-color). Verified the \x1f field split is unaffected: SGR uses \x1b (never \x1f), and the format fields (%H/%an/%ar/%s/%D) stay plain, so line.split('\x1f') still yields art + 5 fields cleanly (asserted end-to-end: subjects/sha/author carry no SGR).
        _graph_translate ANSI-SAFETY: rewrote it to (1) run the art through the shared framework _sanitize_ansi first (keep only complete SGR \e[..m, drop every other CSI + bare/dangling ESC), then (2) glyph-substitute ANSI-safely: a module _SGR_RE = \x1b\[[0-9;]*m splits the line into SGR tokens vs the plain runs between them; str.translate(_GRAPH_TABLE) (* -> bullet, | -> box-vertical, _ -> lower-block) is applied ONLY to the plain runs, each SGR token re-emitted verbatim. So a glyph is never substituted for a byte inside an escape and a colour run is never split. rstrip strips only trailing whitespace (git's right-pad), leaving any trailing reset intact. Kept the structural glyph translation (worth keeping for the bullet/box look); dropped the monochrome-to-terminal-default path entirely.
        SANITIZE APPROACH: _normalize_content sanitizes STRING row content on receipt but passes a SEGMENT LIST through untouched. git_row_content must return a segment list (it owns the styled sha/author/date columns + chips), so returning the whole row as a string is not viable. Clean option: the recipe imports the shared sanitizer (from browse_tui import _sanitize_ansi — browse_tui IS the framework module via --run-py's sys.modules injection; _sanitize_ansi is a top-level function, confirmed reachable in the built binary) and applies it inside _graph_translate before storing in item.col_graph. So only colour codes can ever reach the screen, regardless of the segment vs string path.
        RENDER: git_row_content emits col_graph as (col_graph, None, False) — no framework fg — for BOTH the commit-row art (graph + ' ') and the filler (meta) row art (the blank-pad span stays, art segment is now fg=None). Dropped gfg/gbold = style(''). The per-segment writer truncates the ANSI text ANSI-aware and rule-3 adds the conditional trailing \e[m so colour can't bleed into the subject / next pane.
        ALIGNMENT: confirmed end-to-end (tmux, --no-ff merge repo): the sha/author/date columns + graph + subject still line up; the merge node bullet and the connector art on surrounding filler rows share the same column. The colour codes are zero-width (cell_width/_visible_len strip escapes when measuring), so the visible width is identical to the monochrome version.
        END-TO-END (real binary in tmux on a 2-merge --no-ff repo): colour IS present — distinct lane colours (red 31, green 32, yellow 33, blue 34) wrap the |/\\ connectors; the bullet node is plain where git leaves it plain (native behaviour). Cursor Down skips fillers onto commits; C-a selects only the 4 commits, never a connector filler; no colour bleed into subjects (each lane run closed by git's \e[39m reset before the subject, backed by rule-3).
        TESTS: test/unit/test_browse_git.py — flipped the --no-color assertion to --color=always (+ assertNotIn --no-color); added _sanitize_ansi to the browse_tui stub (mirrors the framework regex 1:1) since the recipe now imports it; added TestGraphTranslate cases for SGR-preserved-and-glyph-substituted-inside-run and non-SGR-escapes-sanitised-out. The git_row_content tests already expected (graph, None, False), now matched. test/ui/test_recipe_browse_git.py — added test_graph_art_is_colored_not_monochrome (asserts >=2 distinct SGR fg colours on connector glyphs + no fg run left open before a subject); existing tree-meta tests (skip/align/unselectable) unchanged and green.
        Full suite: Ran 3092 tests, OK (skipped=4). Baseline 3089 + 3 new tests.
      * Review nits applied (APPROVE-WITH-NITS): {#756:comment:761}

        1. Robustness/doc accuracy: widened recipe _SGR_RE from \x1b\[[0-9;]*m to \x1b\[[^@-~]*m so its param class EXACTLY mirrors the kept-set of the framework sanitize_ansi (a CSI whose final byte is m). Every sequence that survives sanitise is now matched by the split, so the 'no substitution inside an escape' invariant holds even for non-git params. Verified the edge case: sanitize_ansi('\x1b[*m|') keeps '\x1b[*m' (param byte '*'=0x2a is in [^@-~]); _graph_translate now yields '\x1b[*m│' (the '*' inside the escape is preserved, only the plain '|' substituted) — previously the narrow regex would have corrupted that '*' to a bullet. Docstrings/comments updated to match.
        2. API hygiene: added a PUBLIC alias 'sanitize_ansi = _sanitize_ansi' in src-tui/050-render.py (one-line comment: recipe-facing name for sanitising external ANSI before embedding it in segment text, which bypasses _normalize_content's on-receipt string sanitise). Recipe now does 'from browse_tui import ... sanitize_ansi' and calls sanitize_ansi(...) in _graph_translate. Renamed the test stub (test/unit/test_browse_git.py) to sanitize_ansi, regex kept byte-identical to the framework. Confirmed sanitize_ansi resolves in the built binary (and is the same object as _sanitize_ansi). Verified there are now ZERO underscore-prefixed browse_tui imports across all recipes.
        Files: src-tui/050-render.py, recipes/browse-git, test/unit/test_browse_git.py. Full suite: Ran 3092 tests, OK (skipped=4).

* ## Ticket: Task: Replace ctx.message() with flash/log + reworked error {#761}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-06 05:17:11 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-06 06:39:11 UTC

  Implement docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-06-ctx-flash-log-design.md.

  `ctx.message()` is dead (sets `_message_text`, read by no renderer). Replace it
  with two primitives — `ctx.flash(text, log=False)` (transient ~1s info-bar
  notice) and `ctx.log(text)` (silent append to a viewable ring buffer) — and
  rework `ctx.error()` into a red, always-logged info-bar notice that clears on
  the next keypress (min 1s) instead of hijacking the preview pane. Add a `~`
  default action that views the log in the pager. Migrate all 17 call sites.
  Route browse-plan's plan-CLI audit logging through the framework log.

  Read the spec for full detail; each subtask scopes one slice. Subagents must
  use absolute paths under this worktree
  (/home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/flash-log) — file
  tools may otherwise target the main checkout.

  * ## Ticket: Framework: flash/log/error primitives, notice rendering, removals {#762}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-06 05:17:11 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-06 05:51:21 UTC
        links: blocking:#763 blocking:#764 blocking:#765 blocking:#766

    Spec sections: "The three primitives", "Data model (Browser)", "Rendering",
    "Clear policies". Files: src-tui/040-state.py, src-tui/050-render.py,
    src-tui/060-context.py. This is one ticket because the notice/error feature
    spans state + render and must land together to keep tests green.

    Data model on Browser (replaces `_message_text`/`_error_text`):
    - `_notice`: None | Notice dataclass {text:str, kind:str ('flash'|'error'),
      shown_at:float (monotonic), seq:int}; single slot, last-write-wins.
    - `_notice_seq`: int counter (bump on each set).
    - `_log`: collections.deque(maxlen=_LOG_MAXLEN).
    - Constants: _FLASH_DURATION=1.0, _ERROR_MIN_DISPLAY=1.0, _LOG_MAXLEN=1000.
    - Log entries stored pre-formatted "HH:MM:SS  <text>", chronological.

    Browser methods (thread-safe via self.post):
    - flash(text, log=False): set notice (kind 'flash', new seq); if log, append
      to _log; arm a daemon threading.Timer(_FLASH_DURATION) whose callback posts
      a clear that only fires if _notice.seq still matches the captured seq, then
      notify_wake + redraw 'info'. Skip arming the timer when self._headless.
    - log(text): append to _log only — no notice, no redraw.
    - error(text): set notice (kind 'error', new seq); ALWAYS append to _log; no
      timer.
    - Cancel any active flash timer in stop_workers()/quit path.

    Context methods (060-context.py): flash/log/error delegating to browser.

    Error clear policy (040-state.py _handle_one_key): at the top, before
    dispatch, if _notice is an error and monotonic()-shown_at >=
    _ERROR_MIN_DISPLAY, clear _notice and flag 'info' redraw. The key then
    dispatches normally (non-swallowing).

    get_children failure reroute: replace the two `self._error_text = ...` writes
    at the children-fetch boundary with `self.error(...)` (thread-safe; now also
    logged).

    Rendering (050-render.py render_info_bar): in the `info=True` bar's middle
    region, priority prompt(search/filter) > notice > hint. When _notice set,
    write its text in place of the hint — red (e.g. fg=9, bold) for kind
    'error', a quiet distinct style for 'flash'; truncate to fit like the hint.
    Pane headers (info=False) never show the notice. REMOVE the preview-pane
    error takeover: delete every `_error_text` read in render_preview, the
    `not browser._error_text` guards, and the 'Error' case in _preview_label.

    Removals: Browser.message, Browser.message_text, Browser._message_text,
    Context.message, Browser._error_text, Browser.error_text.

    Tests (TDD): flash sets a 'flash' notice and (headless) arms no timer;
    flash(log=True) appends; log appends silently with no notice/redraw; error
    sets a red notice + always logs + no timer; ring buffer caps at _LOG_MAXLEN;
    a stale-seq timer does not clear a newer notice; error clears on a keypress
    at >=1s but not before, key still acts; info bar renders notice with correct
    priority and red error; pane headers omit it. Update test_context.py
    (test_message_sets_message_text, test_error_sets_error_text) and
    test_background.py (asserts _message_text) to the new API.

    * ## Comments {#762:comments}

      * Implemented flash/log/error primitives (040-state.py), info-bar notice rendering + preview-pane error-takeover removal (050-render.py), and Context delegators flash/log/error (060-context.py). Removed Browser.message/_message_text/message_text, Browser.error_text/_error_text, Context.message. Design decisions: (1) seq bump + Notice install + timer-arming all happen inside the SINGLE posted main-thread closure (_set_notice/_arm_flash_timer) so the timer always captures the freshly-assigned seq and all slot writes are serialized on the main thread — cleaner/more-correct than arming in flash() before the post drains. (2) Flash timer cancelled at the TOP of stop_workers() (before the workers_running guard) so an armed timer is cancelled even if workers never started; covers the quit path (main loop finally -> stop_workers). (3) Error keypress-clear at top of _handle_one_key, non-swallowing, gated on monotonic()-shown_at >= _ERROR_MIN_DISPLAY. (4) Notice middle-region priority prompt>notice>hint; error fg=9 bold, flash fg=250 bold (quiet, distinct from fg=242 hint). Tests: rewired test_context (TestNoticePrimitives + updated delegator tests), new test_notice_keypath.py, new TestInfoBarNotice in test_render.py; updated test_background.py per ticket; migrated ~20 error_text/_error_text assertions across test_lifecycle_hooks/test_workers/test_browser/test_children_loaded/test_children_generator/test_run_in_slot/test_context_push to read the message log ('\n'.join(b._log)) since error() always logs. Also injected DEFAULT_HINT into the isolated _render load in test_render.py (pre-existing wiring gap, only surfaced now that a test reaches the hint fallback). {#762:comment:767}

      * Full ./run-tests.sh: Ran 3114, FAILED (failures=7, errors=4, skipped=4). ALL 11 failures are un-migrated ctx.message() call sites owned by the BLOCKING sibling tickets this ticket depends-forward to: (a) 4 errors in test.unit.test_actions.TestViewEditDefaults -> 070-actions.py:406/420 ctx.message('No preview available') = ticket #763; (b) 7 fails in test.ui.test_recipe_browse_claude -> recipes/browse-claude ctx.message at lines 5517/6210/6261 = ticket #764. Verified every failure traceback roots in "'Context' object has no attribute 'message'"; zero regressions from the state/render/context changes. Per ticket scope I did NOT touch 070-actions.py or recipes/. Every other test module in the suite passes (538 tests across all modules this ticket touched are green; smoke-tested the primitives + red-error rendering through the real concatenated browse-tui binary). The suite goes fully green once #763/#764 migrate the call sites (final sweep is #766). {#762:comment:768}

  * ## Ticket: browse-plan: route plan-CLI audit logging through framework log {#765}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-06 05:17:11 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-06 06:23:13 UTC
        links: blocked:#762 blocked:#763 blocking:#766

    Spec section: "browse-plan logging integration". File: recipes/browse-plan
    only. Depends on the framework primitives ticket AND the `~` viewer ticket
    (browse-plan must inherit the framework `~`).

    - Remove `_action_command_log` and its `~` Action binding; browse-plan
      inherits the framework `~` log viewer.
    - Migrate the recipe's own `ctx.message(f'preview: ...')` toggle to
      `ctx.flash(...)`.
    - Wire a module-level log sink: in main(), right after `b = Browser(...)`,
      set a module global `_log_sink = b.log` (thread-safe Browser.log).
    - In `_run_plan`, when the sink is set, log exactly: any call with non-zero
      returncode (incl. the 30s timeout fallback), even reads, as
      `[error] plan <argv>: <stderr>`; AND any DB-mutating call on success —
      every subcommand except the read set (`list`/`-r list`, `get`,
      `project get`, recognized by the `list`/`get` verbs) as `plan <argv>`.
      Successful reads are NOT logged.
    Tests: a mutation logs its argv; a failing call logs `[error] ...`; a
    successful read logs nothing; `~` pages the accumulated log.

    * ## Comments {#765:comments}

      * Read-vs-mutation heuristic: a call is a READ iff its argv tuple contains the verb 'list' or 'get' (covers list, -r list, get, project get). Implemented as _is_read(args) = ('list' in args or 'get' in args). Everything else (create/status/close/move/comment/edit/reopen/add) is a mutation. Logging localized in _run_plan via _log_plan_call(args, result): non-zero returncode (incl timeout/exception fallback) -> '[error] plan <argv>: <stderr>' even for reads; else mutations -> 'plan <argv>'; successful reads log nothing. <argv> = ' '.join(str(a) for a in args) (the meaningful subcommand portion, no plan binary / -f FILE prefix). _log_sink wired to Browser.log (thread-safe) in main() right after Browser construction. {#765:comment:771}

      * Done. Edits to recipes/browse-plan: removed _action_command_log + its ~ binding (now inherits framework ~ -> _view_log); ctx.message -> ctx.flash in _action_toggle_md (zero ctx.message remain anywhere in repo); added module global _log_sink (wired to Browser.log in main() right after Browser construction, before run()); added post-result audit logging in _run_plan via _is_read()/_log_plan_call(). Added test/unit/test_browse_plan_log.py (12 tests): mutation-success, all-mutation-verbs, failing-read-logs-error, failing-mutation, successful-reads-log-nothing, _is_read heuristic, timeout fallback, no-sink-no-crash; plus ~->_view_log keymap resolution (loads the real generated binary as browse_tui, runs main() with Browser.run patched), recipe binds no ~, _action_command_log removed, _log_sink == Browser.log. All 12 new tests pass; existing browse-plan UI tests pass; full ./run-tests.sh green: 3133 tests OK (skipped=4, pre-existing env skips). {#765:comment:772}

Confirms ctx.flash() -> info-bar notice end-to-end. Non-hanging; no interactive pager triggered.

* ## Ticket: browse-plan: log successful create/edit mutations to the audit log {#774}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-06 11:28:40 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-06 11:32:49 UTC

  Follow-up to #765. That ticket routed `_run_plan` calls (errors + DB
  mutations) into the framework log via the module-level `_log_sink`. But
  `_action_create` and `_action_edit` shell out through `ctx.run_external`
  (interactive `$EDITOR`: `plan create -e …` / `plan edit …`), bypassing
  `_run_plan` — so a SUCCESSFUL create/edit never reaches the audit log.

  Note the failure path is ALREADY audited: both handlers do
  `if rc != 0: ctx.error(...)`, and `ctx.error` always appends to the log.
  So only the success (mutation) case is missing.

  File: recipes/browse-plan ONLY.

  Goal — complete the mutation audit trail:
  - In `_action_create.on_confirm`: after `rc = ctx.run_external(cmd)`, on
    success (`rc == 0`) record the mutation via `_log_sink` (guarded by
    `_log_sink is not None`), e.g. `plan create` / `plan create -r`. Keep the
    existing `if rc != 0: ctx.error(...)` failure branch unchanged.
  - In `_action_edit`: same — on `rc == 0` log `plan edit <target>` /
    `plan edit -r <target>` (target is `project` for id 0, else the id).
  - Render the log line cleanly and consistently with existing `plan <argv>`
    entries. Do NOT dump the noisy `-e`, `title="New ticket"`, `move="…"`
    expr — log a concise human-readable form (e.g. `create -r`, `edit 5`,
    `edit project`). Keep the logic tiny and localized; reuse the existing
    `_log_sink`/style. (You need not force-fit `_log_plan_call`, which expects
    a CompletedProcess; a small direct `_log_sink(...)` on success is fine.)

  Tests (extend test/unit/test_browse_plan_log.py — follow its patterns:
  set `_log_sink` to a capture list, monkeypatch `ctx.run_external`):
  - a successful create (rc==0) logs a `plan create…` line; `-r` variant too;
  - a successful edit (rc==0) logs `plan edit <target>` (id, `-r`, and
    `project` for id 0);
  - a failed create/edit (rc!=0) does NOT emit a `plan …` mutation line (the
    error is surfaced/logged via `ctx.error`, not double-logged here);
  - `_log_sink is None` → no crash.

  * ## Comments {#774:comments}

    * Implemented in recipes/browse-plan: on rc==0, _action_create logs 'plan {label}' (label is already 'create' / 'create -r') and _action_edit logs 'plan edit [-r ]<target>' (target='project' for cursor id 0 else the id), both guarded by 'if _log_sink is not None'. Failure branch (ctx.error) left unchanged — it does not emit a 'plan ...' mutation row. Chose the concise label form over the noisy -e / title=…,move=… argv per the audit-readability goal; computed target into a local in _action_edit so the cmd build and log line share it. Did not reuse _log_plan_call (expects a CompletedProcess). Added TestCreateEditLogging (8 tests) with a _FakeCtx stand-in (run_external returns canned rc; insert captures on_confirm so it can be fired directly with (relation, dest_id)). test.unit.test_browse_plan_log: 20/20 OK. Full ./run-tests.sh: 3141 OK (skipped=4, pre-existing). {#774:comment:775}

* ## Ticket: Task: Loose text -> dim [text] nodes (md_doc + browse-md + browse-claude) {#776}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-07 21:53:02 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-07 22:50:48 UTC

  Currently loose text (prose with no heading) lives only inside a parent's
  byte-range -- it is never a navigable node (see `build_doc_tree` in
  `recipes/md_doc.py`). Change: a loose-text run becomes a dim `[text]` leaf
  node, BUT only when its scope already has >=1 heading child (it must have
  structural siblings). Title = first non-blank line; the full run feeds the
  preview via the existing byte-range slice (`text[byte_offset:+byte_size]`).

  Approved design decisions:
  1. Emit only with structural siblings. Leaf-heading bodies and heading-less
     docs stay nodeless, so `build_doc_tree('prose')` still returns `[]` and
     browse-claude's markdown-node gate (`if build_doc_tree(text):`) is kept.
  2. Headings-only mode only -- gate synthesis on `not include_lists`.
     browse-md's `-l` list mode is left byte-for-byte unchanged.
  3. Title = first non-blank line, trimmed, inline markup preserved.

  Example:
      text1
      # heading1
      text2
      ## heading2
      text3
  -> text1 = top-level [text] node; text2 = first child of heading1;
     text3 = NO node (heading2 is a leaf).

  * ## Ticket: md_doc: emit [text] nodes in build_doc_tree {#777}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-07 21:53:02 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-07 22:16:44 UTC

    In `recipes/md_doc.py` `build_doc_tree`, when `include_lists` is False,
    after the heading tree is built (pass 3) synthesize dim text nodes.

    - New `MdNode` kind `'text'` (update the class docstring).
    - For each scope -- the top-level `roots` list, and recursively each
      heading node's `children` -- if the scope has >=1 heading child, inspect
      the gap before the first heading child: content lines in
      `[scope_content_start_line, first_child.line_offset)`, where
      scope_content_start_line is 0 for the root scope, or
      `scope_heading.line_offset + 1` for a heading scope. Find the FIRST
      non-blank line in that gap. If one exists at line L, create a text node
      and insert it as the scope's FIRST child (before the first heading):
        kind='text', level=first_child.level,
        title = that line `.rstrip('\n').strip()` (do NOT strip inline markup),
        line_offset=L, byte_offset=line_starts[L],
        byte_size = first_child.byte_offset - byte_offset, children=[].
    - All-blank gap (e.g. `# h1\n\n## h2`) -> create NO text node.
    - Scope with no heading children (leaf heading / heading-less doc) ->
      NO text node. `build_doc_tree('prose')` stays `[]`; the list tests stay
      green; browse-claude's markdown-node gate is preserved.
    - Gate the WHOLE synthesis on `not include_lists` -> include_lists=True is
      byte-for-byte unchanged (no text nodes).

    Tests in `test/unit/test_md_doc.py`: top-level text1 before first heading;
    text2 as first child of h1 (before h2); text3 under leaf h2 -> no node;
    prose-only -> []; all-blank gap -> no node; multi-line run title = first
    non-blank line; `text[byte_offset:byte_offset+byte_size]` == the run;
    `node_at_line` finds the text node by its line_offset; include_lists=True
    leaves the existing list trees unchanged.

    * ## Comments {#777:comments}

      * Implemented [text]-node synthesis in md_doc.build_doc_tree (pass 4, gated on not include_lists). New MdNode kind 'text'; docstring updated. Added recursive helper _add_text_nodes(scope_children, content_start_line, text, line_starts) + _is_blank_line. Helper recurses into heading children first (content_start = child.line_offset+1), THEN inserts the run's first non-blank line as the scope's index-0 child; root scope called with (roots, 0). Fields per spec: kind='text', level=first_child.level, title=first-non-blank-line rstrip('\n').strip() (markup kept), line_offset=L, byte_offset=line_starts[L], byte_size=first_child.byte_offset-byte_offset, children=[]. {#777:comment:781}

        Tests: test_md_doc green (83, +11 new in TestBuildDocTreeTextNodes: text1/text2 placement, text3-under-leaf=none, prose-only=[], all-blank-gap=none, multiline-title, byte-range slice==run, node_at_line finds text node, include_lists=True unchanged x2). Updated 5 pre-existing headings-only fixtures (test_nesting, test_byte_range_slicing, test_fenced_hash..., TestNodeAtLine.test_no_match..., TestCache.test_invalid_utf8...) via a new _headings() filter / a leaf-scope no-match line, since headings-with-intro-body trees now legitimately gain a text node.
        EXPECTED interim breakage (NOT fixed here -- out of this ticket's md_doc-only scope): the include_lists=False tree feeds browse-md and browse-claude, which still map every node by level. 3 downstream tests now fail: test_browse_md.test_ref_file_heading_node_has_subheading_children (flat/event_kinds 1:1 alignment broken), and test_browse_claude_render TestMarkdownSubtrees.{test_inline_doc_children_are_headings, test_get_children_routes_message_and_md_ids} (text node rendered as fake heading row). These are owned by sibling tickets #779 (browse-md render, blocked:#777) and #778 (browse-claude render). test_md_doc itself is fully green.

Added _md_text_item(base, abspaths, node) (Item with tag='text', tag_style='dim', has_children=False, kind='md-text', id via compose_md_id with node.line_offset) as the dim-leaf sibling of _md_heading_item.   * ## Ticket: browse-md: render [text] nodes (eager + lazy paths) {#779}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-07 21:53:02 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-07 22:37:31 UTC

    links: blocked:#777

    `recipes/browse-md`, two paths.

    Eager primary-file path (`_build_nodes`/`_to_item`, ~browse-md:385):
    - Add a `kind=='text'` branch in `_to_item` BEFORE the heading/list
      dispatch -> tag='text', tag_style='dim', leaf.
    - The events/`event_kinds` 1:1 positional alignment is consulted ONLY in the
      list-item else branch (ul/ol), which runs only in list mode where there
      are NO text nodes -- so alignment stays intact; just return from the text
      branch before touching `event_kinds[index]`. Confirm no runtime assert on
      `len(events)==n` that text nodes would trip.
    - line_size loop (~browse-md:330): add a `kind=='text'` case. A text node's
      section ends at its immediately-following sibling (the first heading
      child), i.e. `line_size = flat[i+1].line_offset - cur.line_offset`
      (byte_size is already set by build_doc_tree). Don't let it fall into the
      list-item else branch.
    - `_collapse_single_child` (browse-md:493) already requires the sole child be
      `kind=='heading'` -> a sole [text] child won't auto-expand (fine, verify).
    - `_resolve_anchor` (~:606) heading scan filters `kind=='heading'` -> text
      nodes excluded from anchor matching (verify). `_node_at_line` by_line index
      will include text nodes (deepest-containing) -- verify anchor-by-line still
      resolves sanely.

    Lazy #md: path: mirror browse-claude -- add `_md_text_item`, branch on
    `ch.kind` in `_md_subtree_children` (~browse-md:997 and ~:1003).
    `_preview_md_node` (~:1050) unchanged.

    Tests in `test/unit/test_browse_md.py`: primary-file tree shows [text] rows
    per the rule (default / no -l); `-l` mode unchanged (no text rows); text row
    is a leaf with dim tag; preview = the run; anchor resolution unaffected;
    lazy #md: ref path shows [text] rows too.

    * ## Comments {#779:comments}

      * Implemented [text] nodes in both browse-md paths. EAGER: added a kind=='text' arm to _to_item BEFORE the heading/list dispatch (tag='text', style='dim', leaf via shared field-stamping) so event_kinds[index] is never reached for text nodes; added an explicit kind=='text' case in the line_size loop (line_size = flat[i+1].line_offset - cur.line_offset, since a text node's next pre-order node is always the heading it precedes); refreshed the now-stale 'len(events)==n' comment (flat>events with text nodes, but the index is only consulted in the list-item else arm, which only runs in lists mode where no text nodes exist). LAZY #md:: mirrored browse-claude with _md_text_item + _md_node_item dispatch, wired into both _md_subtree_children loops. _preview_md_node unchanged (node_at_line finds the text node; byte slice = the run). VERIFIED: _lone_heading_child_id requires kind=='heading' so a (structurally-impossible) sole text child won't auto-expand; _resolve_anchor heading scan filters kind=='heading' so text nodes are excluded from title matching; _node_at_line by-line index includes text nodes so a digit anchor resolves to a text node sanely (valid id, preview=run). No runtime assert on len(events). Tests: updated test_ref_file_heading_node_has_subheading_children to expect the leading [text] row; added TestTextNodes (9 tests: dim text leaf first in headings-only, none in -l, contract fields, by_id count vs events, preview=run, title-anchor skips/line-anchor resolves, lone-text-child guard) + test_ref_document_node_shows_leading_text_leaf for the lazy doc-level path. browse-md 279 pass; deps test_md_doc+test_browse_claude_render 454 pass. {#779:comment:783}

* ## Ticket: Task: Fix: Alt+Right strands uncached boundary (agent) rows on loading… (Option B: never auto-expand boundaries) {#785}

      updated: 2026-06-08 00:17:15 UTC
      status: done
      created: 2026-06-08 00:04:30 UTC

  * ## Comments {#785:comments}

    * Root cause: _expand_subtree (src-tui/070-actions.py:742) adds boundary children to state.expanded but skips the recursion that would call ctx.expand — so an uncached boundary (browse-claude agent row) is marked expanded with no fetch dispatched. Only the cursor-prefetch (_update_children_for_cursor, cursor row only) fetches expanded-but-uncached rows, so it strands on '⧗ loading…' until the cursor lands on it. Option B fix: a boundary child is revealed but NEVER added to expanded by recursive expand (consistent for agent rows / sessions / file-docs). Touches framework path, so also covers browse-md file-docs. TDD: update test_actions boundary tests + add uncached-boundary regression; update boundary contract docstring in 030-data.py. {#785:comment:786}

    * Fixed (Option B) + reviewed (Opus/high: APPROVE). Boundary rows are now revealed but never auto-expanded by recursive expand. Changes: _expand_recursive/_expand_subtree (070-actions.py) skip boundary children entirely; Browser.expand_subtree._walk (040-state.py) skips descendant boundaries but still opens an explicitly-targeted root boundary; Item.boundary contract docstring (030-data.py) + both method docstrings + Context.expand_subtree pass-through updated. Tests: TestExpandRecursiveBoundary reframed + new test_alt_right_does_not_strand_uncached_boundary regression; TestExpandSubtreeBoundary flipped to descendant-not-expanded. Full suite green (3164, skipped=4). Not committed (awaiting user). {#785:comment:787}

  * ## Ticket: Framework groundwork: to_item, hashability choke point, ordered selection {#798}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-08 05:19:40 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-08 05:46:58 UTC

    Spec sections 5.1-5.3. Three framework changes in src-tui/030-data.py and src-tui/040-state.py:
    (1) to_item — "any hashable is an id": Item->identity; dict->Item(**x) (must carry 'id'); else Item(id=x). Remove the positional-tuple and str special-cases. expand_path_rows/_split_path_row stay string-based.
    (2) Hashability validation at an add-side index choke point _index_set(state, item) that wraps `state._items_by_id[item.id] = item` in try/except TypeError -> a clear message naming the id+type. NO hash() in Item.__post_init__ (keep only the title default). Route the recipe child-ingestion write(s) through it; do NOT over-refactor controlled internal synthetic-item sites.
    (3) Selection ordering — make state.selected insertion-ordered (dict-backed ordered set) and emit in selection order; delete sorted(self._state.selected) in _fire_selection_change.
    Tests: to_item(any-hashable)->Item(id=x) and to_item(dict); _index_set raises a clear error on an unhashable (list) id; on_selection_change emits in selection order. Gates every other ticket.

    * ## Comments {#798:comments}

      * Implemented spec 5.1-5.3. {#798:comment:806}

        (1) to_item (030-data.py): 'any hashable is an id' — Item->identity; dict->Item(**x) (known-fields/extras split kept, must carry 'id'); else Item(id=x). Removed the positional-tuple and str special-cases (both subsumed). expand_path_rows/_split_path_row untouched.
        (2) Hashability choke point (040-state.py): added _index_set(state, item) wrapping state._items_by_id[item.id]=item in try/except TypeError -> clear message naming id value + type. Routed _index_add_children (the recipe child-ingestion write, called from apply_children_results) through it. No hash() in Item.__post_init__. Left the 3 update_data op writes + the synthetic scope-stub write alone (controlled internal sites, per ticket).
        (3) Ordered selection (040-state.py): added OrderedSet(set) subclass (parallel insertion-ordered dict for __iter__; overrides add/discard/clear/update; subclasses set so isinstance/==-vs-plain-set/membership work — required by test_ctx_escape_hatches assertIsInstance(...,set) and the many assertEqual(selected,{...}) tests). State.selected default_factory=OrderedSet (bare annotation, not a string, to avoid dataclasses._is_type sys.modules lookup that fails under the tests' standalone file load). _do_select replace path rebuilds OrderedSet(ids). Deleted sorted() in _fire_selection_change -> now list(...) in insertion order.
        Tests added: test_item.py (any-hashable -> Item(id=x): int/None/tuple/empty-tuple/frozenset/frozen-dataclass; tuple is id not (id,title); unhashable list/set deferred-not-rejected); test_state_indexes.py TestIndexSetHashability (hashable writes through; unhashable list id -> clear error naming value+type; child-ingestion via _index_add_children raises clearly); test_lifecycle_hooks.py (on_selection_change payload in selection/insertion order, both replace and incremental).
        Migration fallout fixed (removed positional-tuple shorthand): updated 6 test files that used ('A',)/('A',None,None,'',True)/('c','C') Item shorthands to bare scalars / dicts / tuple-ids — test_browser.py (~104 lines, scripted for the uniform patterns; filters assertion ('f',) and op/(id,kind) tuples preserved), test_children_delivery.py, test_children_generator.py, test_background.py, test_workers.py.

* ## Ticket: Task: Width-dependent preview refetch via broadened on_resize {#827}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-10 00:09:23 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-10 01:59:56 UTC

  Make width-dependent previews (recipes that bake `line_width` into md2ansi output) recompute when the preview pane width changes from ANY source — terminal resize, split-layout change, list-ratio nudge. Mechanism (simple): broaden the existing `on_resize` callback to fire on any pane-layout change; recipes register a handler that calls `ctx.drop_preview_cache()`; the framework auto-refetches and `get_preview` re-renders at the new width. `get_preview` stays a plain `str` function reading `_BROWSER.preview_width` live — no return-wrapper, no per-Item stamp, no per-call recipe state (so no thread-safety issue). Full design (committed on this branch): `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-preview-resize-refetch-design.md`. Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): `/home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/preview-resize`.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Framework — broaden on_resize to fire on any layout change (TDD) {#828}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-10 00:09:23 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-10 00:23:01 UTC

    Broaden the existing `on_resize` callback to fire on ANY pane-layout change (terminal resize, split selector, list-ratio, pane toggles), not just SIGWINCH. Signature unchanged: `on_resize(ctx, cols, rows)`; no `reason` arg. Spec section "Framework change — broaden on_resize".

    Today on_resize fires only via `_fire_resize_if_pending` (`src-tui/040-state.py` ~line 5529), gated on the SIGWINCH-latched `_resize_pending` + a `(cols,rows) != _last_size` check — so a split/ratio change (cols/rows unchanged) never fires it.

    Approach: `_layout_for` (`src-tui/050-render.py`, where `browser._preview_width` is set ~line 2848) runs every paint. Record a layout SIGNATURE there — include `cols`, `rows`, and the pane geometry that matters (e.g. preview-pane width + children-pane width). Fire `on_resize(ctx, cols, rows)` from the run loop (where `_fire_resize_if_pending` is called today — AFTER render, not mid-paint) when the signature differs from the last-fired one; then update the stored signature. Including `cols`/`rows` in the signature preserves the old "terminal resize always fires" contract. Generalize `_fire_resize_if_pending` (fold in or sit beside it). Update the `on_resize` + `BrowserConfig.on_resize` docstrings to the broadened contract. No recipe uses `on_resize` today, so a superset of fires breaks no existing consumer.

    TDD — write FAILING framework tests FIRST. Grep `test/` for the existing `on_resize` test and extend it: on_resize fires on a `set_split` change and a `set_list_ratio` change with `cols`/`rows` unchanged; still fires on a terminal-size change; does NOT fire when the layout is unchanged across paints; an unset `on_resize` is a safe no-op.

    Rebuild `./build-tui.sh`, run `./run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8` (0 failures). Do NOT touch recipes (separate tickets). Do NOT commit — leader commits after review.

    * ## Comments {#828:comments}

      * Implemented TDD. Framework: _layout_for (050-render) records browser._layout_sig = (cols, rows, preview_rect, children_rect) every paint using RAW term_size() cols/rows (unclamped, so no-tty (0,0) still trips the garbage-dims guard). Renamed _fire_resize_if_pending -> _fire_resize_if_layout_changed (040-state): fires on_resize(ctx,cols,rows) when _layout_sig != _last_resize_sig, then advances the baseline (so an unchanged paint never re-fires -> no loop). Removed _resize_pending/_last_size (vestigial; the two SIGWINCH blocks now just force the redraw, which recomputes the signature with new dims). Updated on_resize method docstring + added a BrowserConfig.on_resize Args entry to the broadened contract. Tests: rewrote TestOnResize (test_lifecycle_hooks) to drive set_split/set_list_ratio via the real post->drain path + _layout_for to refresh the sig; all 9 failed RED on the missing method/attr, pass GREEN after. Covers: fires on terminal-size change; fires on split change (cols/rows unchanged); fires on list-ratio change (cols/rows unchanged, via preview height in 'h'); no fire when layout unchanged; no re-fire loop; unset handler no-op; no-layout-yet no-op; no-tty (0,0) no-op; handler exception routed to error + baseline still advances (no error-spam loop). Note: preview-rect signature requires show_preview=True (headless default is False since get_preview is None) — tests set it; recipes always have a preview. build-tui.sh + run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8 = 97 modules, 0 failures. {#828:comment:832}

  * ## Ticket: Task: browse-claude — register on_resize drop_preview_cache (+ integration test) {#829}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-10 00:09:23 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-10 01:57:15 UTC

    Per spec section "Recipe change — register the handler". In `recipes/browse-claude`, add to the `BrowserConfig(...)`: `on_resize=lambda ctx, cols, rows: ctx.drop_preview_cache(),`. That is the ONLY production change — `get_preview` / `_md_voice` already read `_BROWSER.preview_width` live and return `str`; leave them untouched. On a layout change the framework fires on_resize → the handler drops the preview cache → the framework refetches → `get_preview` re-renders at the new width.

    Depends on the framework ticket (broadened `on_resize` must fire on split/ratio).

    TDD — integration test FIRST (tmux): a markdown/voice preview with a TABLE body — md2ansi table layout is NOT re-flowable by the framework's generic display-wrap, so a frozen cache is observably wrong (loose prose gets re-flowed for free and wouldn't discriminate). Assert the table re-renders to the new width after BOTH (i) a terminal resize AND (ii) a split toggle (`alt-1`/`alt-2`); the split-toggle leg is the guard a SIGWINCH-only fix would fail. Confirm it FAILS without the `on_resize` registration and passes with it. Add to `test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py`. A small unit test that the registered handler calls `drop_preview_cache` is optional.

    Run via `./run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8` (0 failures). Do NOT commit.

    * ## Comments {#829:comments}

      * Implemented per spec. Production change (recipes/browse-claude, ~line 6512, alongside on_expand/on_children_loaded/on_scope_change): on_resize=lambda ctx, cols, rows: ctx.drop_preview_cache() (+ explanatory comment). No other edits; no import needed (ctx.drop_preview_cache is a Context method); get_preview/_md_voice untouched (already read _BROWSER.preview_width live, return str). {#829:comment:833}

        TDD integration test (test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py, TestBrowseClaudePreviewResize): fixture session whose cursor voice is a 2-col md TABLE with long wrappable cells (natural ~113 cols). Discriminator = the table's LEFT-COLUMN width (index of first interior tee minus the left border) — a pure md2ansi-layout property the framework's generic display-wrap cannot move (display-wrap only folds whole logical lines). Baseline at 120 cols = 56. Covers BOTH legs: (i) terminal resize 120->80; (ii) split toggle alt-1 (vertical, narrow right-hand preview, NO SIGWINCH). Pumps the loop with redraw() between the layout change and the assert (the broadened on_resize fires off the PREVIOUS paint's layout sig, so drop+refetch lands a couple of iterations later; with no input the loop blocks on read_key between).
        Observed (settled left-col width): WITH handler -> resize:38, split-toggle:38 (both < 56 baseline => md2ansi re-ran at new width). WITHOUT handler (line stashed) -> resize:56, split-toggle:56 (frozen; test fails 'left-column width stayed 56 (baseline 56)'). Both legs fail independently without the line, proving the split-toggle leg is the SIGWINCH-only-fix guard.
        Optional unit test (test/unit/test_browse_claude_render.py, TestVoiceOnlyFilter.test_on_resize_drops_preview_cache): source-inspection of the exact registration (the file's convention for config bindings — main() isn't run under unit test) + a spy-ctx behavioural check that the handler shape calls drop_preview_cache(None).
        Full suite: ./run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8 => 97 modules, 0 failures, exit 0. Tree left dirty (no commit).
