  * ## Ticket: Task: Framework — on_resize must fire promptly (self-completing) after a layout change {#834}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-10 00:55:19 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-10 01:57:15 UTC

    Follow-up to #828, surfaced by #829. The broadened `on_resize` does NOT fire promptly: after a terminal resize OR a split/ratio change the preview stays stale until the user's NEXT keypress (or a worker post, e.g. the ~5s tail tick). The #829 tmux integration test had to inject `redraw()` (Ctrl-L) to make the preview update — and tmux drives the real binary, so that lag is real behaviour, not a test artifact.

    Root cause: `_fire_resize_if_layout_changed` (run loop ~line 7087) reads `_layout_sig`, but `_layout_sig` is computed during `render` (~line 7120), which runs AFTER the fire check in the SAME iteration. So a layout change painted in iteration K is only visible to the fire check in K+1 — and nothing wakes the loop for K+1 (the SIGWINCH/keypress wake was consumed reaching K; `read_key` then blocks). The old `_resize_pending` latch fired in K because it was set PRE-render (in the `g_resize_flag` handling), reachable by the same wake; the sig-based trigger moved it post-render. The comment at ~7082-7086 ("fires on the next drain like the old latch") is the bug — the old latch's drain WAS woken; this one isn't.

    Fix: make the `on_resize` fire self-complete after a layout change with NO user input. Minimal option: after the render block, when `on_resize` is set and `_layout_sig != _last_resize_sig`, call `notify_wake()` so the loop runs the iteration where 7087 fires — the fire posts the recipe's `drop_preview_cache` (its `post` wakes the loop again for the refetch), and the refetch delivery wakes it once more for the corrected repaint, all via the existing wake/self-pipe mechanism. Alternative: fire `on_resize` right after `render` in the same iteration (using the fresh sig) rather than at the pre-render settle pass. Pick the cleaner. Guard against a busy/infinite wake loop (the baseline advances on fire, so once fired `_layout_sig == _last_resize_sig` and no further wake is emitted). Preserve the accepted startup-fire behaviour and the `on_cursor_change`-fires-last ordering contract.

    TDD — END-TO-END test FIRST, exercising the REAL run loop (a headless Browser driving the actual loop, or a pty), NOT manual `drain_main_queue`/`redraw` pumping: register an `on_resize` that records its calls (and/or drops a cache); simulate a terminal-size change and a split change; assert `on_resize` fires AND a re-render completes WITHOUT any injected keypress/redraw. This is the lesson from the #828 miss — do not validate via a pumped/fake drive. Also TIGHTEN the #829 browse-claude integration test to remove its manual `redraw()` pump so it guards the prompt self-completion (if the pump is still needed for unrelated settle reasons, document precisely why).

    Rebuild `./build-tui.sh`; run `./run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8` (0 failures). Do NOT commit — leader commits after review.

    * ## Comments {#834:comments}

      * Fixed. Root cause confirmed: _fire_resize_if_layout_changed runs in the settle pass (top of loop) against the PREVIOUS paint's _layout_sig; render recomputes _layout_sig AFTER it in the same iteration K, so the fire for K's layout change can only happen in K+1 — but nothing woke K+1 (the SIGWINCH/keypress wake was consumed reaching K; read_key then blocks). Fix (src-tui/040-state.py, run loop right AFTER the render block): when self._on_resize is not None and self._layout_sig != self._last_resize_sig, call notify_wake() (one line + comment). That makes read_key return _notify -> continue -> K+1 runs the fire. Self-completing wake chain: fire posts the recipe's drop_preview_cache (its post wakes again), the refetch's get_preview re-renders at the new width, the worker delivery wakes once more for the corrected repaint. Busy-loop guard: the fire advances _last_resize_sig to equal _layout_sig, so the next paint's sig is equal and the post-render check emits no further wake (verified: idle holds steady, no runaway). Ordering + startup preserved: no new fire added to the settle pass (on_resize stays first, on_cursor_change last); startup fire still fires once with no spurious wake (sig==last after its render). Also fixed the wrong 'fires on next drain like the old latch' comment + the two g_resize_flag block comments. TDD END-TO-END (no drain/redraw pumping): new test/ui/recipes/on_resize_probe.py (on_resize bumps a counter + drops cache; get_preview reports FIRES=n W=preview_width) + test/ui/test_resize.py::TestOnResizeSelfCompletes drives the REAL binary via tmux: resize 120->80 and alt-1 split, asserting FIRES advances AND the preview re-fetches at the new width with NO keypress/redraw, plus an idle no-busy-loop check. FAILS-before (times out waiting for FIRES=2 W=80; verified by temporarily disabling the wake) / PASSES-after. Tightened #829: test/ui/test_recipe_browse_claude.py::TestBrowseClaudePreviewResize _settle_left_col no longer injects redraw() (Ctrl-L) — it now guards self-completion; passes without the pump (traced end-to-end: resize -> FIRE cols=80 -> _md_voice width=80 -> table reflows to left-col 38 with no input). Suites: ./run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8 = 97 modules 0 fail; ./run-tests.sh = 3149 tests OK (skipped=4). New UI test non-flaky across 5 reruns. {#834:comment:835}

* ## Ticket: Task: browse-git — delta side-by-side at >=160 + conditional cache-drop on resize {#838}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-10 03:52:28 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-10 04:05:33 UTC

  Add delta side-by-side diffs for wide preview panes in `recipes/browse-git`, and drop the preview cache on resize only when it matters. Builds on the merged broadened-`on_resize` / `drop_preview_cache` mechanism (browse-git is its first *conditional* consumer). Recipe-only — no framework change. Work on branch `main` in `/home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui` (small change, no worktree).

  1. HAVE_DELTA global: add `HAVE_DELTA = shutil.which('delta')` at module load (next to `_MD_COLOR` / `_browser`) and use it instead of calling `shutil.which('delta')` per render — replace the existing `shutil.which('delta')` call in `_colorize_diff` (~line 331). Leave the unrelated `shutil.which('git')` gate in `main()` alone.

  2. `_colorize_diff` (~line 313) — side-by-side at >=160: inside the `if HAVE_DELTA:` block, after `width = (_browser.preview_width if _browser else 0) or 80`, append `--side-by-side` and `--line-fill-method=spaces` to the delta argv when `width >= 160`. `--width <width>` is ALREADY passed in both modes (delta splits it into two columns for side-by-side) — do NOT add a width arg. Below 160 the command is byte-for-byte unchanged.

  3. `on_resize` — conditional cache drop, registered on the `BrowserConfig` (~line 1591). Add a module global `_prev_pw = None` (main-thread only; `on_resize` fires from the run loop) and the handler:

         def _on_resize(ctx, cols, rows):
             global _prev_pw
             cur = ctx.preview_width or 0
             prev, _prev_pw = _prev_pw, cur
             if (prev is not None and cur != prev and HAVE_DELTA
                     and (cur >= 160 or prev >= 160)):
                 ctx.drop_preview_cache()

     Register `on_resize=_on_resize`. Drop iff: delta available AND the preview width actually changed AND side-by-side is/was active (cur or prev >= 160). First fire (prev None) just sets the baseline — no drop (the startup preview is already at the current width). Same-width fires (height-only resizes, which the broadened on_resize also produces) and both-<160 fires do NOT drop. Crossing 160 up (150->170) / down (170->150), and side-by-side width changes (both >=160, different, e.g. 170->200), DO drop.

  4. TDD — failing tests FIRST (browse-git's unit test file, likely `test/unit/test_browse_git.py`):
     - `_colorize_diff`: patch `HAVE_DELTA = True` + capture/mock `subprocess.run` → argv contains `--side-by-side` and `--line-fill-method=spaces` iff `width >= 160` (assert at width 159, 160, 200); with `HAVE_DELTA = False` it returns the raw git-colored input and spawns nothing.
     - `_on_resize`: spy ctx → `drop_preview_cache` called in exactly the right cells across (delta on/off) x (first fire / same width / both<160 / cross-up 150->170 / cross-down 170->150 / wider-both 170->200); `_prev_pw` advances each call. Reset `_prev_pw` / patch `HAVE_DELTA` per test.
     - Integration (tmux, if practical): a wide diff renders side-by-side; a resize across 160 re-renders with no keypress (self-completes via the merged on_resize fix).

  Run `./run-tests-parallel.sh -j 8` (and `./run-tests.sh` for the canonical pass). Recipe-only → no rebuild strictly needed; the runner handles it. Do NOT commit — leader commits after review.

  * ## Comments {#838:comments}

    * Done (recipe-only, branch main, tree left dirty for leader). {#838:comment:839}

      Recipe (recipes/browse-git):
      - HAVE_DELTA = shutil.which('delta') probed once at load (next to _MD_COLOR); _colorize_diff now gates on HAVE_DELTA instead of a per-render shutil.which. The shutil.which('git') gate in main() is untouched.
      - _colorize_diff: at width>=160 appends --side-by-side + --line-fill-method=spaces to the delta argv; --width is passed once in both modes (delta splits it). <160 is byte-for-byte unchanged.
      - _prev_pw=None module global + _on_resize(ctx,cols,rows): drops the preview cache iff delta available AND width changed AND (cur>=160 or prev>=160); first fire (prev None) is baseline-only. Registered on BrowserConfig as on_resize=_on_resize.
      Tests (TDD, failed before / pass after):
      - test_browse_git.py TestColorizeDiff: existing 3 tests retargeted from patching shutil to patching HAVE_DELTA; new argv asserts at width 159 (no sbs flags, --width 159), 160 + 200 (both flags, --width once); no-delta path returns raw input and spawns nothing.
      - TestOnResize: 7-case drop matrix (first/same/both<160/cross-up 150->170/cross-down 170->150/wider 170->200 + a full no-delta sweep); _prev_pw advances each fire.
      - test_recipe_browse_git.py TestBrowseGitSideBySide (skip if no delta): wide(170) launch renders side-by-side (unchanged context line duplicated across columns); resize 120->170->120 reflows with NO keypress.
      Suite: ./run-tests.sh -> 3179 OK (skipped=4). ./run-tests-parallel.sh -j8 -> 98 modules pass; one run hit the KNOWN test.ui.test_recipe_browse_claude parallel-load flake (passes in isolation: 40 OK) — unrelated to this change.
      Surprising: the resize integration test was deterministically failing until I made it wait for the launch-width diff to actually paint before resizing — that forces the baseline on_resize fire to land first, so the resize is a real width change (not the baseline-only first fire). This validates the ticket's 'first fire = baseline, no drop' design rather than contradicting it.

* ## Ticket: stdio/tty: terminal separation + content channels {#849}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-10 03:34:08 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-12 04:27:00 UTC

  Umbrella epic. Make browse-tui usable as a Unix filter: content on
  stdin/stdout, the UI on the controlling terminal. Two stages. Design spec:
  docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-terminal-stdio-separation-design.md

  * ## Ticket: Task: Stage 1 — separate terminal from stdin/stdout {#850}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-10 03:34:08 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-10 06:18:36 UTC

    Route all UI I/O through a dedicated terminal device so the terminal layer
    no longer rides on sys.stdin/sys.stdout. Includes the refactor and its
    natural outcome: the print-exit result becomes cleanly capturable
    (sel=$(browse-tui ...)). See spec sections 3-8.

    Done when: existing unit + UI suites pass; new separation tests pass; the
    binary is rebuilt (build-tui.sh); the capturable-result contract holds.

    * ## Ticket: Task: Terminal device core (020-terminal.py) {#851}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-10 03:34:08 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-10 03:59:31 UTC

      Introduce the terminal-device abstraction and retarget all I/O off
      sys.stdin/sys.stdout. Spec 3.1-3.5, 3.8.

      - Globals _tty_fd_in / _tty_fd_out / _tty_writer.
      - term_init(tty_path=None) resolution (spec 3.2): '-' -> fd 0/1 + reuse
        sys.stdout; a path -> open O_RDWR; None -> open /dev/tty; open failure
        -> raise a clean error (no traceback). Opened fd is O_CLOEXEC and owned
        (closed on term_restore); std fds are never closed.
      - _tty_writer: os.fdopen(_tty_fd_out,'w',encoding='utf-8',newline='') for
        single-device, sys.stdout for --tty -. write()/flush()/.buffer go
        through it. Preserve batch-write + one-flush-per-frame and the DEC 2026
        begin_sync/end_sync bracketing.
      - read_key/input_ready/select + raw-mode termios target _tty_fd_in; the
        self-pipe _notify_r is still selected alongside it.
      - SIGWINCH/SIGTSTP/SIGCONT + term_suspend/term_resume write via
        _tty_writer, termios on _tty_fd_in; keep the _handle_sigtstp
        bytecode-checkpoint trailing-call subtlety.
      - term_size queries os.get_terminal_size(_tty_fd_in) then /dev/tty then
        (80,24); drop the 3-std-fd probe.
      - Expose a child-fd accessor returning (_tty_fd_in, _tty_fd_out) for the
        shell-out ticket.

      Done when: build-tui.sh rebuilds; existing unit + UI suites pass. RISK:
      verify /dev/tty resolves to the pane pty under the tmux fixture (spec
      5/8); if not, adapt the fixture (e.g. pass --tty -) here.

      * ## Comments {#851:comments}

        * Implemented terminal-device core in src-tui/020-terminal.py (spec 3.1-3.5, 3.8). {#851:comment:836}

          CONTRACTS for downstream tickets:
          - term_init(tty_path=None): None -> open /dev/tty O_RDWR; a path -> open that device O_RDWR; '-' -> std streams (fd 0 in / fd 1 out, _tty_writer=sys.stdout). Opened fd (path/None) is O_CLOEXEC + owned (closed on term_restore via the owning writer); std fds never closed. Open failure raises a clean SystemExit (no traceback): /dev/tty case -> 'browse-tui: no controlling terminal; pass --tty - to run over stdin/stdout'; explicit path -> 'browse-tui: cannot open terminal <path>'. --tty - with piped (non-tty) std streams: the raw-mode termios.error is caught and re-raised as SystemExit('browse-tui: not a terminal'). #852 wires the --tty flag to this signature; the call site in 040-state.py is still bare term_init() and stays correct (default /dev/tty).
          - Child-fd accessor: term_child_fds() -> (_tty_fd_in, _tty_fd_out). Single-device: both = the one O_RDWR fd; --tty -: (0,1). For the #853 shell-out handoff.
          Globals: _tty_fd_in / _tty_fd_out / _tty_writer (+ _tty_owns_fd for restore). Helper _resolve_terminal(tty_path) sets them. _tty_writer = os.fdopen(fd,'w',encoding='utf-8',newline='') single-device, .buffer used for the byte-level alt-screen/mouse sequences (mirrors old sys.stdout/.buffer split).
          Retargeted: write()/flush() -> _tty_writer; _enter_raw/_leave_raw termios on _tty_fd_in + bytes via _tty_writer.buffer; read_key/input_ready select+os.read on _tty_fd_in (self-pipe _notify_r still selected alongside); term_size -> os.get_terminal_size(_tty_fd_in) then /dev/tty then (80,24), 3-std-fd probe dropped; term_restore closes owned device fd. PRESERVED verbatim: differential-render row batching + one-flush-per-frame, DEC 2026 begin_sync/end_sync, and the _handle_sigtstp bytecode-checkpoint trailing-call subtlety (signals/suspend/resume funnel through _enter_raw/_leave_raw, untouched).
          TMUX /dev/tty FINDING (the spec 5/8 risk): VERIFIED that /dev/tty inside a tmux pane resolves to the pane pty (wrote a marker to /dev/tty from inside the fixture pane; capture-pane saw it). So the expected case holds: default tty_path=None works under the fixture, snapshots unchanged, NO --tty - fixture fallback needed.
          TESTS: ./run-tests.sh green (3155 passed, 4 skipped). UI suite alone: 188 passed, 4 skipped (skips are live-JIRA-MCP env-gated, pre-existing, unrelated). build-tui.sh rebuilds cleanly.
          Test-harness follow-on (NOT a product change): 020-terminal.py DID have unit coverage (test_terminal_row_shim + render/actions/pick/preview tests) that captured UI bytes by swapping sys.stdout. Since write() now targets _tty_writer, I retargeted those capture helpers to swap _term._tty_writer (a StringIO) instead -- the bytes' new home. Files: test/unit/test_terminal_row_shim.py, test_render_cache.py, test_render.py, test_actions.py, test_preview_render_cache.py, test_preview_tail.py. Also: test_pick._StubTerminal now also no-ops clear_columns (it reaches the real _term.write chain via context); test_actions read_key helper points _term._tty_fd_in at fd 0 (was relying on read_key reading sys.stdin.fileno()).
        * Review follow-up: guarded _leave_raw for idempotent term_restore (was AttributeError on double-restore); suspend/resume unaffected. {#851:comment:837}

    * ## Ticket: CLI wiring: --tty flag (080-cli.py) {#852}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-10 03:34:08 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-10 04:23:35 UTC

      Wire --tty and remove the old stdin-reopen hack. Spec 3.7, 3.8, 4.1.
      Depends on the core ticket (term_init signature).

      - Add --tty TTY_PATH (metavar TTY_PATH, default /dev/tty; sentinel '-' =
        std streams); run_tui passes it to term_init(tty_path=...).
      - Delete _reopen_stdin_from_tty; --root-cmd cat reads sys.stdin but no
        longer reopens.
      - Align _terminal_cols_for_auto to the /dev/tty-first policy (no
        std-fd-first probe).
      - Catch term_init's no-tty error: print 'browse-tui: no controlling
        terminal; pass --tty - to run over stdin/stdout' to stderr, exit
        non-zero.

      Done when: build rebuilds; ls | browse-tui --root-cmd cat ... works and
      sel=$(...) captures only the result.

      * ## Comments {#852:comments}

        * Implemented in src-tui/080-cli.py (CLI/TUI invocation path; recipe --tty auto-detect is #853). {#852:comment:838}

          WHAT CHANGED:
          - argparse: added --tty TTY_PATH (metavar TTY_PATH, default /dev/tty; sentinel '-' = std streams), placed by --split-type in build_argparser. Shows in --help.
          - Deleted _reopen_stdin_from_tty entirely. --root-cmd cat still reads sys.stdin.buffer.read() but no longer reopens fd 0 — keys come from the terminal device (_tty_fd_in, default /dev/tty) via #851's core, so consuming a piped stdin leaves fd 0 alone. No other references existed (only the def + its one call site).
          - _terminal_cols_for_auto(tty_path=None, default=80): realigned to the resolved-device policy (spec §3.5/§1.4). tty_path None/path -> probe THAT device via TIOCGWINSZ ioctl; tty_path '-' -> probe std fds (fd 0 then 1, since the UI rides on them). DROPPED the old std-fd-first fallback (the fd 0/1/2 loop) in /dev/tty mode — that was the pre-#852 #167 fix; it's intentionally gone so the chosen width tracks the real UI device, not a piped stdout/stderr. shutil () + stty(device) fallbacks kept. run_tui passes getattr(args,'tty',None) into it.
          NO-TTY ERROR — decision: PROPAGATE, do not relocate (minimal). #851's term_init already raises clean SystemExit(str) (no traceback): default /dev/tty unopenable -> 'browse-tui: no controlling terminal; pass --tty - to run over stdin/stdout'; --tty - non-tty -> 'browse-tui: not a terminal'; bad path -> 'cannot open terminal'. Verified term_init() is called OUTSIDE run()'s try/finally (040-state.py:6968, the term_restore try starts at :7023) and nothing in run_tui/main wraps it in except Exception (SystemExit is BaseException anyway). So it propagates run->run_tui->main->090-main.py sys.exit() cleanly. Did NOT relocate the branded message to the CLI layer (would churn 020-terminal.py, out of scope; #851 reviewer accepted it there given clean propagation). Added an explanatory comment at the b.run() call site instead of any code.
          #853 BOUNDARY (important): run() calls bare term_init() and I must not touch it (#853 owns making run() read --tty from sys.argv). So a CLI '--tty -' / '--tty PATH' does NOT yet reach term_init — verified: 'printf|browse-tui --root-cmd cat --tty -' under a pipe currently errors with 'no controlling terminal' (run() defaulted to /dev/tty) rather than 'not a terminal'. The core IS correct: term_init('-') on a pipe -> SystemExit('browse-tui: not a terminal') (tested directly). Once #853 lands, the CLI --tty rides through (argparse does not strip it from sys.argv). The default /dev/tty path works end-to-end today, satisfying the Done-when.

    * ## Ticket: Recipe entry: run() --tty auto-detect (040-state.py) {#853}

        * Implemented in src-tui/040-state.py run() only. After the existing -h/--help short-circuit, added a small inline scan of sys.argv[1:] for --tty: matches the bare token '--tty' (value = next token; trailing --tty with no value is ignored, not a crash) and the '--tty=VALUE' form (split on '='); last occurrence wins. Value passed verbatim as term_init(tty_path) ('-' -> std streams, a path -> that device, None -> /dev/tty default). No argparse in run(), no new run() parameter (YAGNI). Same mechanism serves both recipes (./recipe --tty -) and the CLI (argparse leaves --tty in sys.argv, so the value reaches term_init and now agrees with #852's width probe). Touched no other src-tui module. {#853:comment:840}

Real-pty smoke (pty.fork, --tty -): entered alt screen, rendered all rows over the std streams, responded to 'q', left alt screen cleanly, rc=1. Default /dev/tty path covered by the tmux UI suite (panes are real controlling terminals on /dev/tty).

    * ## Ticket: Task: _leave_raw must skip teardown when raw was never entered (--tty - stdout leak) {#841}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-10 04:47:27 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-10 04:53:59 UTC

      Review follow-up surfaced by #853; the defect is in #851's terminal layer.
      On the `--tty -`-over-a-pipe ERROR path, ~30 teardown bytes
      (\033[?1006l\033[?1000l\033[?25h\033[?1049l) leak to stdout before the clean
      `not a terminal` SystemExit.

      Cause: `_enter_raw` raises at `termios.tcgetattr` (pipe) BEFORE writing any
      enter-bytes; `term_init` catches it and runs `term_restore` -> `_leave_raw`,
      but `_leave_raw`'s idempotency guard tests `_tty_writer is None`, and in
      `--tty -` mode `_tty_writer` IS `sys.stdout` (not None) -> it emits teardown
      bytes to stdout.

      Fix (020-terminal.py): track whether raw/alt-screen was actually entered
      (e.g. an `_in_raw` flag set True at the end of a successful `_enter_raw`,
      False at the end of `_leave_raw`); guard `_leave_raw` to no-op when raw was
      never entered. This subsumes the existing `_tty_writer is None` guard (more
      precise) and stops the leak. Preserve suspend/resume + SIGTSTP/SIGCONT, which
      rely on `_leave_raw`/`_enter_raw` round-trips.

      Done when: a piped `--tty -` invocation writes ZERO bytes to stdout (only the
      stderr `not a terminal` message, exit 1); build rebuilds; the suspend UI test
      + terminal unit tests pass.

      * ## Comments {#841:comments}

        * Fixed in src-tui/020-terminal.py via a module-level _in_raw flag (init False alongside _saved_termios). Set True at the END of a fully successful _enter_raw (after enter-bytes flushed); set False at the END of _leave_raw (after teardown emitted). Changed _leave_raw guard from '_tty_writer is None' to 'not _in_raw' -- subsumes the old guard (term_restore's _leave_raw clears _in_raw before the writer is nulled) and skips teardown on the --tty - error path where _enter_raw raises at tcgetattr before entering raw. {#841:comment:842}

          LEAK GONE: printf 'a\nb\n' | ./browse-tui --root-cmd cat --tty - -> stdout 0 bytes (was ~30), stderr exactly 'browse-tui: not a terminal' (27 bytes), rc=1.
          Idempotency (headless pty): enter=30B sets _in_raw True; 1st _leave_raw emits 30B (1049l) sets False; 2nd _leave_raw 0B no raise; suspend/resume round-trip 30/30B. Suspend UI tests (test_ctrl_z_then_fg_round_trip, test_sigstop_then_sigcont_via_fg_pid) pass via real tmux; terminal unit tests (test_terminal_row_shim, test_render_cache) pass. Full ./run-tests-parallel.sh: 97/97 modules pass, rc=0. SIGTSTP/SIGCONT bytecode-checkpoint logic untouched.

    * ## Ticket: Task: Remove --tty - pager degrade safeguard {#848}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-10 06:13:24 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-10 06:17:10 UTC

      non-interactive use — they can be reconfigured or handle it themselves.

      - 060-context.py page(): remove the `if non_headless and term_is_std_streams():`
        degrade branch. page() then always (non-headless) runs the normal
        suspend -> Popen(pager, stdin=PIPE, stdout=out_fd, stderr=out_fd) -> resume
        path, including --tty - mode. The suspend/resume bracket keeps the screen
        safe; whatever the pager does without a private /dev/tty is the user's concern.
      - 020-terminal.py: remove term_is_std_streams() (now unused — verify no other callers).
      - test/ui/test_actions.py: remove test_tty_dash_page_degrade_is_non_corrupting
        (it tested the now-removed degrade).
      - docs/cli.md: revise the "--tty - limitation" note — drop the "we degrade /
        write text directly" claim; brief factual note that in --tty - mode the pager
        has no separate /dev/tty for keys, so configure $PAGER/$EDITOR for
        non-interactive use if needed (we do not special-case it).

      Done when: build rebuilds; full suite green; no term_is_std_streams refs remain; docs accurate.

    * ## Ticket: Task: fd hygiene at startup (020-terminal.py) {#856}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-11 23:11:50 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-11 23:32:32 UTC

      Spec §3.1 + §3.5 (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-stdio-content-channels-design.md).
      Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      In `term_init`, normal mode only (NOT `--tty -`; headless never calls term_init):
      - `os.isatty(0)` → `dup2(devnull_rd, 0)`: mid-session reads get EOF, keystrokes can't be stolen.
      - `os.isatty(1)` → save the real stdout fd (`os.dup(1)`), then `dup2(devnull_wr, 1)`:
        stray raw print() vanishes instead of painting the alt-screen; buffered output is
        written to the SAVED fd at teardown (next ticket).
      Pipes/files left untouched; fd 2 untouched. Zero setup cost when fds are not ttys.

      Expose the state to the rest of the framework via functions (same pattern as
      term_size/term_child_fds; consumer modules never touch privates): suggest
      `term_result_fd()` -> the fd final/buffered output must go to (the saved tty fd if
      stdout was a tty, else 1), plus whatever minimal flag the output ticket needs (e.g.
      "stdout was a tty"). term_restore should close the saved fd ONLY after the teardown
      dump (coordinate: this ticket adds save+accessor+restore plumbing; the output ticket
      owns the dump ordering).

      Verify (no change expected): run_external/page/action-cmd children still get the
      terminal via term_child_fds() — unaffected by fd 0/1 redirects.

      Tests (test/unit + pty where needed): tty-stdin → post-init read returns EOF; tty-stdout
      → fd1 writes vanish, term_result_fd() returns the live terminal; piped stdin/stdout →
      untouched, term_result_fd() == 1; --tty - → no redirects.

      * ## Comments {#856:comments}

        * Implemented fd hygiene in term_init (020-terminal.py), single-device mode only (gated on tty_path != '-'); headless never calls term_init. New private _redirect_std_fds(): tty fd0 -> dup2(/dev/null,0) so mid-session reads get EOF; tty fd1 -> save os.dup(1) (set_inheritable False) then dup2(/dev/null,1) so stray prints vanish. Pipes/files and fd2 untouched; zero cost when not ttys. Done AFTER _enter_raw succeeds so the --tty - piped error path never touches fd0/1. term_init resets the hygiene globals at entry (defensive close of stray saved fd -> no leak across repeated init/restore). {#856:comment:866}

          Accessors (same pattern as term_size/term_child_fds): term_result_fd() -> saved tty fd if stdout was a tty else 1; term_stdout_was_tty() -> the flag #857 needs. term_child_fds() verified unaffected (returns the device fd, not fd0/1) -> run_external/page/action-cmd children still get the terminal.
          term_restore coordination: deliberately does NOT close the saved fd (today's dump runs post-restore); _leave_raw exits alt-screen first so the saved fd is in normal scrollback ready for the write. #857 will fold dump+close into the teardown.
          Interim final-write routing (040-state.py run() teardown ~7262): when term_stdout_was_tty() the quit/print-exit output is written to term_result_fd() via a blocking full os.write (utf-8/surrogateescape) and the saved fd is then closed; otherwise unchanged sys.stdout.write (preserves headless StringIO-patch tests + pipe path). Added 'import os' to 040-state.py; wired term_result_fd/term_stdout_was_tty into test_main.py's symbol injection and the run() docstring.
          Tests: test/unit/test_terminal_fd_hygiene.py (5: tty-stdin EOF, tty-stdout vanish + result_fd reaches terminal, piped untouched + result_fd==1, --tty - no redirect, child_fds off std) via fork+pty; test/ui/test_fd_hygiene.py (2: print-exit selection lands in tty scrollback after teardown). Full suite green: 3191 tests OK (4 skipped) / 100 parallel modules PASS, 0 fail. Stage-1 TestTerminalSeparationErrors + test_main headless stay green.
        * Review (subagent): APPROVE. 2 nits deferred to #857 by design: (1) unify teardown drain encoding to one os.write utf-8/surrogateescape across tty+pipe branches; (2) reset _saved_stdout_fd/_stdout_was_tty globals once #857 owns dump+close. {#856:comment:867}

    * ## Ticket: Output channel: ctx.print + FIFO buffer + loop drain + teardown delivery {#857}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-11 23:11:50 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-12 00:18:03 UTC

      Spec §3.2, §3.5 (write side), D3, D7.
      Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      - `ctx.print(text, end='\n')` (060-context.py): mirrors builtin print; appends
        utf-8-encoded bytes to a Browser-level output buffer. Never blocks the UI.
      - `ctx.quit(code, output)` API unchanged; FIFO holds naturally: prints are drained /
        held in order, quit output is appended at quit time (or written after the print
        buffer at teardown — implementer's choice, as long as stream order is
        prints-then-quit-output).
      - stdout is a pipe/file: event loop (040-state.py) puts fd 1 in the select write-set
        ONLY while the buffer is non-empty and the channel is alive; drain with non-blocking
        os.write (O_NONBLOCK while draining; restore at teardown); partial writes fine.
        Write error (EPIPE etc.) → channel dead PERMANENTLY: drop buffer, remove fd from the
        loop forever, subsequent ctx.print() calls no-op. UI stays alive. At teardown, final
        drain is a blocking write of the remainder (skip if dead).
      - stdout was a tty (term_result_fd() from previous ticket): never in select; hold the
        whole session; at teardown AFTER terminal restore write the buffer to the saved fd
        (blocking), prints-then-quit-output, landing in normal scrollback (fzf model).
      - --tty - and headless: keep today's behavior (quit output via sys.stdout after
        restore); ctx.print buffers and flushes through the same teardown path.
      - Replace/route the current single write at src-tui/040-state.py:~7249 accordingly.

      Tests: FIFO order incl. quit output (pipe); slow consumer does not block the UI
      (ui/pty: tiny pipe, UI keeps responding while consumer stalls); EPIPE mid-run → UI
      alive, later prints no-op, clean exit; tty stdout (pty) → output appears after
      alt-screen teardown: prints then selection; headless path unchanged.

      * ## Comments {#857:comments}

        * Implemented the unified output channel (spec §3.2/§3.5/D3/D7). {#857:comment:868}

          DESIGN
          - Buffer: Browser-level bytearray (_out_buf) + threading.Lock; Browser.print(text, end='\n') (thread-safe section, Context.print pass-through) encodes utf-8+surrogateescape at append time and never blocks; quit output stays in _quit_output and is appended to the stream at teardown -> prints-then-quit-output FIFO by construction. print() from any thread / from on_quit hooks still lands (direct append, not post()), with notify_wake only when the channel is stream-live.
          - Routing flag: run() sets _out_stream_live = not headless and tty_path != '-' and not term_stdout_was_tty() (i.e. pipe/file stdout only).
          - Loop drain: read_key() in 020-terminal gained an optional write_fd watched for writability, returning '_writable' (same precedence model as '_notify'; zero-arg call preserved so existing test stubs keep working). run() passes write_fd=1 ONLY while stream-live + alive + buffer non-empty; '_writable' -> _drain_output(): O_NONBLOCK flipped lazily on first drain, partial writes leave the remainder buffered, BlockingIOError = backpressure (fd re-arms via select), any other OSError = channel dead permanently (buffer dropped, fd never re-added, print() no-ops, UI alive).
          - Teardown (_teardown_output, replaces the #856 interim block): restores fd-1 blocking mode; dead -> skip all; tty -> blocking dump of buffer+quit to term_result_fd() then NEW term_release_result_fd() in 020-terminal closes the saved fd + resets hygiene state (review nit 2: 040 never touches privates); pipe/file -> blocking os.write(1) of remainder+quit; headless/--tty - -> sys.stdout (StringIO-patch compat). fd-level writes use utf-8+surrogateescape on BOTH tty and pipe branches (review nit 1); teardown write errors swallowed (clean exit).
          TESTS (all green; full suite 3212 tests OK, 4 pre-existing env skips; 102 parallel modules PASS)
          - test/unit/test_output_channel.py (15): print semantics (end/str-coercion/surrogateescape round-trip/dead no-op/ctx pass-through), _drain_output vs a real pipe on fd 1 (full, >64KiB backpressure remainder, EPIPE death), teardown FIFO remainder+quit on pipe + nonblock restore, dead-skip, tty saved-fd dump + release, headless sys.stdout FIFO incl. on_quit-hook print, run()-loop write_fd/_writable contract.
          - test/unit/test_terminal_fd_hygiene.py: +term_release_result_fd close/reset/idempotency.
          - test/ui/test_output_channel.py (5, shipped binary): pty(--tty /dev/pts/N)+pipe harness — FIFO prints+big payload+quit output; stalled consumer (128KiB > pipe capacity, zero reads) with flash round-trips proving UI alive, remainder delivered by final blocking drain; EPIPE mid-run -> UI alive, later prints no-op, rc 0, no traceback; --tty - compat (prints flush via sys.stdout after leave-alt-screen, never mid-session); tmux tty scrollback prints-then-selection above sentinel (held, nothing surfaces mid-session). Recipe: test/ui/recipes/output_channel.py (p/b/m keys).
          - Decision: backpressure tests use payload > default pipe capacity (64KiB) instead of F_SETPIPE_SZ — same stall condition, no privilege/platform-sensitive fcntl.
          - Verified baseline: stashed change -> only the new tests fail (they genuinely test the feature); two named headless sys.stdout tests untouched and green.
          - Stale 'interim routing' wording in test/ui/test_fd_hygiene.py docstrings updated; binary rebuilt via build-tui.sh and verified current.
        * Review (subagent): APPROVE, no blocking/should-fix. 3 nits folded into the commit by leader: quit-bookkeeping comment wording (040), term_init stray-fd reclaim now calls term_release_result_fd() (020), module-header keyname list gains _writable (020). {#857:comment:869}

    * ## Ticket: Streaming input: on_stdin hook (raw + record modes) {#858}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-11 23:11:50 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-12 01:34:28 UTC

      Spec §3.4, §3.5 (read side), D6.
      Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      BrowserConfig (040-state.py) gains: `on_stdin` (callable|None), `stdin_delimiter`
      (str|None, default None), `stdin_bytes` (bool, default False). Document in the config
      docstring block alongside on_resize/on_quit.

      Loop integration: only when on_stdin is set and the stream has not ended, fd 0 joins
      the select read-set. O_NONBLOCK on fd 0 for the run phase only (pre-run ingest stays
      blocking). Read via `sys.stdin.buffer.read1(65536)` so BufferedReader read-ahead from a
      recipe's pre-run `sys.stdin.buffer` reads is served FIRST (lossless slurp-then-stream
      hand-off; spec §3.4). BlockingIOError → no data; b'' → EOF; OSError → error end.

      Hook contract — the framework ALWAYS passes all kwargs (no signature validation; that
      is epic #855):
          on_stdin(ctx, data, *, delimiter, is_eof, errno)
      - data: str by default via a codecs incremental utf-8 decoder (errors='replace';
        multibyte sequences split across chunks must decode correctly), bytes when
        stdin_bytes=True. NEVER None; may be empty.
      - raw mode (stdin_delimiter None): one call per chunk read, delimiter=''.
      - record mode: framework buffers partials; ONE complete record per call, delimiter
        stripped and passed in `delimiter` (multi-char delimiters supported); empty records
        preserved ("a\n\n" → "a", ""). EOF flushes the trailing unterminated record in the
        final call.
      - final call: is_eof=True, data = trailing partial or empty, delimiter=''.
      - error end: final call with numeric errno and is_eof=True. errno=0 on all other calls.
      - after EOF/error delivery: fd 0 leaves the read-set forever. No special-casing for
        fd0=/dev/null (tty stdin): first read returns EOF → EOF call → removed, same path.
      - hook exceptions: same swallow policy as other hooks (do not add new machinery).

      Tests: framing unit tests (multi-record chunk, record split across chunks, empty
      records, trailing partial, multi-char delimiter, bytes mode, multibyte utf-8 split
      across reads); e2e via the pty/headed harness: feed a pipe while the UI runs, hook
      upserts items, EOF call arrives, fd leaves the set; error path unit-tested (monkeypatch
      read1 raising OSError).

      * ## Comments {#858:comments}

        * Implemented per spec §3.4/§3.5 + D6. {#858:comment:870}

          Framework (src-tui):
          - 020-terminal.py: read_key gains aux_read_fd=None → returns '_stdin' when that fd is readable; same one-sentinel-per-call precedence model as '_writable'/'_notify' (sentinels first, terminal last); zero-arg path bit-identical.
          - 040-state.py: BrowserConfig gains on_stdin / stdin_delimiter / stdin_bytes (documented in field comments + the __init__ Args block alongside on_resize/on_quit; stdin_delimiter='' rejected with ValueError). Browser keeps a stdin-channel state block mirroring the output channel: _stdin_live (the exact select-membership window; never re-arms), _stdin_delim (utf-8-encoded in bytes mode — the documented matching rule), _stdin_rec_buf, _stdin_decoder (incremental utf-8, errors='replace', created only when the hook is set), _stdin_nonblock_set.
          - run(): arms at loop start via _arm_stdin_stream (gated on_stdin set + not headless + tty_path != '-'): O_NONBLOCK on fd 0 for the run phase only, then a residue drain — read1 loop serving BufferedReader read-ahead from pre-run sys.stdin.buffer ingest BEFORE the first select (select only sees the kernel buffer). Loop passes aux_read_fd=0 only while _stdin_live; '_stdin' → _pump_stdin() one chunk per wake (firehose still yields to renders/keys). finally: _teardown_stdin restores fd 0 blocking.
          - Delivery: _deliver_stdin_chunk (decode → raw per-chunk or record framing via split, partials buffered, empty records preserved, multi-char delimiters ok), _end_stdin (live=False first; decoder final-flush → U+FFFD for a torn trailing sequence; trailing record as data, delimiter='', is_eof=True, errno numeric on error end), _fire_on_stdin (always passes all kwargs; exceptions routed to error() like other hooks).
          Key decision — read1 EOF/EAGAIN ambiguity: on Python 3.10 BufferedReader.read1 on a non-blocking empty pipe returns b'' (swallows the raw would-block; bpo-13322), indistinguishable from EOF. The pump settles a falsy read1 against the raw fd (os.read(0)): BlockingIOError = no data (select re-arms), b'' = genuine EOF, bytes = raced-in data (delivered) — race-free and version-proof; safe because falsy read1 implies the buffer layer is empty.
          Tests (all green; full suite 104 modules pass):
          - test/unit/test_stdin_channel.py (20): record framing (multi-record chunk, split records, empty records, trailing partial, multi-char delimiter), bytes mode (NUL records, undecoded raw), incremental decoding (split multibyte raw+record, invalid → U+FFFD, EOF flush of torn sequence), end shape (errno end, errno-None → EIO fallback), hook-exception swallow, ''-delimiter ValueError, pump vs real pipe on fd 0 (instant EOF never joins the set + blocking restored, would-block keeps live + one chunk per wake + ended-guard, arming serves residue before kernel data), run()-loop integration (aux_read_fd exactly while live: [0, 0, None]).
          - test/ui/test_stdin_channel.py (2) + recipes/stdin_stream.py: shipped binary on a private pty, stdin a test-owned pipe — records grow the tree live, EOF flushes the trailing partial, idle CPU ~0 after EOF (fd left the set; 5/5 stable); compose hand-off (preloaded pipe fully dragged into BufferedReader by a pre-run read(4) → kernel-empty arming still delivers the exact remainder, stream continues for live data).
          - test/unit/test_output_channel.py: #857's fake_read_key stub updated to the new read_key signature (aux_read_fd=None).
        * Contract change from user review: stdin_delimiter type must MATCH the data mode (str mode -> str, stdin_bytes=True -> bytes; mismatch raises ValueError at config time). Supersedes the implemented "encode str delimiter as utf-8 for bytes mode" rule. Spec amended; fix to be applied together with review findings before commit. {#858:comment:871}

        * Second contract change from user review: rename config field stdin_bytes -> stdin_raw_bytes. Spec amended. To be applied with the delimiter type-match rule + review findings before commit. {#858:comment:872}

        * Review follow-ups applied (2 blocking + 2 contract changes + nit 4): {#858:comment:874}

          Blocking 1 — keyboard starvation under saturated stdin: read_key now gives the TERMINAL fd priority over aux_read_fd ('_stdin' returned only when fd 0 is ready AND the terminal is not; 020-terminal.py). Keys are scarce, stdin loses nothing — its data stays buffered for the next select. '_writable' precedence unchanged per #857 review. read_key + _pump_stdin docstrings now state the fairness rule. Regression e2e: TestSaturatedProducer.test_keyboard_quit_wins_under_stdin_flood — 'yes'-flooded stdin while the UI runs on a pty, 'q' must exit within 5s (verified RED on the pre-fix binary: it hung; the red run actually surfaced BOTH blockers — pre-fix the unbounded arming drain never even returned under the flood). Stable 3/3 post-fix.
          Blocking 2 — unbounded arming drain: _pump_stdin(drain=True) is now BOUNDED — after each delivered chunk it returns if self._quit_requested or a zero-timeout select shows kernel-side data (the loop wakes for that on its own; per-wake reads go through read1 so residue-first ordering holds). Arming no longer synchronously ingests whole files / saturated pipes; only BufferedReader residue is served outside select, per spec. import select added to 040-state.py. test_arming_serves_buffer_residue_before_kernel_data adjusted: residue at arming, kernel bytes on the next pump.
          Contract A — delimiter type must match the data mode: stdin_delimiter is str in text mode, bytes in raw-bytes mode; mismatch or empty (either type) is a construction-time ValueError. The implicit encode-str-as-utf-8 rule is GONE; the delimiter kwarg the hook sees is the configured value ('' / b'' typed to match data in raw mode + final call). Field annotation now str | bytes | None. New test test_invalid_delimiters_rejected_at_construction covers all 4 invalid shapes ('', b'', bytes-in-str-mode, str-in-bytes-mode).
          Contract B — stdin_bytes renamed stdin_raw_bytes everywhere (config field, _stdin_raw_bytes attr, docstrings, comments, tests; no alias). grep: zero hits left in code/tests — only plan-history text and the leader-updated design doc remain.
          Nit 4 — added TestRunLoopStdinIntegration.test_tty_dash_never_arms: with '--tty -' in argv the hook never arms (seen == [None], no hook calls, no O_NONBLOCK).
          Tests: unit test_stdin_channel 21 (was 20), e2e test_stdin_channel 3 (was 2); full suite ./run-tests-parallel.sh 104 modules ALL PASS; binary rebuilt.

    * ## Ticket: browse-md -: document from stdin {#860}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-11 23:11:50 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-12 01:56:34 UTC

      Spec §3.7.
      Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      recipes/browse-md: a single argument '-' → slurp sys.stdin (text, to EOF) before run
      and browse that document (heading tree + previews, same as a file). Title shows
      '(stdin)'. Bare `browse-md` keeps browsing the current directory — unchanged. On a tty,
      '-' blocks cat-like until ^D (standard; do not babysit). Empty input → behave as an
      empty document (match whatever an empty .md file does today). Anchor deep-links ('-#x')
      out of scope.

      Editor/source actions that need a file path: disable or degrade gracefully for the
      stdin document (no temp-file machinery).

      Tests: pipe a doc in, assert tree + preview; bare invocation still browses cwd.

      * ## Comments {#860:comments}

        * Implemented browse-md - (document from stdin), spec 3.3/3.7. {#860:comment:877}

          Seam: a module sentinel path _STDIN_PATH (under cwd) + global _STDIN_TEXT. main() slurps sys.stdin.read() for a lone '-' positional BEFORE b.run()/term_init (so a tty blocks cat-like until ^D; no isatty babysitting), stashes the text on the sentinel, and sets _INPUT_FILES=[(_STDIN_PATH,'')]. _reparse() serves _STDIN_TEXT for that path instead of open() and titles the root '(stdin)'. Downstream the sentinel is just another file path -> heading tree + previews come for free from the existing _FILES/_BY_ID machinery (no get_doc). Single doc -> existing single-file auto-expand path. Ctrl-R re-serves the same parse (stdin one-shot). NO temp files.
          '-#x': not a lone '-', starts with '-', so the existing leftover-option scan in main() rejects it with exit 2 ('unrecognised option: -#x') before any stdin read — least-code, never half-works. Verified via binary.
          V/E (on-disk source) on the stdin doc: degrade gracefully — ctx.flash('no source file...') and return (built-in v/e on preview text still work). Ref-following suppressed for the stdin root via a single guard in _file_has_existing_ref (the one chokepoint both the _reparse arrow and the get_children umbrella gate on) so no half-broken References umbrella appears.
          Files: recipes/browse-md (seam + main branch + V/E guard + ref guard + docstring/usage/_USAGE/_HELP_INTRO), test/unit/test_browse_md.py (+TestStdinDocument, 11 tests), test/ui/test_recipe_browse_md.py (+TestBrowseMdStdin, 2 tmux e2e). No src-tui changes -> no rebuild. _HELP_INTRO trimmed to stay within its 25-line budget.
          Tests: full ./run-tests-parallel.sh green (104 modules, 0 fail). browse-md unit+ui: 296 passed, 6 subtests.
        * Review (subagent): APPROVE. 2 nits folded into the commit by leader: title-guard now also requires _STDIN_TEXT is not None (mirrors the text-serve guard; a real file named like the sentinel no longer mislabels in file mode); added test_piped_content_wins_over_same_named_disk_file pinning interception-before-open. {#860:comment:878}

    * ## Ticket: browse-fs -: paths from stdin {#861}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-11 23:11:50 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-12 02:18:05 UTC

      Spec §3.7.
      Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      recipes/browse-fs: argument '-' → read newline-separated paths from sys.stdin before
      run; display exactly those entries as the root level (relative paths resolved against
      cwd). Directories expand into their real children via the normal fs lazy listing;
      files preview as usual. Nonexistent paths: show the row with a clear "missing" marker
      (dim), no crash. Duplicates: keep first occurrence. The recipe's existing single-path
      mode and watcher behavior unchanged (watcher may be inactive for the stdin list — fine,
      note it).

      Tests: pipe a mixed list (file, dir, missing), assert roots/expansion/preview; bare
      and path modes unchanged.

      * ## Comments {#861:comments}

        * Design: stdin mode = root_id=None + get_children(None) returns Items for the verbatim stdin paths (browse-md #860 uses the same root_id=None multi-root shape). Dir Items carry the real abspath id, so expand reuses the existing path-based get_children scandir listing for free; file/dir/missing previews reuse get_preview. Missing path -> dim row + ('missing', label, abspath) tuple id (so it's filtered from e/o/d like the err row) + sensible preview, no crash; broken symlink = exists-but-broken (lstat ok), not missing. Dedupe keeps first occurrence keyed on the verbatim line. '-' + other args -> usage error exit 2 (least-code sane rule; documented in usage). Watcher unchanged: root key is None (skipped by existing isinstance str guard) so the stdin root list isn't polled; expanded real dirs are watched naturally. Slurp sys.stdin in main() BEFORE b.run() per spec §3.3 (no isatty guard). Tests reuse the SourceFileLoader + browse_tui-stub + _RaiseOnRead pattern from test_browse_md.py / test_browse_fs.py. {#861:comment:879}

        * Implemented + full suite green (104 modules, exit 0). Files: recipes/browse-fs (stdin-list mode), test/unit/test_browse_fs.py (+12 tests, TestStdinRoots), test/ui/test_recipe_browse_fs.py (+3 tests, TestBrowseFsStdin). Refinements vs the design note: (1) missing marker is the dim [missing] tag chip (recipe's err-row chip convention) with the title left as the verbatim label — not a '(missing)' title suffix, which would double up with the chip. (2) '-'+PATH rejection uses sys.exit(usage-message) => exits status 1 with the message on stderr (idiomatic; the test asserts the message text, not a code), not exit 2. Refactored the perms/size/mtime column-setting into _set_columns() shared by the scandir loop and the stdin roots. Verified end-to-end via the shipped binary (tmux): mixed list renders roots in order with dir-expand arrow + path-with-spaces, [missing] chip dim, file preview, dir expands to real child, missing preview shows the stat error, empty stdin = clean empty UI; bare/PATH modes never read stdin (_RaiseOnRead stub). {#861:comment:880}

        * Review (subagent): APPROVE, no blocking/should-fix. Tuple id verified inert across all consumers (no stdout leak possible). 2 optional nits declined per simplicity bar: Enter-on-missing unit case (covered by shared err-row guard tests + traced framework chain), verbatim-line dedupe keeps both spellings of one path (intended "displayed verbatim" semantics). {#861:comment:881}

    * ## Ticket: browse-git -: sniff git output from stdin {#862}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-11 23:11:50 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-12 03:21:37 UTC

      Spec §3.7.
      Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths): /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      recipes/browse-git: argument '-' → slurp sys.stdin before run, sniff the content type,
      and build the matching tree FROM THE SLURPED TEXT ONLY (no git execution for ingested
      data; previews come from the text, not refetched):
      - diff (`diff --git ` / `--- a/` hunks) → file tree, per-file diff as preview
        (delta/colorizer pipeline may still apply to the text where it already does).
      - log (`commit <sha>` blocks, incl. --stat/-p variants) → commit list (subject + sha
        chips where natural), per-commit block as preview.
      - status (porcelain `XY path` lines, or `git status` human output 'On branch ...') →
        status tree like the existing status view.
      Sniff on the first non-blank line(s); unrecognized input → clean error to stderr +
      exit 1 BEFORE the UI starts (pre-run, so stderr is fine).
      REUSE the recipe's existing tree-building/parsing helpers wherever they can take text
      input instead of running git — do not reimplement parsers; refactor a data-source seam
      only where strictly needed. Actions that re-run git (refresh, show, etc.): disable or
      degrade gracefully in '-' mode.

      Tests: canned `git diff`, `git log`, `git log -p`, `git status --porcelain` outputs →
      correct mode + tree + previews; unrecognized input → error exit; normal repo mode
      untouched.

      * ## Comments {#862:comments}

        * Implemented browse-git - (stdin ingest). main(): lone '-' → _ingest_stdin() pre-run (slurp sys.stdin, sniff, parse, or stderr+exit 2); '-' + anything else → usage error exit 2, stdin unread; repo/git preconditions skipped in '-' mode (no repo or git binary needed). Sniff on first non-blank SGR-stripped line: 'diff --git '/'--- a/' → diff; ^commit <sha> → log; 'On branch '/'HEAD detached' → human status; porcelain XY-code line (also the -z NUL blob) → porcelain status; else/empty → clean error pre-UI (empty gets its own message; documented in help). Colored (--color=always) input sniffs/splits identically — classification on stripped lines, blocks keep color for previews. {#862:comment:882}

          Data-source seam per view: STATUS reuses _status_root verbatim via one rows-source seam (_STDIN_ROWS instead of git+_parse_porcelain_z) — leaves/sentinel/tags all shared; -z input parses with the existing _parse_porcelain_z; line porcelain + human prose get small adapters to the same (XY,path) rows. DIFF/LOG have no existing text parsers (repo mode uses --format machine output), so a shared _split_blocks splitter + per-block field extractors build new leaf kinds ('sfile', i, path) / ('slog', i); log rows reuse the commit-row column conventions (col_sha/author/date + _parse_decorations(remotes=set()) chips via git_row_content), diff rows reuse the file-leaf shape (_STATUS_LETTER_STYLE tags, new-path display incl. renames). Previews from text only: sfile → _colorize_diff(block) (same delta pipeline); slog → block, through delta iff it carries a patch (log -p); status → the entry line. Reload re-serves the parse (builders rebuild from parsed state).
          Degraded actions: ` (mode switch) and t (graph) flash 'not available for piped input'; E extended to sfile rows (works on the worktree path, degrades to cwd-relative outside a repo); m/v/e untouched. Title: 'browse-git [stdin: KIND]'. Bare/--help never read stdin (raise-on-read tested).
          Tests: +51 unit (sniffer matrix incl. ambiguity/colored/indented-prose cases; all four parsers on literal fixtures; tree+preview serving with _run_git/_git_color poisoned = no-git guarantee; main() ingest/error/combo/bare/help; action gating; plus TestStdinRealGitOutputs validating sniff+parse against real git diff/log/log -p/log --stat/status --porcelain[-z]/status from a temp repo). +8 UI (tmux: piped diff from a NON-repo cwd with preview + backtick flash, log, log -p preview, porcelain + human status; headless: unrecognized/empty/combined-args exit 2, stderr msg, empty stdout). Full suite 104/104 modules green (unit module 201, UI module 28).
        * Review follow-ups landed. (1) Gitless crash fix at the chokepoint: _run_git now catches FileNotFoundError (git binary absent) and returns CompletedProcess(['git',*args], 127, stdout='', stderr='git not found on PATH') — rc 127 per command-not-found convention. Audited all 17 call sites: every one branches on returncode and reads only stdout/stderr (no check=True / check_returncode anywhere); edit_file degrades to the cwd-relative path, _git_color surfaces the message as preview text, builders yield their existing err rows. Regression tests: TestRunGitWithoutGit (3 tests — failed-CP shape; E on an sfile row in a gitless stdin session opens cwd-relative with no exception; _git_color returns the message). Empirically re-ran the reviewer's probe (tmux, PATH=python3-only thin dir, real diff payload, press E): session survives, no traceback, clean q — and verified probe sensitivity by running the same probe against a copy of the recipe with the fix reverted, which crashes as the reviewer observed. (2) --tty - fix: the stdin-positional scan now ignores a '-' that immediately follows a '--tty' token (the framework flag's value; one-token --tty=- never equaled a bare '-'). A true positional '-' still ingests; '-' + other args still errors (incl. '--tty - -'). Unit test test_tty_dash_value_is_not_the_stdin_positional pins --tty -/--tty=- → repo mode + stdin unread (raise-on-read), and the lone-'-' contrast; live tmux probe confirms 'browse-git --tty -' brings up the commit log again. Declined per leader: empty-commit-message subject cosmetic case. Suite: 104/104 modules green; unit module 205 (was 201, +4), UI module 28. {#862:comment:884}

        * Review (subagent): APPROVE + 1 should-fix + 1 nit, both landed: _run_git chokepoint returns CompletedProcess(rc=127) on FileNotFoundError (17 callers audited tolerant; gitless E degrades to cwd-relative edit, probe-sensitivity-confirmed regression tests); --tty - no longer misparsed as the stdin positional (--tty/-tty= forms pinned by test). Empty-commit-message cosmetic case declined. {#862:comment:885}

      anything bigger → new subticket under #835).

GAP: no MIXED cell (tty stdin + pipe stdout, or pipe stdin + tty stdout) -- the two isatty() decisions are independent and untested in isolation.

    * ## Ticket: Recipes: shared --tty argv strip (framework helper) + browse-claude fix + audit {#898}

          status: done
          created: 2026-06-12 03:38:27 UTC
          updated: 2026-06-12 04:17:28 UTC

      Follow-up consolidating what #862/#863 hand-rolled. Worktree root (ABSOLUTE paths):
      /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/stdio

      Browser.run() auto-detects the framework-owned `--tty PATH` / `--tty=PATH` flag in
      sys.argv but does NOT strip it, so every recipe that scans its own argv must skip it.
      Today there are 3 divergent hand-rolled copies: recipes/browse-git (~:2052,
      index-based, handles only the `-` value), recipes/browse-fs and recipes/browse-md
      (identical strip loops from #863). browse-claude is confirmed buggy (misreads
      `--tty` as a bogus ('project', abspath('--tty')) scope arg — cosmetic, no crash).

      1. Add ONE framework helper (src-tui, exposed like the other recipe-facing API —
         e.g. `recipe_argv()` returning sys.argv[1:] with the framework-owned flags
         stripped; today that is just --tty/--tty=X) + docstring. Rebuild binary.
      2. Switch browse-git / browse-fs / browse-md to it (their pinning tests must stay
         green unchanged — they assert behavior, not implementation). browse-git's variant
         currently only special-cases the '-' value: switching to the helper also fixes
         `browse-git --tty /dev/pts/N` misreading the device path as a positional — add a
         pinning test for that case.
      3. Fix browse-claude with the helper + pinning test.
      4. Audit the remaining recipes that read positionals or scan options
         (browse-procs, browse-plan, browse-jira, browse-mcp, browse-files, browse-find,
         browse-ls — check each) and apply where affected, with one pinning test each.

      * ## Comments {#898:comments}

        * DONE. Helper: recipe_argv(argv=None) at src-tui/040-state.py (module-level, right after default_row, just before the Browser class that owns run()); strips --tty VALUE and --tty=VALUE (same forms run() detects), returns a fresh list, leaves sys.argv intact so run() still resolves the device. Exposed via the unqualified browse_tui namespace like the other recipe-facing fns; documented in MANUAL/api.md (Lifecycle section after Browser.run()). Rebuilt the binary. {#898:comment:912}

          AUDIT (recipe -> affected? + fix):
          - browse-git      AFFECTED  _classify_positionals now scans recipe_argv() (fixes --tty /dev/pts/N misread as pathspec); line~2052 '-' check uses recipe_argv() (fixes --tty -, deletes the index special-case). _pop_repo_dir left reading sys.argv (provably safe: skips -prefixed --tty, stops at a non-dir value; recipe_argv reads sys.argv live so the leading-repo-dir drop is honoured).
          - browse-fs       AFFECTED  deleted hand-rolled strip -> recipe_argv().
          - browse-md       AFFECTED  deleted hand-rolled strip -> recipe_argv().
          - browse-claude   AFFECTED  pos_args=recipe_argv() after the flag pops; PROJECT positional reads pos_args[0] (fixes the ('project', abspath('--tty')) bug).
          - browse-jira-mcp AFFECTED  _resolve_jql() uses recipe_argv() (--tty no longer JQL / no shadowing).
          - browse-plan     AFFECTED  ticket-id positional uses recipe_argv() (no shadowing; device path no longer int-parsed -> exit 2).
          - browse-mcp      AFFECTED  cmd=recipe_argv() (--tty no longer leaks into the spawned MCP server cmd; empty-after-strip is a usage error).
          - browse-procs    UNAFFECTED  main() reads no argv (0 sys.argv refs).
          - browse-jira     UNAFFECTED  main() reads no argv (0 sys.argv refs).
          - browse-files / browse-find / browse-ls  UNAFFECTED  pure shell; own $1 positional, build a fixed binary CLI invocation, don't forward/parse --tty (the binary's argparse owns --tty in CLI mode); can't call a Python helper.
          Pinning tests: browse-git (classify-level --tty EXISTING_PATH/--tty=PATH + end-to-end --tty /dev/pts/N => repo mode), browse-claude (TestMainScopeArgv x4), browse-jira-mcp (new test_browse_jira_mcp.py), browse-plan (TestTtyArgv, drives main() vs the real binary), browse-mcp (new test_browse_mcp.py). browse-fs/md kept their existing UNCHANGED tty tests. * ## Ticket: Hook calling contract: signature checking, adaptive passing, error indication {#855}

      status: open
      created: 2026-06-11 22:55:49 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-11 22:55:49 UTC

  Recipe-supplied hooks (`on_enter`, `on_selection_change`, `on_resize`, `on_stdin`, ...)
  are called with fixed arity and exceptions raised by them are swallowed, so a hook
  with a wrong signature fails silently (a TypeError on every call, never surfaced).
  This has already cost a debugging session (`on_selection_change` arity), and the
  exposure grows with every new hook — `on_stdin` adds keyword arguments. Handle it
  framework-wide as one epic:

  1. **Signature checking** — validate at registration (BrowserConfig) that each
     supplied hook can bind the arguments the framework will pass; fail loudly at
     startup with the expected signature in the message.
  2. **Adaptive passing** — where appropriate, inspect the hook once at registration
     and pass only the (keyword) arguments it declares, so simple recipes keep
     minimal signatures.
  3. **Error/exception indication** — swallowed hook exceptions must become visible:
     at minimum routed to the message log (~), maybe a flash; decide the policy per
     hook class.

  The stage-2 stdio work (#835) deliberately assumes correct signatures and builds
  no validation; this epic retrofits the whole hook surface including on_stdin.

  * ## Ticket: browse-git - diff mode: synthesized --stat umbrella root {#917}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 09:33:29 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 10:42:11 UTC

    In stdin diff mode only (log/status untouched): wrap the file rows under ONE
    synthetic umbrella root and auto-expand it (same posted-expand convention
    browse-md uses for a single doc), so effective navigation is unchanged but the
    tree gains a stats row.
    - Counting: one pass over the already-split per-file blocks: added = lines
      starting '+' (not '+++'), removed = lines starting '-' (not '---'). Binary
      files ("Binary files ... differ" / "GIT binary patch") -> Bin entry, no counts.
      Hunkless renames/copies -> 0/0.
    - Umbrella row title: short form like `diff: 12 files +345 -210`.
    - Umbrella preview: a synthesized git-diff-stat-style table — per-file
      `path | NN +++---` with the histogram SCALED to the current preview width
      (git scales to terminal width the same way; bars proportional to the max
      change count, >=1 char for nonzero, longest path column truncated/elided the
      way git does or simplest sensible), ending with the standard summary line
      `N files changed, X insertions(+), Y deletions(-)` (omit a zero part like git
      does).
    - Resize: hook into browse-git's EXISTING on_resize conditional
      preview-cache-drop machinery so the stat preview re-renders at the new width
      (find how the side-by-side delta width handling does it and extend the same
      condition; do not add new machinery).
    - Per-file +/- column chips on file rows: deliberately OUT of scope.
    Tests: counts on canned diffs (plain, rename, binary, hunkless), umbrella
    title/auto-expand, stat preview content incl. scaling behavior at two widths
    (narrow vs wide), resize re-render via the existing pattern's test idiom.

    * ## Comments {#917:comments}

      * Implemented in recipes/browse-git (stdin DIFF mode only; log/status stdin + all repo modes untouched). {#917:comment:927}

        New id kind ('sdiff',) -- a single synthetic umbrella wrapping the per-file ('sfile', i, path) leaves.
        - _stdin_diff_root() now returns [Item(id=('sdiff',), title="diff: N files +X -Y", has_children=True)]; the former leaf builder is renamed _stdin_diff_files() and serves the umbrella's children via get_children(('sdiff',)).
        - get_preview(('sdiff',)) -> _stdin_diff_stat(preview_width). Auto-expand: main() does b.expand(('sdiff',)) before b.run() for _STDIN_KIND=='diff' (same posted-expand convention browse-md uses for a single doc).
        Counting (_diff_block_counts): one pass over SGR-stripped block lines -- a leading + (not +++) => added, a leading - (not ---) => removed; "Binary files ... differ" / "GIT binary patch" => (0,0,True); hunkless rename/copy falls out as (0,0,False) naturally.
        Stat preview (_stdin_diff_stat) matches real "git diff --stat=W" byte-for-byte (verified at widths 20/30/40/80) except a binary row shows bare "Bin" (no "-> bytes", since the diff text carries no sizes -- per ticket). Row layout " name | count bars"; fixed chrome = 5 cols; name front-elided via _elide_path (keep readable tail behind ..., git's own behavior). SCALING RULE (git-faithful): a file's bar is 1 char per changed line (raw) while the busiest file's count fits the graph budget, and ALL bars scale down proportionally only once that file overflows the budget (a nonzero change never drops below 1 char); each bar split into + then - by the add/remove ratio. Summary footer via _diff_stat_summary mirrors git's zero-part elision (show insertions if insertions or not deletions; show deletions if deletions or not insertions) + singular/plural -- so a one-sided change drops the zero half but a binary-only/all-zero diff still prints both zero clauses.
        Resize: extended the EXISTING _on_resize cache-drop (no new machinery). Same baseline/_prev_pw logic; now drops when the delta layout flips (cur/prev straddles 160) OR _STDIN_KIND=='diff' (the stat histogram re-flows on ANY width change). Repo mode (_STDIN_KIND None) behavior byte-identical to before.
        Actions on ('sdiff',): no new guards needed -- existing guards already handle the unknown kind. on_enter flips expand/collapse (has_children); edit_file (E) no-ops (id[0] not in file/status/sfile); the mode/tree keys flash (stdin gate). Per-file +/- chips deliberately NOT added (out of scope).
        Docs: updated the recipe module docstring, the --help text for the - flag, and the id-scheme comment to mention the umbrella. No MANUAL change (stdin mode isn't documented there; per house rules docs aren't required for this).
        Tests -- all green. Unit (test/unit/test_browse_git.py): new TestStdinDiffUmbrella (21 tests: counts on plain/rename-with-hunks/hunkless/binary-differ+patch/single + SGR-stripping; title incl. binary-excluded; umbrella children; main auto-expand for diff AND no-expand for log/status; stat routing at preview_width; rows+summary; relative bar scaling; bar shrink on budget overflow; narrow-width row fit; long-path front-elide; Bin row; summary zero-part forms; hunkless 0 row; empty diff). Updated the existing diff-root test for the umbrella nesting. Added TestOnResize cases (stdin diff drops on any width change; stdin log does not) and TestStdinActionGating cases (on_enter toggles umbrella; E no-op). e2e (TestBrowseGitStdin): umbrella auto-expand + stat preview content; narrow-fit + resize re-render with no keypress (bars grow 35->100 cols); actions don't crash (E/Enter fold-unfold via the expand glyph). Updated the existing piped-diff e2e to step onto the file row.
        Full suite: ./run-tests-parallel.sh -j8 => 107 modules, 0 failures. Did NOT commit (leader reviews).
      * Review fixes (REQUEST-CHANGES round): rewrote the stat layout as a faithful port of git 2.53.0 diff.c show_stats (fetched the exact source), then byte-verified against the installed git. {#917:comment:928}

        Blocking 1 (budget) -- replaced the name-greedy budget with git's: width floors at 16 + 6 + number_width (>=10 name / >=6 graph guaranteed; all tinier widths render identically AT the floor, which also fixes the tiny-width row overflow); number column = digits of max_change, widened to 3 when Bin shares it; desired graph = max_change; on overflow the graph is first capped at width*3/8 - number_width - 6 (floored at 6), then the name takes the remainder (or returns slack to the graph). Name elision is git's verbatim: '...' + last (name_width-3) chars advanced to the next '/' boundary (_stat_name). Long paths can no longer starve the graph -- bars always render.
        Blocking 2 (counts) -- _diff_block_counts now tracks in-hunk state: before the first @@ nothing is counted (the ---/+++ FILE headers are excluded by position) and the binary markers are detected; inside hunks EVERY '+'/'-' line counts, so body lines literally starting '+++ '/'--- ' ('++++ x' / '---- b' with prefix) count exactly like git --numstat.
        Should-fix 3 (rounding) -- no Python round() left at all: _scale_linear is git's integer formula (1 + it*(width-1)//max_change), the smaller side is scaled first and the larger side takes the rest, and git's both-sides guard (total<2 && add && del -> 2) keeps '+-' visible for balanced files.
        Fix 4 -- _on_resize now drops ONLY the umbrella (ctx.drop_preview_cache(('sdiff',))) on a unified-range width change in stdin-diff mode; the full drop remains for the delta 160-flip (which wins when both apply). Fix 6 -- title singularized ('diff: 1 file +X -Y').
        Verification: dev probe compared _stdin_diff_stat(W) vs `git diff --stat=W` byte-for-byte over 5 repo shapes (short+long paths, balanced 1/1, literal +++/--- content, single long-path overflow, 4-file mixed sizes incl. 105-change file) x widths 8..40 step 1 + 45/50/60/70/80/100/120, plus counts vs --numstat: 0 mismatches. The one deliberate deviation stays: binary rows show bare 'Bin' (no 'X -> Y bytes' -- sizes aren't in the slurped text); renames show the new path only (pre-existing display convention) -- both kept out of byte-equality fixtures and pinned by canned tests.
        Tests: new TestStdinDiffStatVsGit (5 real-git byte-equality tests: short+long at 20/30/40/80; balanced-guard at 22/24/30/40/80; literal markers vs numstat + --stat; tiny widths 8/12/16/24 clamp like git; single long-path keeps bars at 35). New canned tests: literal-marker counts (3,2) + corrected title; singular title; tiny-width clamp (stat(1)==stat(12)==stat(20)==stat(floor), rows fit the floor, bars survive); balanced guard '+-' at the 6-col graph; long-path elide now pins the slash-snapped '.../longfilename.txt' AND that bars render. Resize: _ResizeCtx records drop ids; stdin-diff test asserts the targeted ('sdiff',) drops; new delta-flip-in-stdin-mode test asserts the full (None) drop wins. e2e: resize test now uses the reviewer's exact shape (single long-path file at cols=35 -- asserts elided name + bars present + row fits + bars grow 35->100); single-file piped-diff e2e updated for the singular title.
        Full suite: ./run-tests-parallel.sh -j8 => 107 modules PASS, 0 FAIL (single-run log). browse-git files: 242 unit + 31 ui = 273. Not committed.
      * Review round 2 outcome: all REQUEST-CHANGES findings fixed via a faithful port of git show_stats (width floor, 3/8 graph cap, slash-snap elision, integer scale_linear, in-hunk counting, targeted sdiff cache drop, singular title); byte-equality re-proven across 5 repo shapes x widths 8-40+步 incl. long paths/balanced/literal-marker fixtures. Leader gated suite 0 failures. Committed. {#917:comment:929}

  * ## Ticket: browse-md: repeatable --root DIR for reference resolution {#918}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 09:33:29 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 11:00:19 UTC

    Purpose: candidate base directories for resolving a document's relative
    cross-file references — fixes silent resolution failure when the real root is
    not anywhere browse-md auto-searches (e.g. content piped from another project
    root or worktree; a caller like the claude recipe passes the right roots when
    piping a doc: `browse-md - --root <project-root> --root <worktree>`).
    Resolution semantics ONLY — labels/display and browse-root selection unchanged.
    - Flag: `--root DIR`, repeatable, value-taking (follow the recipe's existing
      hand-rolled flag parsing conventions; also accept `--root=DIR` if the existing
      conventions do that for other flags). Relative DIR resolves against cwd at
      startup. A DIR that is not an existing directory -> one-line stderr warning at
      startup, non-fatal (a typo'd root must not silently fail either).
    - Resolution order, file-mode docs: the file's own directory first (today's
      behavior, unchanged), then the supplied roots in given order.
    - Stdin doc ('-'): today reference-following is fully suppressed (no on-disk
      dir). With --root supplied, lift the suppression and resolve against the
      roots in order (without --root, suppression stays exactly as today).
    - Implement at the existing resolution chokepoint(s) (the existence probe
      _file_has_existing_ref and the actual ref resolution share one seam from the
      stdin work — extend that, do not fork the logic). Keep the mechanism a clean
      candidate-list parameter at the md_doc/browse-md seam so other recipes can
      reuse it later by passing roots when they invoke browse-md.
    Tests: file-mode doc with a ref resolvable only via a supplied root (and the
    file-dir-first precedence when both exist); stdin doc + --root resolves refs /
    builds the References umbrella; stdin doc without --root stays suppressed;
    nonexistent root warns but works; --root=. form if supported; bare/file modes
    without the flag byte-identical behavior.

    * ## Comments {#918:comments}

      * Implemented repeatable --root DIR for browse-md reference resolution. {#918:comment:936}

        SEAM: extended md_doc.resolve_md_ref with a keyword-only extra_bases=() param; for a relative ref the candidate order is now [doc_dir, *extra_bases, cwd, project_root]. Empty default => byte-identical to prior behavior. The recipe threads its _ROOTS global as extra_bases at BOTH existing resolution sites — _file_has_existing_ref (existence probe) and _md_resolved_refs (full resolve) — so the logic is not forked.
        FLAG: new _pop_value_flag(name) helper mirrors the existing _pop_flag (in-place sys.argv mutation, repeatable, runs before recipe_argv()) but is value-taking: accepts both --root DIR and --root=DIR (matching how recipe_argv treats --tty). _resolve_roots() abspath+expanduser's each value against startup cwd, drops a non-directory with ONE non-fatal stderr warning ('browse-md: --root: not a directory: <raw>'), keeps the rest in order. main() sets _ROOTS = _resolve_roots(_pop_value_flag('--root')) right after the -l pop.
        ORDERING: file's own dir FIRST (unchanged), then supplied roots in given order, first existing wins. Roots sit before the cwd/project_root fallbacks.
        STDIN: the _STDIN_PATH suppression in _file_has_existing_ref is lifted ONLY when _ROOTS is non-empty (without --root: unchanged). _md_chain_doc now serves _STDIN_TEXT (via md_doc.build_doc_tree, not the on-disk get_doc, and not polluting the shared cache) for the empty-chain stdin sentinel so its References umbrella can resolve/expand against the roots. A non-empty chain never names the sentinel (resolve_md_ref only returns existing realpaths), so the special-case is safe.
        DOCS: _USAGE + _HELP_INTRO updated with a --root entry (resolution-only note); two help tests updated to track the new usage line + line budget.
        TESTS (all green): md_doc TestResolveMdRef +7 (extra_bases precedence/first-wins/doc-dir-beats/absolute-ignored/default-unchanged). browse-md unit: TestArgvValueFlag (8), TestResolveRoots (6), TestMdRefFollowingWithRoot (8, on-disk fixture asserting md_abspath since label of an out-of-root file is its abspath), TestStdinDocument +5 (stdin+root resolves/preview/equals-form/nonexistent-warns; stdin-no-root stays suppressed). tmux e2e TestBrowseMdRoot (3: file-mode resolves-only-via-root, file-mode-no-root-no-umbrella control, stdin+root). No src-tui changes.
        FULL SUITE: ./run-tests-parallel.sh => 107 modules, 0 fail, exit 0.
