* ## Ticket: Task: browse-git - diff stat: Option B simplification (drop bars) + green/red counts {#938}

      updated: 2026-06-12 22:00:54 UTC
      status: done
      created: 2026-06-12 21:47:26 UTC

  Simplify the stdin diff-stat umbrella (#917) per the user's Option-B decision: stop
  matching `git diff --stat` byte-for-byte; drop the histogram bars; let colored per-file
  counts carry the magnitude signal instead. Work on main.

  ## Drop (the byte-for-byte-vs-git apparatus)
  - `_scale_linear` — delete (no bars).
  - The slash-snap elision in `_stat_name` — replace with the simplest readable long-path
    fit (plain truncation; a leading ellipsis is fine; NO `/`-boundary snapping).
  - The width-budget negotiation in `_stdin_diff_stat` — delete the `width*3//8` graph cap,
    the name/graph give-and-take, and the `16 + 6 + number_width` floor.

  ## Keep
  - `_diff_block_counts` (per-file +/- counting — unchanged; had the real bug, keep its
    in-hunk logic and tests).
  - `_diff_stat_summary` (the `N files changed, X insertions(+), Y deletions(-)` footer with
    git's zero-part elision — unchanged).
  - The umbrella structure: `_stdin_diff_root` / `_stdin_diff_files`, auto-expand, the row
    title `diff: N files +X -Y`, the targeted `drop_preview_cache(('sdiff',))` resize hook.

  ## New preview layout (Option B)
  Per file: `path | +N -M`. Readable at the current preview width — simple truncation of an
  over-long path; right-align numbers if trivial but don't reintroduce width math. Binary →
  `Bin`. Hunkless rename/copy → `+0 -0` (or whatever reads cleanly). End with the existing
  summary footer. Keep using preview width only for truncation; keep the resize re-render.

  ## Color (git-style)
  - Preview per-file counts: `+N` GREEN, `-M` RED (git default diff colors). Emit raw SGR
    (reuse an existing green/red constant/helper if one exists; else `\033[32m`/`\033[31m`
    + reset). Must survive `sanitize_ansi` (keeps complete SGR) — verify colored end-to-end.
  - Umbrella ROW TITLE `diff: N files +X -Y`: color `+X` green, `-Y` red the same way —
    browse-git already renders SGR in row-text (the commit graph). If row-title SGR doesn't
    work cleanly for this row kind, leave it plain and note why (preview color is must-have).
  - Respect the recipe's existing color gating (NO_COLOR / how delta color is conditionally
    applied). No color when disabled.
  - Zero counts: cleanest choice (e.g. a `0` side uncolored/dim) — your call, document it.

  ## Tests
  - DELETE the byte-for-byte-vs-git oracle (`TestStdinDiffStatVsGit` and any width-sweep-vs-
    `git diff --stat=W` assertions) — that comparison no longer applies; this is the point.
  - KEEP/adapt: count correctness (literal-`+++`/`---`-content vs numstat, binary, hunkless,
    single/multi-file), umbrella title totals + singular `1 file`, auto-expand, summary
    zero-part elision, action gating, the e2e that the umbrella appears with files.
  - ADD: color tests — `+N` carries green SGR / `-M` red SGR in the preview (assert the
    escape, not a screenshot); color-off → no SGR; row-title color if implemented. Replace
    any bar-asserting e2e with the new `+N -M` layout.

  Re-verify the full suite green (./run-tests-parallel.sh).

  * ## Comments {#938:comments}

    * Option B done (recipes/browse-git + tests). {#938:comment:939}

      DELETED:
      - _scale_linear (whole fn).
      - _stat_name slash-snap elision -> simplest front-elision (leading '...', no '/'-boundary snap, no padding).
      - _stdin_diff_stat width-budget negotiation: the 16+6+number_width floor, width*3//8 graph cap, name/graph give-and-take, and the entire bar-scaling loop body.
      Stat-function executable LOC: 59 -> 21 (-38, ~64%); recipe net -39 lines.
      NEW LAYOUT: per file ' <path> | +N -M' (binary -> 'Bin'; hunkless rename -> '+0 -0'); summary footer unchanged (_diff_stat_summary kept). New helper _stat_counts builds the colored cell. width is used only to front-elide a long path (max(width-16,10) name budget); resize re-render hook (drop_preview_cache(('sdiff',))) kept since truncation stays width-dependent.
      COLOR: green +N / red -M via raw SGR constants _GREEN/_RED/_RESET (git diff defaults; none existed before). Survives sanitize_ansi (complete SGR). Row TITLE 'diff: N files +X -Y' colored the same way (embedded SGR in Item.title -> default_row_content keeps it -> _write_segments truncates ANSI-aware + trailing reset), matching the commit-graph row-text model. Verified end-to-end via tmux capture -e: preview rows carry \033[32m/\033[31m; title carries them on non-cursor rows (suppressed under the reverse-video cursor highlight, exactly like the commit graph art).
      GATING: browse-git has no NO_COLOR check (commit graph is always colored); preview color is gated by the framework's preview_ansi (R / --no-preview-ansi), which maps ESC->'?' so the SGR never reaches the screen. Verified R toggles it off. Zero-count sides: always shown and always colored (+0 green / -0 red) - the number is the magnitude signal; dimming a zero would only add code. Documented in _stat_counts docstring.
      TESTS:
      - DELETED TestStdinDiffStatVsGit (byte-for-byte-vs-git oracle + width sweeps) and the _bars histogram helper.
      - KEPT/ADAPTED in TestStdinDiffUmbrella: all counting tests, title totals + singular '1 file' (now SGR-stripped via _title()), auto-expand, summary zero-part elision, binary/hunkless, empty diff. Rewrote the stat-preview tests to the '+N -M' layout.
      - ADDED unit: counts colored green/red (assert the escapes), zero-side colored, survives sanitize_ansi, no-bars regex, short-path-not-padded, long-path refit (wide keeps full path), Bin uncolored, umbrella title colored.
      - ADDED UI: test_piped_diff_stat_counts_are_colored (capture -e green+red present; R strips them). Rewrote the bar e2e -> '+N -M' layout assertion; rewrote the resize test (bars-grow -> path-refit-grows).
      Full suite green: ./run-tests-parallel.sh -> 107 modules, 0 failures.
      MANUAL: MANUAL/recipes.md line ~69 still says 'a synthesized git diff --stat umbrella row ... the stat table as the umbrella's preview' - leader should reword to the Option-B layout (per-file path | +N -M colored counts, no --stat byte-match / no bars).
    * Leader review (simplification, leader-diff not subagent — avoid ceremony on a reduction): deletions clean (no _scale_linear/budget remnants), cell_ljust still used, _diff_block_counts/_diff_stat_summary 0 changed lines (counting untouched), net recipe -39. New layout/color minimal + correct SGR. MANUAL/recipes.md synced to the +N -M green/red layout. Suite 0 failures. {#938:comment:940}

* ## Ticket: Preview flicker fix: debounce + stale-hold + loading indicator {#960}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-12 16:24:13 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-12 23:42:52 UTC

  Fix the preview pane flashing when the cursor runs over items.
  Spec (read it first): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-preview-flicker-design.md
  in worktree /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/preview-flicker.

  Three composable changes: (A) fixed sleep-and-recheck debounce in the
  preview worker, (B) renderer holds the last painted preview until
  replacement content arrives, (C) a loading prefix on the Preview label
  while a fetch is outstanding.

  Constraints for all subtasks: work ONLY under the absolute worktree
  path above (never the main repo root). Run tests with
  ./run-tests-parallel.sh (or unittest discover) from the worktree.
  Do not commit — the leader commits after review. Modern Python style
  (f-strings, no char-loops). Keep it simple; reuse existing
  primitives (post queue, latest-wins slot, pane row cache).

  * ## Ticket: Task: Debounce preview fetches in _preview_worker (preview_debounce knob) {#939}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 16:24:13 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 16:42:07 UTC

    Spec section A. In src-tui/040-state.py:

    - Add `preview_debounce: float = 0.15` to BrowserConfig (near
      preview_buffer_cap_chars); mirror onto Browser the same way
      sibling preview knobs are mirrored. 0 disables.
    - In `_preview_worker`, after adopting a new id (`local_id = req`,
      event cleared) and BEFORE `_abandon_paused_preview_if_any` +
      `get_preview`: if debounce > 0, `time.sleep(debounce)`; then if
      `self._stop` bail out of the loop; re-read `self._preview_req`;
      if it changed, `continue` (the loop top adopts the new id and
      debounces again); if unchanged, fall through to the existing
      fetch path. No timestamps, no timers, no main-loop changes.
      Update the worker docstring.
    - Tests (headless, follow existing test/async_ preview-worker test
      patterns): counting get_preview stub — a burst of cursor
      moves/request_preview calls within the window yields exactly one
      fetch, for the final id; preview_debounce=0 fetches immediately;
      move-away-and-back within one sleep window still fetches that id;
      run_until_idle returns once the debounced fetch delivers.
    - Run the FULL suite. The 0.15 default adds latency wherever tests
      await a preview delivery; most use run_until_idle's 2s default and
      just get slower. Fix any test that becomes timing-sensitive by
      passing preview_debounce=0 in its Browser config — only where the
      test's purpose is not the debounce itself. Report which tests
      needed it.

    * ## Comments {#939:comments}

      * Implemented: preview_debounce field in BrowserConfig (+Args doc), mirrored as _preview_debounce next to the request-slot setup, sleep-and-recheck in _preview_worker (re-check _stop -> break, slot changed -> continue), worker docstring updated. New test/async_/test_preview_debounce.py: 6 tests, all green. Running full suite next. {#939:comment:944}

      * Full suite green twice (108 modules, 4 workers, ~36s; parallel runner does not rebuild the binary, so UI tests ran the committed pre-debounce binary — rebuild is #942). One real failure fixed plus four staged-race tests given preview_debounce=0 in test/async_/test_workers.py: test_latest_wins_all_deliveries_land_via_post_queue (failed: A superseded during debounce, never fetched), test_preview_latest_wins, test_worker_delivery_lands_after_recipe_when_recipe_queues_first, test_rapid_requests_keep_max_concurrency_at_one, test_single_flight_invariant_under_rapid_requests (the last four passed but their in-flight/burst staging was voided by the settle wait). Streaming generator/resume tests all _drain_until-gated, no changes needed; preview modules ran 3x stable. MANUAL listing of BrowserConfig fields left to #943. {#939:comment:945}

      * Review hardening applied (LOW finding): the debounce slot-changed continue now sets local_id = None first — req was adopted but never fetched, so a slot flipping back to it in the gap before the top-of-loop read is no longer absorbed by the same-id dedup (previously recovered only by the #442 per-tick re-fire). Worker docstring step 3 notes the memo drop; intro reverted to 'most recently fetched' (exact again). test_preview_debounce + test_workers green 3x; full suite green (108 modules).   * ## Ticket: Stale-hold: render last preview until replacement arrives {#940}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 16:24:13 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 17:18:02 UTC

    Spec section B. In src-tui/050-render.py (+ Browser attr init in
    040-state.py):

    - Browser-level snapshot of the last successfully painted per-item
      preview: raw text, the (clamped) scroll it was showing, wrapped
      rows + (width, ansi_on) geometry — mirroring PreviewRender.
      Captured at the end of every normal per-item content paint in
      render_preview (reference assignments, no copying).
    - Paint rule in render_preview, only when not in help mode:
      HOLD (paint the snapshot) when the cursor entry is a pending
      placeholder row OR resolves to an item with `preview is None`,
      and a snapshot exists. Re-wrap from snapshot text when pane
      geometry/ANSI policy changed (resize, screen-restore), reusing
      the normal sanitize+wrap path. The held view is frozen at the
      snapshot's scroll (clamped to the pane).
      BLANK (current behavior) when the visible list is empty, no
      snapshot exists yet, or the item has a delivered value —
      including '' (delivered-empty must blank).
    - The stale branch is self-contained: never write the snapshot's
      wrap into the cursored item's preview_render; never update the
      snapshot from the stale paint; skip the #274 demand-signal block
      and the tail-pin/scroll writebacks.
    - Tests: move from a painted item to an uncached one — pane keeps
      old content until delivery, then swaps; delivered '' blanks;
      streaming first chunk swaps; abandoned-partial revisit still
      refetches fresh (#456 guard — existing tests must stay green);
      resize while holding re-wraps; the new item's preview_render is
      not polluted; empty visible list blanks. Full suite green.

    * ## Comments {#940:comments}

      * Code study done. Design: _PreviewSnapshot namedtuple (text, scroll, wrapped, width, ansi_on) in 050-render.py; Browser._preview_snapshot=None init in 040-state.py; render_preview classifies cursor entry (non-normal kind or preview-None => pending), holds snapshot via self-contained early-return branch painting through begin_row/end_row; sanitize+wrap block extracted to _sanitize_and_wrap_preview shared by normal path and hold re-wrap; capture at end of per-item delivered paints (incl ''). Tests: new test/unit/test_preview_stale_hold.py probing pane content via _pane_cache['preview'].lines with stubbed term_size. {#940:comment:947}

      * Implemented. 050-render.py: _PreviewSnapshot namedtuple (text/scroll/wrapped/width/ansi_on) + _sanitize_and_wrap_preview extraction (shared by normal paint and hold re-wrap); render_preview now classifies the cursor row (non-'normal' entry or preview-is-None => pending), holds the snapshot via a self-contained early-return branch painting through begin_row/end_row (frozen at snapshot scroll, clamped; no preview_render write, no snapshot update, skips #274 + scroll/tail-pin writebacks), and captures the snapshot at the end of every per-item delivered paint (including ''). 040-state.py: Browser._preview_snapshot=None init next to _preview_at_tail. Tests: test/unit/test_preview_stale_hold.py (12 tests) covering hold-then-swap, delivered-'' blank, streaming first-chunk swap, #456 clear survival + revisit refetch, invalidate-cursored hold, resize re-wrap (snapshot not updated), frozen + clamped held scroll, empty-list blank, no-snapshot blank, placeholder-row hold, help-mode unaffected, preview_render non-pollution. Full suite: 109/109 modules PASS via run-tests-parallel.sh. Binary not rebuilt (per #942); nothing committed. {#940:comment:948}

      * Review fixes applied. (1) MEDIUM: pending classification no longer lumps meta rows in with placeholders — only entry.kind=='pending' is placeholder-pending; meta entries resolve their item exactly like pre-#940 (_items_by_id lookup: delivered preview paints, preview-None blanks, never holds — no delivery is ever requested for meta so a hold would never end). Inline comment at ~2050 rewritten to match. New TestMetaRows: meta row with delivered preview paints it (and not the held content); meta row without one blanks rather than holding, snapshot retained. (2) LOW: test/unit/test_preview_stale_hold.py _make_browser now sets preview_debounce=5.0 (setdefault) with a docstring note — all deliveries in this module are injected synchronously via _deliver_preview/append_preview, so the hold-window assertions no longer lean on the 0.15s default keeping a worker asleep; no test wants a live fetch, so none needs 0. Verified: module 14/14 OK; related render/preview unit modules PASS; full ./run-tests-parallel.sh 109/109 PASS (exit 0). Nothing committed. {#940:comment:949}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Loading indicator in preview label {#941}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 16:24:13 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 17:58:44 UTC

    Spec section C. In src-tui/050-render.py and 040-state.py:

    - Predicate (same logic run_until_idle's preview_busy uses): a
      preview is loading for display iff `_preview_req` is not None,
      equals `_preview_cursor_id`, and there is no paused stream for
      that same id. Implement once (small Browser method or render
      helper) — do not duplicate the logic.
    - `_preview_label` returns the loading variant — the label
      prefixed so it reads with the glyph before the right-aligned
      word: use the existing pending glyph, i.e. '⧗ Preview'.
      Help mode never shows it.
    - Repaint wiring: in the run() main loop, once per tick right
      after the in-loop `_update_preview_for_cursor()` call, memoize
      the predicate value on the Browser; when it flips, add both
      'preview' and 'info' to _needs_redraw (the label lives in the
      'h'-layout preview header AND the standalone bottom info bar).
      All transitions already wake the loop (cursor moves are in-loop;
      deliveries post + wake; the streaming pause path and exhaustion
      paths call notify_wake) — verify, don't add worker-side wiring.
    - Tests: with a slow/streaming get_preview stub, the label shows
      the glyph during fetch and while a stream is actively pulling;
      plain after a delivery and after stream exhaustion; gone while
      paused at the buffer cap; back after a demand-signal resume.
      Existing label/help tests stay green. Full suite green.

    * ## Comments {#941:comments}

      * Implemented §C: Browser._preview_loading() display predicate (req set, == _preview_cursor_id, no paused stream for that id) + _flag_preview_loading_if_changed() memo helper, both in 040-state.py right before _update_preview_for_cursor; memo attr _preview_loading_memo init'd next to the preview attrs in __init__. run() calls the flag helper once per tick right after the in-loop _update_preview_for_cursor/_update_children_for_cursor pair; a flip flags {'preview','info'}. _preview_label (050-render.py) returns '⧗ Preview' while loading; Help short-circuits first. run_until_idle's preview_busy left separate per spec (it gates on the slot alone, not the cursor id). Verified every transition reaches the memo check: cursor moves/slot sets are same-tick (in-loop); non-streaming delivery posts + notify_wake; streaming chunks append (post+wake); pause-at-cap calls notify_wake right after setting _preview_paused; exhaustion/error clear the slot + notify_wake; demand-resume flips ON via the next chunk's wake (spec-stated mechanism). Note: request_preview itself wakes only the worker — fine, all production call sites are main-thread in-loop. Tests: test/async_/test_preview_loading.py (7 tests — gated fetch, debounce window, streaming pull, exhaustion, pause at cap, demand-resume + re-pause, non-cursored request, help mode, exact flag set + no-flip quiet). Full suite green via ./run-tests-parallel.sh: 110/110 modules, exit 0. Binary not rebuilt (#942). {#941:comment:950}

      * Review follow-up (LOW doc nit): render_preview docstring in 050-render.py still described the header label as Help/Preview only — updated to mention the ⧗ Preview loading variant with a pointer to _preview_label. Re-ran test.unit.test_render + test.async_.test_preview_loading: 204 tests, OK. {#941:comment:951}

        No now-false statements found in README.md or docs/internals.md: README is high-level + keymap only; internals.md describes the worker as 'latest-wins/single-slot' which stays true (debounce is an addition, not a contradiction) — left untouched per 'no new internals prose' constraint. Deliberately did NOT add preview_debounce to the api.md constructor example (it's a curated subset that also omits the preview_buffer_cap fields — matched that convention) and did NOT add a CLI flag mention beyond noting none exists. No internal attribute names (_preview_loading etc.) in user-facing text. Glyph bytes verified to match source (U+29D7). Default/disable/paused-cap claims verified against src-tui/040-state.py + 050-render.py and the spec.

  * ## Ticket: Cached previews: settle before swap (always-request + settle nudge) {#954}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 21:00:26 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 21:30:13 UTC

    Follow-up gap: a cursor move onto a row with a CACHED preview skips the
    worker entirely (the #442 early-return in `_update_preview_for_cursor`),
    so the pane swaps content immediately on every move — rapid scrolling
    over visited rows still churns. Fix: route every cursor move through
    the worker and make delivery the single swap signal, cached or not.
    The #442 promise "a cached revisit never re-runs get_preview" MUST be
    preserved — the worker serves cache hits with a nudge, not a refetch.

    All in worktree /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/preview-flicker
    (absolute paths). Spec to amend in place (no new doc):
    docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-preview-flicker-design.md

    ### Code (src-tui/040-state.py, src-tui/050-render.py)

    - `_update_preview_for_cursor`: remove the cached-skip early return —
      on a cursor change to a normal item, always `request_preview(new_id)`.
      The new-id-is-None path and the same-cursor #442 re-fire stay as is.
    - New per-visit bit on Browser (init next to `_preview_cursor_id`;
      pick a house-style name, e.g. `_preview_visit_delivered`): cleared
      in `_update_preview_for_cursor` when the cursor changes to a new id;
      set (only when the delivered id equals `_preview_cursor_id`) by:
      `_deliver_preview`, the append/set preview apply paths at the point
      where they flag the cursored preview redraw, and the new settle
      nudge.
    - Worker `_preview_worker`: after the debounce settles (slot
      unchanged), before `_abandon_paused_preview_if_any` + `get_preview`:
      read the item's cached preview; if non-None, post a settle-nudge
      closure instead of fetching and `continue` (keeping the same
      local_id memo semantics as the non-generator delivery path). The
      nudge, on the main thread: drain `_preview_req` if it still equals
      the id (as `_deliver_preview` does), set the visit bit + flag the
      preview redraw when the id is still cursored, then notify_wake.
      The off-thread cache read races main-thread mutation the same way
      existing `_preview_req` reads do; misreads are recovered by the
      #442 per-tick re-fire / a harmless refetch — note it in the
      docstring, do not add locking.
    - Renderer hold rule in `render_preview`: for normal/placeholder
      rows the hold trigger becomes `item.preview is None OR not
      <visit bit>` (snapshot must exist, help mode excluded, meta-row
      handling from #940 unchanged). Rationale to encode in the comment:
      `preview is None` keeps its #940 meaning (delivery pending — also
      preserves the invalidate-on-cursored-item hold); the bit adds the
      cached-row settle window; a streaming first chunk sets the bit so
      progressive streaming still swaps immediately.
    - Indicator: no changes expected — the slot is set during the settle
      so the glyph shows on cached moves too (~debounce-long blink), and
      the nudge drains it. Verify rather than wire.

    ### Tests

    - New async tests (real worker, explicit preview_debounce): cached
      move holds old content through the settle, then swaps WITHOUT
      get_preview being called (count it — must stay zero for the cached
      row); a burst over cached rows nudges only the settled row;
      run_until_idle returns after the nudge drains the slot; glyph shows
      during a cached settle and clears after.
    - Adjust existing tests that assert the old immediate-cached-paint /
      no-request-when-cached behavior (find them via the #442 wording);
      the preserved contract is "no get_preview re-run", not "no request".
      Stale-hold unit tests: injected deliveries set the bit, so most
      should pass; adjust minimally where the new hold window changes an
      assertion, with a one-line constraint comment.

    ### Spec amendment (same file, integrate inline — no new doc)

    - Problem/Approach: note the cached path also churned and is now
      routed through the worker.
    - Section A: always-request; cache hits become a settle nudge (no
      refetch).
    - Section B: hold rule now `preview is None OR no delivery this
      visit`, with the streaming-vs-cached-settling distinction spelled
      out.
    - Section C: glyph also blinks during cached settles.
    - Out of scope / rejected: add drop-cache-and-refetch (4-line
      alternative; rejected — disables the preview cache for navigation,
      expensive for browse-claude-class recipes) and main-loop timer
      (rejected — read_key has no timeout; would need new wake machinery).

    ### Docs + integration

    - MANUAL/api.md hold-last-preview subsection + MANUAL/cli.md
      --preview-cmd paragraph: one-line amendments — the pane swaps only
      once the cursor settles, even for already-cached previews (no
      re-run of the preview callback/command on revisit).
    - Rebuild ./browse-tui via ./build-tui.sh; full ./run-tests-parallel.sh
      green twice consecutively; pty smoke: sweep the cursor across
      ALREADY-VISITED rows and confirm the pane holds steady until settle
      (this is the scenario the original smoke did not cover).

    * ## Comments {#954:comments}

      * Implemented: always-request in _update_preview_for_cursor (cached-skip early return removed); per-visit bit Browser._preview_visit_delivered (init True next to _preview_cursor_id — no visit window exists until the cursor helper observes one, keeps helper-bypassing render paths painting from cache; cleared on cursor change); worker cache-hit branch after the debounce posts _settle_cached_preview nudge + continue (local_id memo kept, race note in docstring, no locking); bit set by _deliver_preview, the nudge, and _mark_visit_delivered_from_ops called from both update_data._apply preview-dirty sites (covers recipe set/append AND streaming first chunk). Renderer hold rule for normal rows: preview is None OR not _preview_visit_delivered (snapshot must exist; help/meta/placeholder handling unchanged). Indicator needed no changes — verified by new async test: slot set during cached settle shows the glyph, nudge drains it. {#954:comment:955}

      * Tests adjusted (3 files): test/unit/test_preview_stale_hold.py — test_delivered_empty_blanks now injects b._settle_cached_preview('c') before asserting blank (the ''-blank applies after settle now; one-line constraint comment), module docstring updated, NEW test_move_to_cached_holds_until_settle_nudge pins render-level hold->nudge->swap; test/async_/test_workers.py — #442 section comment + test_skip_fetch_when_cursor_moves_to_already_cached_item reworded to the preserved contract (no get_preview re-run, not no request) + asserts the nudge landed (bit True, cache untouched), conditional-redraw test comment updated; test/ui/test_children_pane_redraw.py — test_children_pane_survives_repeated_bounce gained wait_for('preview-of-A') after the final bounce (a holding screen is already 'stable', so wait_stable returned mid-settle; same #942 pattern as the sibling test). NEW async module test/async_/test_preview_cached_settle.py (real worker, explicit debounce): cached move settles with zero get_preview calls + redraw flagged by nudge; burst over 10 cached rows nudges only the settled row (counted via wrapping _settle_cached_preview); run_until_idle returns after nudge drains slot (>=0.14s busy); glyph during cached settle, clears after. {#954:comment:956}

      * Docs: spec amended in place (Problem follow-up para; Approach 'Together' note; §A worker steps gained the cache-hit nudge step + always-request feeding note + scope-of-delay covers the nudge and confirms the invalidate kick takes the fetch path; §B new 'Per-visit delivery bit' subsection + hold rule preview-is-None OR no-delivery-this-visit with streaming-vs-cached distinction; §C cached-move blink para; Out-of-scope gained drop-cache-and-refetch and main-loop-timer rejections; Testing gained the cached-settle bullet). One pre-discovered flake fixed en route: the repeated-bounce UI test raced the settle (failed 1 of 3 suite runs before the wait was added). {#954:comment:957}

      * Review APPROVE; applied the three cosmetic doc nits pre-commit: (1) _preview_loading docstring summary broadened to 'request outstanding — a fetch in flight, or a cached row's settle window (#954)' + OFF list now names the settle nudge as a slot drain; (2) BrowserConfig.preview_debounce Args: 'serves the request — a get_preview fetch or the cached-settle nudge', burst coalesces 'into one serve', 0 disables '(no settle wait)'; (3) spec §A move-away-and-back bullet: worker 'proceeds to serve it (fetch or nudge)'. Verified: test.async_.test_preview_cached_settle + test.async_.test_preview_loading solo PASS; rebuilt ./browse-tui (docstrings land in the artifact); full ./run-tests-parallel.sh -j8 green once more (111/111, exit 0). {#954:comment:958}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Children pane settles with the preview (displayed-id hold) {#959}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-12 22:45:38 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-12 23:22:32 UTC

    Goal: the children pane stops churning during scrolling and ALWAYS
    describes the same row the preview pane does. Agreed invariant:
    old/old before settle; new/new or new+"loading hint" after settle;
    never new-preview/old-children.

    Worktree (absolute paths only):
    /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui/.claude/worktrees/preview-flicker
    Spec to amend in place: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-preview-flicker-design.md
    The children REQUEST pipeline (prefetch slot, FIFO, children worker,
    `_update_children_for_cursor`) is explicitly out of bounds — zero
    changes there. This is a render-side displayed-id lag only.

    ### Code (src-tui/040-state.py, src-tui/050-render.py)

    - New Browser attr `_children_displayed_id` (init None, near the
      other children attrs): the parent id the children pane currently
      renders; lags the cursor.
    - Per-tick advance rule in run()'s maintenance block (next to
      `_flag_preview_loading_if_changed`), inert when
      `show_children_pane` is off. Resolve the visible cursor entry:
        * normal row: target = its id; advance only when
          `_preview_visit_delivered` is set (settle);
        * meta row: advance immediately (no settle gate — mirrors the
          #940 meta exemption: the preview is honest on meta rows, the
          children pane must match; navigation skips meta so this can't
          fire mid-scroll);
        * pending placeholder / no cursor row: no advance (hold).
      On an actual change of the displayed id, flag a full repaint
      ('all' — the pane's geometry derives from the displayed item, so
      an id change can reshape the layout; this matches what a children
      delivery flags today).
    - `_layout_for` (050-render.py ~2904): derive `children_rows` from
      the DISPLAYED id's item (`_items_by_id.get(...)`) instead of
      `_cursor_item(browser)`. The existing cached/None/leaf logic is
      untouched — cached -> sized grid, None -> 1-row loading hint,
      leaf/[] -> hidden. A displayed item that has been removed by a
      data update resolves to None -> pane hides (honest; the next
      settle re-advances).
    - `render_children_grid` + any other site that resolves the
      children pane's subject from the cursor: same displayed-id
      substitution. Grep for `_cursor_item` consumers to find them all.
    - No-mismatch consequences that fall out (verify, encode in
      comments/tests, do NOT add special cases):
        * children cached at settle -> one paint swaps preview + pane;
        * children still fetching at settle -> the SAME paint swaps the
          preview and shows the loading-hint row for the NEW branch
          (existing rendering for cached-is-None); the delivery already
          flags 'all' + wakes the loop, growing it — no new wiring;
        * leaf at settle -> pane hides in the settle paint, not mid-scroll;
        * show_preview=False -> the #954 bit inits True and is never
          cleared, so the rule degrades to advance-every-tick = today's
          immediate behavior. Verify, don't special-case.

    ### Tests

    - Unit/async: both orderings (children faster -> joint swap;
      children slower -> settle paint shows hint for the new branch,
      then grows on delivery); leaf-hide deferred to settle; placeholder
      holds old pane; meta advances immediately; show_preview=False
      degrade; displayed parent removed mid-hold hides the pane;
      clear/invalidate children on the settled cursor row -> hint
      reappears and regrows (the existing cursor-based re-fire covers
      the refetch). Pin preview_debounce explicitly everywhere.
    - UI: test/ui/test_children_pane_redraw.py asserts move-time pane
      swaps — update to the settle contract using the existing
      wait_for patterns from #942/#954. Triage order for any other red:
      assertion-of-old-behavior > too-tight waits > preview_debounce=0
      pin (last resort, with constraint comment). Do NOT change feature
      code to appease a test — stop and note on the ticket instead.

    ### Spec amendment (in place, no new doc)

    - New subsection (after §B or as §D): children pane settles with the
      preview — displayed-id lag, the advance rule (settle-gated;
      meta-immediate; placeholder-hold), the no-mismatch invariant, the
      deferred loading hint, show_preview=False degrade.
    - Out of scope / rejected: children fetched by the preview worker
      and delivered as one batch (rejected: serializes the two fetches,
      breaks get_children's single-thread contract, needs cross-worker
      dedup/waiting); joint release that also delays the preview on slow
      children (superseded by the hint-at-settle rule).

    ### Docs + integration

    - MANUAL/api.md: children-pane documentation + the hold-last-preview
      subsection — the children pane swaps together with the preview at
      settle; the loading hint appears only after settle. MANUAL/cli.md
      only if it describes children-pane timing. Match style, no counts.
    - Rebuild ./browse-tui (./build-tui.sh); full ./run-tests-parallel.sh
      green twice consecutively; pty smoke: sweep across branches with
      mixed cached/uncached children and leaves — no per-row pane
      reshape mid-scroll, joint swap at settle, slow-children case shows
      new preview + hint together, never new preview + old children.

    * ## Comments {#959:comments}

      * Implemented. Code: _children_displayed_id init (040-state.py ~3733, by the prefetch slot); _advance_children_displayed_id per-tick helper (040-state.py ~7982: pending->hold, normal->gated on _preview_visit_delivered, meta->immediate; id change flags 'all'); called in run()'s maintenance block right after _flag_preview_loading_if_changed (~7477). Render: new _children_displayed_item (050-render.py ~2255) resolving _items_by_id.get(_children_displayed_id); substituted in render_children_grid, render_children_list, _layout_for. Dispatch mirror: _cursor_item_for_dispatch -> _children_displayed_item_for_dispatch (070-actions.py ~1668) so mouse hit-testing sizes the grid from the painted subject. _cursor_item itself became dead and was removed. Request pipeline untouched. {#959:comment:960}

      * Tests. New test/unit/test_children_settle.py (12 tests, full state+render wiring, non-headless layout, deliveries injected synchronously a la test_preview_stale_hold — preview_debounce pinned 5.0 everywhere): hold-then-advance + 'all' once per settle; both orderings (children faster -> one paint swaps both; slower -> settle paint = new preview + hint, grows on delivery); leaf-hide deferred; placeholder/empty hold; meta immediate (+pane hides for divider); show_preview=False degrade; removed displayed parent hides pane; clear_children on settled row re-hints and regrows. Adjusted (all assertion-of-old-behavior -> settle contract): test_render.py TestRenderChildrenList mocks seed _items_by_id + _children_displayed_id; test_render_cache.py TestVacatedPaneClear seeds displayed id 'A'; test_actions.py mouse-dispatch tests seed displayed parent via new _seed_children_display helper; test/ui/test_children_grid.py test_grid_hidden_for_leaf waits for the pane to drop (new _wait_gone) instead of wait_stable (a holding screen is stable); test/ui/test_children_pane_redraw.py left-to-parent test waits for 'preview-of-A' (settle evidence) before slicing the pane. No feature code changed for any test. {#959:comment:961}

      * Docs+integration. Spec amended in place: Approach gains D bullet, new '## D. Children pane settles with the preview (#959)' section (displayed-id lag, advance rule, no-mismatch fallouts), two rejected alternatives (preview-worker-fetched children batch; joint release delaying the preview), Testing bullet. MANUAL/api.md hold-last-preview subsection gains a children-pane paragraph (joint settle swap, hint only after settle, show_preview=False degrade); cli.md untouched (no children-pane timing prose there). Smoke green 3x. One smoke iteration earlier exposed only my too-strict expectation (worker busy on a slow fetch -> fast branch legitimately settles to hint-then-grow), not a code issue. {#959:comment:962}

      * Review fixes applied (doc-level only, no code changes): (1) spec — §C's 'Repaint wiring:' paragraph un-stranded from the end of §D, moved back above the '## D' heading (section order now C-body -> Repaint wiring -> ## D); (2) over-broad 'preview holds its snapshot over these too' reworded in spec §D's placeholder/no-cursor bullet AND the _advance_children_displayed_id docstring: a placeholder holds both panes; an empty visible list blanks the preview while the children pane holds (deliberate — hold-on-no-cursor is the contract); (3) stale cursor-derived-children docstrings updated: mark_cursor_changed (children flag = repaint of HELD content; content changes via the advance's 'all' + deliveries), plus the 'cache may have just filled the cursor item's children' comments in BOTH delivery lanes (_post_children_delivery ~6720 and its line-wrapped twin in apply_children_results ~5960) now say DISPLAYED parent. Verified: test.unit.test_children_settle solo green (12/12); ./build-tui.sh rebuilt (docstrings land in the binary); full ./run-tests-parallel.sh green (112 modules, 0 failures). {#959:comment:963}

* ## Ticket: Task: Children pane first-appearance stale cells in vertical layout (PaneCache sentinel gap) {#961}

      updated: 2026-06-13 05:28:15 UTC
      status: done
      created: 2026-06-13 05:16:46 UTC

  * ## Comments {#961:comments}

    * Root cause: PaneCache.update_rect stamps the disappeared-pane sentinel only when the cache holds a real rect, so a NEVER-shown hidden pane keeps rect=None. Its first appearance invalidates from None -> prev_rect=None -> end_row 'first paint, no padding' branch -> the neighbour cells it took over are not cleared. Visible only in vertical layout because render_children_list writes short rows and relies on end_row padding, while render_children_grid (stacked) self-pads to full width. Pre-existing (cache logic from #186/#228/#718), independent of #959. Fix: stamp the sentinel for a never-shown hidden pane too. {#961:comment:962}

    * Fixed: PaneCache.update_rect now stamps the sentinel for a never-shown hidden pane too (dropped the 'self.rect is not None' guard), so the children column's first-ever appearance routes through the rect-changed full-pad path and clears the preview's vacated cells. Added unit tests (test_state.py: updated unpainted-cache test to the new sentinel contract + new never-shown→first-appearance full-pad test) and a pty regression test + recipe (childless→has-children in vertical, -e capture defeats trailing-space trimming). Reviewed (Opus, max effort): APPROVE, no regressions to first-paint/Ctrl-L/screen-restore. Full suite 113 modules green (3x). Binary rebuilt deterministically. {#961:comment:963}

* ## Ticket: Auto-detect piped stdin: synthesize `-` when stdin is non-tty (browse-md/fs/git) {#964}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-13 05:43:51 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-13 06:06:06 UTC

  Remove the friction of forgetting the explicit `-`: when stdin is a pipe/redirect (not a
  tty), the recipe behaves as if `-` were passed. Decision settled in discussion (no design
  doc). Repo root: /home/ubuntu/sandvault/src/browse-tui (work on main).

  ### Rule (per stdin-capable recipe: browse-md, browse-fs, browse-git)
  In the recipe's arg handling, AFTER flags are popped and the positional list is known,
  BEFORE the existing `-`-vs-positional conflict check:

      if '-' not in <positionals> and not os.isatty(0):
          inject '-' into <positionals>

  Then the recipe's EXISTING `-` logic does everything else for free:
  - no positional + non-tty  -> `['-']`            -> stdin mode
  - positional + non-tty     -> `['-', PATH]`      -> existing `- + positional` => ERROR
  - tty stdin                -> unchanged          -> bare/positional mode
  - explicit `-` already present -> no injection (idempotent)
  Inject into the POSITIONAL list only — behavior flags (`--root`, `-l`) are not positionals
  and must still compose with the synthesized `-`.

  ### Scope / non-goals
  - Lean on `os.isatty(0)` ALONE — NO `--tty -` exemption code and NO shared helper. Rationale:
    a normal `--tty -` session has a tty on fd 0 (it IS the terminal), so isatty(0) is true and
    auto-detect already doesn't fire; the only uncovered case is the exotic "piped keystrokes
    over `--tty -`", which is not a real workflow. Keep it to pure isatty.
  - The isatty check must happen pre-run (before term_init's fd hygiene), where fd 0 is still
    the caller's stdin — that is already where the recipes read stdin / parse args, so place it
    there.
  - Framework CLI (`browse-tui --root-cmd -`) is unchanged — auto-detect is recipe-only.
  - Empty input is NOT special-cased: a non-tty empty stdin still synthesizes `-` and flows into
    each recipe's existing empty-input handling (browse-git: "empty stdin input" error; browse-md:
    empty doc; browse-fs: empty list). Do not add emptiness checks.
  - Explicit `-` keeps working exactly as today (including blocking cat-like on a tty for ^D).

  ### Tests (per recipe; follow the existing stdin test patterns)
  - piped (non-tty) + no positional -> stdin mode (no explicit `-`). Simulate non-tty stdin the
    way the existing tests do (a real pipe in e2e; for unit, patch the recipe's `os.isatty` /
    feed a non-tty stdin stub). 
  - piped (non-tty) + a positional data arg -> the existing `-`+positional ERROR fires.
  - tty stdin (bare) -> bare/cwd/repo mode unchanged (the existing isatty=true path; use the
    raise-on-read / isatty-true stub so it does NOT read stdin).
  - explicit `-` still works; behavior flags (`--root`/`-l`) compose with auto-detected stdin.
  - At least one e2e per recipe via the real binary + a real pipe (no `-` on the command line)
    showing stdin mode engages.

  ### Docs
  - Update each recipe's own `--help`/usage + module docstring: `-` is now optional when piping
    (a pipe is auto-detected); `-` remains the explicit form and is required only to read stdin
    when stdin is a tty (type/paste + ^D).
  - MANUAL/recipes.md "Reading from stdin (`-`)" section: note that piping auto-engages stdin
    mode (no `-` needed); `-` is the explicit equivalent; piped data + a positional path is still
    an error. Keep the existing per-recipe bullets accurate.

  Rebuild not required (recipes aren't in the binary), but run the FULL suite green
  (./run-tests-parallel.sh).

  * ## Comments {#964:comments}

    * Discovered during impl: auto-injecting '-' under a non-tty collides with a help flag. 'cmd | browse-X --help' yields argv ['--help','-'] -> the recipe's '-'+positional conflict check fires BEFORE Browser.run()'s -h/--help auto-detect, so help exits 2 instead of printing. This broke 4 existing tests in test/ui/test_help_text.py (subprocess.run inherits the runner's non-tty stdin). Fix: gate the auto-injection on 'no -h/--help in sys.argv' too (matching browse-git's existing precondition guard and the framework's help check at 040-state.py:7362). This is NOT a --tty exemption (the ticket's isatty-ALONE non-goal targets --tty -, which isatty already exempts); it's a necessary correctness guard so a help request still reaches the framework. Explicit 'browse-X - --help' (user-typed) keeps its pre-existing conflict behavior — only AUTO-injection is suppressed when help is asked. {#964:comment:965}

    * DONE. Injection landed in each recipe's main(), between 'positionals known' and the existing '-'-vs-positional conflict check, appending '-' to the POSITIONAL list only (flags compose): {#964:comment:966}

      - browse-fs (recipes/browse-fs ~L337): after args=recipe_argv(), before 'if - in args' conflict check. + help guard.
      - browse-git (recipes/browse-git ~L2272): after args=recipe_argv(), before 'if - in args'; lone '-' takes ingest path and skips git/repo preconditions via the following elif; _classify_positionals re-reads sys.argv but skips '-'-prefixed tokens so the inject is inert there. + help guard.
      - browse-md (recipes/browse-md ~L2179): after the leftover/positional scan, before 'if positionals==[-]'. NO help guard needed - the leftover-option scan already _die's on -h/--help (any '-'-prefixed non-'-' token) upstream of the inject, so a guard would be dead code.
      Help-flag guard (browse-fs/browse-git only): inject also requires 'not any(a in (-h,--help) for a in sys.argv[1:])', matching the framework's check at src-tui/040-state.py:7362. Without it, 'cmd|browse-X --help' => argv [--help,-] => recipe conflict-error (exit 2) before Browser.run() help auto-detect; this broke 4 tests in test/ui/test_help_text.py (subprocess inherits the runner's non-tty fd 0).
      Non-tty simulation in unit tests: the test runner's fd 0 is itself non-tty, which would spuriously trip auto-detect in the many existing bare/FILE/repo-mode main()-driving tests. Fix: each of test/unit/test_browse_{fs,md,git}.py gained a module-level setUpModule/tearDownModule patching os.isatty->True (interactive default, the historical assumption), plus a _piped_stdin() context manager (isatty->False) for the new auto-detect tests.
      Tests added: 12 unit (4 per recipe: no-positional engages stdin / +positional errors / empty flows into existing empty-handling / [fs,git] help-flag-exempt or [md] --root+-l compose) + 3 e2e (one per recipe via the real binary + a real pipe/redirect, no '-' on the command line). Full suite green: ./run-tests-parallel.sh => exit 0, 113/113 modules PASS.
      Docs updated: each recipe module docstring + _HELP_INTRO/usage ('-' optional when piping; explicit form for tty stdin), and MANUAL/recipes.md 'Reading from stdin (-)' (piping auto-engages; '-' is the explicit equivalent; piped+positional still an error; examples drop the now-optional '-'). browse-md _HELP_INTRO kept within its tested 32-newline budget. No src-tui changes. Per-ticket review subagent (Opus): goal met, minimal/no over-engineering, no stale code, no high-confidence bugs.
    * Leader review (diff, not subagent — proportionate to the change): injection placed pre-run after recipe_argv in all 3, positional-only, isatty(0)-gated. Verified the fs/git --help guard is the minimal correct fix (their "-" must be alone; no stdin-composing flags; without the guard a synthesized "-" turns --help into a premature sys.exit that shadows run()s help). browse-md needs no guard (leftover-option scan handles --help; --root/-l compose, positional-scoped check). Test-infra patches the runner non-tty fd0 to interactive default, torn down. Suite 113/113, 0 failures. {#964:comment:967}

* ## Ticket: Task: Modal dialog facility {#968}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-13 08:52:15 UTC

  Implement the modal dialog facility specified in
  `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-modal-dialogs-design.md` (rev 2).
  One blocking overlay window at a time, drawn over the regular UI,
  that suspends pane repaints and takes all input until closed. Backs
  `ctx.pick` / `ctx.confirm` / `ctx.prompt` (replacing the info-bar /
  preview-pane implementations) and adds `ctx.menu` / `ctx.alert`.

  **Read the design doc first** — it is the source of truth; each
  ticket below points at the relevant section.

  **Build/test mechanics:**
  * New source file `src-tui/055-modal.py`, concatenated by
    `build-tui.sh` between `050-render.py` and `060-context.py`. Just
    write the `.py`; the build adds SOURCE markers.
  * Unit tests load each `src-tui/NNN-*.py` as a standalone module via
    `test/unit/_loader.py` (`load(name, file)`), wiring cross-module
    refs by hand — see `test/unit/test_pick.py` for the established
    pattern. The modal module needs `Rect`, the cell-width helpers,
    `style`, the preview wrap pipeline, `begin_row`/`end_row`,
    `PaneCache`, `term_size`, `read_key`, `input_ready` wired in.
  * `_read_key` injection seam (callable returning decoded key names)
    drives deterministic key streams without a TTY — same pattern the
    current picker uses.
  * Run tests: `./run-tests.sh` (rebuilds the binary, runs discover),
    or targeted `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_MODULE`.

  **Conventions:** each ticket is its own commit (the coordinator
  commits after review; workers do NOT self-commit). Reuse existing
  primitives rather than reimplementing. Modern Python style
  (f-strings, etc.), scoped to the changeset.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Modal placement & sizing helpers (pure, TDD) {#969}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 06:47:35 UTC

    Pure geometry helpers in the new `src-tui/055-modal.py`. No
    terminal I/O — fully unit-testable. See design §"Placement and
    sizing" and §"Tiny terminals".

    `Rect(left, top, right, bottom)` is defined in `050-render.py`,
    1-based, `width = right-left`, `height = bottom-top`. Wire it into
    the test module from the render module.

    Implement:
    * `_modal_caps(cols, rows) -> (max_w, max_h)` — `max_w =
      int(0.8*cols)`, `max_h = rows - 4`.
    * `_frame_size(content_w, content_h) -> (w, h)` — frame adds a
      1-col border + 1-col inner pad each side (`w = content_w + 4`)
      and a 1-row border top+bottom (`h = content_h + 2`).
    * `_modal_is_tiny(cols, rows) -> bool` — `cols < _MODAL_MIN_COLS
      (20) or rows < _MODAL_MIN_ROWS (8)`.
    * `_modal_place(cols, rows, w, h, *, placement, anchor) -> Rect` —
      `placement='center'`: centered. `placement='anchor'`,
      `anchor=(row,col)`: top-left at `(row+1, col)`; flip above the
      anchor if the frame overflows the bottom; shift left if it
      overflows the right; clamp both axes into the screen. When
      `_modal_is_tiny`, return the whole-screen Rect
      `Rect(1, 1, cols+1, rows+1)` regardless of placement.

    TDD: write `test/unit/test_modal_geometry.py` FIRST (failing),
    then implement. Cover: centering (even/odd remainders), anchor
    below / flip-above / shift-left / clamp, tiny→full-screen, caps,
    frame-size arithmetic.

    DoD: `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_geometry` green.

    * ## Comments {#969:comments}

      * Implemented pure geometry helpers in new src-tui/055-modal.py (module header + 4 helpers + 2 constants): {#969:comment:977}

        - _MODAL_MIN_COLS=20, _MODAL_MIN_ROWS=8
        - _modal_caps(cols,rows) -> (int(0.8*cols), rows-4)
        - _frame_size(cw,ch) -> (cw+4, ch+2)  [1-col border+1-col pad each side; 1-row border top+bottom]
        - _modal_is_tiny(cols,rows) -> cols<20 or rows<8
        - _modal_place(cols,rows,w,h,*,placement,anchor) -> Rect: tiny->whole-screen Rect(1,1,cols+1,rows+1); center uses floor-div (extra cell biased right/bottom); anchor lands top-left at (row+1,col), flips above on bottom overflow, shifts left on right overflow, then clamps both axes (max(1,min(edge))) so frame stays on-screen and clamps to 1 when larger than screen.
        TDD: wrote test/unit/test_modal_geometry.py first (failing: no module), then implemented to green. 25 tests cover caps arithmetic+fractional floor, frame-size arithmetic, tiny predicate incl. exact-threshold-is-not-tiny, centering even/odd leftover+exact-fit+wider-than-screen, anchor below/flip-above/shift-left/clamp-left/clamp-top/corner/flip+shift-together, and tiny->full-screen for center & anchor.
        Module follows the build's auto-glob ([0-9]* in build-tui.sh) so it slots between 050 and 060 by name; test loader wires Rect from 050-render.py by bare name (_modal.Rect = _render.Rect), matching the test_pick.py / test_render.py cross-module wiring pattern.
        Results: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_geometry -> 25 tests OK. test.unit.test_render still 197 OK (only read from 050-render, no edits).

  * ## Ticket: Modal engine: loop, frame draw, cache-poison restore {#970}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:07:26 UTC

    The `run_modal` lifecycle in `src-tui/055-modal.py`. See design
    §"Content protocol", §"Drawing through the row cache",
    §"Resize / screen-lost while open", §"Close and restore: cache
    poisoning". Depends on the geometry helpers.

    Signature: `run_modal(browser, content, *, placement='center',
    anchor=None, delay_interaction=False, _read_key=None) -> result`.
    Accept `delay_interaction` and thread it but treat it as a no-op
    here (its behavior is a follow-up ticket; default False changes
    nothing). Raise `RuntimeError` if a modal is already open (guard
    with a flag on `browser`).

    Content protocol (informal): `content.title` (str|None),
    `content.measure(max_w, max_h) -> (w, h)`,
    `content.draw_row(row, width)` (emits one content row's writes,
    called inside an active `begin_row` capture),
    `content.handle_key(key) -> (done, result)`.

    Loop:
    * Geometry at open: `_modal_caps` → `content.measure` →
      `_frame_size` → `_modal_place`.
    * Draw through a PRIVATE `PaneCache` (never registered in
      `browser._pane_cache`), bracketed by `begin_sync`/`end_sync`.
      Compose each frame row — top border `┌─ title ─…┐` (title bold,
      border in the dim/gray separator chrome), content rows wrapped
      in `│` + 1-col pad, bottom border `└─…┘` — via
      `begin_row(cache, rel, abs_row, left, right, rightmost=False)` /
      `end_row()`, fully filling to frame width so stale cells can't
      bleed. See `test/unit/test_render_cache.py` /
      `test_terminal_row_shim.py` for the begin/end_row test pattern.
    * Key read: `(_read_key or read_key)(write_fd=…, aux_read_fd=…)`,
      passing the content-channel fds under the SAME conditions as the
      main loop (`040-state.py` ~line 7414): output fd `1` only while
      `browser._out_stream_live and not browser._out_dead and
      browser._out_buf`; aux fd `0` only while `browser._stdin_live`.
    * Event handling before content sees the key:
      - `'_writable'` → `browser._drain_output()`; continue.
      - `'_stdin'` → `browser._pump_stdin()`; continue.
      - `'_notify'` → `browser.drain_main_queue()`;
        `browser.apply_children_results()`; continue — NO render.
      - `g_resize_flag` / `g_screen_lost_flag` (module globals,
        checked after each read like the main loop) → clear screen,
        `browser._pane_cache.clear()`, recompute geometry (re-measure),
        repaint frame+content; continue.
      - mouse (`mouse-click:`/`scroll-up:`/`scroll-down:`) → swallow.
      - `'esc'`/`'ctrl-c'` → cancel: result None, break.
      - else → `done, result = content.handle_key(key)`; break if done.
    * Restore in `try/finally`: cache-poison — for each cache in
      `browser._pane_cache.values()` whose `.rect` intersects the
      frame rect, set each intersecting row's entry to
      `(cache.rect.width, _MODAL_POISON)` where
      `_MODAL_POISON` contains `\x00` (no renderer can produce it, so
      the next paint misses and pads to full width). Then
      `browser._needs_redraw.add('all')`. (After a resize-while-open
      the caches are already cleared, so poisoning is a harmless no-op.)

    Tests `test/unit/test_modal_engine.py` with a stub content +
    injected `_read_key` + captured/no-op terminal writes: frame draw
    (border chars present), `handle_key` dispatch + done/result, cancel
    returns None, mouse swallowed, `_notify` drains without an extra
    draw, channel events call drain/pump (stub flags+methods on the
    browser), restore poisons intersecting cache rows + flags `'all'`,
    restore runs even when content raises.

    DoD: `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_engine` green.

    * ## Comments {#970:comments}

      * Implemented run_modal engine in src-tui/055-modal.py: lifecycle loop, frame draw through a PRIVATE PaneCache (never registered in browser._pane_cache), and the cache-poison restore in a try/finally. {#970:comment:978}

        Details:
        - Content protocol (title/measure/draw_row/handle_key) documented in run_modal docstring.
        - Frame composed per-row via begin_row/end_row in a begin_sync/end_sync+flush bracket; update_rect called exactly once per paint. Top border '┌─ {title} ─…─┐' (title bold, box chars dim fg=8 like render_separator), content rows '│ '+draw_row(inner_w)+' │', bottom '└──┘'. Engine clamps content size to the caps so a misbehaving content can't push borders off-screen.
        - _read_key seam: injected callable is called with NO args (matches test_pick's zero-arg _scripted); only the real read_key gets write_fd/aux_read_fd, computed with the same conditions as the main loop (wfd=1 only while _out_stream_live and not _out_dead and _out_buf; rfd=0 only while _stdin_live).
        - Event order before content: g_resize/g_screen_lost (via globals().get) -> \e[2J + _pane_cache.clear() + re-measure + repaint; _writable->_drain_output; _stdin->_pump_stdin; _notify->drain_main_queue+apply_children_results (NO render); mouse-click:/scroll-up:/scroll-down: swallowed; esc/ctrl-c -> result=None; else content.handle_key.
        - Restore: _rects_intersect(pane.rect, frame) helper (skips non-Rect sentinels); poisons lines[rel]=(pane.rect.width, _MODAL_POISON) for 0<=rel<len(lines); _MODAL_POISON='\x00\x00MODAL\x00\x00'; then _needs_redraw.add('all') and clear _modal_open. RuntimeError guard on browser._modal_open fires before the try/finally.
        Tests: test/unit/test_modal_engine.py — 19 tests, all green. Covers frame/title/borders, draw_row inner-width, handle_key dispatch, esc/ctrl-c cancel, mouse swallow, _notify/_writable/_stdin routing, poison of intersecting rows + 'all' flag + a follow-up end_row that repaints/pads the poisoned row, non-intersecting cache untouched, restore-on-exception, reentry RuntimeError, and a resize-mid-loop repaint (clears screen + pane cache). test_modal_geometry still green (25). build-tui.sh parses (not git added).
      * Code-review round: fixed all 3 must-fix issues in src-tui/055-modal.py + test additions in test/unit/test_modal_engine.py. {#970:comment:979}

        FIX 1 (UnboundLocalError on open-time measure failure): bind 'frame = None' immediately before the try. The finally's restore loop now sees a non-Rect frame, _rects_intersect returns None → clean no-op, the real exception propagates (not masked), _modal_open is cleared. Test: TestOpenFailure.test_measure_raises_propagates_and_clears_modal_open — content.measure raises on first call with a populated _pane_cache (the production trigger); asserts the original RuntimeError propagates (not UnboundLocalError), _modal_open False, no rows poisoned, AND a subsequent run_modal succeeds (no spurious 'already open').
        FIX 2 (wide-glyph title overflow): replaced the code-point slice title[:avail] + manual _visible_len conditional with _render._truncate_by_cells(title, avail) -> (text, cells), wide-char aware, cells <= avail, no-op when it fits. Dash-fill now uses the exact returned cell count. _visible_len is no longer referenced by the module. Test: TestFrameDraw.test_wide_glyph_title_does_not_overflow_frame_width — a 10x'東' title in a width-12 frame; asserts the top-border row's visible (wide-aware) cells <= frame width.
        FIX 3 (dead content_h + tiny-terminal out-of-range draw_row): made content_h live. The interior loop now calls content.draw_row only for content_row < content_h and blank-fills extra interior rows ('│ ' + spaces + ' │') to full width. Removes the dead param and the latent IndexError for any list-backed content when _modal_place returns a full-screen frame on a tiny terminal. Test: TestFrameDraw.test_tiny_terminal_blank_fills_extra_interior_rows — 18x6 tiny screen → full-screen frame, interior 4 rows > content_h 2; uses _ListBackedContent (indexes a length-2 list, would IndexError on over-read); asserts drawn_rows == [0,1] and the frame spans rows 1..6.
        Nit: removed unused _fixed_size helper.
        Contract lock: added TestChannelFdComputation (5 tests) — monkeypatches the modal module's real read_key with an fd-capturing fake and calls run_modal WITHOUT _read_key (real fd branch), asserting (wfd, rfd) matches the 040-state.py main loop: wfd=1 only while _out_stream_live and not _out_dead and _out_buf; rfd=0 only while _stdin_live. Covers no-channel, buffered-output, dead-output, empty-buffer, armed-stdin.
        Test-loader note: _truncate_by_cells (in 050-render) internally calls _char_width (in 020-terminal) by bare name, so the isolated load also wires _render._char_width = _term._char_width (the established render-test convention).
        Result: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_engine test.unit.test_modal_geometry -v -> Ran 52 tests, OK (27 engine + 25 geometry). build-tui.sh parses (not git added). test_render + test_cell_helpers still green (242).

  * ## Ticket: delay_interaction: input drain + threshold gate {#971}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:12:28 UTC

    Implement the `delay_interaction` behavior in `run_modal`. See
    design §"Delayed interaction". Small, self-contained.

    When `delay_interaction=True`:
    * At open, drain pending input: `while input_ready(): read_key()`
      (discard) — `input_ready` is the zero-timeout poll from
      `020-terminal.py`. Wire it into the test module.
    * Until a threshold has elapsed since the first paint
      (`time.monotonic`), discard normal keys but still process
      `'_notify'` / `'_writable'` / `'_stdin'` / resize. Threshold is
      module const `_MODAL_INTERACTION_DELAY = 0.5`, injectable for
      tests (parameter defaulting to the const, or a patchable
      module-level value — pick the simpler).

    User-invoked dialogs default to `False`; the param exists for the
    future error-reporting path.

    Tests in `test/unit/test_modal_engine.py` (or a sibling):
    pending keys drained at open, keys inside the window discarded,
    keys after it dispatched, `_notify` still serviced during the
    window. Use a zero/patched threshold.

    DoD: targeted modal engine tests green.

    * ## Comments {#971:comments}

      * Implemented delay_interaction in run_modal (src-tui/055-modal.py); previously a documented no-op. {#971:comment:980}

        Behavior (design §'Delayed interaction'), active only when delay_interaction=True (default False = unchanged for everything ctx exposes):
        - Open-time input drain: 'while poll_ready(): rk()' (discard) eats keystrokes the user typed at the previous screen so a self-appearing dialog isn't instantly dismissed. Drain reads go through the SAME key source the loop uses (rk = _read_key when injected, else real read_key), so one scripted stream drives drain + loop in tests.
        - Threshold gate: gate_until = now() + threshold computed at first paint. In the loop, after the resize / _writable / _stdin / _notify handlers (so channels + background work keep flowing during the window) and ABOVE the mouse/cancel/content dispatch (so even esc/ctrl-c can't fire early), 'if gate_until is not None and now() < gate_until: continue' discards normal keys until the window elapses.
        Injection seams added (all default to production, no real sleeps in tests):
        - _delay_threshold=None -> module const _MODAL_INTERACTION_DELAY = 0.5 (new). Tests pass 0.0 to disable the gate.
        - _now=None -> time.monotonic (bare name; time imported in 040-state). Zero-arg callable returning seconds; tests pass a controllable _FakeClock.
        - _input_ready=None -> input_ready (bare name from 020-terminal). Zero-arg poll used ONLY by the open-time drain; tests pass a stand-in simulating N pending keys then empty.
        Chose per-call params over patchable module globals to mirror the existing _read_key seam (reviewer-familiar) and keep tests isolated.
        Test loader: wired _modal.input_ready and _modal.time so the production-default path resolves the bare names.
        Tests (test/unit/test_modal_engine.py, new TestDelayInteraction, 8 tests; helpers _KeySource + _FakeClock):
        pending keys drained at open when True; NOT drained when False (poll never consulted); keys inside window discarded; key after window dispatched; _notify serviced during window; channel (_writable) serviced during window; default-False no gating (existing behavior); esc gated during window then cancels after.
        Result: python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_engine test.unit.test_modal_geometry -v -> Ran 60 tests, OK (35 engine + 25 geometry). build-tui.sh parses (not git added). test_render + test_pick still green (215).

  * ## Ticket: Task: ListContent — selection list (pick + menu) {#972}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:20:56 UTC

    `ListContent(options, *, filter=True, title=None)` in
    `055-modal.py`, implementing the content protocol. Backs both the
    fzf picker (`filter=True`, centered) and the context menu
    (`filter=False`, anchored). See design §"ListContent".

    * `measure`: width = `clamp(max(option cell widths, floor 8),
      max_w)`; height = `len(options) + (2 if filter else 0)` (filter
      row + separator), capped at `max_h`. Use `cell_width`.
    * State: `filter_query`, `cursor`, scroll window.
    * `draw_row`: with filter, row 0 = `> {query}` prompt, row 1 =
      `───` separator, remaining rows = the filtered options windowed
      to keep the cursor visible; without filter, options start at row
      0. Selected row: plain VISIBLE text in reverse video (reuse the
      list pane's selection rule). Non-selected option rows: embedded
      ANSI rendered normally. Use `cell_trim` / `_write_segments`.
    * `handle_key`: with filter, printable/`space` edit the query,
      `backspace` deletes; `up`/`ctrl-p`, `down`/`ctrl-n` move the
      selection (wrapping); `home`/`end` jump; `enter` →
      `(True, selected)` or no-op on an empty filtered list. Returns
      the option string.

    Selection windowing must keep the cursor on-screen when options
    exceed the visible rows (an improvement over the current picker).

    Tests `test/unit/test_modal_list.py`: filter narrows, selection
    move+wrap, windowing keeps cursor visible, enter returns, empty
    no-op, ANSI passthrough on unselected vs plain-reverse on selected,
    menu mode (no filter row).

    DoD: `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_list` green.

    * ## Comments {#972:comments}

      * Implemented ListContent in src-tui/055-modal.py below the engine. Reused the list pane's selection rule verbatim (render_list): selected option -> _collapse_visible(segments) (strips embedded SGR) + _truncate_by_cells + _write_highlighted(reverse=True, pad_to=width); unselected -> _write_segments([(opt,None,False)], width, pad_to=width) so embedded ANSI renders normally. measure: width=clamp(max(cell_width(opt), floor 8), max_w), height=min(len(opts)+chrome, max_h), chrome=2 if filter else 0; stores (_w,_h); _rows_visible=_h-_chrome. Windowing via _clamp(filtered): clamps cursor to the filtered list then scrolls _scroll just enough to contain it (down/up edges) and clamps to max(0, n-rows) so a narrowing filter never parks the window past the end. Wrapping down/up jumps the window (clamp re-derives scroll). Keys match the old _pick_on_info_bar (wrap via modulo, home/end, printable/space append, backspace deletes) plus the new windowing. Tests: test/unit/test_modal_list.py, 43 tests, all green; engine+geometry unchanged (103 total green). build-tui.sh parses. {#972:comment:981}

  * ## Ticket: Task: ChoiceContent — message + buttons (confirm + alert) {#973}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:39:08 UTC

    `ChoiceContent(message, buttons, *, title=None)` in `055-modal.py`.
    Backs `ctx.confirm` (multi-button) and `ctx.alert` (single button).
    Empty `buttons` → `ValueError`. See design §"ChoiceContent" and
    §"Text handling".

    * Body: render `message` through the preview ANSI pipeline
      (`_sanitize_preview` + `_wrap_preview_line`, ansi on) wrapped to
      content width and vertically clipped to the rows left after the
      spacer + button row; when clipped, the last visible row ends with
      a dim `…` marker.
    * Buttons: `&` hotkey convention — `&X` renders `X` underlined
      (`set_style(underline=True)`), `&&` is a literal `&`, no `&`
      means no hotkey. Render `[ Label ]` cells in one centered row;
      focused button reverse-video; first button focused initially.
    * `measure`: width = `max(longest wrapped body line, button row
      width)` capped; height = `body lines + 1 spacer + 1 button row`
      capped.
    * `handle_key`: `left`/`right`/`tab` move focus (wrapping); `enter`
      activates the focused button; a hotkey letter (case-insensitive)
      activates directly; with a single button, `space` also activates.
      Returns the chosen label with `&` stripped.

    Tests `test/unit/test_modal_choice.py`: body wrap + clip + `…`
    marker, ANSI in body, focus move+wrap, enter returns stripped
    label, hotkey activates, `&&` literal, single-button space, empty
    buttons `ValueError`.

    DoD: `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_choice` green.

    * ## Comments {#973:comments}

      * Added ChoiceContent to src-tui/055-modal.py (message + centered button row; backs ctx.confirm/ctx.alert). Parses each '&'-label once into a _Button(display, label, hotkey, hot_index): &X -> display X + hotkey X (first wins), && -> literal '&' (no hotkey), no '&' -> no hotkey; label is the resolved display ('&Yes'->'Yes', 'O&K'->'OK', 'a&&b'->'a&b'). Body reuses the preview pipeline (_sanitize_preview + _wrap_preview_line, ansi_on=True) char-wrapped to content width; vertically clipped to (max_h - spacer - buttons) with a dim (fg=8) '…' on the last visible row via _truncate_visible + _write_segments(pad_to=width-1). Button row composed to exactly width: boxes '[ disp ]' joined by single spaces, centered with left/right space pads; focused box reverse-video, hotkey char underlined (set_style(underline=True)). Keys: left/right/tab wrap focus (tab=right); enter -> focused label; hotkey letter (case-insens) -> that label immediately; single-button space activates. Empty buttons -> ValueError. 47 new tests in test/unit/test_modal_choice.py (also wires SgrState into _render for the wrap pipeline). All green; test_modal_list/engine/geometry (103) green; build-tui.sh parses. {#973:comment:982}

      * Code-review fix: clamped the button row to EXACTLY width cells. _draw_button_row now composes the row as ordered (text, reverse, underline) spans (left pad + boxes-with-gaps + right pad) and emits them through a new budget-bounded _emit_spans, which cell-trims each plain span to the remaining budget (so visible width never exceeds width), stops once spent, and emits a trailing reset iff any styled span was drawn (no SGR dangles into the frame border) before padding to exactly width. _draw_button was refactored to a pure _button_spans helper returning the styled spans (no behavior change to the focused/underline styling). Previously, when the button row's cell count exceeded width (e.g. ('&Yes','&No','&Cancel')=25 cells on a <25 col content area, or '[ OK ]'=6 cells at width<6) the row emitted all its cells and overflowed: end_row only PADS short rows, never truncates long ones, so the extra cells pushed the right border out of column and shifted the next physical row. Removed the misleading comment that claimed end_row clips. Added two regression tests (multi-button + single-button alert) asserting the emitted button row's visible cell count == width exactly at several narrow widths, plus a no-dangling-SGR check. Kept _body_lines_shown (used by tests + reads clearer than len(self._body); removing it would churn tests for no gain). All 152 tests across test_modal_choice/list/engine/geometry green; build-tui.sh exit 0, parses. {#973:comment:983}

  * ## Ticket: Task: InputContent — single-line entry (prompt) {#974}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:43:39 UTC

    `InputContent(prompt, *, default='')` in `055-modal.py`. Backs
    `ctx.prompt`. See design §"InputContent".

    * Body: wrapped prompt text above a one-row entry field; the field
      shows the buffer's tail when it overflows (suffix-trim by cells),
      with a visible cursor cell at the end.
    * `handle_key`: printable/`space` append, `backspace` deletes,
      `enter` → `(True, buffer)`. End-only editing (no in-buffer cursor
      movement this round). Returns the string (possibly empty); `None`
      comes only from engine cancel.
    * `measure`: width = `clamp(max(prompt width, default width, a
      small floor), max_w)`; height = `prompt wrapped lines + 1 field`.

    Tests `test/unit/test_modal_input.py`: append/backspace/enter,
    `default` prefill, tail-trim on overflow.

    DoD: `python3 -m unittest test.unit.test_modal_input` green.

    * ## Comments {#974:comments}

      * Implemented InputContent in src-tui/055-modal.py (after ChoiceContent) + test/unit/test_modal_input.py (33 tests, all green). Prompt wrapped via the preview pipeline (_sanitize_preview + _wrap_preview_line), emitted with _write_segments(pad_to=width). One-row entry field composed to exactly width: tail via _suffix_by_cells(buffer, width-1) (raw cell-aware suffix, no ellipsis — shows the buffer END), then a reverse-video space cursor cell, then plain pad. measure: width=min(max(longest wrapped prompt line, cell_width(default), floor 16), max_w), two-pass wrap (max_w then final width) like ChoiceContent; height=min(prompt lines + 1, max_h); both stored. handle_key: printable/space append, backspace deletes (no-op when empty), enter -> (True, buffer) (empty string is valid; None only from engine cancel); end-only editing, all other keys ignored. Sibling modal tests (choice/list/engine/geometry, 152) green; build-tui.sh parses (not git-added). {#974:comment:984}

    Wire the modal facility into `ctx`, delete the superseded

    See design §"ctx API surface" and §"Removed code".

    Convenience wrappers in `055-modal.py`: `modal_pick`, `modal_menu`,
    `modal_confirm`, `modal_input`, `modal_alert`.

    `ctx` methods in `060-context.py` (all take `delay_interaction` kw,
    threaded through; headless browsers keep short-circuiting to None):
    * `ctx.pick(label, options) -> str|None` — `ListContent`
      filter=True, centered, title=label.
    * `ctx.menu(items, *, anchor=None) -> str|None` — `ListContent`
      filter=False, `placement='anchor'`; default anchor = the list
      cursor's screen cell, derived from the list pane rect + cursor
      index − `browser._list_scroll` (see `render_list` in
      `050-render.py`); fall back to center when geometry is
      unavailable (headless). Empty items → None without opening.
    * `ctx.confirm(message, buttons=('&Yes','&No'), *, title=None)
      -> str|None` — returns the chosen label (`'Yes'`/`'No'`/…) or
      None on cancel. **Contract change from bool.**
    * `ctx.prompt(prompt, default='') -> str|None`.
    * `ctx.alert(text, *, title=None) -> None` — `ChoiceContent` with
      a single `('&OK',)` button.

    Remove from `060-context.py`: `_draw_info_prompt`,
    `_read_line_on_info_bar`, `_confirm_on_info_bar`,
    `_pick_on_info_bar`, `_info_bar_geometry` (their only callers are
    the three ctx methods + tests).

      `if ctx.confirm(...) != 'Yes'`).
    * `recipes/browse-procs` (same).

    Rebuild: run `./build-tui.sh` so the regenerated `browse-tui`

    regenerated binary alongside the source).

    DoD: full unit suite green (`./run-tests.sh` up to UI, or
    `python3 -m unittest discover -t . -s test/unit`).
