  * ## Ticket: Task: UI / integration tests (headless pty) {#976}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:43:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 08:50:40 UTC

    End-to-end tests under `test/ui/` driving the real binary via pty.
    See design §"Testing" (UI tests). Reference the existing pty
    harness (`test/ui/test_ctrl_c_parity.py` and siblings).

    Cover:
    * Open each dialog kind (pick, menu, confirm, prompt, alert) over a
      populated UI, close it, assert the regular UI repaints with no
      leftover dialog cells.
    * Resize while a dialog is open: screen clears, dialog repaints at
      the new geometry, close restores the full UI.
    * A streaming-input recipe keeps ingesting while a dialog is open
      (channel events serviced from the modal loop).

    `run-tests.sh` rebuilds the binary before running, so no manual
    rebuild needed here.

    DoD: the new UI tests green under `./run-tests.sh`.

    * ## Comments {#976:comments}

      * Added test/ui/test_modal.py (11 UI/integration tests, all deterministically green) covering the dialog kinds the picker tests don't: confirm (hotkey/arrow+enter/esc), menu (anchored, items render, select/esc), input (type+enter/esc), alert (enter/space). Each asserts the OUTCOME via a recipe log (modal_demo.py writes the resolved result to $MODAL_LOG) since selection is reverse-video, plus a STRICT restore check reusing test_pick's _assert_ui_restored approach (poll the box top-border away, then assert all border glyphs/title/dialog-content gone AND the regular UI back). Two new recipes: modal_demo.py (no-quit; one key per kind) and modal_stdin_demo.py (on_stdin hook + key-bound alert; logs each ingested record). Resize test: open alert, resize 80x24->120x40, assert box survives + re-centers (left-edge moves right), close, assert full restore. Streaming test (pty + owned stdin pipe, _PtyStdinApp idiom): feed a record before the dialog, open the alert, feed records WHILE the box is up and prove they land in the log (hook ran via the modal loop's _stdin servicing) before any close key is sent — repaint-independent, deterministic; close and confirm rows repaint; feed one more after close. NB: the resize test exposed a real bug in 055-modal.py — a resize-while-open clears the pane caches, so the post-close render_full rebuilds them fresh and first-paints with NO padding (end_row prev_rect-is-None branch), leaving the dialog's own cells on screen (the resize's \e[2J was overdrawn by the dialog repaint). Fixed: on a resized close, blank the screen again so first-paint lands on a genuinely empty screen (the precondition the design assumes). Added a focused unit test (test_modal_engine.TestResizeRepaint.test_resized_close_blanks_screen_again) locking in the two-\e[2J resized close vs zero on a normal close. Full parallel suite: 119/119 modules PASS in ~51s. {#976:comment:992}

      * Post-review doc-only fixes (review APPROVED). (1) test/ui/recipes/modal_demo.py docstring: corrected 'written with a trailing newline' to 'written as the exact string with NO trailing newline' to match _record (the tests assert exact log equality). (2) docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-modal-dialogs-design.md: reconciled the spec with the shipped resize/restore fix — §'Resize/screen-lost while open' no longer claims the post-resize blank screen alone suffices (the dialog repaints over it; cleared caches make the close-time render take end_row's no-padding first-paint branch), and §'Close and restore' now states that a close AFTER a resize/screen-lost re-blanks the screen (\e[2J) instead of poisoning empty caches, so the next full repaint's first-paint lands on a genuinely empty screen. Re-verified: full parallel suite 119/119 modules PASS (~51s), test_modal + all modal unit modules green, browse-claude clean (no flake). No code changes; no commit. {#976:comment:993}

Engine regression test (test_repaints_after_nonterminal_key, red->green) + two UI tests (filter narrows live; Down moves the reverse-video highlight off 'open' onto 'in-progress'). Full parallel suite green (119 modules). {#994:comment:996}

* ## Ticket: Task: Map dialog buttons/options to return values {#997}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-14 01:28:45 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-14 02:02:39 UTC

  Let recipe authors map a dialog choice to an arbitrary return value, not
  just the displayed label. Today ctx.confirm returns the resolved button
  label string (e.g. 'Yes'), and ctx.pick / ctx.menu return the chosen option
  string — forcing callers into string comparisons and the `== 'Yes'`
  footgun.

  CONVENTION (all three dialogs): each button/option may be EITHER a plain
  string (current behavior) OR a 2-tuple (display, value). When a tuple is
  given the dialog returns `value` on activation; `display` is what's shown
  (and, for pick, what the substring filter matches on). A bare string
  returns its DISPLAYED text: for ChoiceContent that is the &-resolved
  display ('&Yes' -> 'Yes'); lists have no & convention so it is the string
  verbatim. The two forms are mixable in one sequence. NO dict form (it would
  muddy ordering). Backward compatible: existing string-only callers (incl.
  shipped recipes browse-fs/browse-procs/browse-plan/browse-git) are
  unaffected.

  Cancel (esc/ctrl-c) still returns None — document that mapping a button to
  the value None makes a confirmed-None indistinguishable from cancel.

  Main branch, no worktree. Each implementation ticket: TDD (failing tests
  first), then rebuild the generated browse-tui binary (./build-tui.sh) and
  run the FULL ./run-tests-parallel.sh (this is a ctx + rendering surface).
  Worker does NOT self-commit; reports results, leader reviews + commits the
  source plus rebuilt binary together. After both tickets, a review subagent
  (Opus, max effort) assesses: goal met? simple / not overengineered? stale
  code left?

  * ## Ticket: Task: ChoiceContent value mapping (confirm / alert) {#998}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-14 01:28:45 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-14 01:36:11 UTC

    modal_confirm), src-tui/060-context.py (ctx.confirm), and docs/tests
    below. TDD: write failing tests first.

    _Button.__init__(self, raw): accept raw as a str (current) OR a 2-tuple
    (label, value). If a tuple, unpack `label, value = raw`, parse `label`
    for display/hotkey EXACTLY as today, and set self.value = value. If a
    str, parse as today and set self.value = self.display (bare string
    returns its resolved display). Keep self.label = self.display unchanged —
    existing tests assert b.label.

    activation spots (enter; hotkey match loop; single-button space).

    signature default ('&Yes', '&No') unchanged. ctx.confirm: return
    annotation Optional[str] -> Optional[Any]; docstring documents the tuple
    form and that a bare string still returns its resolved label; replace the
    `== 'Yes'` footgun guidance with the value-mapping idiom, e.g.
    `if ctx.confirm(msg, [('&Yes', True), ('&No', False)]): ...`.

    (display, value) tuple, dialog returns value, bare string returns
    displayed text, cancel returns None. MANUAL/recipes/writing-recipes.md:
    if it shows the `== 'Yes'` idiom, add the value-mapping alternative. Do
    NOT rewrite working recipes.

    via enter / hotkey / single-button space; bare string still returns
    resolved display; mixed string+tuple sequence; `&&` literal unaffected;
    a non-string value (bool / int / object) round-trips.

  * ## Ticket: Task: ListContent value mapping (pick / menu) {#999}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-14 01:28:45 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-14 01:45:59 UTC

    Files: src-tui/055-modal.py (class ListContent, modal_pick, modal_menu),
    src-tui/060-context.py (ctx.pick, ctx.menu), and docs/tests below. TDD:
    write failing tests first.

    ListContent.__init__: accept each option as a str OR a 2-tuple
    (display, value). Normalize internally to self._options as a list of
    (display, value) pairs (a bare string s -> (s, s)). This keeps display
    and return value separate WITHOUT a display->value dict (displays need
    not be unique).

    _filtered(): substring-match on the DISPLAY; return matching
    (display, value) pairs preserving order; empty query returns all pairs.
    measure: width from cell_width(display) over the pairs. draw_row /
    _draw_option: render the DISPLAY (pair[0]); selection-highlight logic
    unchanged. handle_key: enter returns the focused pair's VALUE (pair[1]);
    cursor / scroll / filter-editing unchanged.

    modal_pick / modal_menu: empty-collection short-circuit unchanged;
    docstring updates. ctx.pick / ctx.menu: return annotation -> Optional[Any];
    docstrings document the tuple form. Docs: MANUAL/api.md pick + menu
    entries (option may be a (display, value) tuple, dialog returns value,
    filter matches the display).

    Tests (test/unit/test_modal_list.py and test/unit/test_pick.py): tuple
    option returns its value on enter; bare string returns itself; filter
    matches on display text NOT the value; mixed sequence; menu
    (filter=False) tuple round-trips.

    * ## Comments {#999:comments}

NO & hotkey convention for lists. Existing tests in test_modal_list.py assert _filtered() returns plain strings + handle_key enter returns string + test_pick.py asserts _options==['open','done'] — these internal-shape assertions must update to pairs (ticket explicitly says _filtered returns pairs now); public enter-returns-string behavior preserved via (s,s). {#999:comment:1003}

ListContent.__init__ normalizes options to (display, value) pairs (bare str s -> (s,s)); measure uses cell_width(display); _filtered() matches pair[0] and returns pairs; draw_row unpacks display=filtered[vis_idx][0]; handle_key enter returns filtered[cursor][1].   * ## Ticket: Task: Update shipped recipes to use confirm value mapping {#1005}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-14 01:48:13 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-14 01:52:23 UTC

    Per user request: recipes should do reasonable things with confirm —
    Yes => True, everything else (No / cancel) => False — using the new
    (label, value) mapping from #998.

    Change these TWO sites (both currently `!= 'Yes'` string checks guarding a
    destructive action):

    recipes/browse-fs, def delete (~line 237):
      OLD: if ctx.confirm(f'delete {label}?') != 'Yes':
      NEW: if not ctx.confirm(f'delete {label}?', [('&Yes', True), ('&No', False)]):

    recipes/browse-procs, def kill_proc (~line 117):
      OLD: if ctx.confirm(f'send SIGTERM to pid {pid} ({ctx.cursor.title})?') != 'Yes':
      NEW: if not ctx.confirm(f'send SIGTERM to pid {pid} ({ctx.cursor.title})?', [('&Yes', True), ('&No', False)]):

    Rationale: cancel returns None (falsy), No returns False, Yes returns True,
    so only Yes proceeds — the safe default for a destructive action, and the
    truthiness idiom is now correct. Button DISPLAY and hotkeys are unchanged
    (still Yes/No), so existing UI tests that drive the dialog by key + assert
    the side effect keep passing.

    Also grep the recipes/ dir for any OTHER ctx.confirm usage and apply the
    same pattern if found. Do NOT touch the ctx.pick sites (browse-plan,
    browse-git) — their picked string IS the value the recipe needs, already
    reasonable.

    Recipes are NOT part of build-tui.sh (standalone files loaded at runtime),
    so NO rebuild is needed. Run the FULL ./run-tests-parallel.sh — especially
    test/ui/test_recipe_browse_fs.py and test/ui/test_recipe_browse_procs.py —
    to confirm the confirm flow still works.

    * ## Comments {#1005:comments}

      * Updated both ctx.confirm sites to use (label, value) mapping: recipes/browse-fs:237 (delete) and recipes/browse-procs:117 (kill_proc). Both now use [('&Yes', True), ('&No', False)] with 'if not ctx.confirm(...)' so only Yes (True) proceeds; No (False) and cancel (None) abort. Grep of recipes/ for 'ctx.confirm(' found exactly these two sites, no others. Left ctx.pick sites (browse-plan, browse-git) untouched per ticket. {#1005:comment:1006}

      * Found a third site needing a change: the _ActionCtx test double in test/unit/test_browse_fs.py (confirm(self, _msg) accepted only the prompt; default answer was the string 'Yes'). Updated it to confirm(self, _msg, _buttons=None) with default confirm=True, and refreshed its docstring to describe the new (label, value)/truthiness idiom. This is the unit-test mirror of the recipe change, not a production confirm call. * ## Ticket: browse-fs: markdown launcher rows (validate launcher-detach pattern) {#1009}

      status: done
      created: 2026-06-13 06:56:30 UTC
      updated: 2026-06-13 07:14:44 UTC

  First real exercise of the markdown launcher-detach machinery on a recipe
  that has no markdown today (browse-fs). A `.md` file row gains an optimistic
  expansion arrow; expanding it shows `»`-prefixed launcher rows that open
  `browse-md` as an external process (`ctx.run_external`). This is the
  low-risk validation before browse-claude is carved over (see
  docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-md-launcher-rows-design.md).

  Scope: extension-scoped (`.md` / `.MD` file rows only — no content-sniffing
  of other files yet). Faithful subtree shape (self-open row + one row per
  resolved link), NOT an Enter-on-file-row override. All launch targets are
  real on-disk files, so launches are plain argv (no stdin).

  Acceptance: a `.md` row expands to `» <label>` launcher rows (self first,
  then deduped/sorted existing-link rows); Enter on a launcher row launches
  `browse-md <abspath> --root <project_root>` via run_external; Enter on every
  other row keeps today's edit behavior; launcher-row preview shows the target
  file rendered; non-`.md` rows and dir rows are unchanged; tests pass.

  * ## Ticket: Task: Implement detection, launcher subtree, preview, and Enter dispatch {#1010}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:56:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:04:32 UTC

    Single cohesive change to `recipes/browse-fs` (~120 lines).

    1. Optional import of `md_doc` mirroring the existing `md2ansi_lib`
       try/except (set `_md_doc = None` on failure); whole feature is inert
       when absent.
    2. Listing-time arrow: set `has_children=True` for existing `.md`/`.MD`
       files in BOTH row builders — the `get_children` scandir loop and
       `_stdin_root_item`. Extension check only; no file read at listing time
       (stays lazy). Dirs unchanged.
    3. `get_children`: when `path` is a `str` naming an existing `.md`/`.MD`
       file, return `_md_launcher_children(path)` instead of falling through to
       scandir (which would error). Build: a self-open row for the file itself
       FIRST, then one row per resolved link — `md_doc.find_md_refs(text)` over
       the file's contents, `resolve_md_ref(captured, doc_dir=dirname(path),
       cwd=os.getcwd(), project_root=find_git_root(dirname) or dirname)`,
       existing-only, deduped by abspath (drop the self path), sorted by label.
       Each launcher row: id `('launch', path, 'md-file', target_abspath)`
       (hashable tuple, leaf — `has_children=False`), `tag='md'`,
       `tag_style='blue'`, `title='» ' + label`. Label = `os.path.relpath`
       from the file's project root (fallback cwd, then `~`-collapsed abs),
       matching the design's ref-label anchoring. A self-open row guarantees
       >=1 child, so NO self-heal hook is needed.
    4. `get_preview`: when id is `('launch', _, 'md-file', target)`, delegate
       to the existing file-preview path on `target` (so the pane shows the
       target file's rendered markdown — "see before launch").
    5. Enter dispatch: change `on_enter` from `'action:e'` to a callable
       `_on_enter(ctx)` that, when `ctx.cursor.id` is a `('launch', ...)`
       tuple, launches `run_external(['browse-md', target, '--root',
       project_root_of(target)])`; otherwise falls back to `edit(ctx)` (today's
       behavior, byte-for-byte for non-launcher rows). `project_root_of` =
       `md_doc.find_git_root(dirname(target)) or dirname(target)`.
    6. Keep launcher rows out of e/o/d: their ids are tuples, and those
       actions already skip non-`str` ids — confirm no regression.

    * ## Comments {#1010:comments}

      * Implemented in recipes/browse-fs: optional md_doc import; .md file rows get has_children (both row builders, gated on md_doc); get_children intercepts an existing .md path -> _md_launcher_children (self-open row first, then deduped/sorted existing .md links via find_md_refs/resolve_md_ref anchored on dir/cwd/project-root); get_preview delegates a ('launch',...) id to the target file; on_enter is now a callable dispatching launcher ids to browse-md <target> --root <gitroot>, else edit. Smoke-tested end-to-end; existing 30 browse-fs tests still pass. {#1010:comment:973}

  * ## Ticket: Task: Unit tests {#1011}

        status: done
        created: 2026-06-13 06:56:30 UTC
        updated: 2026-06-13 07:08:09 UTC

    Add to `test/unit/test_browse_fs.py` (follow TESTING.md; headless Browser
    / direct function calls; stub `run_external`). Cover:
    - `has_children` True for an existing `.md`, False for `.txt`/missing.
    - `_md_launcher_children`: self-open row present and first; resolved links
      deduped by abspath and sorted by label; non-existent / non-`.md` tokens
      dropped; self path excluded from the link rows; `()` -> still the lone
      self-open row (never empty).
    - launcher ids are leaf tuples (`('launch', ...)`, `has_children=False`).
    - `get_preview` on a launch id returns the TARGET file's preview (md2ansi
      branch honored when `_MD_COLOR` on).
    - `_on_enter`: a launch-id cursor builds argv `['browse-md', target,
      '--root', <gitroot>]` (run_external stubbed/captured); a regular file-row
      cursor still routes to edit.
    - `_md_doc is None` -> `.md` rows are plain leaves, no arrow, no launcher.

    * ## Comments {#1011:comments}

      * Added TestMdLauncher (15 cases) + _EnterCtx to test/unit/test_browse_fs.py: has_children gate (.md/.MD vs .txt/dir/stdin-root, inert when _md_doc None); launcher children (self-open first, links deduped+sorted, non-existent/non-md dropped, self excluded, lone-self on no-links/unreadable, get_children intercept parity, leaf+md-tag+» checks); preview delegation to target; on_enter dispatch (launcher→browse-md --root argv, regular row→edit, no-cursor noop). Full suite: 113 modules pass. {#1011:comment:974}
